profile.domain: SEC EDGAR Regulatory Filing Ingestion Pipeline

project_goal.value: Automated nightly ingestion of SEC EDGAR submissions across 10-K, 10-Q, and 8-K form types with normalization into a compliance data warehouse for audit-trail purposes at large financial institutions
project_goal.rationale: Core compliance need for tracking public company disclosures systematically

target_users.value: Compliance analysts, audit teams, and data engineers at financial institutions monitoring public company filings
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Batch ingestion and transformation with no interactive frontend

capabilities.rationale: Pipeline requires ETL, scheduled jobs, EDGAR API integration, and downstream analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-form-type parsing, compliance schema validation, and audit-trail requirements add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Confidential financial data and regulatory audit-trail obligations require high-assurance controls

data_sensitivity.rationale: Warehouse holds normalized financial disclosures used in regulatory review

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline output is consumed exclusively by internal compliance and analytics teams

access_model.value: IAM role-based access; pipeline runs under least-privilege ECS task roles; warehouse read access via named IAM roles per team
access_model.rationale: AWS-native access model avoids credential sprawl

feature_scope.value: Nightly EDGAR polling, SGML/XML parsing for 10-K/10-Q/8-K, compliance schema validation, discrepancy flagging, warehouse load, audit event recording, pipeline-run dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope covering ingestion through reporting

mvp_scope.value: Nightly 10-K and 10-Q ingestion from EDGAR Full-Text Search API, normalized load to PostgreSQL warehouse, basic pipeline-run status reporting
mvp_scope.rationale: 8-K and discrepancy flagging deferred to phase 2

security_baseline.value: Encryption at rest via AWS KMS, TLS 1.3 for all egress to EDGAR API, no raw filing data persisted outside encrypted volumes, pipeline secrets in AWS Secrets Manager
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential data classification requires encryption and secrets management

frontend_stack.value: None — internal pipeline with no browser UI; pipeline-run metrics exposed via Datadog dashboard
frontend_stack.rationale: data_pipeline project class requires no frontend

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.9 for orchestration; custom parser modules per form type
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow provides scheduling, retry logic, and DAG visibility; Python ecosystem has strong XML/SGML parsing libraries

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Amazon RDS as compliance warehouse; Redis 7 on ElastiCache for deduplication cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational warehouse suits structured normalized filings; Redis for fast filing-ID deduplication

hosting_target.value: Apache Airflow on ECS Fargate with Celery executor; RDS PostgreSQL in same VPC
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with VPC isolation for confidential data

privacy_retention_policy.value: Normalized filing records retained indefinitely per FINRA record-retention rules; raw SGML/XML source files retained 7 years in S3 Glacier; pipeline logs retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FINRA and SEC Reg S-K mandate long-term retention of disclosure records

future_scope.value: 8-K ingestion, real-time EDGAR streaming via RSS, ML-based anomaly detection on financial fields, self-service query layer for analysts
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2+ enhancements after warehouse is stable

constraints.value: Must not store raw PII beyond what EDGAR publishes; pipeline must complete nightly run within 4-hour window; budget under $2000/month
constraints.rationale: Regulatory and operational constraints from compliance team

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for Airflow task traces, structured JSON logging per DAG run, PagerDuty alerts on pipeline failure and SLA breach
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard for data platform

execution_preference.value: Iterative 2-week sprints with separate data-engineering and compliance-review tracks
execution_preference.rationale: Allows parallel parser development and compliance validation

compliance_context.value: SEC Regulation S-K disclosure requirements, SOC 2 Type II controls for data pipeline, FINRA record-retention rules (Rule 4511), internal audit trail obligations
compliance_context.rationale: risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=confidential mandate compliance framework

project.goal: Ingest and normalize SEC EDGAR filings into compliance warehouse with full audit trail
project.target_users: Compliance analysts and data engineers at financial institutions
project.timeline: Phase 1 in 10 weeks

security.auth: IAM roles for ECS tasks; Secrets Manager for API credentials
security.encryption: KMS at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.archive: S3 Glacier for raw source files

devops.ci: GitHub Actions

reasoner_reviews.product: The nightly EDGAR ingestion scope is well-bounded for MVP. Prioritizing 10-K and 10-Q parsers before 8-K is correct given 10-K/10-Q have more stable SGML structure. The discrepancy flagging workflow is critical for compliance team adoption and should not slip past phase 2.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Airflow on Fargate with Celery executor is the right fit for this workload. The 4-hour nightly window is achievable for current EDGAR filing volumes if DAG tasks are parallelized by CIK batch. Consider partitioning the PostgreSQL warehouse by filing_date to keep query performance predictable as volume grows.
reasoner_reviews.security: SOC 2 Type II controls require that all pipeline-run actions be logged to an immutable audit table. KMS key rotation policy must be documented. Secrets Manager rotation for EDGAR API credentials should be automated to prevent credential staleness.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $2000/month budget is tight for multi-AZ RDS plus Fargate. Recommend single-AZ RDS in dev/staging and reserved Fargate capacity for baseline workers to control costs. The 4-hour nightly window must be monitored with SLA alerts.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The normalization_rules entity is under-specified. Form-type-specific parsing logic should be codified in versioned rule manifests stored in the database, not hardcoded, so compliance teams can update field mappings without code deployments.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG per form type (dag_10k_ingest, dag_10q_ingest, dag_8k_ingest); shared operator library for EDGAR API polling, SGML tokenization, field extraction, and warehouse upsert
backend.api_patterns: EDGAR Full-Text Search API (EFTS) for submission discovery; direct S3 download of filing archives; internal warehouse upsert via SQLAlchemy bulk operations
backend.business_modules: edgar_poller (discovers new accession numbers), sgml_tokenizer (splits SGML into document sections), form_parser_10k / form_parser_10q (extract financial fields by section), schema_validator (checks against compliance schema registry), warehouse_loader (upserts to PostgreSQL), audit_emitter (writes pipeline_runs and audit_events)
backend.background_jobs: Nightly DAG triggered at 02:00 UTC; weekly reconciliation job comparing pipeline_runs counts against EDGAR total filing counts; monthly retention-sweep job archiving raw files to S3 Glacier
backend.failure_handling: Airflow task retries with exponential backoff (3 retries, 5/15/30 min delays); dead-letter queue in SQS for accession numbers that fail all retries; PagerDuty alert on DLQ depth > 0

security.auth_design: ECS task role with least-privilege IAM policy scoped to specific S3 buckets and RDS proxy endpoint; no long-lived credentials
security.secrets_management: EDGAR API credentials and RDS password in Secrets Manager with 30-day auto-rotation; secrets injected as environment variables at task startup via ECS secrets integration
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable audit_events table with INSERT-only IAM policy on the pipeline role; CloudTrail for all AWS API calls; structured JSON logs per Airflow task with accession_number, form_type, and outcome
security.security_risks: Third-party EDGAR API downtime could break nightly SLA; mitigate with local accession-number cache and fallback to direct S3 bulk download from EDGAR full-index files

data.entities: filings (id, accession_number, filer_cik, form_type ENUM 10-K/10-Q/8-K, filing_date, period_of_report, raw_s3_key, status ENUM pending/parsed/validated/loaded/failed, created_at), filers (id, cik, entity_name, sic_code, state_of_incorporation, first_seen_at), normalization_rules (id, form_type, field_key, extraction_xpath, version, active, updated_by), pipeline_runs (id, dag_id, run_date, filings_discovered, filings_loaded, filings_failed, duration_seconds, status), audit_events (id, pipeline_run_id, accession_number, action ENUM discovered/parsed/validated/loaded/flagged, detail_json, occurred_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with table partitioning on filings.filing_date (monthly ranges); read replica for analytics queries; S3 standard for raw files during processing, lifecycle to Glacier after 30 days
data.retention_and_deletion: Warehouse records retained indefinitely; raw S3 files transitioned to Glacier at 30 days, deleted at 7 years per FINRA Rule 4511; pipeline logs retained 1 year in CloudWatch
data.data_risks: Normalization logic drift if EDGAR changes SGML structure without notice; mitigate by versioning normalization_rules and alerting on field-extraction failure rate spikes

devops.deployment_topology: Airflow webserver and scheduler on ECS Fargate (dedicated tasks); Celery workers in auto-scaling ECS service (2-8 tasks based on SQS queue depth); RDS PostgreSQL in same VPC private subnet; ElastiCache Redis in same VPC
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (flake8/mypy), unit tests (pytest with 85% coverage gate), DAG integrity check (airflow dags test), Docker build and ECR push, ECS task definition update, Airflow DAG deployment via S3 sync
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with Airflow plugin for task-level traces; custom metrics: filings_ingested_total (counter), parser_error_rate (gauge per form_type), warehouse_load_latency_p99 (histogram); CloudWatch Logs with log group per DAG
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous Docker image tag pinned in ECS task definition; Airflow DAG rollback by re-deploying prior S3 DAG version; database schema migrations via Alembic with down-migration scripts

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a nightly EDGAR filing ingestion pipeline with Airflow on ECS Fargate. Key decisions: form-type-specific DAGs for independent scheduling and failure isolation; versioned normalization_rules table so compliance teams can update field mappings without code changes; INSERT-only audit_events policy to satisfy SOC 2 immutability requirements; monthly partition on filings.filing_date to keep warehouse queries fast as multi-year data accumulates.
target_output.title: SEC EDGAR Regulatory Filing Ingestion Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A nightly batch data pipeline built with Apache Airflow 2.9 on ECS Fargate that polls the EDGAR Full-Text Search API, parses 10-K, 10-Q, and 8-K SGML/XML filings using form-type-specific parser modules, validates extracted fields against a versioned compliance schema registry, and loads normalized records into a partitioned PostgreSQL 16 warehouse. Full audit trail via an immutable audit_events table satisfies SOC 2 Type II and FINRA record-retention obligations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-layer pipeline architecture: ingestion layer polls EDGAR EFTS API nightly for new accession numbers, downloads SGML/XML archives to ephemeral ECS task storage; transformation layer runs form-type-specific parsers (form_parser_10k, form_parser_10q) that extract structured financial fields and validate against the compliance schema registry stored in PostgreSQL; load layer performs bulk upserts into the partitioned filings warehouse. Airflow DAGs (dag_10k_ingest, dag_10q_ingest) coordinate task sequencing with inter-task XComs passing accession-number batches. ElastiCache Redis provides a filing-ID deduplication cache to prevent reprocessing. All processed raw SGML files are archived to S3 and transitioned to Glacier after 30 days. The pipeline runs entirely within a VPC private subnet with no public internet exposure beyond the EDGAR API egress.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 with Celery executor, Redis 7 broker. Runtime: Python 3.12 with lxml for SGML/XML parsing, SQLAlchemy 2.0 for ORM, Pydantic 2 for schema validation models. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Amazon RDS with monthly range partitioning, read replica for analytics. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for deduplication. Storage: S3 standard for active raw files, S3 Glacier for 7-year archive. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate for Airflow webserver, scheduler, and Celery workers; ECR for container images. Observability: Datadog APM with Airflow integration, PagerDuty for SLA alerting.
target_output.functional_feature_map: EDGAR Polling Module: queries EFTS API for accession numbers newer than last successful run date, deduplicates against Redis cache, enqueues new IDs for download. Form-Type Parser Engine: routes each accession number to the correct parser based on form_type field; parsers use versioned extraction rules from normalization_rules table. Compliance Schema Validator: runs each parsed record through Pydantic models derived from the schema registry, flags discrepancies with field-level error codes. Warehouse Loader: performs bulk upsert into filings partition for the current month, updates filers dimension table. Audit Emitter: writes one audit_events row per pipeline action (discovered, parsed, validated, loaded, flagged) with full context JSON. Pipeline Run Dashboard: Datadog dashboard showing daily filings_ingested_total, parser_error_rate by form_type, and warehouse_load_latency_p99.
target_output.system_components: EDGAR EFTS API (external source), S3 bucket for raw filing archives, ECS Fargate service for Airflow scheduler and webserver, ECS auto-scaling service for Celery workers (2-8 tasks), ElastiCache Redis 7 for Celery broker and deduplication cache, RDS PostgreSQL 16 primary with monthly-partitioned filings table and read replica, SQS dead-letter queue for failed accession numbers, Secrets Manager for EDGAR credentials and RDS password, CloudWatch Logs for structured task logs, Datadog for APM traces and dashboards.
target_output.workflows: EDGAR Nightly Ingestion: Airflow DAG triggers at 02:00 UTC; edgar_poller task calls EFTS API with date filter, receives list of new accession numbers, checks each against Redis deduplication cache, writes new IDs to pipeline_runs table, enqueues CIK-batched download tasks. Download and Parse tasks run in parallel Celery workers, each downloading SGML archive from EDGAR S3, tokenizing into document sections, routing to form_parser_10k or form_parser_10q, extracting fields using normalization_rules XPaths. Validated records are bulk-upserted into filings partition; audit_emitter writes one row per accession number. Discrepancy Flagging Workflow: schema_validator compares extracted fields against Pydantic compliance models; fields failing validation are recorded in a discrepancies JSON column on the filings row and the status is set to flagged; a daily digest email lists all flagged filings with error codes for compliance analyst review.
target_output.data_model: filings (id UUID PK, accession_number VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE, filer_cik VARCHAR(10) FK filers.cik, form_type ENUM 10-K/10-Q/8-K, filing_date DATE PARTITION KEY, period_of_report DATE, raw_s3_key TEXT, normalized_fields_json JSONB, discrepancies_json JSONB, status ENUM pending/parsed/validated/loaded/flagged, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) PARTITION BY RANGE (filing_date). filers (id UUID PK, cik VARCHAR(10) UNIQUE, entity_name TEXT, sic_code CHAR(4), state_of_incorporation CHAR(2), first_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). normalization_rules (id UUID PK, form_type ENUM, field_key TEXT, extraction_xpath TEXT, version INT, active BOOL, updated_by TEXT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, dag_id TEXT, run_date DATE, filings_discovered INT, filings_loaded INT, filings_failed INT, duration_seconds INT, status ENUM success/partial/failed, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, accession_number TEXT, action ENUM discovered/parsed/validated/loaded/flagged, detail_json JSONB, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ). INSERT-only IAM policy on audit_events for pipeline role.
target_output.api_design: Internal Airflow REST API for DAG management (used by CI/CD only): POST /api/v1/dags/dag_10k_ingest/dagRuns (trigger manual run), GET /api/v1/dags/dag_10k_ingest/dagRuns/{run_id} (check run status). Internal warehouse query endpoints exposed to analytics team via read-only RDS proxy: no public API surface. EDGAR EFTS integration: GET https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=%22form-type%3A10-K%22&dateRange=custom&startdt={date} for accession discovery. S3 presigned URL pattern for raw file downloads to Celery workers.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 Type II: immutable audit_events table enforced via INSERT-only IAM policy on pipeline task role; all pipeline-run outcomes logged with accession_number and action enum. FINRA Rule 4511: raw SGML files retained in S3 Glacier for 7 years; warehouse records retained indefinitely. SEC Reg S-K: field extraction rules versioned in normalization_rules table with updated_by audit column. KMS encryption for RDS storage and S3 buckets. TLS 1.3 for all EDGAR API egress. Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation for EDGAR credentials. VPC private subnets with no direct internet ingress. CloudTrail enabled for all AWS API calls in the pipeline account.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: flake8 linting, mypy type checking, pytest with 85% coverage gate, Airflow DAG integrity check via airflow dags test, Docker build and ECR push with commit SHA tag. ECS task definitions updated via aws ecs register-task-definition; Airflow DAG files synced to S3 DAG bucket triggering Airflow DAG refresh. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, 2 Celery workers, shortened nightly window), staging (mirrors production topology, run against EDGAR staging index), production (multi-AZ RDS, auto-scaling 2-8 Celery workers, SLA alert if nightly run exceeds 4 hours). Rollback: re-deploy previous ECR image tag via ECS task definition update; Airflow DAG rollback by restoring previous DAG file version from S3 versioning. Database migrations via Alembic with tested down-migration scripts.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with Airflow integration captures task-level traces with accession_number as span tag. Custom metrics: filings_ingested_total (counter, tagged by form_type), parser_error_rate (gauge per form_type), warehouse_load_latency_p99 (histogram), sqs_dlq_depth (gauge). CloudWatch Logs structured JSON per task with dag_id, task_id, accession_number, and outcome. PagerDuty alerts: nightly run duration > 4 hours (SLA breach), parser_error_rate > 5% for any form_type, sqs_dlq_depth > 0, RDS connection pool > 80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ~$1600/month: RDS PostgreSQL single-AZ production ~$400, Fargate Airflow webserver+scheduler ~$150, Celery workers (avg 3 tasks) ~$300, ElastiCache Redis ~$100, S3 + Glacier ~$50, Datadog ~$400, misc ~$200. Scaling: Celery worker auto-scaling on SQS queue depth allows parallel processing of CIK batches; monthly filing volumes have low variance so reserved Fargate baseline (2 workers) covers 80% of nightly runs with on-demand burst for quarter-end 10-K spikes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): EDGAR EFTS polling DAG, 10-K and 10-Q SGML parsers with initial normalization_rules for key financial fields (revenue, net_income, EPS, period_of_report), PostgreSQL warehouse with monthly partitioning, audit_events logging, Datadog observability, CI/CD pipeline, SOC 2 controls documented. Phase 2 (6 weeks): 8-K ingestion DAG, discrepancy flagging with analyst email digest, versioned normalization_rules UI for compliance team, SQS dead-letter queue with retry dashboard. Phase 3 (8 weeks): ML-based anomaly detection on extracted financial fields, real-time EDGAR RSS streaming to supplement nightly batch, self-service SQL query layer via read-only RDS proxy for analyst team.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type annotations required throughout; mypy strict mode in CI. Pydantic v2 models for all schema validation — no ad-hoc dict validation. Parser modules follow a common BaseParser interface: parse(sgml_bytes) -> ParsedFiling. Normalization rules must be updated via database migration, not code changes, to maintain audit trail. 85% pytest coverage gate enforced in CI. All Airflow DAGs must pass airflow dags test before merge. ADR documents required for changes to partitioning scheme or normalization approach.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EDGAR SGML structure changes without notice breaking parser XPaths. Mitigation: monitor field_extraction_failure_rate per normalization_rule; alert if any rule fails on > 1% of filings in a run; versioned rules allow fast rollback. Risk: Nightly 4-hour SLA breach during Q4 10-K filing surge. Mitigation: Celery worker auto-scaling on SQS depth plus pre-scaled reserved capacity in October-November. Tradeoff: monthly partition granularity chosen over quarterly for more predictable partition pruning on date-range queries. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over a dedicated time-series DB because normalized financial fields require relational joins across filers and normalization_rules, not time-series aggregations.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected PostgreSQL 16 over Redshift because filing volumes (~500K records/year) do not justify MPP overhead and relational joins are primary access pattern. Confirmed S3 Glacier over Deep Archive for 7-year raw file retention to meet FINRA's 48-hour retrieval SLA. Chose Celery executor over KubernetesExecutor to avoid GKE overhead given ECS-native infrastructure. Airflow 2.9 selected over Prefect to align with existing institutional Airflow expertise.

metadata.notes: SEC EDGAR nightly ingestion pipeline with Airflow on Fargate, SOC 2 and FINRA compliance controls, versioned normalization rules.

profile.domain: Federal Court Docket Graph Analysis Research Prototype

project_goal.value: Research prototype ingesting PACER bulk export files to build a case-relationship graph across federal circuits and generate LLM-assisted statistical trend summaries for legal researchers
project_goal.rationale: Core research objective to surface anomalous litigation clusters and circuit-level trends

target_users.value: Legal researchers and empirical law scholars at academic institutions analyzing federal litigation patterns
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers identified in research grant proposal

project_class.rationale: Exploratory research system with evolving analysis requirements

capabilities.rationale: Graph ingestion, analytical Cypher queries, LLM summarization, and Voila researcher interface

complexity_level.rationale: Graph database with multi-hop traversal and LLM integration adds significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Internal research data with no PHI; risk from data accuracy and LLM output reliability

data_sensitivity.rationale: PACER data is public record but treated as internal research dataset per institutional policy

external_exposure.rationale: Voila dashboards accessible only to authenticated researchers on institutional VPN

access_model.value: Custom JWT RS256 issued on institutional SSO login; researcher read-only access to graph and summary APIs; admin role for ingest endpoint
access_model.rationale: Lightweight auth appropriate for internal research prototype

feature_scope.value: PACER bulk export ingestion, case-relationship graph construction, circuit-level trend statistics, LLM trend report generation, Voila researcher dashboard, consolidation cluster detection
feature_scope.rationale: Full research feature set per grant deliverables

mvp_scope.value: PACER ZIP ingestion into Neo4j, appeal and citation edge construction, FastAPI statistics endpoints, Vertex AI Gemini trend summaries, Voila notebook interface
mvp_scope.rationale: Consolidation edges and anomaly detection added in round 2

security_baseline.value: Custom JWT RS256 with Redis blacklist, HTTPS via GKE ingress TLS, no PACER credentials in code, VPN-only access to Voila
security_baseline.rationale: Internal prototype requires transport and auth controls

frontend_stack.value: Jupyter Notebooks with Voila; Plotly for graph and trend visualizations
frontend_stack.rationale: Researchers are comfortable with notebook interface; Voila serves without code exposure

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; Neo4j async driver; google-cloud-aiplatform SDK for Vertex AI function calling
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async suits concurrent researcher queries; Vertex AI Gemini enforces structured output via function calling

data_platform.value: Neo4j 5 with GDS library on GKE StatefulSet; GCS for PACER ZIP archive storage
data_platform.rationale: Native graph storage for multi-hop traversal; GDS for community detection algorithms

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in us-central1; FastAPI and Voila as Deployments; Neo4j as StatefulSet
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Kubernetes reduces operational overhead for research prototype

privacy_retention_policy.value: PACER data retained 3 years per institutional data governance; LLM summaries retained 1 year; researcher access logs retained 6 months
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: PACER data use policy and institutional governance

future_scope.value: Automated daily PACER delta ingestion, cross-circuit judge mobility analysis, citation PageRank scoring, public research portal with anonymized export
future_scope.rationale: Post-grant deliverables contingent on continued funding

constraints.value: LLM provider must be GCP Vertex AI per institutional data governance; budget under $1500/month on GCP; no bulk redistribution of raw PACER docket text via API
constraints.rationale: Institutional data governance and budget constraints

observability_baseline.value: Grafana plus Prometheus for GKE metrics; structured JSON logs per API request with researcher_id and query_type; Neo4j query duration metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard GKE observability stack for research infrastructure

execution_preference.value: 4-week research sprints with biweekly demos to PI and grant reviewers
execution_preference.rationale: Research prototype cadence aligned with academic review schedule

llm_integration.value: Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro with function calling and strict ResponseSchema for trend report generation; Pydantic model validates returned function call arguments before Voila display
llm_integration.rationale: Institutional mandate for GCP Vertex AI; function calling eliminates free-form response risk identified in round 1

project.goal: Graph-based litigation pattern analysis with LLM-assisted trend reporting
project.target_users: Legal researchers on institutional VPN
project.timeline: Round 2 iteration implementing consolidation edges and fixing LLM output

frontend.visualization: Plotly for case network graphs and trend charts

backend.llm: Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro via google-cloud-aiplatform SDK with function calling

security.auth: Custom JWT RS256 with Redis blacklist

data.db: Neo4j 5 with GDS on GKE StatefulSet

devops.ci: GitHub Actions

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 1 delivered core ingestion and basic Cypher statistics. Consolidation edge construction is the most valuable missing research feature — consolidated cases often show circuit-shopping patterns that are the PI's primary research interest. LLM summaries received positive feedback but unreliable JSON output blocks dashboard functionality for about one in four requests.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Switching Gemini to function calling with a ResponseSchema is the correct fix — the Vertex AI platform validates returned arguments against the schema at the API level, providing a stronger guarantee than prompt instructions. For consolidation edge construction, the PACER docket XML consolidation_docket_number field is the right mapping source. GDS Louvain community detection is appropriate for identifying multi-case consolidation clusters with transitive relationships.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 1 JWT implementation lacked token revocation. A Redis SET keyed by token jti is the lowest-overhead solution — O(1) lookup, TTL matches token expiry. PACER access logs need to be immutable for institutional audit compliance.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GCP budget at $1500/month is workable. Gemini function call costs are predictable with ResponseSchema — shorter than free-form responses, reducing token spend per summary.

backend.service_design: FastAPI with routers: /circuits (aggregate stats per circuit), /cases (node lookup and neighbor traversal), /trends (LLM summary generation), /ingest (admin PACER ZIP processing)
backend.business_modules: pacer_ingester (ZIP extraction, docket XML parsing, Neo4j node creation), edge_builder (APPEALED_TO, CITED_BY, CONSOLIDATED_WITH edge construction), consolidation_detector (GDS Louvain on CONSOLIDATED_WITH subgraph), llm_summarizer (Vertex AI function calling with ResponseSchema, Pydantic validation), stats_aggregator (Cypher aggregations for circuit metrics)
backend.failure_handling: Vertex AI failures fall back to cached summary from previous run stored as Neo4j Circuit node property; Neo4j query timeout 30s with graceful error response; PACER ingestion logs partial progress with last processed docket_id for resume

security.auth_design: RS256 JWT signed with institutional SSO private key; middleware validates token and checks jti against Redis blacklist SET on every request; researcher_id extracted from sub claim for structured access logs
security.authorization_model: researcher role: read-only access to all graph query endpoints; admin role required for POST /ingest
security.secrets_management: GCP Secret Manager for Neo4j credentials and Vertex AI service account key; mounted as environment variables in GKE pod specs

data.entities: Case (neo4j node: case_id, docket_number, case_title, circuit ENUM 1st-11th/DC/Fed, filing_date, disposition), Docket (neo4j node: docket_id, case_id, entry_date, entry_text, docket_type), Party (neo4j node: party_id, party_name, party_type), Judge (neo4j node: judge_id, judge_name, circuit), Circuit (neo4j node: circuit_id, name, state_count), consolidated_groups (neo4j node: group_id, lead_case_id, member_count, detected_at). Relationships: APPEALED_TO (Case->Case), CITED_BY (Case->Case, citation_type), CONSOLIDATED_WITH (Case<->Case, consolidation_date, lead_case_id)
data.analytics_events: Researcher query events logged to structured JSON: researcher_id, endpoint, circuit_filter, date_range, response_time_ms, result_count

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot: FastAPI Deployment 2 replicas, Voila Deployment 2 replicas, Neo4j StatefulSet primary plus read replica, Redis Deployment for JWT blacklist, Prometheus and Grafana Deployments in monitoring namespace
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest 80% coverage gate, mypy strict, Docker build with commit SHA tag, push GCR, kubectl apply with kustomize overlay (dev uses single-replica Neo4j community edition)
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping FastAPI /metrics and Neo4j query metrics; Grafana: llm_function_call_success_rate, cypher_query_duration_p95 by query_type, pacer_ingestion_node_count, gds_community_detection_duration_seconds

ISS-001.title: LLM summaries return free-form text instead of structured JSON
ISS-001.detail: Gemini 1.5 Pro responses used prompt-only JSON instructions in round 1; model occasionally returned narrative prose causing Voila dashboard parsing failures on ~25% of requests.

history.note: Identified during researcher demo; three of twelve test summaries were malformed

ISS-002.title: JWT token revocation not implemented

previous_plan.title: Federal Court Docket Graph Analysis System v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Round 1 prototype ingesting PACER bulk exports into Neo4j with FastAPI statistics API and Voila dashboards. Gemini trend summaries via prompt-only JSON instructions. Consolidation edge construction deferred.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI on GKE querying Neo4j 5. PACER ZIPs ingested via admin endpoint. Gemini 1.5 Pro called with prompt-only JSON instructions for trend summaries. No JWT revocation.
previous_plan.data_model: Cases, dockets, parties, judges, circuits as Neo4j nodes. APPEALED_TO and CITED_BY edges built. CONSOLIDATED_WITH defined in schema but not populated by edge_builder.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: RS256 JWT auth, HTTPS via GKE ingress. No Redis blacklist for token revocation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 fixes both high-severity issues: switching Gemini to function calling with a strict ResponseSchema eliminates the ~25% Voila parsing failure rate, and implementing CONSOLIDATED_WITH edge construction in edge_builder unblocks the PI consolidation cluster analysis. Also adding Redis JWT blacklist to resolve ISS-002. Key architectural decision is using GDS Louvain community detection for consolidation clusters because cases are sometimes consolidated transitively across multiple consolidation orders, requiring community detection rather than simple connected-components.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced prompt-only JSON instructions in llm_summarizer with Vertex AI function calling using a strict ResponseSchema defining the TrendSummary object (circuit, period, filing_count, disposition_rates dict, anomaly_clusters list, narrative_text). Added Pydantic TrendSummary model validation on returned function call arguments before passing to Voila. Gemini function call response schema validated at Vertex AI API level.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Gemini always returns structured JSON matching ResponseSchema; Voila parsing failures eliminated; narrative_text provides researcher-readable prose alongside structured statistics

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented CONSOLIDATED_WITH edge construction in edge_builder by parsing consolidation_docket_number field from PACER docket XML entries. When entry contains non-null consolidation_docket_number, edge_builder creates CONSOLIDATED_WITH relationship between Case nodes with consolidation_date and lead_case_id properties. Added consolidation_detector module running GDS Louvain algorithm on CONSOLIDATED_WITH subgraph to create consolidated_group nodes.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CONSOLIDATED_WITH edges populated on next full PACER re-ingestion; consolidation_clusters.ipynb Voila dashboard functional; PI consolidation analysis deliverable unblocked

target_output.title: Federal Court Docket Graph Analysis System v2
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype on GKE Autopilot ingesting PACER bulk export ZIPs into Neo4j 5 to build a federal case relationship graph with APPEALED_TO, CITED_BY, and CONSOLIDATED_WITH edges. FastAPI serves circuit statistics and LLM trend summaries consumed by Voila notebook dashboards. Round 2 resolves both high-severity issues: Gemini integration switched to function calling with strict ResponseSchema (eliminating ~25% parsing failure rate), and consolidation edge construction implemented via PACER consolidation_docket_number field mapping with GDS Louvain community detection for cluster analysis.
target_output.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot hosts four workloads in research-prod namespace: FastAPI backend (2 replicas) serving /circuits, /cases, /trends, and /ingest endpoints; Voila server (2 replicas) rendering notebook dashboards; Neo4j 5 StatefulSet with persistent GCP Persistent Disk and a read replica; Redis Deployment for JWT blacklist (new). PACER bulk ZIPs land in GCS, processed by pacer_ingester via the admin /ingest endpoint. edge_builder now constructs all three relationship types including CONSOLIDATED_WITH from the consolidation_docket_number XML field. consolidation_detector runs GDS Louvain on the CONSOLIDATED_WITH subgraph to surface consolidated_group nodes. LLM trend summaries use Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro with function calling enforcing a strict ResponseSchema, replacing the unreliable prompt-only approach. All endpoints protected by RS256 JWT with Redis blacklist, accessible only via institutional VPN.
target_output.technology_stack: Graph DB: Neo4j 5 with GDS library on GKE StatefulSet. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI async, neo4j async driver, google-cloud-aiplatform SDK. LLM: Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro with function calling ResponseSchema. Validation: Pydantic v2 TrendSummary model. Frontend: Jupyter with Voila, Plotly for network and trend charts. Auth: RS256 JWT with Redis 7 blacklist. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot us-central1, GCS, GCP Secret Manager. Observability: Prometheus plus Grafana. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with kustomize overlays.
target_output.functional_feature_map: PACER Ingestion: admin-triggered ZIP processing creates Case, Docket, Party, Judge nodes and all three edge types including new CONSOLIDATED_WITH. Circuit Trend Statistics: /circuits endpoint aggregates filing counts, disposition rates, and median resolution time per circuit per year via Cypher. LLM Trend Summaries (fixed): Gemini function calling returns structured TrendSummary JSON — Pydantic validated before Voila render. Consolidation Cluster Analysis (new): GDS Louvain on CONSOLIDATED_WITH subgraph surfaces consolidated_group nodes; consolidation_clusters.ipynb shows circuit-shopping patterns. Case Neighbor Traversal: /cases endpoint with multi-hop Cypher for appeal chains and citation clusters.
target_output.system_components: GCS bucket for PACER ZIP staging, FastAPI Deployment (2 replicas) with async Neo4j driver, Neo4j 5 StatefulSet (primary plus read replica) with GDS library, Voila Deployment (2 replicas) serving notebook apps, Redis Deployment for JWT blacklist, Prometheus and Grafana Deployments in monitoring namespace, GCP Secret Manager for Neo4j password and Vertex AI service account, Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro via google-cloud-aiplatform (GCP-internal).
target_output.workflows: PACER Bulk Ingestion: researcher admin POSTs GCS archive path to /ingest; pacer_ingester unzips, parses docket XML, creates Case nodes (circuit, filing_date, disposition), Docket nodes, Party nodes; passes docket entries to edge_builder which creates APPEALED_TO on appeal_case_number, CITED_BY on citation_reference, and CONSOLIDATED_WITH on consolidation_docket_number (new). After ingestion consolidation_detector runs GDS Louvain on CONSOLIDATED_WITH subgraph and creates consolidated_group nodes. LLM Trend Report (fixed): researcher selects circuit and year range in circuit_trends.ipynb; Voila calls GET /trends; FastAPI runs Cypher aggregation for filing stats; llm_summarizer formats stats as Vertex AI function call payload with strict TrendSummary ResponseSchema; Vertex AI validates and returns structured function call arguments; Pydantic model validates; Voila renders narrative_text alongside Plotly disposition_rates bar chart and anomaly_cluster highlights.
target_output.data_model: Case (neo4j node: case_id UUID, docket_number TEXT, case_title TEXT, circuit ENUM 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/5th/6th/7th/8th/9th/10th/11th/DC/Fed, filing_date DATE, disposition ENUM pending/dismissed/appealed/affirmed/reversed, judge_id). Docket (neo4j node: docket_id UUID, case_id, entry_date, entry_text, docket_type ENUM order/motion/judgment). Party (neo4j node: party_id UUID, party_name TEXT, party_type ENUM plaintiff/defendant/appellant/appellee). Judge (neo4j node: judge_id UUID, judge_name TEXT, circuit, appointment_year INT). Circuit (neo4j node: circuit_id, name, state_count). consolidated_groups (neo4j node: group_id UUID, lead_case_id, member_count INT, detected_at DATETIME). Relationships: APPEALED_TO (Case->Case, appeal_date), CITED_BY (Case->Case, citation_type ENUM binding/persuasive), CONSOLIDATED_WITH (Case<->Case, consolidation_date, lead_case_id — new in round 2). Indexes on Case.circuit, Case.filing_date, Case.docket_number for Cypher performance.
target_output.api_design: GET /circuits/{circuit_id}/stats?start_year=2018&end_year=2024 returns {filing_count, disposition_breakdown, median_resolution_days, appeal_rate}. GET /cases/{case_id}/neighbors?depth=2&rel_types=APPEALED_TO,CITED_BY returns subgraph for Plotly network render. GET /trends?circuit={circuit_id}&year_range=2020-2024 triggers Gemini function call, returns validated TrendSummary JSON. GET /cases/consolidated?group_id={group_id} returns consolidated_group with member cases. POST /ingest (admin JWT required) accepts {gcs_path: 'gs://pacer-archives/export-2024.zip'}, triggers async ingestion.
target_output.security_and_compliance: RS256 JWT middleware on all FastAPI routes; token jti checked against Redis blacklist SET before accepting (new in round 2) — account suspension triggers SET add with TTL matching token expiry. researcher_id from JWT sub claim logged on every request as structured JSON. PACER data use policy: /ingest restricted to admin role; no raw docket text bulk export via API. GCP Secret Manager for Neo4j password and Vertex AI service account. HTTPS via GKE ingress with GCP-managed TLS certificate. Researcher access logs (researcher_id, endpoint, query_params, response_code) retained 6 months in GCS.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest 80% coverage gate, mypy strict, Docker build with commit SHA tag, push to GCR, kubectl apply with kustomize overlay (dev: single-replica Neo4j community edition, research-prod: StatefulSet with read replica and GDS library in custom image). GCS-backed PACER archive bucket with uniform bucket-level access and 3-year object lifecycle. GKE Autopilot manages node provisioning; resource requests set for Neo4j StatefulSet (4 CPU, 16GB) to prevent autopilot under-provisioning. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo deployment/fastapi-backend; Neo4j data preserved on persistent volume; GDS library version pinned in Dockerfile.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics and Neo4j metrics endpoint. Custom metrics: llm_function_call_success_rate (gauge — target 100%), cypher_query_duration_seconds histogram by query_type, pacer_ingestion_node_count counter by node_label, gds_louvain_detection_duration_seconds. Grafana dashboards: LLM Reliability (function_call_success_rate, pydantic_validation_failure_rate), Graph Query Performance (cypher_p95 by circuit), Ingestion Progress (nodes and edges created by type). Alert: function_call_success_rate < 99% triggers Slack notification; cypher_p95 > 5s triggers investigation.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ~$1200/month on GCP: GKE Autopilot nodes for Neo4j e2-standard-4 (~$500), FastAPI and Voila pods (~$150), Vertex AI Gemini token costs at ~2000 summaries/month (~$200), GCS storage (~$50), Prometheus/Grafana (~$100), misc (~$200). Cost control: Gemini function call responses cached as TrendSummary JSON on Circuit node property in Neo4j with 24-hour TTL to reduce token spend; cache invalidated on new ingestion run for that circuit's date range.
target_output.phased_implementation: Round 2 (current, 4 weeks): CONSOLIDATED_WITH edge construction from PACER consolidation_docket_number, GDS Louvain consolidation_detector, Gemini function calling with ResponseSchema, Redis JWT blacklist. Round 3 (4 weeks): Automated daily PACER delta ingestion replacing manual ZIP uploads, citation network PageRank via GDS PageRank algorithm, judge mobility subgraph analysis. Round 4 (4 weeks): Public anonymized research export API, cross-circuit comparative trend dashboards, performance optimization for 10M+ node graph scale.
target_output.development_guidelines: Cypher queries must be parameterized — no researcher input string interpolation. Vertex AI function call schemas defined as Pydantic models and exported to both the SDK ResponseSchema and API response validation. GDS algorithm runs preceded by graph projection creation in the same transaction. New edge types require a Cypher index on the source node property used for lookup. 80% pytest coverage enforced; graph construction tests use seeded Neo4j test container via testcontainers-python.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PACER XML structure varies across districts causing docket field parsing failures. Mitigation: parser emits field_extraction_failure metric per field_key; alert on spike; versioned parser configs in GCS allow fast rollback. Risk: Gemini function call ResponseSchema changes between SDK versions. Mitigation: pin google-cloud-aiplatform SDK version; integration test validates function call response shape on each CI run. Tradeoff: GDS Louvain over connected-components for consolidation detection because cases consolidate transitively across multiple orders; community detection handles transitive membership that simple components miss.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed function calling over prompt-engineering for Gemini JSON — Vertex AI validates returned arguments against ResponseSchema at the API level. Chose GDS Louvain over connected-components for consolidation because of transitive consolidation chains. Redis selected over Neo4j property for JWT blacklist to avoid adding write load to the graph database on every authenticated request.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision fixing Gemini structured output via function calling and adding CONSOLIDATED_WITH edge construction for court docket graph research prototype.

profile.domain: SharePoint to CLM Contract Repository Migration Tool

project_goal.value: Automation tool migrating legacy contract repositories from on-premise SharePoint document libraries to a cloud-native CLM platform, preserving metadata mappings, version histories, and translated access controls for mid-size law firms
project_goal.rationale: Core migration need for firms moving off aging SharePoint infrastructure

target_users.value: IT administrators and legal operations managers at mid-size law firms executing SharePoint-to-CLM migrations
target_users.rationale: Primary operators identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Batch migration orchestration with no interactive user UI

capabilities.rationale: SharePoint REST API integration, CLM API integration, and batch document processing

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-system API integration with metadata mapping and ACL translation adds moderate complexity

risk_level.rationale: Confidential legal documents in transit; migration errors could cause data loss or privilege exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Contract documents include attorney-client privileged material and GDPR-covered client data

external_exposure.rationale: Tool runs within law firm network; no public API surface

access_model.value: Azure AD service principal with SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll scope; tool invoked by IT admin under named user account for audit trail
access_model.rationale: Azure-native identity model; named account enables per-migration audit logging

feature_scope.value: SharePoint library enumeration, metadata field mapping via configurable manifest, document download and CLM upload with ACL translation, version history preservation, per-document migration log, reconciliation report
feature_scope.rationale: Full migration feature set

mvp_scope.value: Single SharePoint library migration with static metadata mapping manifest, document upload to CLM, per-document migration log, basic reconciliation CSV
mvp_scope.rationale: Multi-library batch scheduling and ACL translation added in round 2

security_baseline.value: Azure AD service principal with least-privilege SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll scope; TLS 1.3 for all API calls; no document content persisted to local disk beyond transient buffer; migration logs exclude document content
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential data requires minimal local footprint and strong transport

frontend_stack.value: None — automation tool invoked via CLI; migration status output to stdout and structured log files
frontend_stack.rationale: automation_tool project class uses CLI invocation, not browser UI

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with TypeScript; @azure/msal-node for Azure AD auth; @microsoft/microsoft-graph-client for SharePoint; CLM vendor SDK for upload
backend_stack.rationale: TypeScript for type-safe metadata mapping; Azure SDK ecosystem for SharePoint integration

data_platform.value: SQLite for migration state tracking (source_documents, migration_logs); no persistent document storage
data_platform.rationale: Lightweight embedded DB for single-run migration state; no server infra required

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Instances for scheduled batch runs; local Node.js invocation for dry-run and planning
hosting_target.rationale: ACI provides isolated container execution within law firm Azure tenant

privacy_retention_policy.value: Migration logs retained 7 years per attorney-client privilege obligations; SQLite state DB purged after successful reconciliation; no contract document content retained outside CLM
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Attorney-client privilege and GDPR data transfer requirements

future_scope.value: Bulk multi-library batch scheduling, SharePoint Online support, CLM taxonomy auto-mapping via ML, automated post-migration access audit
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enhancements after core migration validated

constraints.value: Must run within law firm Azure tenant; no document content outside firm Azure subscription; batch run must complete within 8-hour maintenance window for 10K documents
constraints.rationale: Data sovereignty and maintenance window constraints from legal ops team

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for ACI container logs; structured JSON migration log file per run; per-document outcome recorded in SQLite migration_logs table
observability_baseline.rationale: Azure-native monitoring within firm tenant

execution_preference.value: Phased migration with dry-run mode before each live batch
execution_preference.rationale: Dry-run validation reduces risk of bulk migration errors

compliance_context.value: Attorney-client privilege retention under ABA Model Rule 1.6; GDPR Article 46 data transfer requirements for EU-based client documents; firm data governance policy requiring migration audit trail
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=confidential and risk_level=medium mandate compliance tracking

project.goal: Migrate SharePoint contract libraries to CLM preserving metadata, versions, and ACLs
project.target_users: IT admins and legal ops managers
project.timeline: Round 2 adds ACL translation and multi-library scheduling

security.auth: Azure AD service principal with SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll scope

data.storage: No persistent document storage outside CLM

devops.ci: Azure DevOps

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 1 validated the core migration path for a single SharePoint library. The two features most requested by legal ops pilots are ACL translation (documents currently land with default-open CLM permissions, exposing confidential contracts) and multi-library batch scheduling so entire practice-group repositories migrate in one maintenance window.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ACL translation should use a configurable permission_sets manifest mapping SharePoint principals (AD group SIDs, site group names, named users) to CLM role IDs. Dry-run mode must simulate ACL translation and report unmapped principals before live execution. Multi-library scheduling via SQLite migration_batches queue with a batch coordinator is the right pattern for sequential library processing within the 8-hour window.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 1 ACL drop is a high-severity privilege-exposure risk for confidential attorney-client documents. ACL translation must be verified in dry-run mode before any live batch. Service principal scope should be narrowed from Sites.Read.All to SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 8-hour window for 10K documents requires ~20 documents/minute throughput. CLM vendor API allows 5 concurrent uploads. A 5-worker concurrent pool with backoff on 429 responses achieves this comfortably.

backend.service_design: CLI entrypoint (migrate.ts) with subcommands: enumerate, plan, dry-run, execute, reconcile
backend.business_modules: library_enumerator (SharePoint Graph API pagination), metadata_mapper (field_mapping_manifest to CLM schema), acl_translator (SharePoint principals to CLM roles via permission_sets manifest), document_uploader (CLM SDK with retry), version_history_fetcher (Graph API versions), migration_state_manager (SQLite reads/writes)
backend.failure_handling: Per-document retry with 3 attempts and exponential backoff; failed documents written to migration_logs with error_code; batch coordinator skips failed documents and continues

security.auth_design: @azure/msal-node ConfidentialClientApplication with service principal client credentials; scope narrowed to SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll and CLM API scope
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for service principal client secret and CLM API token; fetched via DefaultAzureCredential at startup; never written to disk
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Per-document migration_logs entry with source_url, source_document_id, target_clm_id, acl_translation_result, outcome, migrated_at; logs written to Azure Blob Storage for 7-year retention

data.entities: source_documents (id, library_id, source_url, title, content_type, source_metadata_json, version_count, status ENUM pending/uploading/migrated/failed), migration_batches (id, library_path, batch_name, scheduled_at, started_at, completed_at, total_docs, migrated_count, failed_count, status ENUM planned/dry_run_complete/executing/completed/failed), metadata_mappings (id, source_field, target_field, transform_rule, active), permission_sets (id, sharepoint_principal, principal_type ENUM ad_group/site_group/named_user, clm_role_id, active), migration_logs (id, source_document_id FK, batch_id FK, outcome ENUM success/failed/skipped, error_code, clm_document_id, acl_translation_json, migrated_at)
data.migration_notes: SQLite state DB initialized per migration project; migration_logs exported to Azure Blob before purge after reconciliation sign-off

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Instances for scheduled batch execution within firm Azure tenant; local Node.js for dry-run and planning
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps: tsc type-check, eslint strict, jest 75% coverage gate, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, ACI deployment manifest update
devops.rollback_strategy: Re-run failed batch from last successful checkpoint using SQLite batch state; dry-run mode verifies re-run plan before execution
