previous_plan.title: SharePoint to CLM Contract Migration Tool v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Round 1 tool migrating a single SharePoint library to CLM with metadata mapping via manifest and per-document migration log. ACL translation and multi-library scheduling not implemented.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Node.js TypeScript CLI using Microsoft Graph API for SharePoint enumeration and CLM vendor SDK for upload. SQLite for migration state. Metadata mapping via JSON manifest. ACLs dropped on upload.
previous_plan.data_model: source_documents, migration_batches (single-library only), metadata_mappings, migration_logs. permission_sets table defined but not applied during upload.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD service principal with Sites.Read.All scope. TLS 1.3. No document content on disk. ACL translation absent — CLM default permissions applied to all migrated documents.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 targets ISS-001 (ACL translation) as the blocking high-severity issue. The acl_translator module maps SharePoint site groups and AD SIDs to CLM role IDs via a configurable permission_sets manifest. Dry-run mode previews ACL mapping and flags unmapped principals before live execution, satisfying the attorney-client privilege exposure risk. Multi-library batch scheduling via SQLite migration_batches queue addresses ISS-002. Service principal narrowed to SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll. Five concurrent upload workers achieve 10K document throughput within the 8-hour maintenance window.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented acl_translator module that fetches SharePoint document permissions via Graph API /permissions endpoint, maps each principal (AD group SID, site group name, named user) to CLM role ID using the permission_sets manifest, and applies translated permissions via CLM SDK setPermissions call after document upload. Dry-run mode generates acl_translation_preview.json listing each source_principal, matched clm_role, and any unmapped_principals before live execution. Service principal scope narrowed to SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Migrated documents receive CLM permissions matching source SharePoint ACLs; no default-open permission exposure; dry-run report allows legal ops to correct unmapped principals before live batch

target_output.title: SharePoint to CLM Contract Migration Tool v2
target_output.executive_summary: A Node.js TypeScript automation tool running in Azure Container Instances that migrates SharePoint on-premise contract document libraries to a cloud-native CLM platform. Preserves document metadata via configurable field-mapping manifest, version histories via Graph API version enumeration, and access controls via the new acl_translator module mapping SharePoint principals to CLM roles through a permission_sets manifest. Round 2 resolves the high-severity ACL privilege-exposure gap and adds multi-library batch scheduling with SQLite migration_batches queue.
target_output.architecture_overview: CLI tool (migrate.ts) with five subcommands: enumerate, plan, dry-run, execute, and reconcile. Runs as Azure Container Instance within the law firm Azure tenant. Microsoft Graph API client (scope: SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll) enumerates document libraries and fetches metadata, version histories, and per-document permissions. acl_translator (new in round 2) maps SharePoint site groups and AD SIDs to CLM role IDs using a permission_sets manifest JSON file. document_uploader calls CLM vendor SDK in a 5-worker concurrent pool with retry on 429 responses. SQLite migration_batches queue enables multi-library scheduling in a single ACI run, processing libraries sequentially to respect CLM API rate limits. All migration_logs written to Azure Blob Storage in the firm tenant for 7-year retention. Dry-run mode simulates metadata mapping and ACL translation and generates acl_translation_preview.json without touching CLM.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Node.js 20 TypeScript. Auth: @azure/msal-node ConfidentialClientApplication with service principal client credentials, scope SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll. SharePoint: @microsoft/microsoft-graph-client. CLM: vendor SDK (CLM-client-ts). State: SQLite via better-sqlite3. Manifest validation: Zod schemas. Secrets: Azure Key Vault via @azure/keyvault-secrets with DefaultAzureCredential. Infrastructure: Azure Container Instances, Azure Container Registry, Azure Blob Storage for log export. CI/CD: Azure DevOps. Monitoring: Azure Monitor container logs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Enumerate Command: lists all document libraries in target SharePoint site with document counts and estimated sizes via Graph API pagination. Plan Command: reads field-mapping-manifest.json and permission-sets-manifest.json, validates both with Zod, generates migration_batches SQLite records per library. Dry-Run Command: simulates metadata mapping for all documents and fetches permissions for first 100 docs per library, runs acl_translator, outputs acl_translation_preview.json with source_principal, clm_role, and unmapped_principals list; execution blocked if unmapped_principals present. Execute Command: processes migration_batches queue sequentially, runs 5-concurrent upload workers per library, writes migration_logs per document. Reconcile Command: compares SQLite migrated_count to CLM document count API per library, exports reconciliation_report.csv.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Instance (migration tool container), Microsoft Graph API client (library enumeration, document download, version history, permissions fetch), CLM vendor SDK (document upload, setPermissions, addVersion), SQLite state DB (migration_batches, source_documents, migration_logs, permission_sets), Azure Key Vault (service principal secret and CLM token), Azure Blob Storage (migration_logs export and acl_translation_preview archive in firm tenant), Azure Container Registry (tool Docker image), Azure DevOps (CI/CD pipeline), Azure Monitor (container log streaming).
target_output.workflows: Migration Planning and Dry-Run: IT admin runs 'migrate plan --manifest field-mapping-manifest.json --permissions permission-sets-manifest.json'; plan command enumerates SharePoint libraries via Graph API pagination and creates migration_batches records in SQLite. Admin runs 'migrate dry-run --batch-id BATCH-001'; dry-run fetches permissions for first 100 documents per library via Graph API /permissions, runs acl_translator against permission_sets manifest, outputs acl_translation_preview.json with source_principal, matched clm_role, and unmapped_principals; if unmapped_principals count > 0, execution is blocked and admin updates permission-sets-manifest.json. Live Batch Execution: Admin runs 'migrate execute --batch-id BATCH-001' during maintenance window; batch_coordinator reads migration_batches queue and processes each library in sequence; for each document: library_enumerator fetches metadata and version list, metadata_mapper applies field_mapping_manifest to produce CLM-schema object, acl_translator fetches document permissions and maps to CLM roles, version_history_fetcher downloads each version binary, document_uploader calls CLM SDK createDocument with translated metadata then setPermissions with CLM role list then addVersion for each historical version; migration_logs row written with outcome, clm_document_id, and acl_translation_json.
target_output.data_model: source_documents (id UUID PK, library_id TEXT, source_url TEXT, title TEXT, content_type TEXT, source_metadata_json TEXT, version_count INT, status ENUM pending/uploading/migrated/failed, created_at DATETIME). migration_batches (id UUID PK, library_path TEXT, batch_name TEXT, scheduled_at DATETIME, started_at DATETIME, completed_at DATETIME, total_docs INT, migrated_count INT, failed_count INT, status ENUM planned/dry_run_complete/executing/completed/failed). metadata_mappings (id UUID PK, source_field TEXT, target_field TEXT, transform_rule TEXT, active BOOL). permission_sets (id UUID PK, sharepoint_principal TEXT, principal_type ENUM ad_group/site_group/named_user, clm_role_id TEXT, active BOOL). migration_logs (id UUID PK, source_document_id UUID FK, batch_id UUID FK, outcome ENUM success/failed/skipped, error_code TEXT, clm_document_id TEXT, acl_translation_json TEXT, migrated_at DATETIME). version_histories (id UUID PK, source_document_id UUID FK, version_label TEXT, modified_at DATETIME, clm_version_id TEXT).
target_output.api_design: Microsoft Graph API calls: GET /sites/{site-id}/drives for library enumeration, GET /drives/{drive-id}/items/{item-id}/versions for version list, GET /drives/{drive-id}/items/{item-id}/permissions for per-document ACL fetch. CLM SDK calls: sdk.documents.create({metadata, file_stream}) returns clm_document_id, sdk.documents.setPermissions(clm_document_id, [{role_id, principal}]) applies translated ACLs, sdk.documents.addVersion(clm_document_id, version_stream) for version history. Internal CLI: migrate enumerate --site-url URL, migrate plan --manifest PATH --permissions PATH, migrate dry-run --batch-id ID, migrate execute --batch-id ID --concurrency 5, migrate reconcile --batch-id ID.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD service principal with SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll scope (narrowed from Sites.Read.All in round 1). Service principal secret in Azure Key Vault; fetched via DefaultAzureCredential at startup; never written to environment variables or disk. TLS 1.3 for all Graph API and CLM API calls. Document content held only in transient Node.js Buffer during upload; never written to local disk or container volume. Migration logs in Azure Blob Storage within firm tenant; document content excluded from log schema. 7-year retention on migration_logs per ABA Model Rule 1.6. GDPR compliance: migration_logs for EU client documents tagged with gdpr_transfer_basis for Article 46 documentation. Dry-run ACL preview required (execution blocked if unmapped_principals > 0) before any live execute run.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps pipeline: tsc type-check, eslint with @typescript-eslint strict config, jest unit tests with 75% coverage gate (covering acl_translator and metadata_mapper with fixture manifests), Docker build with commit SHA tag, push to Azure Container Registry, ACI deployment manifest update. ACI container runs in firm Azure subscription with managed identity access to Key Vault and Blob Storage. Non-secret environment variables: SHAREPOINT_SITE_ID, CLM_API_BASE_URL. Secrets injected from Key Vault at startup via DefaultAzureCredential. Maintenance window execution: IT admin starts ACI via Azure CLI; logs streamed to Azure Monitor; container auto-deletes on completion. Local dry-run: node migrate.ts dry-run for pre-migration ACL preview.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor container log group captures structured JSON log lines. Per-batch metrics logged at completion: total_docs, migrated_count, failed_count, acl_unmapped_count, duration_seconds, throughput_docs_per_minute. Per-document migration_logs in SQLite provide full audit trail exportable to Azure Blob. Reconciliation report exports discrepancy CSV: source_library_path, source_count, clm_count, discrepancy_count, status. Alert pattern: if acl_unmapped_count > 0 in dry-run output, execution command is blocked with exit code 1 and human-readable error listing unmapped principals.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Minimal ongoing cost: ACI per-second billing for 10-hour migration run ~$2. Azure Blob Storage for migration_logs ~$5/year per firm. Azure Container Registry ~$10/month. Total under $20/month operational. Scaling: 5-concurrent upload workers handle 10K documents within 8-hour window at CLM API rate limits; concurrency configurable via --concurrency flag; backoff on 429 responses prevents rate limit failures.
target_output.phased_implementation: Round 1 (completed): Single SharePoint library migration, metadata mapping manifest, per-document migration log, reconciliation CSV. Round 2 (current): ACL translation via permission_sets manifest with dry-run preview, multi-library batch scheduling via SQLite migration_batches queue, service principal scope narrowing to SharePoint.Selected.ReadAll. Round 3 (planned): SharePoint Online support via delegated Graph API permissions, CLM taxonomy auto-mapping for managed metadata columns, automated post-migration access audit comparing CLM permissions to original SharePoint ACLs.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode with @typescript-eslint no-explicit-any. Manifest schemas validated with Zod at startup — fail fast on malformed manifests before any API calls. acl_translator logs every unmapped principal to migration_logs even in dry-run mode. No document binary content in any log or SQLite column. jest unit tests cover acl_translator with fixture permission_sets manifests including edge cases: user in multiple groups, no matching CLM role, inherited site permissions. All CLM SDK calls wrapped in typed result objects to prevent silent upload failures.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CLM API rate limit changes breaking 5-worker throughput target for the 8-hour window. Mitigation: --concurrency flag allows adjustment; exponential backoff on 429 responses. Risk: SharePoint permission inheritance produces large /permissions responses for deeply inherited document libraries. Mitigation: acl_translator flattens inherited permissions and deduplicates before CLM setPermissions; dry-run preview exposes permission complexity before live execution. Tradeoff: SQLite over Azure SQL for state — chosen for zero-infra deployment since tool runs locally or in ACI without a managed DB dependency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SQLite over Azure SQL for state given zero-infra deployment requirement. Chose permission_sets manifest over dynamic AD group lookup because CLM role mapping is firm-specific and requires legal ops review before migration. Version history preserved via CLM SDK addVersion to maintain CLM native versioning semantics rather than document metadata fields.

metadata.notes: Round 2 implements ACL translation via permission_sets manifest with dry-run preview for SharePoint-to-CLM contract migration automation tool.

profile.domain: TypeScript SDK for Real-Time Regulatory Change Feed Subscriptions

project_goal.value: TypeScript SDK enabling downstream compliance applications to subscribe to real-time regulatory change feeds from EUR-Lex, Federal Register, and UK Legislation.gov.uk, normalizing jurisdiction-specific XML/JSON payloads into a unified ChangeEvent schema with typed event streams
project_goal.rationale: Core library need for compliance platform teams consuming multi-jurisdiction regulatory updates

target_users.value: TypeScript and Node.js developers at compliance software vendors building downstream applications that react to regulatory instrument changes
target_users.rationale: SDK consumer profile identified in developer outreach

project_class.rationale: Distributed as npm package; no hosted platform or server runtime

capabilities.rationale: Feed polling/webhook handling, event normalization, and multi-source integration

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source integration with different polling protocols and normalization complexity

risk_level.rationale: Public regulatory data; no PII or financial data processed by the SDK itself

data_sensitivity.rationale: Regulatory feed data is public but API keys used for feed access are internal credentials

external_exposure.rationale: Feed sources require API key authentication; SDK manages key usage on behalf of consumer

access_model.value: Consumer provides API keys per jurisdiction via SDK configuration object; SDK manages per-source authentication headers
access_model.rationale: Consumers own their feed API credentials; SDK abstracts auth per source

feature_scope.value: EUR-Lex OJ feed polling, Federal Register API polling, UK Legislation.gov.uk feed polling, ChangeEvent normalization, interest filter routing by jurisdiction and instrument_type, content-hash deduplication, typed event stream emission via EventEmitter or async generator
feature_scope.rationale: Full SDK feature set

mvp_scope.value: Federal Register and EUR-Lex polling, ChangeEvent normalization, jurisdiction filter routing, EventEmitter-based subscription API, content-hash deduplication
mvp_scope.rationale: UK Legislation.gov.uk and async generator interface deferred to phase 2

security_baseline.value: API keys never logged or included in emitted ChangeEvents; HTTPS enforced for all feed HTTP calls; no credential persistence beyond in-process configuration object
security_baseline.rationale: Low-risk public data but API key hygiene required

frontend_stack.value: None — SDK is a Node.js npm package with no browser UI
frontend_stack.rationale: library_sdk project class distributes as a package, not a deployed service

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with TypeScript; node-fetch for HTTP polling; xml2js for XML feed parsing; EventEmitter for subscription callbacks
backend_stack.rationale: Minimal dependency surface; node-fetch and xml2js are lightweight and widely trusted

data_platform.value: No persistent storage — SDK is stateless beyond in-memory deduplication cache (Map keyed by content hash)
data_platform.rationale: library_sdk must not introduce database dependencies on consumers

hosting_target.value: npm package published to npmjs.com; consumers install and run in their own Node.js environments
hosting_target.rationale: Standard npm distribution for SDK

privacy_retention_policy.value: SDK retains no data beyond in-process deduplication cache; cache cleared on SDK instance destruction; no telemetry collected from consumers
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SDK must not retain consumer regulatory feed data

future_scope.value: UK Legislation.gov.uk feed, async generator subscription interface, webhook receiver mode, persistent deduplication cache option for long-running consumers
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enhancements based on consumer feedback

constraints.value: Zero production dependencies beyond node-fetch and xml2js; TypeScript strict mode; must work in Node.js 18+ without compilation step for consumers using ts-node or Bun
constraints.rationale: Minimal dependency footprint and broad runtime compatibility

observability_baseline.value: SDK emits debug events on EventEmitter for feed poll timing, parse errors, and deduplication hits; no mandatory telemetry sink
observability_baseline.rationale: Consumer-optional observability rather than mandatory telemetry

execution_preference.value: Single-person library maintainer; 2-week release cycles with semantic versioning
execution_preference.rationale: Small team SDK development cadence

project.goal: TypeScript SDK for multi-jurisdiction regulatory change feed subscriptions with normalized ChangeEvent output
project.target_users: TypeScript/Node.js developers at compliance vendors
project.timeline: MVP in 6 weeks

backend.framework: Pure TypeScript library with EventEmitter; no framework
backend.parsing: xml2js for XML feeds; native JSON.parse for JSON feeds

security.auth: Consumer-provided API keys per jurisdiction in config object
security.transport: HTTPS enforced for all outbound feed HTTP calls

data.storage: In-memory Map for content-hash deduplication cache
data.schema: ChangeEvent TypeScript interface as canonical output type

devops.ci: GitHub Actions

constraints.dependencies: Zero prod deps beyond node-fetch and xml2js

reasoner_reviews.product: The ChangeEvent normalization design is the core value of this SDK. Consumers need a single TypeScript interface that works across EUR-Lex, Federal Register, and UK Legislation.gov.uk without source-specific parsing code in their application layer.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The SDK should follow an adapter pattern per feed source: each source adapter implements a common FeedAdapter interface with poll() and parse() methods. The central RegulatoryFeedClient orchestrates poll scheduling and routes parsed events to the ChangeEvent normalizer. Content-hash deduplication using a Map is appropriate for in-process use.
reasoner_reviews.security: API keys must never appear in emitted events or debug logs. The config validation layer should sanitize config object before any debug emission.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero dependency goal is achievable but xml2js adds a transitive dependency chain. Consider using Node.js built-in stream XML parsing or a lighter XML parser if consumer bundle size is a concern.

backend.service_design: RegulatoryFeedClient class (main entry point) with register(adapter) method, subscribe(filter) for EventEmitter callbacks, start() and stop() for poll lifecycle
backend.business_modules: FeedAdapter interface (poll(): Promise<RawFeedItem[]>, sourceId: string), EurLexAdapter (EUR-Lex OJ REST API polling), FederalRegisterAdapter (federalregister.gov API polling), ChangeEventNormalizer (maps RawFeedItem to ChangeEvent), DeduplicationCache (in-memory Map<string,boolean> keyed by content SHA-256), InterestFilter (routes ChangeEvents by jurisdiction and instrument_type)
backend.api_patterns: EventEmitter-based: client.on('change', (event: ChangeEvent) => {}); client.on('error', (err: FeedError) => {}); client.on('debug', (msg: DebugEvent) => {})

security.auth_design: FeedConfig type requires apiKey per source; RegulatoryFeedClient validates config at construction time; apiKey values redacted in any debug event emission
security.secrets_management: No SDK-side storage; consumer is responsible for loading API keys from their secrets manager into FeedConfig

data.entities: ChangeEvent interface: {id: string, source: 'eur_lex'|'federal_register'|'uk_legislation', jurisdiction: string, instrument_type: 'regulation'|'directive'|'notice'|'rule'|'order', title: string, publication_date: string (ISO8601), effective_date: string|null, document_url: string, change_type: 'new'|'amendment'|'repeal', content_hash: string}. RawFeedItem: source-specific parsed object before normalization. FeedConfig: {sources: {eur_lex?: {apiKey: string, pollIntervalMs: number}, federal_register?: {apiKey: string, pollIntervalMs: number}}}
data.schema_notes: ChangeEvent id is constructed as '{source}:{document_url_hash}' for global uniqueness; content_hash is SHA-256 of title+publication_date+document_url for deduplication

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: tsc --noEmit type-check, eslint strict, jest unit tests (80% coverage gate), npm pack dry-run, semantic-release for npm publish on main merge
devops.deployment_topology: npm package; no deployed infrastructure; consumers install via npm install regulatory-feed-sdk

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a TypeScript SDK with adapter pattern for multi-jurisdiction regulatory feed consumption. Key decisions: FeedAdapter interface per source for extensibility, ChangeEvent as single canonical output type for consumer simplicity, EventEmitter for subscription API in MVP (async generator deferred), in-memory Map for deduplication with content SHA-256 hash. Zero production dependency goal constrained to node-fetch and xml2js per contract.
target_output.title: Regulatory Change Feed SDK
target_output.executive_summary: A TypeScript npm package exposing a RegulatoryFeedClient that polls EUR-Lex and Federal Register feeds, normalizes jurisdiction-specific XML/JSON payloads into a typed ChangeEvent interface, filters by consumer interest (jurisdiction and instrument_type), deduplicates via content SHA-256 hash, and emits events through an EventEmitter subscription API. Zero production dependencies beyond node-fetch and xml2js.
target_output.architecture_overview: SDK follows an adapter pattern: FeedAdapter interface with poll() and parse() methods implemented per source (EurLexAdapter, FederalRegisterAdapter). RegulatoryFeedClient orchestrates polling schedules via setInterval per registered adapter, passes raw items to ChangeEventNormalizer, checks DeduplicationCache, routes passing events through InterestFilter, and emits on the client EventEmitter. No server runtime or database — the SDK runs in the consumer process.
target_output.technology_stack: TypeScript 5.x in strict mode. Node.js 20 runtime target. Production dependencies: node-fetch 3 for HTTP polling, xml2js for EUR-Lex SGML/XML parsing. DevDependencies: jest, ts-jest, @typescript-eslint, semantic-release. Distributed as CommonJS and ESM dual-build via tsup. Published to npmjs.com. Changesets for versioned changelog management. vitest for unit tests alongside jest during migration window. Type coverage enforced at >95% via type-coverage CLI in CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: FeedAdapter registration: consumer registers adapters for desired sources. Poll scheduling: RegulatoryFeedClient calls each adapter's poll() on configured pollIntervalMs. ChangeEvent normalization: EurLexAdapter produces ChangeEvents from OJ REST API atom+xml responses; FederalRegisterAdapter produces ChangeEvents from federalregister.gov JSON API. Deduplication: DeduplicationCache checks content_hash before emission, discards duplicates. Interest filtering: InterestFilter routes events to subscriber callbacks matching jurisdiction and instrument_type. Event emission: client.on('change', callback), client.on('error', callback), client.on('debug', callback).
target_output.system_components: RegulatoryFeedClient (main class, EventEmitter subclass), FeedAdapter interface, EurLexAdapter (polls EUR-Lex OJ REST API, parses atom+xml via xml2js), FederalRegisterAdapter (polls federalregister.gov JSON API), ChangeEventNormalizer (maps adapter-specific objects to ChangeEvent), DeduplicationCache (in-memory Map keyed by content SHA-256), InterestFilter (matches ChangeEvents against subscriber filter predicates), FeedConfig type (API keys and poll intervals per source).
target_output.workflows: Feed Poll Cycle: RegulatoryFeedClient.start() registers setInterval per adapter using pollIntervalMs from FeedConfig; on each tick adapter.poll() calls source API (EUR-Lex OJ endpoint or Federal Register /documents.json), returns RawFeedItem array; ChangeEventNormalizer maps each RawFeedItem to ChangeEvent setting source, jurisdiction, instrument_type, title, publication_date, document_url, change_type, and content_hash; DeduplicationCache checks hash — if seen, item discarded; if new, hash stored and event passed to InterestFilter; InterestFilter evaluates subscriber predicates and emits 'change' to matching subscribers. Error Handling: any adapter.poll() network error emits 'error' event with FeedError containing sourceId and cause; polling continues for other adapters; consumer decides whether to stop or continue.
target_output.data_model: ChangeEvent TypeScript interface: {id: string, source: 'eur_lex'|'federal_register'|'uk_legislation', jurisdiction: string, instrument_type: 'regulation'|'directive'|'notice'|'rule'|'order', title: string, publication_date: string, effective_date: string|null, document_url: string, change_type: 'new'|'amendment'|'repeal', content_hash: string}. RawFeedItem: {sourceId: string, rawData: Record<string, unknown>}. FeedConfig: {sources: Record<string, {apiKey: string, pollIntervalMs: number}>}. DeduplicationCache: Map<string, number> keyed by content_hash, value is first_seen_timestamp.
target_output.api_design: SDK public API: new RegulatoryFeedClient(config: FeedConfig), client.register(adapter: FeedAdapter), client.subscribe(filter: InterestFilter, callback: (event: ChangeEvent) => void), client.on('change'|'error'|'debug', handler), client.start(), client.stop(). FeedAdapter interface: {sourceId: string, poll(): Promise<RawFeedItem[]>}. EUR-Lex integration: GET https://eur-lex.europa.eu/OJXML/api/ojxmlinterface?type=OJ&dateFrom={date}. Federal Register integration: GET https://www.federalregister.gov/api/v1/documents.json?publication_date[gte]={date}&per_page=100.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API keys provided in FeedConfig; RegulatoryFeedClient constructor validates presence of apiKey per configured source. apiKey values redacted before any 'debug' event emission — config is cloned with apiKey replaced by '[REDACTED]'. HTTPS enforced: node-fetch calls to all feed APIs use https:// URLs; HTTP URLs rejected with FeedConfigError at construction. No credential persistence: FeedConfig held in constructor closure, not stored to disk or emitted in events. SDK emits no telemetry to external services.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on every PR: tsc --noEmit, eslint @typescript-eslint strict, jest with 80% coverage gate, npm pack dry-run to verify package contents. On merge to main: semantic-release runs based on conventional commit messages, publishes to npmjs.com with CHANGELOG update. Package built as CommonJS and ESM dual output via tsup for maximum consumer compatibility. Peer dependency declaration: Node.js >=18.
target_output.observability: SDK emits DebugEvent on client 'debug' channel: {type: 'poll_complete'|'parse_error'|'dedup_hit'|'filter_miss', sourceId: string, durationMs?: number, eventCount?: number}. Consumer opts in by attaching 'debug' listener. No mandatory telemetry. FeedError type includes sourceId, httpStatusCode (if available), and cause for structured error handling in consumer applications.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost — SDK runs in consumer process. npm package size target under 50KB packed. Polling frequency is consumer-controlled via pollIntervalMs; recommended minimum 60000ms to respect EUR-Lex and Federal Register rate limits. DeduplicationCache grows proportional to unique events seen since start(); consumers calling stop() and recreating the client will clear cache.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): EurLexAdapter and FederalRegisterAdapter, ChangeEventNormalizer, DeduplicationCache, InterestFilter, EventEmitter subscription API, TypeScript strict build, jest unit tests, npm publish. Phase 2 (4 weeks): UK Legislation.gov.uk adapter, async generator subscription interface alongside EventEmitter, webhook receiver helper for push-based sources. Phase 3: Persistent deduplication cache option (consumer-provided storage adapter interface) for long-running processes surviving restarts.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode; no-explicit-any enforced by @typescript-eslint. All public types exported from index.ts — no barrel files burying types in subdirectories. ChangeEvent interface is the contract; normalizer must produce valid ChangeEvents from all adapters. FeedAdapter implementations tested with recorded fixture responses from each feed source — no live API calls in unit tests. Semantic versioning: breaking ChangeEvent interface changes require major version bump.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EUR-Lex or Federal Register API schema changes breaking normalizer field extraction. Mitigation: RawFeedItem captures full raw response; normalizer logs missing fields as 'debug' events rather than throwing; consumers can inspect raw data via FeedAdapter extension. Risk: In-memory deduplication cache unbounded growth in long-running processes. Mitigation: document cache growth rate in README; phase 2 persistent cache option addresses this. Tradeoff: EventEmitter over async generator for MVP — lower cognitive overhead for most consumer use cases; async generator added in phase 2.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed EventEmitter over async generator for phase 1 subscription API due to broader consumer familiarity. Chose content SHA-256 hash over document URL alone for deduplication because EUR-Lex republishes documents at the same URL with amended content. Selected node-fetch 3 over native Node.js fetch for Node.js 18 compatibility where native fetch is unflagged but not fully stable.

metadata.notes: TypeScript regulatory change feed SDK with adapter pattern, EUR-Lex and Federal Register integration, ChangeEvent normalization. Weak quality: some sections at minimum depth.

profile.domain: B2B Legal E-Discovery SaaS Marketing Landing Page

project_goal.value: High-performance marketing landing page for a B2B legal e-discovery SaaS platform targeting corporate legal departments and law firms, driving demo request conversions through case-study testimonials, feature breakdowns, and a CRM-integrated demo request form
project_goal.rationale: Core conversion objective with measurable demo request KPI

target_users.value: Corporate legal department heads, litigation partners, and e-discovery managers at law firms and Fortune 500 companies evaluating e-discovery software
target_users.rationale: ICP identified in sales and marketing alignment sessions

project_class.rationale: Static marketing site with form submission; no server-side application runtime

capabilities.rationale: Static site with client-side analytics; no backend capabilities required

complexity_level.rationale: Static site generation with form submission and A/B testing

risk_level.rationale: No authentication, no PII storage beyond CRM-forwarded form data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Marketing page serves no sensitive data; form data forwarded to HubSpot, not stored on-site

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing page with no authentication

access_model.value: Fully public; no authentication required for any page
access_model.rationale: Marketing landing page must be open to all visitors

feature_scope.value: Hero section with A/B test variant management, client testimonials with case study metrics, feature breakdown sections, pricing tier display, demo request form with HubSpot CRM integration, GDPR cookie consent banner, Core Web Vitals tracking
feature_scope.rationale: Full conversion-optimized feature set

mvp_scope.value: Hero, testimonials, feature sections, demo request form with HubSpot integration, basic Vercel Analytics
mvp_scope.rationale: A/B testing and GDPR cookie consent added in round 2

security_baseline.value: HTTPS via Vercel Edge; Content-Security-Policy header restricting scripts to Vercel and HubSpot origins; no form data stored in Vercel; GDPR cookie consent before loading analytics scripts
security_baseline.rationale: Public page requires CSP and GDPR consent for legal compliance

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript; Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling; no client-side JS framework — islands architecture for interactive components only
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro islands minimize JavaScript payload for optimal Core Web Vitals on B2B audience

backend_stack.value: Vercel serverless function for demo request form submission; no persistent backend
backend_stack.rationale: Single serverless function for form proxy; no application server required

data_platform.value: No database; HubSpot CRM as the record of truth for demo requests; SendGrid for confirmation emails
data_platform.rationale: landing_page project class requires no data platform

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with global CDN; serverless functions on Vercel Node.js runtime
hosting_target.rationale: Optimal static site delivery with edge caching for global B2B audience

privacy_retention_policy.value: GDPR cookie consent required before loading Vercel Analytics or Google Analytics; form data forwarded to HubSpot and not retained on Vercel; CAN-SPAM compliant confirmation email footers
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR and CAN-SPAM compliance for EU visitors and email follow-ups

future_scope.value: Multi-language localization for EU markets, HubSpot chat widget integration, case study detail pages, resource download gating with HubSpot form
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 conversion optimization features

constraints.value: Lighthouse score >= 95 on mobile and desktop; LCP < 2.5s on 3G throttling; no third-party scripts loaded before GDPR consent; page must work with JavaScript disabled for hero and feature content
constraints.rationale: Performance and accessibility requirements from marketing and legal teams

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals tracking (LCP, CLS, FID); Vercel Edge Config for A/B test variant flags; HubSpot form submission tracking for demo request funnel analytics
observability_baseline.rationale: Marketing needs Core Web Vitals and conversion funnel data

execution_preference.value: Continuous deployment on merge to main; content updates via Astro content collections without code changes
execution_preference.rationale: Marketing team needs to update testimonials and case studies without engineering involvement

project.goal: High-performance B2B e-discovery SaaS conversion landing page
project.target_users: Corporate legal and law firm e-discovery buyers
project.timeline: Round 2 adds A/B testing and GDPR consent

frontend.interactivity: Astro islands for demo form and GDPR consent components only

backend.function: Single form submission proxy to HubSpot API and SendGrid

security.csp: Content-Security-Policy restricting to Vercel and HubSpot origins
security.consent: GDPR cookie consent before analytics scripts

devops.ci: GitHub Actions

constraints.js_disabled: Hero and feature content renders without JavaScript

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 1 launched the core landing page with hero, testimonials, feature sections, and HubSpot form integration. Marketing reported that the demo request form conversion rate is below benchmark, and legal flagged that analytics scripts load before the GDPR cookie consent banner is clicked — a compliance violation for EU visitors. Both issues must be resolved in round 2.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: GDPR consent must block analytics script loading entirely — use a cookie consent library as an Astro island that conditionally loads Vercel Analytics after consent. The A/B test for the hero section should use Vercel Edge Config to serve the variant flag at the CDN edge without a client-side JavaScript experiment library, keeping JavaScript payload minimal.
reasoner_reviews.security: Analytics scripts loading before GDPR consent is a legal compliance violation, not just a best-practice issue. The CSP header must also be tightened to include the GDPR consent library CDN origin and block inline scripts. Vercel serverless function should validate the HubSpot form payload with Zod before forwarding to prevent injection via form fields.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse >=95 constraint makes the A/B testing implementation critical — no client-side experiment framework can be loaded. Edge Config flag resolved at CDN level keeps the page static with variant-specific static builds or edge middleware serving the correct hero component.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 pages: src/pages/index.astro (main landing page), src/pages/api/demo-request.ts (Vercel serverless function). Components: Hero.astro (A/B variant-aware), Testimonials.astro, Features.astro, PricingTiers.astro, DemoRequestForm (Astro island, client:load), GdprConsent (Astro island, client:load). Content collections: src/content/testimonials/, src/content/features/ for markdown-driven content.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero section renders A/B variant based on Vercel Edge Config flag (variant_hero_cta: 'schedule_demo'|'start_free_trial'); testimonials display Fortune 500 case study metrics (doc review time reduction %, cost per GB); demo request form collects name, email, company, team_size, primary_use_case (litigation/internal_investigation/merger_review); GDPR consent island blocks Vercel Analytics script load until consent cookie set.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro zero-JS default for hero, testimonials, features, and pricing sections. Only DemoRequestForm and GdprConsent components use client:load. Images served via Astro image integration with WebP/AVIF format negotiation and width/height attributes to prevent CLS. Google Fonts replaced with system font stack to eliminate render-blocking font requests.

security.auth_design: No authentication — fully public page
security.abuse_prevention: Vercel serverless demo request function validates request with Zod (name max 100 chars, email format, company max 200 chars, team_size ENUM); Vercel rate limiting middleware at 10 requests/min per IP on /api/demo-request to prevent form spam
security.privacy_controls: GdprConsent island sets consent_given cookie; all analytics script tags use Astro client:idle with consent guard — Vercel Analytics script injected into DOM only after consent_given cookie is present; CAN-SPAM-compliant footer in SendGrid confirmation template

data.entities: No database entities. Form submission payload: {first_name, last_name, email, company_name, company_size ENUM 1-50/51-200/201-1000/1000+, primary_use_case ENUM litigation/internal_investigation/merger_review/regulatory_response, message?}. HubSpot contact properties mapped: firstname, lastname, email, company, numberofemployees, ediscovery_use_case (custom property).
data.analytics_events: Vercel Analytics custom events: demo_form_submit (success), demo_form_error (validation failure), hero_cta_click (with variant label), testimonial_viewed (intersection observer), pricing_tier_hover

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network: static HTML/CSS/JS assets served from CDN edge nodes globally; /api/demo-request served from Vercel serverless Node.js function in us-east-1; Edge Middleware for A/B variant flag injection from Edge Config.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Astro build (bun run build), Lighthouse CI with budget assertions (performance >=95, LCP <2500ms, CLS <0.1), Vercel preview deployment per PR. Merge to main triggers Vercel production deployment.

ISS-001.title: Analytics scripts load before GDPR cookie consent — EU compliance violation
ISS-001.detail: Vercel Analytics script is injected unconditionally in the round 1 page head. For EU visitors, analytics data is collected before consent is given, violating GDPR Article 7. Legal has flagged this as a blocking compliance issue.

history.note: Flagged by legal team after EU launch review

ISS-002.title: A/B test hero variant not implemented — demo request CTA conversion below benchmark
ISS-002.detail: Marketing wants to A/B test two hero CTA variants ('Schedule a Demo' vs 'Start Free Trial') to improve demo request conversion. Round 1 shipped only the 'Schedule a Demo' variant with no experiment infrastructure.

history.note: Marketing benchmark shows 1.2% demo request rate vs 2.1% industry benchmark

focus_issues.title: Analytics scripts load before GDPR consent — EU compliance violation
focus_issues.detail: Vercel Analytics script injected unconditionally in page head; EU visitors tracked before consent given.

issue_updates.title: Analytics before GDPR consent
issue_updates.detail: Vercel Analytics injected unconditionally; EU compliance violation

previous_plan.title: Legal E-Discovery SaaS Landing Page v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Round 1 Astro 4 landing page with hero, testimonials, features, pricing, and HubSpot demo request form. Vercel Analytics in page head without consent gate. No A/B test infrastructure.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site on Vercel Edge. DemoRequestForm Astro island (client:load) posts to /api/demo-request serverless function forwarding to HubSpot and SendGrid. Vercel Analytics script in page head loaded unconditionally.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS via Vercel. CSP header present but does not include GDPR consent library origin. No Zod validation on form endpoint. No rate limiting on /api/demo-request.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions builds Astro, deploys to Vercel. Lighthouse CI assertions present but LCP assertion not yet enforcing <2.5s on 3G simulation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves the GDPR compliance violation (ISS-001) by implementing a GdprConsent Astro island that conditionally injects Vercel Analytics only after consent cookie is set, and adds Vercel Edge Config A/B testing for the hero CTA to address ISS-002 without loading a client-side experiment library. Also adding Zod validation and rate limiting on the demo request serverless function as flagged in the round 1 audit. Core design constraint: Lighthouse >=95 requirement means all solutions must avoid additional JavaScript payload — Edge Config flag at CDN edge and consent-gated analytics script injection are both zero-JS-overhead solutions.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced unconditional Vercel Analytics script in page head with a GdprConsent Astro island (client:load) that checks for consent_given cookie on mount. If consent not given, island renders GDPR banner; on accept, sets consent_given cookie with 1-year expiry and dynamically injects Vercel Analytics script via document.createElement. On subsequent page loads with consent_given cookie present, island skips banner and injects analytics immediately. No analytics data collected before consent. CSP header updated to include cookieyes.com (consent library CDN) origin.
fix_report.expected_outcome: EU visitors not tracked before consent; GDPR Article 7 compliance restored; legal stop-order on EU traffic lifted

target_output.title: Legal E-Discovery SaaS Landing Page v2
target_output.executive_summary: A high-performance Astro 4 marketing landing page for a B2B legal e-discovery SaaS platform deployed on Vercel Edge, targeting corporate legal departments and law firms. Features case-study testimonials with e-discovery cost metrics, feature breakdowns, pricing tier comparison, and a HubSpot-integrated demo request form with Zod-validated Vercel serverless function. Round 2 resolves the GDPR analytics consent violation via a GdprConsent Astro island, adds Vercel Edge Config A/B testing for the hero CTA, and adds rate limiting and input validation on the form endpoint. Achieves Lighthouse >=95 with LCP <2.5s on 3G.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site generation with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS, deployed to Vercel Edge Network global CDN. Islands architecture isolates all client-side JavaScript to two interactive components: DemoRequestForm and GdprConsent (both client:load). All remaining sections (hero, testimonials, features, pricing) render as zero-JS server-generated HTML. Vercel Edge Middleware reads hero CTA variant flag from Vercel Edge Config and injects a variant meta tag into the HTML response at the CDN edge — no client-side experiment library needed. GdprConsent island gates Vercel Analytics script injection behind consent_given cookie. Vercel serverless function /api/demo-request validates form payload with Zod, forwards to HubSpot Contacts API, and triggers SendGrid confirmation email. Rate limiting enforced at Vercel edge: 10 requests/minute per IP on the form endpoint.
target_output.technology_stack: Static site: Astro 4 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 3. Content: Astro content collections with Zod schemas for testimonials and features markdown. Form: Astro island (React 18 minimal, client:load) posting to Vercel serverless function. Validation: Zod 3 in serverless function. CRM: HubSpot Contacts API v3. Email: SendGrid transactional template. Analytics: Vercel Analytics (consent-gated). A/B testing: Vercel Edge Config + Edge Middleware (zero client JS). Hosting: Vercel Edge Network. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section (A/B): Edge Middleware reads variant_hero_cta from Vercel Edge Config and renders 'Schedule a Demo' or 'Start Free Trial' CTA; Vercel Analytics custom event hero_cta_click fires with variant label on click. Testimonials: Fortune 500 case studies with specific metrics (e.g., '60% reduction in document review time', '$2.10 per GB vs $8.40 industry average'); driven by Astro content collections. Feature Sections: e-discovery-specific breakdown (predictive coding accuracy, custodian hold management, early case assessment dashboards). Pricing Tiers: tiered by GB of data processed per month. Demo Request Form: Astro island with client-side validation before POST to /api/demo-request; fields: first_name, last_name, email, company_name, company_size, primary_use_case (litigation/internal_investigation/merger_review/regulatory_response). GDPR Consent: Astro island blocks Vercel Analytics until consent_given cookie set.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 static build output (HTML/CSS served from Vercel Edge CDN), Vercel Edge Middleware (hero A/B variant injection from Edge Config), GdprConsent Astro island (consent banner and conditional analytics injection), DemoRequestForm Astro island (client-side validation and fetch POST), Vercel serverless function /api/demo-request (Zod validation, HubSpot API POST, SendGrid email trigger), Vercel Edge Config (variant_hero_cta feature flag), Vercel Analytics (Core Web Vitals and custom event tracking, consent-gated), HubSpot Contacts API v3 (demo request lead creation), SendGrid transactional email (demo request confirmation).
target_output.workflows: Demo Request Submission: Visitor fills DemoRequestForm island fields with client-side Zod validation on blur; on valid submit, island POSTs JSON to /api/demo-request; Vercel rate limiter checks IP rate (10 req/min); serverless function validates payload with Zod (name max 100 chars, email RFC5322, company max 200 chars, company_size enum, primary_use_case enum); on valid payload, POSTs to HubSpot Contacts API creating or updating contact with firstname, lastname, email, company, numberofemployees, ediscovery_use_case custom property; triggers SendGrid transactional template with demo scheduling link and CAN-SPAM footer; returns 200 with {success: true}; island shows confirmation message. GDPR Consent Flow: GdprConsent island mounts on page load, checks for consent_given cookie; if absent, renders consent banner over page bottom; on 'Accept' click, sets consent_given=1 cookie (1-year expiry, SameSite=Strict), dynamically creates and appends Vercel Analytics script tag; on subsequent visits with cookie present, island immediately injects analytics script without showing banner. Hero A/B Variant: Vercel Edge Middleware runs on every request to /, reads variant_hero_cta from Edge Config (value: 'schedule_demo' or 'start_free_trial'), injects <meta name='hero-variant' content='{variant}'> into HTML; Hero.astro reads this meta tag at build-aware runtime and renders the appropriate CTA text and button label.
target_output.data_model: No database. Form payload type (Zod schema): {first_name: z.string().max(100), last_name: z.string().max(100), email: z.string().email(), company_name: z.string().max(200), company_size: z.enum(['1-50','51-200','201-1000','1000+']), primary_use_case: z.enum(['litigation','internal_investigation','merger_review','regulatory_response']), message: z.string().max(1000).optional()}. HubSpot contact properties: firstname, lastname, email, company, numberofemployees, ediscovery_use_case (custom). Vercel Analytics custom events: demo_form_submit, demo_form_error, hero_cta_click (property: variant), testimonial_viewed, pricing_tier_hover. Astro content collection schemas: testimonials (company_name, role, quote, metric_label, metric_value, logo_src), features (title, description, icon, e_discovery_category ENUM).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/demo-request: accepts Content-Type application/json with DemoRequestPayload; returns {success: true} on 200, {error: string} on 400 (Zod validation failure) or 429 (rate limit). HubSpot Contacts API: POST https://api.hubapi.com/crm/v3/objects/contacts with Bearer token from HUBSPOT_API_KEY env var (set in Vercel environment variables, not in code). SendGrid API: POST https://api.sendgrid.com/v3/mail/send with SENDGRID_API_KEY env var. Vercel Edge Config: @vercel/edge-config SDK getAll() in Edge Middleware to read variant_hero_cta flag.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Content-Security-Policy header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' cdn.vercel-insights.com cookieyes.com; connect-src 'self' api.hubapi.com api.sendgrid.com cdn.vercel-insights.com; img-src 'self' data:. GDPR Article 7 compliance: GdprConsent island blocks all analytics script loading until consent_given cookie is set; no visitor tracking before consent. CAN-SPAM compliance: SendGrid confirmation email includes physical mailing address and unsubscribe link in footer. Vercel rate limiting: 10 requests/minute per IP on /api/demo-request. Zod validation in serverless function prevents malformed or injected data from reaching HubSpot API. HUBSPOT_API_KEY and SENDGRID_API_KEY stored as Vercel environment variables; never exposed in client bundle. Astro build output contains no API keys.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on every PR: bun run build (Astro production build), Lighthouse CI with budget file asserting performance >=95, LCP <=2500, CLS <=0.1, TBT <=200 on mobile throttled simulation; Vercel preview deployment per PR with unique URL for stakeholder review. Merge to main triggers Vercel production deployment (zero-downtime CDN cache invalidation). Content updates to testimonials and features via markdown files in src/content/ directories — marketing team commits or uses Netlify CMS if added later. Vercel Edge Config variant_hero_cta flag updated by marketing via Vercel dashboard without deployment.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics tracks Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) per page and per visitor segment. Custom events: demo_form_submit (tracks conversion funnel), demo_form_error (tracks validation failure types), hero_cta_click (with variant property for A/B analysis), testimonial_viewed (intersection observer fires on 50% visibility), pricing_tier_hover. HubSpot reports demo request volume and source attribution from UTM parameters passed through form as hidden fields. Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions enforces performance budget on every PR — failing budget blocks merge.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan ~$20/month for team collaboration and preview deployments. Vercel Analytics included in Pro. HubSpot CRM on existing marketing subscription. SendGrid free tier (100 emails/day) adequate for demo request volume. Vercel Edge Config included in Pro. Total incremental hosting cost under $25/month. Scaling: Vercel Edge CDN handles global traffic spikes (e.g., conference campaigns) without configuration; serverless function auto-scales for form submission bursts.
target_output.phased_implementation: Round 1 (completed): Astro 4 static build, hero, testimonials, features, pricing, HubSpot form integration, SendGrid confirmation, Vercel Analytics, Vercel deployment. Round 2 (current): GdprConsent island resolving GDPR violation, Vercel Edge Config A/B test for hero CTA, Zod validation and rate limiting on form endpoint, Lighthouse CI performance budget enforcement. Round 3 (planned): Multi-language localization for EU markets (German, French) via Astro i18n routing, HubSpot chat widget as Astro island (client:idle), case study detail pages with gated PDF download forms.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro components default to zero client-side JS unless an island directive is explicitly added. New interactive components require client:load or client:idle justification in PR description. Content updates (testimonials, features) via Astro content collections only — no hardcoded content in .astro files. Tailwind config extended with e-discovery brand tokens (navy, gold, slate). Lighthouse CI budget file committed to repo and enforced on every PR. No third-party script tags added to page head without consent-gating evaluation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vercel Edge Config flag propagation delay (up to 2 seconds) means A/B variant could briefly show wrong CTA on cold edge node. Mitigation: acceptable for A/B test purposes; not a correctness issue. Risk: HubSpot API downtime causes demo request form to fail. Mitigation: serverless function returns user-friendly error message; form submissions could be queued in Vercel KV in a future enhancement. Tradeoff: Astro islands with React for DemoRequestForm over vanilla JS — React adds ~40KB to island bundle but provides familiar component model for team; acceptable given islands isolation keeps it off the critical path.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Vercel Edge Config over client-side experiment library for A/B testing to preserve Lighthouse >=95 score — client-side libraries add 20-40KB and execution time that would fail the performance budget. Chose GdprConsent as Astro island over a third-party consent management platform to minimize script payload and maintain CSP control. Confirmed HubSpot over Salesforce for CRM integration because marketing team uses HubSpot and the HubSpot Contacts API v3 is simpler than Salesforce REST API for this use case.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolves GDPR analytics consent violation and adds Edge Config A/B testing for B2B legal e-discovery SaaS Astro landing page.

profile.domain: Legal Technology - Litigation Matter Management

project_goal.value: Real-time matter management web application for boutique litigation firms providing live docket notifications, task assignment, and deadline tracking synced from court scheduling orders
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need replacing email chains and spreadsheet deadline tracking

target_users.value: Litigation attorneys and paralegals at boutique law firms managing active dockets
target_users.rationale: Primary workflow users identified in discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Browser-based collaborative app requiring real-time state sync across firm members

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Real-time collab, RBAC, and firm-level analytics drive capability set

complexity_level.rationale: WebSocket state sync with NLP-based date extraction and conflict detection adds significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Attorney-client privileged case data governed by ABA Model Rules

data_sensitivity.rationale: Matter files contain privileged attorney-client communications and work product

external_exposure.rationale: All access requires Auth0 authentication; no public-facing endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: attorney, paralegal, billing-admin, firm-admin; MFA required for all
access_model.rationale: ABA competence rules require access controls proportionate to confidentiality risk

feature_scope.value: Live docket board with WebSocket push, scheduling-order NLP date extraction, task assignment with deadlines, conflict detection, matter timeline view, firm analytics dashboard, admin user management
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope capturing core litigation workflow requirements

mvp_scope.value: Docket board with real-time notifications, manual deadline entry, task assignment to team members, matter detail view
mvp_scope.rationale: Delivers immediate deadline-tracking value before NLP automation features

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, MFA via Auth0, WAF on ALB, audit log for all matter access
security_baseline.rationale: ABA Rule 1.6 and SOC 2 Type II baseline controls

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, Server Components for initial render, WebSocket client for real-time updates
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for fast matter page loads, WebSocket for live docket board

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Fastify, WebSocket server via ws, Bull queue for background NLP jobs
backend_stack.rationale: Fastify low-overhead for high-frequency WebSocket messages; Bull for scheduling-order parsing jobs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 primary with read replica, Redis 7 for pub/sub and WebSocket session state
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational matter/deadline data; Redis pub/sub fan-out for multi-tab WebSocket delivery

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1, ALB with WAF, RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis cluster
hosting_target.rationale: Managed infrastructure with data residency and HA for firm-critical deadline tracking

privacy_retention_policy.value: Matter records retained for minimum 7 years per state bar rules; audit logs 3 years; deleted matters soft-deleted for 90 days then archived to S3 Glacier
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State bar record-keeping obligations and litigation hold requirements

future_scope.value: Court docket API integration (PACER, state EFSP), mobile app for deadline alerts, billing time-tracking integration
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 automation roadmap items

constraints.value: Must deploy to us-east-1 only; no client data leaves AWS boundary; budget $2,000/month; 50 concurrent users per firm
constraints.rationale: Data residency and firm budget constraints

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured JSON logs with matter_id and user_id correlation, PagerDuty for P1 alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; matter_id correlation required for compliance investigations

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with weekly firm stakeholder demos
execution_preference.rationale: Frequent feedback loop with attorney users to validate deadline workflow UX

compliance_context.value: ABA Model Rules 1.1 (competence), 1.6 (confidentiality); SOC 2 Type II audit scope; matter data must not be accessible across firm tenants
compliance_context.rationale: Privileged data and multi-tenant isolation are non-negotiable compliance requirements

project.goal: Real-time litigation matter management with live docket notifications and deadline tracking
project.target_users: Litigation attorneys and paralegals
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks, full scope 20 weeks

frontend.state: Zustand for local UI, SWR for server state, native WebSocket for real-time
frontend.rendering: SSR for matter pages, CSR for docket board

backend.queues: Bull backed by Redis for NLP parsing jobs

security.auth: Auth0 with MFA and RBAC

data.cache: Redis 7 pub/sub for WebSocket fan-out, session store

devops.ci: GitHub Actions

reasoner_reviews.product: The docket board with live push notifications is the highest-value feature; attorneys cited missed deadline notifications as the primary pain point. NLP scheduling-order parsing reduces manual entry by an estimated 70% for routine court orders but must be surfaced as suggestions, not auto-applied, to preserve attorney review responsibility under ABA Rule 1.1.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: WebSocket fan-out via Redis pub/sub is the right pattern for multi-tab and multi-user synchronization across Fargate tasks. The NLP date-extraction job should run asynchronously in a Bull worker to avoid blocking the upload API response. Conflict detection logic comparing new extracted dates against existing deadline_entries must execute atomically within a PostgreSQL transaction to prevent race conditions.
reasoner_reviews.security: Multi-tenant isolation is the primary risk; row-level security policies on matters, deadlines, and task_assignments tables must enforce firm_id scoping at the database layer, not solely at the API layer. Auth0 JWT claims must include firm_id and role, validated on every Fastify route. All matter file access must emit audit_events records with user_id, matter_id, action, and IP address.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $2,000/month supports ECS Fargate (2 tasks auto-scaling to 6), RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium, ElastiCache cache.t3.small, and ALB within budget. NLP parsing via a Bull worker on the same Fargate cluster avoids a separate ML inference cost until court-order volume justifies a dedicated service.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 3 introduced Redis pub/sub for WebSocket fan-out but the prior plan did not address reconnection and message replay for clients that disconnect during a docket update event. This must be resolved with a short replay buffer using Redis Streams or a last-event-id watermark on the WebSocket handshake.

backend.service_design: Fastify app split into domain plugins: matters, deadlines, tasks, notifications, admin. WebSocket upgrade handled in a dedicated ws-server plugin attached to the Fastify instance. Bull worker process as a separate ECS task consuming court_order_parse queue.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful JSON API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec. WebSocket endpoint at /ws/firm/:firmId authenticated via Auth0 token in query param on handshake. Idempotency keys on deadline creation to prevent duplicates from retry storms.
backend.failure_handling: Bull jobs use exponential backoff with 3 retries for NLP parse failures. Dead-letter queue alerts via PagerDuty. WebSocket clients implement exponential reconnect with last-event-id replay from Redis Streams buffer (30-minute retention).
backend.scaling_notes: ECS auto-scaling on CPU 60% and WebSocket connection count custom metric. Read replica used for analytics dashboard queries and matter search to protect transactional primary.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with server components for matter detail pages. Client component islands for the real-time docket board and task assignment panel. Route groups: (auth), (matters), (admin).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (Auth0 redirect), Matter List with search and filter, Matter Detail with docket board and deadline timeline, Task Assignment modal, Scheduling Order upload with NLP extraction preview, Admin user management, Analytics dashboard.
frontend.state_management: Zustand slice per domain: mattersSlice (list cache), deadlinesSlice (current matter deadlines), notificationsSlice (unread count). SWR for background refetch. WebSocket messages dispatch to Zustand actions, optimistically updating deadline boards.
frontend.performance_notes: Next.js SSR for matter detail initial load under 300ms. Docket board virtualized with react-window for firms with 500+ deadline entries. WebSocket messages throttled to 50ms batching on the server to prevent client render thrashing.

security.auth_design: Auth0 Universal Login with MFA enforcement. JWT includes firm_id, user_id, role claims. Token refresh handled client-side via Auth0 SPA SDK with silent authentication.
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL row-level security policies: matters.firm_id = current_setting('app.firm_id'), enforced on all SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE. Fastify preHandler sets app.firm_id from JWT claim before every query.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table (id, firm_id, user_id, action ENUM view/create/update/delete, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, user_agent, occurred_at). Append-only via PostgreSQL policy. Exported to S3 monthly for 3-year retention.
security.security_risks: WebSocket hijacking risk if token not validated on handshake. Mitigated by Auth0 token validation in ws-server upgrade handler and per-message firm_id verification before fan-out.

data.entities: matters (id UUID PK, firm_id UUID FK, case_name, court, docket_number, matter_type ENUM civil/criminal/appellate, lead_attorney_id, status ENUM active/closed/archived, created_at). deadlines (id UUID PK, matter_id FK, firm_id FK, deadline_type ENUM filing/hearing/discovery/statute_of_limitations, due_date, description, source ENUM manual/nlp_extracted, assigned_to_id, status ENUM pending/completed/waived, created_at). docket_entries (id UUID PK, matter_id FK, entry_date, description, court_document_id, imported_at). task_assignments (id UUID PK, deadline_id FK, assignee_id FK, assigner_id FK, notes, completed_at). court_calendars (id UUID PK, matter_id FK, event_type, scheduled_at, location, judge). notifications (id UUID PK, firm_id FK, user_id FK, event_type, payload_json, read_at, created_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with pgcrypto extension for UUID generation. Separate schema per firm not used due to migration complexity; row-level security preferred. S3 bucket for scheduling order document uploads, presigned URLs with 15-minute expiry.
data.schema_notes: deadline_entries has composite index on (firm_id, matter_id, due_date) for docket board queries. notifications indexed on (user_id, read_at IS NULL) for unread badge count.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with two task definitions: api-server (Fastify + WebSocket, 2-6 tasks) and nlp-worker (Bull consumer, 1-3 tasks). ALB with WAF rules, target group sticky sessions disabled (WebSocket affinity handled by Redis pub/sub).
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (ESLint), type-check (tsc --noEmit), test (Vitest 80% coverage gate), Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with health check verification. DB migrations via node-pg-migrate in a pre-deploy ECS task.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM via dd-trace Node.js agent. Custom metrics: active_websocket_connections, deadline_board_latency_ms, nlp_parse_success_rate, docket_events_per_minute. PagerDuty escalation on WebSocket error rate >2% or NLP worker dead-letter queue depth >10.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS deployment circuit breaker enabled. On health check failure, ECS reverts to previous task definition. DB migration rollback scripts maintained alongside forward migrations.

ISS-001.title: WebSocket reconnection does not replay missed docket events
ISS-001.detail: Clients that disconnect during a batch docket update miss intermediate events and show stale deadline boards until manual refresh. No replay buffer mechanism was present in rounds 1-3.

history.note: Identified: no reconnection strategy
Redis pub/sub added but no message buffering
Replay buffer described in reasoner but not in plan sections

ISS-002.title: Row-level security not enforced at DB layer
