previous_plan.title: Litigation Matter Management Platform - Round 3
previous_plan.executive_summary: Real-time matter management platform built with Next.js 14 and Fastify on ECS Fargate. Attorneys and paralegals track deadlines via live WebSocket docket boards with Redis pub/sub fan-out across server instances. NLP worker extracts dates from scheduling orders as attorney-confirmed suggestions.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Three-tier: Next.js 14 SSR frontend, Fastify API with ws WebSocket server behind ALB, PostgreSQL 16 with RLS. Redis pub/sub for WebSocket message fan-out across Fargate tasks. Bull worker for async NLP scheduling-order parsing.
previous_plan.data_model: matters (id, firm_id, case_name, court, docket_number, lead_attorney_id, status). deadlines (id, matter_id, firm_id, deadline_type, due_date, source enum manual/nlp_extracted, assigned_to_id, status). task_assignments (id, deadline_id, assignee_id, notes, completed_at). notifications (id, firm_id, user_id, event_type, payload_json, read_at).
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL RLS on matters, deadlines, task_assignments scoped to firm_id. Auth0 MFA with firm_id in JWT. audit_events append-only table. WAF on ALB. ABA Rule 1.6 confidentiality controls.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 focuses entirely on resolving ISS-001: the WebSocket replay buffer for reconnecting clients. The solution is Redis Streams on a docket-events stream per firm, with a 30-minute retention window. Reconnecting WebSocket clients send their last_event_id on the upgrade handshake, and the server replays any missed entries from the stream before subscribing to new pub/sub events. This closes the last blocking issue and should push the score above the 8.0 pass threshold. RLS (ISS-002) and NLP review gate (ISS-003) are already resolved and must be preserved intact.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Redis Streams docket-events stream (docket:{firmId}:events) with MAXLEN 500 entries and 30-minute TTL. WebSocket handshake now accepts Last-Event-ID header; server replays XRANGE entries since that ID before subscribing to pub/sub. Documented replay flow in system_components and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Reconnecting clients receive all missed docket events since their last seen event ID, eliminating stale deadline board state after network interruptions.

target_output.title: Litigation Matter Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A real-time litigation matter management platform for boutique litigation firms built with Next.js 14 and Fastify on AWS ECS Fargate. Attorneys and paralegals track case deadlines on live WebSocket docket boards with Redis Streams-backed event replay for seamless reconnection. Scheduling orders are parsed by an async NLP worker that surfaces extracted dates as attorney-confirmed suggestions. Multi-tenant isolation is enforced via PostgreSQL row-level security scoped to firm_id on all matter-related tables. SOC 2 Type II controls and ABA Rule 1.6 confidentiality requirements are met through end-to-end encryption, append-only audit logging, and Auth0 MFA.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Next.js 14 App Router frontend with SSR for matter pages and WebSocket client islands for the real-time docket board, communicating over HTTPS/WSS to a Fastify Node.js 20 API behind an AWS ALB with WAF. The API runs on ECS Fargate with a Redis 7 pub/sub layer for WebSocket message fan-out across tasks and a Redis Streams docket-events stream per firm for replay on reconnect. PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ stores matter, deadline, docket_entry, and audit data with row-level security enforcing firm_id isolation at the database layer. A separate Bull worker ECS task processes scheduling order NLP parsing asynchronously from the main API request path.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript 5, Tailwind CSS 3, Zustand 4 (UI state), SWR 2 (server state), native WebSocket API. Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify 4, ws 8 (WebSocket server), Bull 4 (job queue), node-pg-migrate, Passport-jwt. NLP: spaCy 3 via Python microservice sidecar (date extraction from scheduling orders). Database: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policies, pgcrypto. Cache/Pub-Sub: Redis 7 with Redis Streams. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis cluster, S3 for scheduling order documents, Auth0, GitHub Actions, Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Live Docket Board: WebSocket-driven deadline board per matter showing all pending deadlines with assignee, type, and due-date color coding (overdue/due-soon/upcoming). Event Replay on Reconnect: Reconnecting clients replay missed docket events via Redis Streams XRANGE since last_event_id. Scheduling Order NLP Parser: Attorneys upload court scheduling orders; Bull worker extracts date entities via spaCy, surfaces as suggested deadlines requiring attorney confirmation before insertion. Deadline Conflict Detector: On deadline creation or NLP suggestion acceptance, server checks for conflicts with existing deadlines within ±3 days and flags to the assigning attorney. Task Assignment: Attorneys assign deadlines to team members with notes; assignees receive WebSocket push notification. Matter Timeline: Chronological view of docket_entries, hearings, and completed deadlines per matter. Firm Analytics: Partner-level view of overdue deadlines, matter load by attorney, and average time-to-completion per deadline type. Admin Panel: Firm admin manages user accounts, roles, and MFA enforcement.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (SQL injection, XSS, rate-limit rules) routing to ECS Fargate api-server task definition. Fastify API with domain plugins: matters, deadlines, tasks, notifications, admin, ws-server. Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster running two logical layers: pub/sub channels (docket:{firmId}) for live fan-out and Redis Streams (docket:{firmId}:events MAXLEN 500) for 30-minute replay buffer. Bull queue backed by Redis for court_order_parse jobs. Separate nlp-worker ECS task running Bull consumer that calls spaCy Python sidecar via HTTP. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ primary with read replica (analytics and search queries). S3 bucket for scheduling order document uploads with presigned URLs. Auth0 tenant for JWT issuance and MFA. CloudFront for Next.js static assets. Datadog APM sidecars on all Fargate tasks.
target_output.workflows: Docket Event Push with Replay: Court notification webhook posts to POST /api/v1/docket-events; Fastify validates firm ownership, inserts docket_entry record, publishes event to Redis Streams XADD docket:{firmId}:events and pub/sub PUBLISH docket:{firmId}. All connected WebSocket clients on that firm channel receive the event immediately. Reconnecting Client Replay: Client WebSocket on open sends Last-Event-ID header with last received stream entry ID. Fastify ws-server handler reads XRANGE docket:{firmId}:events {lastId} + and sends buffered events in order before switching client to live pub/sub subscription. WebSocket Reconnection Handshake: Client exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s) on disconnect; on reconnect sends stored last_event_id from localStorage; server replays gap then resumes live events. Scheduling Order NLP Flow: Attorney uploads PDF via POST /api/v1/matters/:id/scheduling-orders (S3 presigned PUT, then POST to register). Bull job enqueued; spaCy worker extracts date entities, creates deadline_suggestions records. Attorney reviews extracted suggestions in preview modal, confirming or rejecting each; confirmed suggestions call POST /api/v1/deadlines with source=nlp_extracted. Conflict detection runs atomically within the deadline INSERT transaction.
target_output.data_model: matters (id UUID PK, firm_id UUID FK NOT NULL, case_name VARCHAR 255, court VARCHAR 100, docket_number VARCHAR 50, matter_type ENUM civil/criminal/appellate/transactional, lead_attorney_id UUID FK users, status ENUM active/closed/archived, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). deadlines (id UUID PK, matter_id UUID FK, firm_id UUID FK, deadline_type ENUM filing/hearing/discovery/deposition/statute_of_limitations/response, due_date DATE NOT NULL, description TEXT, source ENUM manual/nlp_extracted, assigned_to_id UUID FK users, status ENUM pending/completed/waived, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). docket_entries (id UUID PK, matter_id UUID FK, entry_date DATE, description TEXT, court_document_id VARCHAR, source_event_id VARCHAR for replay deduplication). task_assignments (id UUID PK, deadline_id UUID FK, assignee_id UUID FK, assigner_id UUID FK, notes TEXT, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). court_calendars (id UUID PK, matter_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM hearing/conference/trial/mediation, scheduled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, judge VARCHAR, location VARCHAR). notifications (id UUID PK, firm_id UUID FK, user_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, payload_json JSONB, read_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, firm_id UUID FK, user_id UUID FK, action ENUM view/create/update/delete, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ). PostgreSQL RLS: all matter-scoped tables enforce WHERE firm_id = current_setting('app.firm_id')::uuid.
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1 with Auth0 JWT validation on all routes. GET /matters?status=active (paginated matter list for firm). POST /matters (create matter). GET /matters/:id (matter detail with deadlines). POST /matters/:id/deadlines (create deadline, runs conflict check). GET /matters/:id/docket-entries (paginated docket history). POST /matters/:id/scheduling-orders (register uploaded court order, enqueues NLP job). GET /matters/:id/deadline-suggestions (pending NLP extractions). POST /deadlines/:id/confirm (attorney confirms NLP suggestion, inserts deadline). POST /deadlines/:id/assign (assign to team member). GET /admin/users (firm admin user list). WebSocket endpoint: WSS /ws/firm/:firmId?token=... (Auth0 token validated on upgrade, Last-Event-ID header for replay).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 Universal Login with MFA enforced for all roles (attorney, paralegal, billing_admin, firm_admin). JWT contains firm_id and role claims; Fastify preHandler extracts and sets PostgreSQL session variable app.firm_id before every query, activating RLS policies. PostgreSQL RLS USING policies on matters, deadlines, docket_entries, task_assignments, notifications scoped to firm_id. Append-only audit_events table (INSERT only role for API user; SELECT only for compliance reports). WAF on ALB with rules blocking SQL injection, XSS, and rate-limiting to 200 req/min per IP. WebSocket upgrade validates Auth0 token and firm_id match. S3 scheduling order bucket uses server-side encryption (SSE-S3) and presigned URLs with 15-minute expiry. ABA Rule 1.6 confidentiality: no matter data in client-side localStorage except last_event_id (non-sensitive stream cursor). SOC 2 Type II controls: access logging, MFA, encryption at rest (RDS encryption enabled), change management via PR review and ECS rolling deploys.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster with two task definitions: api-server (Node.js 20 Fastify, 0.5 vCPU, 1GB RAM, auto-scale 2-6 tasks on CPU 60% and custom WebSocket connection count metric via Datadog) and nlp-worker (Bull consumer + spaCy sidecar, 1 vCPU, 2GB RAM, scale 1-3 tasks on Bull queue depth). ALB routes HTTP/WebSocket traffic to api-server target group; WebSocket connections use ALB with stickiness disabled (Redis pub/sub handles fan-out across tasks). GitHub Actions CI: ESLint + tsc --noEmit, Vitest (80% coverage gate on API routes), Docker build + ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with 100% minimum healthy tasks. Pre-deploy ECS run-task executes node-pg-migrate up with connection to RDS. Environments: dev (single AZ Fargate, RDS t3.micro), staging (multi-AZ mirror of prod), production (us-east-1a/1c multi-AZ RDS, ElastiCache with automatic failover, ECS with 2 baseline tasks). Rollback: ECS deployment circuit breaker on health check failure auto-reverts to previous task definition. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for DB credentials, Auth0 client secret, rotation every 30 days.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM via dd-trace Node.js agent injected as ECS sidecar. Structured JSON logs with fields: firm_id, matter_id, user_id, request_id, level, message. Custom Datadog metrics: active_websocket_connections (gauge, per Fargate task), docket_event_publish_latency_ms (histogram), nlp_parse_duration_seconds (histogram), nlp_success_rate (gauge), deadline_conflict_detections_total (counter), redis_stream_replay_events_total (counter). PagerDuty P1 alerts: WebSocket error rate >2%, Bull dead-letter queue depth >10, RDS replica lag >30s, API p99 latency >2s. Datadog dashboard: docket board showing live WebSocket connection count, NLP worker throughput, and overdue deadline count by firm.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ~$1,600/month for 50 concurrent users across 10 firms: ECS Fargate (2-6 api-server + 1-3 nlp-worker tasks) ~$400, RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium ~$400, ElastiCache cache.t3.small ~$80, ALB ~$30, S3 storage ~$20, Auth0 B2B plan ~$400, CloudFront ~$50, Datadog ~$200 (within $2,000 budget). Scaling path: read replica activated when analytics dashboard query load exceeds 40% CPU on primary; ECS task count scales to 8 api-server tasks for 200 concurrent users at ~$2,800/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-8): Auth0 integration with MFA, matter CRUD, manual deadline entry, task assignment, PostgreSQL RLS policies on all tables, audit_events logging, WebSocket docket board with Redis pub/sub, Redis Streams replay buffer (ISS-001 fix), ECS Fargate deployment with GitHub Actions CI/CD, Datadog APM. Phase 2 (Weeks 9-14): Scheduling order upload, spaCy NLP date extraction Bull worker, attorney confirmation preview workflow, deadline conflict detection, court_calendar events. Phase 3 (Weeks 15-20): Firm analytics dashboard, admin user management panel, PACER/state EFSP docket pull integration, PDF export of matter timelines.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on both Next.js and Fastify codebases. ESLint with @typescript-eslint/recommended. Vitest for API unit and integration tests (80% coverage gate on route handlers). Playwright E2E for docket board reconnection flow, scheduling order NLP confirmation workflow, and task assignment notifications. PostgreSQL RLS tested via separate connection with app.firm_id set to a different firm asserting zero rows returned. PR requires 1 senior engineering review; migrations reviewed separately by tech lead. ADR records for WebSocket transport choice, Redis Streams replay approach, and RLS policy design.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis Streams replay buffer lost on ElastiCache failover during a 30-second window. Mitigation: docket_entries table in PostgreSQL serves as authoritative durable log; clients can fall back to REST GET /matters/:id/docket-entries on connect if Redis stream is unavailable. Tradeoff: row-level security adds per-query overhead (~2ms) vs application-layer filtering; accepted given firm_id index on all matter tables. Risk: spaCy NER model false positives on court order date extraction causing suggested deadlines with wrong dates. Mitigation: attorney confirmation step is mandatory; NLP suggestions are never auto-applied. Risk: ECS WebSocket sticky sessions not feasible with ALB; Redis pub/sub is required for multi-task fan-out, adding Redis as a critical dependency. Mitigation: ElastiCache Multi-AZ with automatic failover.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: WebSocket transport chosen over SSE because SSE is unidirectional and task assignment notifications require server-to-client push from multiple event sources. Redis Streams chosen over a database polling approach for replay because it provides sub-millisecond fan-out with ordered delivery. PostgreSQL RLS chosen over separate schemas per firm due to migration complexity at scale; RLS with firm_id indexes provides equivalent isolation with simpler operational model. spaCy chosen for NLP date extraction over a cloud NLP API because matter document content must not leave the AWS boundary per ABA Rule 1.6.

metadata.notes: Round 4 litigation matter management; resolves WebSocket replay buffer ISS-001 via Redis Streams with last_event_id replay on reconnect; RLS and NLP review gate already resolved in prior rounds.

project.goal: Drive free-trial signups for regulatory alert SaaS among compliance officers
project.target_users: Compliance officers at financial institutions
project.timeline: 2-week single sprint

frontend.bundler: Vite with manual chunk splitting for above-fold content

devops.ci: GitHub Actions Netlify deploy preview on PR

reasoner_reviews.product: The page must speak directly to the compliance officer pain of keeping up with daily rule publications from FinCEN, OCC, CFPB, and SEC without manual monitoring. Feature highlights should lead with the regulation diff view rather than generic real-time alerts since diff comparison is the most differentiated feature for this persona.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Vite static build deployed to Netlify CDN is the right architecture. The email form proxy as a Netlify function avoids exposing the product API key in browser JavaScript. No database or backend runtime is needed; adding one would be over-engineering for a marketing page.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP header should block inline scripts except for GA4 gtag. Cookie consent banner must gate the GA4 gtag initialization script. No user credentials or financial data pass through this page; risk is limited to third-party script injection.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 300KB gzip budget is achievable with Vite tree-shaking and Tailwind CSS purge. Above-fold content in the main bundle; below-fold sections lazy-loaded via React.lazy on intersection observer.

frontend.app_structure: Single-page React 18 app with section-based components: HeroSection, FeatureHighlights, PricingPlans, Testimonials, TrialSignupForm, CookieConsentBanner. No routing library; single URL with anchor scroll.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single page with anchor navigation. CTA buttons scroll to TrialSignupForm. Form submission calls Netlify function /api/signup, shows success state with onboarding redirect link for regulatory alert product.
frontend.performance_notes: Vite code-split: main bundle (hero, nav) under 120KB gzip. FeatureHighlights, PricingPlans, Testimonials lazy-loaded on intersection. Images served as WebP via Netlify Image CDN under 100KB each.
frontend.accessibility_notes: All CTA buttons have aria-label. Feature highlight icons have aria-hidden. Form inputs have associated labels. Color contrast AA on hero text over background gradient.

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify CDN with global edge nodes. GitHub repo connected to Netlify for automatic deploy on push to main. Branch deploy previews for all pull requests.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint, Vite build, Lighthouse CI check (score above 90 gate on performance/accessibility), deploy to Netlify via CLI. Deploy preview URL posted to PR as comment.
devops.cost_controls: Netlify free tier covers static hosting and 125K function invocations per month. GA4 free tier. Total cost zero at launch volume.

security.abuse_prevention: Netlify function for email form includes reCAPTCHA v3 score check to prevent bot signups. Rate limit 10 requests per minute per IP via Netlify function middleware.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR cookie consent banner gates GA4 gtag script initialization. No cookies set before consent. localStorage used only for consent preference key.

target_output.thinking_summary: Simple static landing page; the key architectural constraint is keeping everything truly static with no server-side runtime except a thin Netlify function for email capture. The 300KB gzip budget drives Vite code-splitting strategy with lazy section loading. GDPR consent gating GA4 initialization is the only non-trivial implementation detail requiring care.
target_output.title: Regulatory Alert Service - Trial Acquisition Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A static React 18 landing page for a regulatory alert SaaS product targeting compliance officers at financial institutions. Hosted on Netlify CDN with a Vite build delivering a sub-300KB gzip bundle. A single Netlify serverless function proxies free-trial email captures to the product CRM API. GA4 tracks scroll depth and CTA conversion events behind a GDPR cookie consent gate.
target_output.architecture_overview: Static React 18 single-page application built with Vite and deployed to Netlify CDN. No application backend; a single Netlify serverless Node.js function handles email form submission, forwarding lead data to the product external CRM API without exposing the API key in client-side JavaScript. Google Analytics 4 is initialized only after explicit GDPR cookie consent. All assets served over HTTPS via Netlify managed TLS with Content Security Policy headers blocking unauthorized script sources including inline scripts.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript 5, Vite 5, Tailwind CSS 3, react-intersection-observer for lazy section loading. Form proxy: Netlify function Node.js 18 runtime. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with gtag.js, consent-gated. Cookie consent: vanilla cookie-consent v3. Build tooling: Vite with @vitejs/plugin-react, Vitest for Netlify function unit tests. CI: GitHub Actions with Netlify CLI and Lighthouse CI plugin.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: headline targeting compliance officer pain of manual FinCEN/OCC/CFPB/SEC monitoring, primary CTA scrolling to trial form. Feature Highlights: three cards for push notifications delivering rule publication alerts within 60 seconds, regulation diff view with side-by-side redline comparison, and multi-jurisdiction coverage spanning federal and state regulators. Pricing Plans: Starter/Professional/Enterprise comparison table with per-seat pricing. Testimonials: compliance officer quotes with institution type and title. Free Trial Form: email input with reCAPTCHA v3, submission states (idle/submitting/success/error), onboarding redirect on success. GDPR Cookie Banner: accept/decline controls gating GA4 initialization on first visit.
target_output.system_components: Netlify CDN edge nodes serving pre-built static assets (HTML, JS chunks, CSS, WebP images). Single Netlify serverless function at /.netlify/functions/signup receiving form POST, validating reCAPTCHA score via Google API, forwarding to CRM API with API key stored in Netlify environment variable. GA4 property with custom events for CTA clicks and scroll depth. GitHub repository connected to Netlify for deploy automation via webhook.
target_output.workflows: Free Trial Signup: Visitor enters email in TrialSignupForm, reCAPTCHA v3 executes client-side generating a score token, form POSTs to /.netlify/functions/signup with email and token. Netlify function verifies reCAPTCHA score against Google API (threshold 0.5), forwards email to CRM API, returns success with redirect URL or error. Client shows success state with link to product onboarding page. GDPR Consent Flow: On first visit, cookie consent banner renders before any GA4 script loads. On accept, vanilla cookie-consent sets consent cookie and dynamically appends gtag.js script tag. On decline, GA4 is never loaded for that session. Subsequent visits check consent cookie and suppress the banner accordingly. Scroll Conversion Tracking: GA4 fires cta_click event with button_id parameter on each primary and secondary CTA interaction. Enhanced measurement scroll depth fires at 25/50/75/90 percent thresholds for funnel analysis.
target_output.data_model: No database or server-side data model. React component state only: form_state enum idle/submitting/success/error in TrialSignupForm useState. Consent preference stored in localStorage key regulatory_alert_consent_v1 as string accept or decline. No user data persisted on landing page infrastructure. Lead email data is passed through to the product CRM API and not retained in any Netlify storage.
target_output.api_design: Single internal endpoint: POST /.netlify/functions/signup accepting JSON body with email string and recaptcha_token string. Returns 200 with JSON success true and redirect_url string on valid submission, 400 with error string on reCAPTCHA score below threshold, 502 on CRM API upstream error. No versioning. No public REST API surface.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via Netlify managed TLS with HSTS. Content Security Policy response header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.google-analytics.com https://www.googletagmanager.com https://www.google.com/recaptcha/; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:. reCAPTCHA v3 invisible bot protection on form submission with 0.5 score threshold. GDPR compliance: cookie consent banner gates GA4 initialization; no tracking cookies set before explicit consent; privacy policy link in footer. CRM API key stored in Netlify environment variable, never bundled in client JavaScript.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Netlify continuous deployment: push to main triggers Vite production build and CDN deploy with cache invalidation. Pull requests receive branch deploy previews with unique subdomain URLs for stakeholder review. GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs ESLint, TypeScript check, Vite build, Vitest unit tests for Netlify function reCAPTCHA and CRM proxy logic, Lighthouse CI scoring (blocks merge if performance score below 90 or accessibility below 90). Netlify deploy hooks post Slack notifications on production deploy success or failure. No infrastructure provisioning required; Netlify manages CDN distribution, TLS certificate renewal, and function cold-start scaling automatically.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 dashboard: pageviews, session duration, scroll depth milestones, cta_click events per button ID, form_submission_success count, form_submission_error count. Conversion rate reported as form_submission_success divided by unique pageviews. Netlify analytics for raw CDN request volume and function invocation counts. Netlify deploy status notifications in Slack channel.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Netlify free tier covers 100GB bandwidth and 125K function invocations per month with zero cost. GA4 and reCAPTCHA free tiers apply at expected launch traffic volumes. Upgrade path to Netlify Pro at 19 dollars per month if bandwidth exceeds 100GB or password-protected preview deploys are needed. No autoscaling configuration required; Netlify CDN handles traffic spikes automatically.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Week 1): Vite project setup with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS, HeroSection with primary and secondary CTA buttons, TrialSignupForm component with Netlify function integration and reCAPTCHA v3, CookieConsentBanner gating GA4, GitHub Actions CI pipeline with Lighthouse CI gate, Netlify production deploy. Phase 2 Content Complete (Week 2): FeatureHighlights section with regulation diff view callout, PricingPlans comparison table for Starter/Professional/Enterprise tiers, Testimonials section with compliance officer quotes, below-fold section lazy loading via React.lazy and intersection observer, WebP image optimization for all hero and feature graphics.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in Vite config. ESLint with react-hooks and jsx-a11y plugins enforced in CI. All section components in src/sections/ directory. Netlify function in netlify/functions/signup.ts with Vitest unit tests covering reCAPTCHA validation logic and CRM API forwarding. No inline styles; Tailwind utility classes only. Images as WebP under 100KB each. Lighthouse CI threshold set to performance 90, accessibility 90, best-practices 90 in lighthouserc.json.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: reCAPTCHA v3 score threshold of 0.5 may block legitimate signups from compliance officers using corporate network proxies that elevate bot-score heuristics. Mitigation: log borderline scores between 0.4 and 0.6 to Netlify function logs for manual review and threshold tuning. Risk: CRM API downtime causes form errors for visitors during an outage, reducing signup conversion. Mitigation: Netlify function returns a fallback message with a direct email address so visitors can reach the team. Tradeoff: vanilla cookie-consent library over a full-featured CMP reduces bundle size by approximately 80KB but provides less granular consent category logging than IAB TCF-compliant alternatives.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Static Netlify hosting chosen over Vercel because the product backend already uses Netlify functions for other integrations. reCAPTCHA v3 invisible mode chosen over v2 checkbox to avoid signup friction for time-constrained compliance officer persona. GA4 enhanced measurement scroll events used instead of a custom scroll observer to reduce JavaScript maintenance overhead on a static marketing page.

metadata.notes: Landing page for regulatory alert SaaS; static Netlify CDN with Netlify function form proxy; GDPR consent-gated GA4; weak quality reflects minimal architecture complexity for a simple marketing page.

profile.domain: Enterprise Risk & Compliance - Audit Management

project_goal.value: Internal compliance audit management web application tracking audit engagements, control testing evidence, deficiency findings, and remediation plans across SOX, ISO 27001, and NIST SP 800-53 frameworks, replacing spreadsheet-based workflows for internal audit teams at mid-to-large enterprises
project_goal.rationale: Eliminating spreadsheet-based audit tracking reduces evidence management errors and improves audit cycle time

target_users.value: Internal auditors, control owners, and risk managers at mid-to-large enterprises running SOX, ISO 27001, and NIST audit programs
target_users.rationale: Primary user groups identified in discovery; control owners are secondary users who upload evidence artifacts

project_class.rationale: Browser-based internal application with role-based workflows for auditors, control owners, and audit managers

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, admin_panel, batch_jobs, devops
capabilities.rationale: Batch evidence export, scheduled risk score recalculation, admin for framework management drive full capability set

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-framework control mapping, residual risk scoring engine, Helm-based AKS deployment, and evidence artifact versioning add substantial complexity

risk_level.rationale: Contains SOX audit evidence and ISO 27001 control test results subject to external auditor review and regulatory scrutiny

data_sensitivity.rationale: Audit findings, deficiency details, and remediation status are confidential internal documents

external_exposure.rationale: Application accessible only within corporate network; no public internet exposure

access_model.value: RBAC via Azure AD groups: auditor, control_owner, audit_manager, risk_officer, admin; SAML SSO enforced; no local accounts permitted
access_model.rationale: Azure AD integration enables centralized identity governance and MFA enforcement at the IdP level

feature_scope.value: Audit engagement lifecycle management, control objective library with SOX/ISO/NIST mappings, test procedure assignment and execution tracking, evidence artifact upload with version history, findings and deficiency logging with severity ratings, remediation plan tracking with due-date alerting, automated residual risk score computation, multi-framework dashboard, batch evidence export for external auditors
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope covering audit lifecycle from planning through closure

mvp_scope.value: Audit engagement creation, control testing task assignment, evidence upload, finding creation with severity, basic remediation tracking, Azure AD SAML login
mvp_scope.rationale: Core workflow for Q2 SOX pilot with 20 auditors

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SAML SSO with MFA, TLS 1.3 in transit, Azure Disk Encryption at rest, audit trail for all data mutations, WAF on AKS ingress
security_baseline.rationale: SOX and ISO 27001 baseline controls for internal financial audit system

frontend_stack.value: Vue.js 3 with TypeScript and Composition API, Pinia for state management
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise and enterprise component library availability for Vue 3

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3, Spring Security for SAML, Spring Batch for bulk operations
backend_stack.rationale: Java ecosystem alignment with enterprise standards; Spring Batch for evidence export jobs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Redis 7 for session cache and job queues
data_platform.rationale: Managed PostgreSQL with HA failover; Redis for Spring Session and Batch job coordination

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service with Helm chart deployments, Azure Blob Storage for evidence artifacts
hosting_target.rationale: AKS replaces legacy manual deployment; Helm enables versioned rollback and environment promotion

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit evidence and findings retained for 7 years per SOX requirements; remediation records 5 years; audit trail logs 3 years in Azure Monitor
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX Section 802 document retention obligations

future_scope.value: External auditor portal with read-only evidence access, AI-assisted control gap analysis, integration with GRC platforms (ServiceNow, Archer)
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap items post-SOX pilot success

constraints.value: Must deploy to Azure West Europe only; no data egress outside EU boundary; budget 3000 euros per month; must integrate with existing Azure AD tenant
constraints.rationale: EU data residency and Azure AD identity governance constraints

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor, Application Insights for Spring Boot APM, Log Analytics workspace for centralized logging
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard enterprise Azure monitoring stack

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with Azure DevOps boards, pipeline-gated promotions from dev to staging to production
execution_preference.rationale: Azure DevOps alignment with enterprise CI/CD tooling

compliance_context.value: SOX Section 302/404 for financial controls testing, ISO 27001 Annex A control evidence, NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 control assessment; external auditor evidence package requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Multi-framework audit scope drives compliance requirements for data integrity and audit trail completeness

project.goal: Replace spreadsheet-based SOX/ISO/NIST audit workflows with a structured web application
project.target_users: Internal auditors, control owners, audit managers
project.timeline: MVP Q2, full scope Q4

backend.batch: Spring Batch for evidence export and risk recalculation jobs

security.auth: Azure AD SAML SSO via Spring Security SAML2
security.audit: Append-only audit_trail table with entity_id, actor_id, action, diff_json

data.db: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server
data.storage: Azure Blob Storage for evidence artifacts

devops.ci: Azure DevOps Pipelines

reasoner_reviews.product: The audit engagement lifecycle is the core workflow: engagements contain control objectives, each objective has test procedures, each test procedure produces evidence artifacts and either a passed result or a finding. The residual risk score is computed per control objective based on finding severity and remediation status. This hierarchical model must be central to the data schema.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot on AKS with Helm charts is the right infrastructure modernization from manual deployments. Spring Batch is appropriate for the nightly residual risk score recalculation job scanning all open findings and the batch evidence export that packages artifacts per engagement for external auditors. Redis session store is needed because AKS pods are stateless and Spring Session requires a distributed store.
reasoner_reviews.security: SAML SSO via Azure AD with MFA enforced at the IdP removes all local credential management risk. The audit trail must be an append-only table with a INSERT-only database role for the application user; SELECT access for compliance reports via a separate read role. Azure Blob Storage for evidence must use Azure AD-delegated access with SAS tokens rather than shared access keys.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3000 EUR/month budget supports AKS Standard tier (2 node pool with 2x Standard_D2s_v3 nodes), Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 2 vCores, Azure Cache for Redis C1, and Azure Blob Storage. Spring Batch nightly jobs can run as Kubernetes Jobs on the same AKS cluster, avoiding a separate Azure Batch account cost.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 3 introduced Helm chart deployments but the plan still describes Azure DevOps pipeline steps for manual kubectl apply, which contradicts the Helm-based deployment goal. Round 4 must align the CI/CD pipeline steps with helm upgrade --install and a values.yaml per environment promotion pattern.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3 monolith with domain packages: engagements, controls, testing, findings, remediation, batch, admin. Spring Security SAML2 for Azure AD SSO. Spring Batch job definitions for nightly risk recalculation and evidence export ZIP generation.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful JSON API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec auto-generated via springdoc-openapi. HATEOAS links on engagement and control resources for UI navigation. Pagination via Spring Data Pageable on all list endpoints.
backend.background_jobs: Spring Batch jobs: risk_score_recalc (nightly 02:00 UTC, chunks of 100 control objectives, computes residual_risk_score from open finding severities weighted by control criticality) and evidence_export (triggered on demand, packages artifact blobs per engagement into a ZIP via Azure Blob batch copy, stores export_jobs record with download URL).
backend.failure_handling: Spring Batch jobs use skip policy on individual item failures with dead-letter logging to batch_job_errors table. AKS pod disruption budget ensures at least 1 API pod during node maintenance.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Vue Router, Pinia stores per domain (engagements, controls, findings, remediation). PrimeVue for data tables, file upload, and form components. Route guards check SAML session and role claims.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dashboard (engagement summary, overdue findings by framework), Engagement Detail (control testing matrix, completion progress), Control Test Execution (evidence upload, test result entry), Finding Detail (severity, root cause, remediation plan), Remediation Tracker (due date, owner, status), Admin (framework configuration, user role assignment).
frontend.state_management: Pinia engagements store caches active engagement data. Findings store holds current finding list with optimistic status updates. Auth store holds SAML session user, roles, and engagement permissions.

security.auth_design: Spring Security SAML2 SP configured against Azure AD IdP. Assertion consumer service at /saml/acs. RelayState preserves return URL post-login. Spring Session Redis stores authenticated sessions with 8-hour timeout.
security.authorization_model: Method-level @PreAuthorize annotations on service methods. Roles: AUDITOR (read/write own assignments), CONTROL_OWNER (upload evidence for assigned controls), AUDIT_MANAGER (full engagement CRUD), RISK_OFFICER (read all, approve findings), ADMIN (user and framework management).
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_trail table (id, engagement_id, entity_type, entity_id, actor_id, action ENUM create/update/delete/view, diff_json, occurred_at). Spring AOP @Auditable aspect on service methods. INSERT-only DB role for audit trail writes.

data.entities: audit_engagements (id, framework ENUM sox/iso27001/nist, engagement_name, fiscal_year, status ENUM planning/fieldwork/review/closed, lead_auditor_id, start_date, end_date). control_objectives (id, engagement_id, control_id FK control_library, test_status ENUM not_started/in_progress/completed/deficient, residual_risk_score DECIMAL, updated_at). test_procedures (id, control_objective_id, description, assigned_to_id, due_date, result ENUM pass/fail/not_tested). evidence_artifacts (id, test_procedure_id, file_name, blob_storage_path, uploaded_by_id, version INTEGER, uploaded_at). findings (id, control_objective_id, engagement_id, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, title, root_cause, management_response, status ENUM open/remediated/accepted). remediation_plans (id, finding_id, owner_id, due_date, status ENUM not_started/in_progress/completed/overdue, updated_at). risk_ratings (id, control_objective_id, inherent_risk, control_effectiveness DECIMAL, residual_risk DECIMAL, calculated_at).
data.storage_design: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with automated backups and point-in-time restore. Azure Blob Storage container per engagement for evidence artifacts with Azure AD delegated SAS tokens (2-hour expiry). Redis 7 for Spring Session and Spring Batch job metadata.
data.retention_and_deletion: audit_engagements soft-deleted; purged after 7 years via scheduled Spring Batch job per SOX 802. evidence_artifacts retained 7 years in Blob Storage Lifecycle policy (hot tier year 1, cool tier years 2-7, archive tier post-7 years then delete).

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster in Azure West Europe with two node pools: system (Standard_D2s_v3 x2) and workload (Standard_D4s_v3 x2-4 auto-scale). Kubernetes Deployments for API server and Vue static server via nginx. Spring Batch jobs as Kubernetes CronJob resources.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline stages: build (Maven 3.9 compile + Surefire test), Docker build + ACR push, Helm lint, deploy-dev (helm upgrade --install audit-app ./helm/audit-app -f values-dev.yaml), integration test, promote-staging gate (manual approval), deploy-staging, promote-production gate (audit manager approval), deploy-production (helm upgrade --install -f values-prod.yaml --atomic).
devops.rollback_strategy: helm rollback audit-app [revision] on production incident. AKS deployment rollout undo as fallback. PostgreSQL point-in-time restore for data corruption scenarios.

target_output.title: Compliance Audit Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: An internal compliance audit management web application for mid-to-large enterprises running SOX, ISO 27001, and NIST SP 800-53 audit programs. Built with Vue.js 3 and Spring Boot 3 on Azure Kubernetes Service with Helm chart deployments. Tracks audit engagements, control testing evidence, findings, and remediation plans with automated residual risk score computation via Spring Batch. Azure AD SAML SSO enforces identity governance with role-based access for auditors, control owners, and audit managers.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-tier browser application: Vue.js 3 SPA served by a Kubernetes nginx Deployment communicating over HTTPS to a Spring Boot 3 REST API running in a separate AKS Deployment. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server stores engagement, control, finding, and audit trail data. Azure Blob Storage holds evidence artifact files accessed via Azure AD delegated SAS tokens. Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis provides distributed Spring Session storage and Spring Batch job coordination. All deployments managed by Helm 3 charts with per-environment values.yaml files and Azure DevOps pipeline promotion gates.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue.js 3, TypeScript 5, Vite 5, Pinia 2, Vue Router 4, PrimeVue 3 (data tables, file upload, forms). Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.2, Spring Security SAML2, Spring Data JPA, Spring Batch 5, springdoc-openapi. Database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server, Flyway for migrations. Cache/Session: Azure Cache for Redis 7. Storage: Azure Blob Storage SDK v12. Infrastructure: AKS Standard tier, Helm 3, Azure Container Registry, Azure DevOps Pipelines, Azure Monitor, Application Insights.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Audit Engagement Management: create, assign, and track SOX/ISO 27001/NIST engagements through planning, fieldwork, review, and closure phases. Control Testing Matrix: per-engagement grid of control objectives with test procedure assignments, evidence upload slots, and completion status. Evidence Artifact Management: versioned file upload to Azure Blob Storage with download via SAS token, audit trail on every upload and access. Finding and Deficiency Logging: severity-rated findings linked to control objectives with root cause, management response, and status. Remediation Plan Tracking: owner-assigned remediation tasks with due-date alerting via scheduled job. Automated Risk Score Engine: nightly Spring Batch recalculation of residual_risk per control objective from finding severity and control effectiveness. Multi-Framework Dashboard: engagement completion rates and open finding counts segmented by SOX/ISO/NIST. Batch Evidence Export: on-demand ZIP packaging of all artifacts per engagement for external auditor delivery. Admin Panel: framework configuration, control library management, user role assignment.
target_output.system_components: AKS cluster with system and workload node pools in Azure West Europe. api-server Deployment (Spring Boot 3 JAR in Docker, 2-4 replicas auto-scaled on CPU 60%). static-server Deployment (nginx serving Vite-built Vue SPA, 2 replicas). Spring Batch Kubernetes CronJob for nightly risk recalculation at 02:00 UTC. Spring Batch Kubernetes Job triggered on evidence export requests. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with read replica for reporting queries. Azure Cache for Redis C1 for Spring Session and Batch metadata. Azure Blob Storage container hierarchy: engagements/{engagement_id}/artifacts/. Azure Container Registry for Docker images. Azure DevOps pipeline for CI/CD. Application Insights agent on API pods. Azure Monitor Log Analytics workspace.
target_output.workflows: Control Testing and Evidence Upload: Audit manager assigns test procedures to auditors in the control testing matrix. Auditor navigates to assigned test procedure, selects result (pass/fail/not_tested), uploads evidence file via Vue file upload component. Vue calls POST /api/v1/test-procedures/{id}/evidence (multipart). Spring controller requests Azure AD delegated SAS upload token, returns presigned URL. Client uploads directly to Blob Storage using SAS URL. On successful upload, controller inserts evidence_artifacts record with blob_storage_path and increments version. audit_trail records upload action. Finding and Remediation Workflow: Auditor creates finding on a failed control objective via POST /api/v1/findings with severity, root cause, and management response fields. Audit manager assigns finding to control owner for remediation via PUT /api/v1/remediation-plans with owner_id and due_date. Nightly Spring Batch job checks remediation_plans.due_date; sends overdue alert notifications for plans past due with status not_started or in_progress. Residual Risk Recalculation: Nightly CronJob triggers Spring Batch risk_score_recalc job at 02:00 UTC. Job reads all control_objectives with status not_started or deficient in chunks of 100. For each, aggregates open findings by severity (critical weight 1.0, high 0.6, medium 0.2, low 0.05) and multiplies by control criticality_weight, stores result in risk_ratings.residual_risk with calculated_at timestamp.
target_output.data_model: audit_engagements (id UUID PK, framework ENUM sox/iso27001/nist, engagement_name VARCHAR 200, fiscal_year INTEGER, status ENUM planning/fieldwork/review/closed, lead_auditor_id UUID FK users, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). control_objectives (id UUID PK, engagement_id UUID FK, control_id UUID FK control_library, test_status ENUM not_started/in_progress/completed/deficient, residual_risk_score DECIMAL(5,2), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). test_procedures (id UUID PK, control_objective_id UUID FK, description TEXT, assigned_to_id UUID FK users, due_date DATE, result ENUM pass/fail/not_tested). evidence_artifacts (id UUID PK, test_procedure_id UUID FK, file_name VARCHAR 255, blob_storage_path TEXT, uploaded_by_id UUID FK users, version INTEGER DEFAULT 1, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). findings (id UUID PK, control_objective_id UUID FK, engagement_id UUID FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, title VARCHAR 255, root_cause TEXT, management_response TEXT, status ENUM open/remediated/accepted). remediation_plans (id UUID PK, finding_id UUID FK, owner_id UUID FK users, due_date DATE, status ENUM not_started/in_progress/completed/overdue, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). risk_ratings (id UUID PK, control_objective_id UUID FK, inherent_risk DECIMAL(5,2), control_effectiveness DECIMAL(5,2), residual_risk DECIMAL(5,2), calculated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_trail (id UUID PK, engagement_id UUID, entity_type VARCHAR, entity_id UUID, actor_id UUID FK users, action ENUM create/update/delete/view, diff_json JSONB, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 via springdoc-openapi. GET /engagements?framework=sox&status=fieldwork (paginated). POST /engagements (create engagement). GET /engagements/{id}/control-matrix (control objectives with test status). POST /test-procedures/{id}/evidence (request SAS upload token, register artifact). GET /evidence/{id}/download (return SAS download URL with 2-hour expiry). POST /findings (create finding on control objective). PUT /remediation-plans/{id} (update owner, due_date, status). GET /dashboard/framework-summary (open finding counts by framework and severity). POST /engagements/{id}/export (trigger evidence export batch job, returns job_id). GET /export-jobs/{id}/status (poll export job status and download URL when complete).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SAML2 SSO via Spring Security SAML2 SP; MFA enforced at Azure AD IdP for all user accounts; no local password accounts. Spring Security method-level @PreAuthorize RBAC: AUDITOR (read/write own test procedures and evidence), CONTROL_OWNER (upload evidence for assigned controls), AUDIT_MANAGER (full engagement CRUD, finding assignment), RISK_OFFICER (read all data, approve remediation), ADMIN (framework and user management). Append-only audit_trail table with INSERT-only Postgres role for application service account; SELECT via dedicated compliance_reader role. Azure Blob Storage: no shared access keys; all upload and download operations use Azure AD delegated SAS tokens with 2-hour expiry generated by backend. TLS 1.3 enforced on AKS ingress. WAF policy on AKS Application Gateway ingress blocking OWASP Top 10. SOX Section 302/404 compliance: audit trail covers all control test result changes; evidence version history maintained. ISO 27001 Annex A: RBAC aligns with access control objective A.9.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS Standard tier cluster in Azure West Europe with system node pool (Standard_D2s_v3 x2 fixed) and workload node pool (Standard_D4s_v3 x2-4 auto-scale on CPU 60%). Helm chart structure: helm/audit-app/templates/{api-deployment, static-deployment, service, ingress, configmap, cronjob, hpa}.yaml with values.yaml (base) overridden by values-dev.yaml, values-staging.yaml, values-prod.yaml. Azure DevOps Pipeline stages: (1) build: Maven 3.9 compile + Surefire unit tests + JaCoCo coverage gate 80%; (2) docker-build: Docker multi-stage build + ACR push tagged with git commit SHA; (3) helm-lint: helm lint + helm template validation; (4) deploy-dev: helm upgrade --install audit-app ./helm/audit-app -f values-dev.yaml --namespace dev --atomic --timeout 5m; (5) integration-tests: Spring Boot test profile against dev namespace; (6) promote-staging: manual approval gate; (7) deploy-staging: helm upgrade --install audit-app ./helm/audit-app -f values-staging.yaml --namespace staging --atomic; (8) promote-production: audit manager approval gate; (9) deploy-production: helm upgrade --install audit-app ./helm/audit-app -f values-prod.yaml --namespace production --atomic. Flyway migrations run as Kubernetes Job pre-deploy hook in chart. Rollback: helm rollback audit-app [revision] --namespace production for fast revert; helm history audit-app tracks all revisions. Spring Batch CronJobs defined in Helm chart CronJob resource with configurable schedule in values.yaml.
target_output.observability: Application Insights Java agent attached to api-server Deployment via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING env var from Kubernetes Secret. Custom metrics tracked: evidence_upload_count (counter), finding_created_by_severity (counter with severity label), risk_score_recalc_duration_seconds (histogram), export_job_completion_time_seconds (histogram), remediation_overdue_count (gauge). Azure Monitor Log Analytics workspace aggregates AKS container logs and Application Insights telemetry. Alert rules: API error rate above 5% triggers P2 PagerDuty; Spring Batch CronJob failure triggers P1 alert; PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion triggers P2 alert.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated 2600 EUR/month: AKS workload nodes Standard_D4s_v3 x2 baseline approximately 900 EUR, system nodes Standard_D2s_v3 x2 approximately 400 EUR, Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 2 vCores approximately 300 EUR, Azure Cache for Redis C1 approximately 60 EUR, Azure Blob Storage approximately 40 EUR (evidence artifacts), Azure Container Registry Basic approximately 5 EUR, Azure DevOps approximately 30 EUR, Application Insights approximately 50 EUR. Scaling path: workload node pool scales to 4 nodes at peak audit cycle (Q1 SOX fieldwork) adding approximately 900 EUR staying within 3000 EUR budget.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-8): Azure AD SAML SSO, audit engagement CRUD, control objective assignment, evidence artifact upload with SAS tokens, finding creation with severity, basic remediation tracking, append-only audit_trail, AKS Helm deployment with Azure DevOps pipeline (ISS-001 fix), Application Insights. Phase 2 (Weeks 9-16): Residual risk score recalculation Spring Batch CronJob, multi-framework dashboard, control library with SOX/ISO/NIST mappings, batch evidence export ZIP job, read replica for reporting queries, overdue remediation alert notifications. Phase 3 (Weeks 17-24): External auditor read-only portal, advanced framework dashboard with trend charts, GRC integration API endpoints for ServiceNow/Archer export.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 strict null analysis with SpotBugs in Maven build. Spring Boot test slice tests (@WebMvcTest, @DataJpaTest) for all controllers and repositories; JaCoCo 80% line coverage gate in CI. Vue.js TypeScript strict mode; Vitest for Pinia store unit tests; Playwright E2E for control testing matrix and evidence upload workflows. Flyway migration scripts reviewed in PR by DBA before merge. Helm chart changes validated via helm template and helm lint in CI. ADR documents for SAML SSO implementation, Spring Batch job design, and Helm promotion strategy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server failover causes 30-60 second downtime during node maintenance. Mitigation: application connection pool retry logic with exponential backoff; maintenance window scheduled during 03:00-04:00 UTC Sunday off-peak. Risk: Spring Batch risk recalculation CronJob running during peak morning audit hours could contend for DB connections. Mitigation: CronJob scheduled at 02:00 UTC; separate read replica connection pool for batch jobs isolated from API traffic. Tradeoff: monolithic Spring Boot over microservices enables single Helm chart deployment and simpler Spring Security SAML2 integration, with module package boundaries preserving future extraction path. Risk: AKS node pool auto-scale lag of 3-5 minutes during sudden load spikes at Q1 SOX audit start. Mitigation: pre-warm workload node pool to minimum 2 nodes one week before known audit cycle start dates.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Helm 3 upgrade --install chosen over kubectl apply for atomic deployments with built-in revision history and helm rollback; resolves ISS-001 inconsistency. Azure AD delegated SAS tokens chosen over storage account keys for blob access; resolved in round 3. Spring Batch chosen over Quartz Scheduler for batch jobs due to native Spring Boot 3 integration, chunk-oriented processing, and Kubernetes Job support. Read replica activated for reporting queries in Phase 2 to prevent analytics dashboard load from impacting transactional API performance.

metadata.notes: Round 4 compliance audit management on AKS; resolves ISS-001 kubectl-vs-Helm inconsistency by aligning Azure DevOps pipeline with helm upgrade --install and helm rollback; moderate quality reflects deployment section now strong but some sections at minimum depth.

profile.domain: Legal Technology - Court E-Filing SDK

project_goal.value: Python SDK wrapping court e-filing system APIs (Tyler Technologies File and Serve, Odyssey) exposing a unified interface for submitting filings, polling submission status in near-real-time, and retrieving clerk-stamped return documents for legal document automation vendors
project_goal.rationale: Reduce integration burden for vendors connecting to multiple state court e-filing systems behind incompatible SOAP and REST APIs

target_users.value: Software engineers at legal document automation vendors building filing automation workflows that integrate with Tyler Technologies File and Serve and Odyssey state court systems
target_users.rationale: Primary SDK consumer is integration developers, not end-user attorneys

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a PyPI package; no hosted service, no web UI, no database infrastructure

capabilities.rationale: SDK provides integration adapters and Python client logic only; no frontend or auth capabilities in the package itself

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-system adapter pattern with SOAP XML serialization, OAuth token management, and polling state machine adds moderate complexity

risk_level.rationale: Handles court filing credentials and document content; SDK misuse could result in erroneous court submissions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Filing documents may contain attorney-client privileged content and party PII

external_exposure.rationale: SDK is published to PyPI and consumed by external legal technology vendor partners

access_model.value: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow per court system; filer account credentials provided by SDK consumer via EfilingClient constructor; SDK does not store credentials beyond request lifecycle
access_model.rationale: Each court system uses its own OAuth or API key credential scheme; SDK abstracts credential management but does not persist secrets

feature_scope.value: Unified FilingEnvelope model, Tyler Technologies File and Serve SOAP adapter, Odyssey REST adapter, OAuth 2.0 token management with refresh, submission status polling with configurable intervals, clerk-stamped document retrieval, local submission record with resume-on-failure, batch filing support, jurisdiction registry for supported court systems
feature_scope.rationale: Full SDK feature set covering multi-system filing lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: FilingEnvelope model, Tyler Technologies File and Serve SOAP adapter, OAuth token management, submission status polling, stamped document retrieval
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP targets Tyler Technologies as the highest-volume state court system

security_baseline.value: OAuth 2.0 client credentials for court system auth, TLS 1.2+ for all HTTP calls enforced in httpx client, no credential logging, no document content in log output, secrets passed via constructor not environment discovery
security_baseline.rationale: Filing credentials and document content are confidential; logging must never capture either

frontend_stack.value: None; library SDK with no UI
frontend_stack.rationale: PyPI package; no browser or desktop UI component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12, httpx for async HTTP, zeep for SOAP, pydantic v2 for models, tenacity for retry logic
backend_stack.rationale: httpx provides async-first HTTP with TLS enforcement; zeep for Tyler SOAP; pydantic for validated FilingEnvelope model

data_platform.value: No hosted database; optional local SQLite submission journal via sqlite3 for resume-on-failure; consumer-provided if needed
data_platform.rationale: SDK is stateless by default; local SQLite journal is an optional feature for consumers that need submission resumability

hosting_target.value: PyPI package distribution; no hosted infrastructure
hosting_target.rationale: Library SDK distributed via PyPI; consumers install into their own environments

privacy_retention_policy.value: SDK does not retain filing documents or party data; local SQLite journal stores envelope metadata only (envelope_id, submission status, timestamps) not document content; SDK consumers are responsible for document retention compliance
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SDK is infrastructure; data retention obligations rest with the consuming application

future_scope.value: Additional court system adapters (Catalis, Tyler Odyssey v2), async streaming for large document sets, mock court system for integration testing without live credentials
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items post-Tyler MVP

constraints.value: Python 3.10 minimum compatibility; no mandatory dependencies beyond httpx, zeep, pydantic, tenacity; pyproject.toml packaging; semver versioning
constraints.rationale: Broad Python version support for enterprise consumer environments; minimal dependency footprint to avoid version conflicts

observability_baseline.value: Python logging module with structured records at DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR; no external monitoring infrastructure; consumers configure handlers; submission events emitted as structured log records with envelope_id and court_system fields
observability_baseline.rationale: Library SDK uses standard logging module; consumers integrate with their own APM

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development on main; semver releases via GitHub Actions PyPI publish workflow; changelog maintained in CHANGELOG.md
execution_preference.rationale: Standard open-source library release pattern

compliance_context.value: State e-filing rules and court standing orders govern acceptable filing formats and submission timing; Tyler Technologies File and Serve certified integration requirements; SDK must not submit invalid envelope XML that could trigger court rejection and missed deadlines
compliance_context.rationale: Court submission errors have legal consequences including missed deadlines and potential sanctions under FRCP Rule 11

project.goal: Unified Python SDK for multi-system court e-filing with Tyler Technologies and Odyssey adapters
project.target_users: Legal document automation vendor engineers
project.timeline: MVP (Tyler adapter) in 6 weeks, Odyssey adapter in 10 weeks

devops.ci: GitHub Actions
devops.testing: pytest with pytest-asyncio, responses for HTTP mocking

reasoner_reviews.product: The SDK must present a single FilingEnvelope abstraction that shields vendor engineers from the Tyler Technologies SOAP XML schema and Odyssey REST payload differences. The most critical reliability feature is the polling state machine with configurable timeout and the ability to resume an interrupted batch using the local submission journal.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The adapter pattern with a BaseCourtAdapter abstract class and TylerFileServeAdapter and OdysseyAdapter concrete implementations is the right design. pydantic v2 FilingEnvelope validation should catch malformed envelopes before they reach the court API, preventing irreversible submissions. The optional SQLite journal should be disabled by default and explicitly opt-in to avoid surprise local file creation.
reasoner_reviews.security: The SDK must never log document content or filing credentials at any log level. httpx must be configured with verify=True and minimum TLS 1.2. OAuth tokens should be stored only in memory as instance variables on the adapter, not written to disk. The SDK should provide a credential validation check method that tests auth without submitting a filing.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Python 3.10 minimum is achievable with the chosen dependencies. zeep requires lxml which has a C extension; this should be documented as a build dependency requirement. The local SQLite journal uses only stdlib sqlite3 keeping it a zero-cost optional feature.

backend.service_design: Package structure: efiling_sdk/__init__.py (public exports), efiling_sdk/client.py (EfilingClient facade), efiling_sdk/models.py (FilingEnvelope, SubmissionRecord, StampedReturn pydantic models), efiling_sdk/adapters/base.py (BaseCourtAdapter ABC), efiling_sdk/adapters/tyler.py (TylerFileServeAdapter), efiling_sdk/adapters/odyssey.py (OdysseyAdapter), efiling_sdk/journal.py (optional SQLite SubmissionJournal), efiling_sdk/exceptions.py (EfilingError hierarchy).
backend.api_patterns: Public API surface: EfilingClient(court_system, credentials, journal=None) constructor. client.submit(envelope: FilingEnvelope) -> SubmissionRecord. client.poll_status(submission_id) -> SubmissionStatus. client.retrieve_stamped(submission_id) -> StampedReturn. client.validate_credentials() -> bool. FilingEnvelope(case_number, court_code, filing_type, documents, filer_id, service_contacts) pydantic model.
backend.failure_handling: tenacity @retry decorator on adapter HTTP calls with exponential backoff (wait=wait_exponential(min=2, max=60), stop=stop_after_attempt(5)) and retry on httpx.TimeoutException and httpx.HTTPStatusError 5xx. EfilingError hierarchy: SubmissionValidationError (invalid envelope before submission), CourtSystemError (court API returned rejection), CredentialError (auth failure), PollingTimeoutError (status poll exceeded max_wait). Journal records failed submissions with last_error for manual resume.
backend.scaling_notes: SDK is stateless and thread-safe per EfilingClient instance. Consumers can instantiate multiple clients for parallel court system calls. async/await throughout allows concurrent batch submissions in a single event loop.

security.auth_design: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow implementation in BaseCourtAdapter._authenticate(). Access token stored as self._access_token in adapter instance memory. Token expiry tracked via self._token_expires_at; auto-refresh before expiry with 60-second buffer. No token persistence to disk.
security.secrets_management: Credentials passed to EfilingClient constructor as a Credentials dataclass (client_id, client_secret, filer_code). SDK never reads credentials from environment variables by default. SDK never writes credentials to log output; logger.debug calls use placeholder strings for credential fields.
security.security_risks: Risk of consumer accidentally logging the Credentials object; mitigated by __repr__ override on Credentials that masks client_secret. Risk of MITM on court system API calls; mitigated by httpx verify=True default with no override path exposed in public API.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflows: ci.yml (on push: ruff lint, mypy strict type check, pytest with coverage gate 90%, test matrix Python 3.10/3.11/3.12). publish.yml (on tag v*: hatch build, publish to PyPI via trusted publisher OIDC token, no API key storage required). Pre-commit hooks: ruff, mypy, pytest.
devops.environments: No deployment environments; PyPI as distribution target. Test PyPI used for pre-release validation before production PyPI publish.

ISS-001.title: FilingEnvelope validation does not enforce court-specific envelope XML schema constraints
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 FilingEnvelope pydantic model validates field types but does not enforce Tyler-specific XML namespace requirements, court_code allowlist, or required service_contacts count per court standing orders. Invalid envelopes pass Python validation but are rejected by the Tyler court API, causing missed deadlines.

history.note: pydantic model validates types only; court-specific envelope rules not enforced pre-submission

focus_issues.title: FilingEnvelope validation does not enforce court-specific envelope XML schema constraints
focus_issues.detail: FilingEnvelope pydantic model validates types but not Tyler-specific XML schema constraints; invalid envelopes pass Python validation then fail at court API causing missed deadlines.

previous_plan.title: Court E-Filing Python SDK - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python SDK providing a unified FilingEnvelope model and adapters for Tyler Technologies File and Serve SOAP API and Odyssey REST API. OAuth token management, tenacity retry, and optional SQLite submission journal for resume-on-failure.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Package with EfilingClient facade, BaseCourtAdapter ABC, TylerFileServeAdapter and OdysseyAdapter implementations. pydantic v2 FilingEnvelope model for type-safe envelope construction. httpx async client for all HTTP calls.

SubmissionRecord (submission_id, court_system, status, envelope_id, submitted_at, last_polled_at). StampedReturn (submission_id, stamped_document_bytes, clerk_stamp_date, court_case_number).

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 targets ISS-001: pre-submission envelope validation for court-specific XML schema constraints. The solution is a two-layer validation approach: pydantic v2 FilingEnvelope base model validates field types and formats, plus a CourtEnvelopeValidator class parameterized by court_system that enforces court_code allowlist, minimum service_contacts count per jurisdiction, and document MIME type allowlist before any SOAP or REST call is made. The jurisdiction registry stores these per-court rules. This prevents the category of Tyler court API rejection that causes missed deadlines.

fix_report.action_taken: Added CourtEnvelopeValidator class in efiling_sdk/validators.py with validate(envelope, court_system) method checking court_code against jurisdiction registry allowlist, service_contacts minimum count per court standing orders, and document MIME type against per-court allowlist. EfilingClient.submit() calls validator.validate() before invoking adapter.submit_envelope(), raising SubmissionValidationError with specific field violation details if validation fails. JurisdictionRegistry stores per-court validation rules as CourtValidationRules dataclass.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Envelopes that would be rejected by Tyler court API are caught at the Python validation layer before submission, with a structured SubmissionValidationError indicating the specific constraint violated; no court submission attempted on invalid envelopes.

target_output.title: Court E-Filing Python SDK
target_output.executive_summary: A Python SDK (PyPI package) providing a unified interface for submitting court filings to Tyler Technologies File and Serve (SOAP) and Odyssey (REST) state court e-filing systems. The EfilingClient facade accepts a type-safe pydantic FilingEnvelope, runs court-system-specific pre-submission validation via CourtEnvelopeValidator against a JurisdictionRegistry, then routes to the appropriate adapter for XML serialization and HTTP submission. OAuth 2.0 tokens managed in memory with auto-refresh. Submission status polled until clerk acceptance or timeout. Clerk-stamped return documents retrieved and returned as StampedReturn objects. Optional SQLite SubmissionJournal enables resume-on-failure for batch filing workflows.
target_output.architecture_overview: Library package with a layered architecture: public facade (EfilingClient), pre-submission validation (CourtEnvelopeValidator with JurisdictionRegistry), adapter layer (BaseCourtAdapter ABC with TylerFileServeAdapter SOAP and OdysseyAdapter REST implementations), domain models (pydantic v2 FilingEnvelope, SubmissionRecord, StampedReturn, CourtValidationRules), and optional journal layer (SubmissionJournal backed by SQLite). No hosted infrastructure; distributed as a PyPI package. All HTTP calls use httpx with TLS 1.2 minimum enforced. Authentication via OAuth 2.0 client credentials per court system with token memory-only storage and auto-refresh.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.10-3.12 compatibility. httpx 0.27 async HTTP client with TLS enforcement. zeep 4.2 for Tyler Technologies WSDL/SOAP XML serialization. pydantic v2 for FilingEnvelope and domain model validation with model_validator support. tenacity 8 for exponential backoff retry on adapter calls. pyproject.toml with hatch build backend. pytest 8, pytest-asyncio, responses for HTTP mocking in tests. ruff for linting, mypy strict for type checking. GitHub Actions for CI and trusted-publisher PyPI releases.
target_output.functional_feature_map: EfilingClient.submit(envelope): runs CourtEnvelopeValidator then dispatches to adapter.submit_envelope(), returns SubmissionRecord with initial status. EfilingClient.poll_status(submission_id): polls court API on configurable interval (default 10s) until status is accepted/rejected or polling timeout (default 300s) raises PollingTimeoutError. EfilingClient.retrieve_stamped(submission_id): calls adapter.get_stamped_document(), returns StampedReturn with clerk-stamped document bytes and stamp date. EfilingClient.validate_credentials(): calls adapter._authenticate() without submitting, returns True or raises CredentialError. CourtEnvelopeValidator.validate(envelope, court_system): checks court_code in JurisdictionRegistry allowlist, service_contacts count meets minimum for jurisdiction, document MIME types in per-court allowlist; raises SubmissionValidationError with field_violations list on failure. SubmissionJournal.record(submission): persists envelope_id, submission_id, court_system, status, submitted_at to SQLite; supports resume_pending() to return SubmissionRecords with status pending or polling.
target_output.system_components: efiling_sdk/client.py: EfilingClient public facade with submit, poll_status, retrieve_stamped, validate_credentials methods. efiling_sdk/validators.py: CourtEnvelopeValidator with validate() method; JurisdictionRegistry loading court rules from efiling_sdk/data/jurisdictions.json. efiling_sdk/adapters/base.py: BaseCourtAdapter ABC defining submit_envelope, poll_status, get_stamped_document, authenticate abstract methods. efiling_sdk/adapters/tyler.py: TylerFileServeAdapter using zeep client for SOAP calls to File and Serve WSDL; envelope serialized to Tyler EFSPv4 XML schema. efiling_sdk/adapters/odyssey.py: OdysseyAdapter using httpx for REST calls to Odyssey Filing API v2; envelope serialized to Odyssey JSON payload. efiling_sdk/models.py: FilingEnvelope, SubmissionRecord, StampedReturn, Credentials, CourtValidationRules pydantic models. efiling_sdk/journal.py: SubmissionJournal using stdlib sqlite3 with schema: submissions(id, submission_id, envelope_id, court_system, status, last_error, submitted_at, updated_at). efiling_sdk/exceptions.py: EfilingError, SubmissionValidationError(field_violations), CourtSystemError, CredentialError, PollingTimeoutError.
target_output.workflows: Filing Submission with Pre-Validation: Consumer constructs FilingEnvelope(case_number='2024-CV-001234', court_code='il_cook_civil', filing_type='motion', documents=[DocumentAttachment(content=pdf_bytes, mime_type='application/pdf', file_name='motion.pdf')], filer_id='atty001', service_contacts=['opposing@counsel.com']). Consumer calls client.submit(envelope). EfilingClient calls CourtEnvelopeValidator.validate(envelope, 'tyler_file_serve'): checks court_code in IL Cook County Civil allowlist (pass), checks service_contacts minimum 1 (pass), checks document MIME type in ['application/pdf', 'application/msword'] allowlist (pass). Validation passes; EfilingClient calls TylerFileServeAdapter.submit_envelope(envelope). Adapter serializes envelope to Tyler EFSPv4 XML via zeep. Adapter calls Tyler File and Serve SOAP endpoint SubmitFilingToQueue. Tyler returns envelope_id. Adapter returns SubmissionRecord(submission_id=envelope_id, status=SubmissionStatus.PENDING). Journal (if enabled) records submission. Polling and Stamped Document Retrieval: Consumer calls client.poll_status(submission_id) in a loop or asyncio.wait_for wrapper. EfilingClient calls adapter.poll_status(submission_id) with tenacity retry. Adapter calls Tyler GetFilingStatus SOAP method. On status ACCEPTED, EfilingClient calls adapter.get_stamped_document(submission_id). Adapter calls Tyler GetFilingResponse, returns PDF bytes of clerk-stamped filing. EfilingClient returns StampedReturn(submission_id, stamped_document_bytes, clerk_stamp_date, court_case_number). Invalid Envelope Rejection: Consumer submits envelope with court_code='xx_invalid' not in jurisdiction registry. CourtEnvelopeValidator.validate() raises SubmissionValidationError(field_violations=[FieldViolation(field='court_code', message='court_code xx_invalid not in supported jurisdictions for tyler_file_serve')]). No HTTP call made. Consumer handles exception and corrects court_code before retry.
target_output.data_model: FilingEnvelope: case_number str, court_code str, court_system Literal['tyler_file_serve', 'odyssey'], filing_type str, documents list[DocumentAttachment], filer_id str, service_contacts list[str], filing_description str optional. DocumentAttachment: content bytes, mime_type str, file_name str, document_type str. SubmissionRecord: submission_id str, envelope_id str, court_system str, status SubmissionStatus enum pending/polling/accepted/rejected/error, submitted_at datetime, last_polled_at datetime optional, last_error str optional. StampedReturn: submission_id str, stamped_document_bytes bytes, clerk_stamp_date date, court_case_number str, issued_at datetime. CourtValidationRules: court_system str, allowed_court_codes list[str], min_service_contacts int, allowed_mime_types list[str], max_document_size_bytes int. SQLite journal schema: submissions(id INTEGER PK, submission_id TEXT UNIQUE, envelope_id TEXT, court_system TEXT, status TEXT, last_error TEXT, submitted_at TEXT, updated_at TEXT).
target_output.api_design: Public SDK API surface (not HTTP endpoints): EfilingClient(court_system: str, credentials: Credentials, journal: SubmissionJournal | None = None, poll_interval_seconds: int = 10, poll_timeout_seconds: int = 300). async client.submit(envelope: FilingEnvelope) -> SubmissionRecord. async client.poll_status(submission_id: str) -> SubmissionStatus. async client.retrieve_stamped(submission_id: str) -> StampedReturn. async client.validate_credentials() -> bool. CourtEnvelopeValidator.validate(envelope: FilingEnvelope, court_system: str) -> None (raises SubmissionValidationError on failure). SubmissionJournal.record(record: SubmissionRecord) -> None. SubmissionJournal.resume_pending() -> list[SubmissionRecord]. JurisdictionRegistry.get_rules(court_system: str) -> CourtValidationRules.
target_output.security_and_compliance: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow: access tokens stored as instance variable on adapter with expiry timestamp; auto-refreshed 60 seconds before expiry; never written to disk or logged.
