fix_report.action_taken: Added ClickHouse row policy definitions to security_and_compliance and deployment_and_operations sections; specified CREATE ROW POLICY per consumer role partitioned by sanctions_program_code; added role grants table to data_model; updated access_model description in architecture_overview
fix_report.expected_outcome: Consuming systems with OFAC-only licenses cannot query World-Check or Refinitiv rows; vendor licensing compliance enforced at database layer

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced fixed Jaro-Winkler 0.85 threshold with per-script-family threshold configuration in workflows, data_model, and deployment_and_operations; added calibration step to phased_implementation; documented held-out labelled entity set calibration methodology
fix_report.expected_outcome: False-positive alias cluster rate for Arabic and CJK name transliterations reduced to <2% after threshold calibration on 1000-record labelled set

target_output.title: AML Sanctions Watchlist Ingestion Pipeline Architecture — Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: High-volume nightly Airflow DAG orchestrating Spark jobs on EMR Serverless to ingest OFAC SDN, World-Check, and Refinitiv vendor feeds from SFTP into S3, normalise to a canonical entity schema, deduplicate 40M+ records using MinHash LSH blocking and per-script-family Jaro-Winkler scoring calibrated on labelled data, resolve alias graphs via Spark GraphX, and load versioned snapshots into ClickHouse with row-level security enforced by sanctions_program_code. Addresses round 1 gaps in ClickHouse access control and deduplication threshold calibration.
target_output.architecture_overview: Airflow MWAA nightly DAG triggers sequential EMR Serverless Spark jobs: SFTP ingestion to S3 SSE-KMS, feed checksum validation, canonical schema normalisation from OFAC XML / World-Check CSV / Refinitiv JSON, MinHash LSH blocking (checkpoint to S3 Iceberg after completion), per-script-family Jaro-Winkler + identifier scoring, GraphX alias cluster resolution, versioned ClickHouse snapshot load. ClickHouse row policies enforce vendor licensing boundaries: ofac_readers_role, worldcheck_readers_role, and refinitiv_readers_role each scoped to their respective sanctions_program_code values. Redis 7 caches top-N MinHash candidate pairs to reduce Spark shuffle during scoring step.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on MWAA. Compute: Scala 3 with Apache Spark 3.5 on EMR Serverless (auto-scale 2-200 vCPUs). Deduplication: Spark MLlib MinHash LSH for blocking; custom Scala Jaro-Winkler implementation with per-script-family threshold configs. Graph resolution: Spark GraphX connected components. Watchlist store: ClickHouse r6i.8xlarge cluster with ReplacingMergeTree and row-level security policies. Archive: S3 with Apache Iceberg for raw feeds and dedup intermediate state. Cache: Redis 7 for MinHash candidate pairs. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day SFTP credential rotation. CI: GitLab CI with sbt test and Spark local-mode integration tests.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SFTP Ingestion: scheduled download of OFAC SDN full file (weekly), World-Check daily delta, and Refinitiv delta with checksum verification. Canonical Normalisation: Spark job mapping each vendor's field schema to watchlist_entities canonical schema with script-family detection (Latin/Arabic/CJK) per entity name. MinHash LSH Blocking: generates candidate pairs with Jaccard similarity >0.5 on trigram-tokenised normalised names; output checkpointed to S3 Iceberg. Per-Script Scoring: Jaro-Winkler scoring with script-family threshold (Latin 0.88, Arabic 0.82, CJK 0.78) plus identifier exact-match bonus (+0.15 per matching LEI/ISIN/passport). Alias Graph Resolution: GraphX connected components clusters transitive aliases into consolidated entity records. ClickHouse Load: atomic snapshot version commit; row policies enforce licensing. Reconciliation Report: HTML report comparing entity counts and coverage overlap across vendors.
target_output.system_components: MWAA Airflow environment running aml_watchlist_nightly DAG. EMR Serverless Spark application with auto-scaling worker pool. S3 buckets: aml-vendor-feeds (SSE-KMS, versioned), aml-dedup-intermediate (Iceberg checkpoints), aml-reports (HTML reconciliation reports). ClickHouse primary + replica cluster on r6i.8xlarge in private subnet with TLS listener. Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster for candidate pair cache. AWS Secrets Manager with three vendor SFTP credential secrets. CloudWatch Log Groups for Airflow task logs. Grafana + Prometheus for pipeline metrics. PagerDuty integration for SLA breach alerts.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Pipeline Execution: MWAA triggers aml_watchlist_nightly at 02:00 UTC. ingest_vendor_feeds task downloads OFAC SDN from sftp.ofac.treasury.gov, World-Check delta from sftp.worldcheck.com, Refinitiv delta from sftp.refinitiv.com using credentials from Secrets Manager; uploads to S3 aml-vendor-feeds/{vendor}/{date}/. validate_feed_checksums verifies SHA-256 against vendor-provided manifest; alerts and halts DAG on mismatch. normalize_to_canonical_schema Spark job reads raw files, applies vendor-specific parsers, detects script family per entity name (Latin/Arabic/CJK via Unicode block analysis), writes Parquet to S3 dedup intermediate. run_dedup_blocking Spark MinHash LSH job produces candidate pairs, writes to S3 Iceberg checkpoint table dedup_candidates_{run_id}. run_dedup_scoring Spark job applies per-script-family Jaro-Winkler thresholds loaded from pipeline config; scores pairs, flags clusters above threshold as aliases. resolve_alias_graph Spark GraphX job resolves transitive alias chains into entity clusters, assigns stable cluster_id UUIDs. load_clickhouse_snapshot atomically inserts new snapshot version; does not update max_snapshot_version metadata row until full load completes. Threshold Calibration Workflow (offline, run before production): Data engineer runs calibrate_thresholds.py against 1000-record manually labelled entity set (500 Latin, 300 Arabic, 200 CJK); script outputs precision-recall curves per script family; engineer selects operating point targeting recall >0.98; updates pipeline_config.yaml threshold values committed to repo.
target_output.data_model: watchlist_entities (entity_id UUID, canonical_name TEXT, script_family ENUM latin/arabic/cjk/other, entity_type ENUM individual/organization/vessel/aircraft, sanctions_program_code TEXT[], identifier_hashes TEXT[], alias_cluster_id UUID, source_vendor_codes TEXT[], snapshot_version UInt64, effective_date Date, is_active Bool) ENGINE=ReplacingMergeTree(snapshot_version) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(effective_date). entity_aliases (alias_id UUID, entity_id UUID, alias_name TEXT, alias_type ENUM primary/aka/fka/transliteration, language_code TEXT, source_vendor_code TEXT). deduplication_clusters (cluster_id UUID, member_entity_ids Array(UUID), confidence_score Float32, script_family ENUM, resolved_at DateTime, algorithm_version TEXT, threshold_config JSON). pipeline_runs (run_id UUID, dag_run_id TEXT, started_at DateTime, completed_at DateTime, vendors_ingested Array(String), entities_before_dedup UInt32, entities_after_dedup UInt32, new_aliases_discovered UInt32, sla_met Bool). ClickHouse row policies: CREATE ROW POLICY ofac_only ON watchlist_entities FOR SELECT USING has(sanctions_program_code, 'OFAC_SDN') TO ofac_readers_role; CREATE ROW POLICY worldcheck_access ON watchlist_entities FOR SELECT USING has(sanctions_program_code, 'WORLDCHECK') TO worldcheck_readers_role; CREATE ROW POLICY refinitiv_access ON watchlist_entities FOR SELECT USING has(sanctions_program_code, 'REFINITIV_WC1') TO refinitiv_readers_role.
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API; downstream systems query ClickHouse directly via JDBC or ClickHouse HTTP interface. Internal Airflow REST API (MWAA-native) for DAG trigger and status: GET /api/v1/dags/aml_watchlist_nightly/dagRuns (list runs), POST /api/v1/dags/aml_watchlist_nightly/dagRuns (manual trigger for ad-hoc OFAC update). S3 presigned URL generated by lambda for compliance team to download reconciliation report HTML. Spark jobs accept CLI arguments: --input-s3-path, --output-s3-path, --run-id, --threshold-config-path (S3 path to pipeline_config.yaml).
target_output.security_and_compliance: S3 SSE-KMS (aws/s3 key) for aml-vendor-feeds and aml-dedup-intermediate buckets; bucket policy denies all access except from designated EMR execution IAM role. ClickHouse TLS 1.3 enforced on all connections; plaintext connections rejected. ClickHouse row-level security policies partition read access by sanctions_program_code: ofac_readers_role (OFAC SDN only), worldcheck_readers_role (World-Check), refinitiv_readers_role (Refinitiv); no role grants cross-vendor access without explicit approval. SFTP credentials for all three vendors in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day automated rotation; Airflow fetches at DAG runtime, never stored in Airflow metadata DB. Audit: all ClickHouse SELECT queries logged to system.query_log with user, query_hash, rows_read, and query_start_time; Airflow task logs in CloudWatch include entity counts per vendor per run for compliance attestation. OFAC SDN compliance: pipeline must complete and make new OFAC updates available to screening systems within 24 hours of OFAC publication; SLA monitored by Grafana alert.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: MWAA Airflow 2.8 deployed via CloudFormation in us-east-1 VPC with private subnets. EMR Serverless application configured with pre-initialised worker pool of 4 workers during 01:00-05:00 UTC nightly window to reduce cold-start latency. Spark JAR built via sbt assembly and uploaded to S3 deployment bucket on GitLab CI tag push; Airflow DAG references JAR S3 path parameterised by version tag. ClickHouse cluster on r6i.8xlarge primary + r6i.4xlarge replica in same AZ; replica lag monitored; if replica lag >30s at snapshot load time, load proceeds to primary only and alert fires. Staging environment uses EMR Serverless with synthetic vendor feed files (1M records) to validate pipeline changes before production deployment. Rollback: if ClickHouse load fails mid-snapshot, max_snapshot_version metadata row is not updated and screening systems continue reading prior snapshot version; failed snapshot rows are deleted and re-run from alias graph output checkpoint.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboard panels: entities_ingested_by_vendor (gauge per run), dedup_reduction_rate (entities_before_dedup / entities_after_dedup), pipeline_duration_minutes with 240m SLA threshold line, MinHash_blocking_candidate_pairs (volume indicator for scoring step workload), Jaro-Winkler_score_distribution_by_script_family (histogram to detect threshold drift). Prometheus metrics emitted by custom Spark MetricsSink: spark.aml.entities_normalised, spark.aml.candidate_pairs_generated, spark.aml.clusters_resolved, spark.aml.clickhouse_rows_written. PagerDuty P1 alert on: DAG failure, pipeline_duration >210m (early SLA warning), ClickHouse replica lag >60s. CloudWatch Alarm on SFTP download failure (vendor unavailability) with separate escalation path to vendor SLA team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EMR Serverless: ~$180/month for nightly 4-hour Spark jobs at 200 vCPU peak (EMR Serverless pricing per vCPU-hour). ClickHouse cluster: ~$800/month for r6i.8xlarge primary + r6i.4xlarge replica reserved instances. MWAA: ~$150/month for mw1.small environment. S3 storage: ~$60/month for vendor feeds and Iceberg tables (7-year retention). Redis ElastiCache: ~$80/month. Total ~$1270/month. Scaling path: EMR Serverless auto-scaling handles volume growth up to 500M records with same configuration; ClickHouse sharding required beyond 10B rows in watchlist_entities.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 OFAC MVP (8 weeks): SFTP ingestion for OFAC SDN, canonical normalisation Spark job, MinHash LSH blocking with S3 Iceberg checkpoint, Jaro-Winkler scoring calibration on 500-record Latin-script labelled set, alias graph resolution, ClickHouse schema with row policies for ofac_readers_role, GitLab CI pipeline, staging smoke test, PagerDuty integration. Threshold calibration: run calibrate_thresholds.py offline, target Latin recall >0.98. Phase 2 Multi-vendor (6 weeks after Phase 1): World-Check and Refinitiv SFTP ingestion, Arabic and CJK threshold calibration on labelled sets (300 Arabic, 200 CJK records), per-script-family threshold config in pipeline_config.yaml, worldcheck_readers_role and refinitiv_readers_role ClickHouse policies, reconciliation report generation, vendor coverage comparison analytics. Phase 3 Near-real-time (future): OFAC delta feed via webhook, sub-1-hour update propagation, API layer for downstream screening engine.
target_output.development_guidelines: Scala code style enforced via Scalafmt in GitLab CI. All Spark jobs must be idempotent: output Iceberg tables use run_id as partition key so re-runs overwrite rather than duplicate. Jaro-Winkler thresholds are pipeline config, not hardcoded constants — any change requires PR review and calibration evidence. ClickHouse row policy changes require security team review before merge. Spark job integration tests run against local Spark session with synthetic 10K-record vendor feed fixtures; must cover Latin, Arabic, and CJK entity name sets. Pipeline runbook documents resume procedure from each checkpoint: blocking, scoring, alias graph, ClickHouse load.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vendor SFTP unavailability delays ingestion past SLA. Mitigation: vendor SLA escalation contact documented in runbook; prior day snapshot remains live for screening during delay. Risk: Jaro-Winkler threshold miscalibration causes false-negative alias misses (distinct entities not merged that should be). Mitigation: quarterly offline re-calibration on updated labelled set as vendor data characteristics drift. Risk: ClickHouse r6i.8xlarge insufficient for query volume at peak AML screening load. Mitigation: read replica absorbs reporting queries; horizontal sharding design documented for scaling beyond 10B rows. Tradeoff: in-process MinHash blocking over Approximate Nearest Neighbor service — simpler operational model with Spark-native MLlib, acceptable for nightly batch; revisit for near-real-time phase.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree over versioned tables for snapshot history — simpler query pattern for point-in-time reads using max(snapshot_version) filter. Chose GraphX connected components over custom union-find for alias resolution — Spark-native, handles transitive chains correctly at scale. Chose per-script-family Jaro-Winkler over language-model embeddings for scoring — embedding approach has higher accuracy but requires GPU infrastructure not available in current stack; revisit in Phase 3.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision addressing ClickHouse row-level security gaps (ISS-001) and uncalibrated Jaro-Winkler thresholds for non-Latin entity names (ISS-002) in AML sanctions watchlist pipeline.

profile.domain: Legal Operations Microservices Platform

project_goal.value: Full-stack legal operations platform built as independently deployable microservices covering matter intake, contract review, vendor onboarding, and compliance reporting for large in-house legal departments
project_goal.rationale: Legal departments need modular scaling of individual services without full platform redeployment

target_users.value: In-house legal teams including attorneys, paralegals, compliance officers, legal ops managers, and vendor administrators
target_users.rationale: Diverse legal department roles with different access and workflow needs

project_class.rationale: Requires unified micro-frontend shell with multiple backend microservices and persistent data stores per service

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, admin_panel, integrations, analytics, devops
capabilities.rationale: Full legal ops platform requires all capability layers including event-driven integrations and operational tooling

complexity_level.rationale: Multiple independent microservices with module federation, cross-service event coordination, and per-service databases

risk_level.rationale: Handles confidential legal matters, contracts, and vendor relationships with attorney-client privilege implications

data_sensitivity.rationale: Matter data, contract terms, and vendor financials are attorney-client privileged or commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Internal legal department tool, all access requires SSO authentication via Okta SAML

access_model.value: RBAC per service with Okta SAML SSO; roles: attorney, paralegal, legal-ops-admin, compliance-officer, vendor-admin, system-admin
access_model.rationale: Each microservice enforces its own RBAC derived from centrally issued JWT claims

feature_scope.value: Matter intake triage and routing, AI-assisted contract review queue, vendor onboarding checklist and document collection, compliance reporting dashboards, cross-service audit trail, admin panel for user and role management
feature_scope.rationale: Core legal ops workflows identified in stakeholder discovery sessions

mvp_scope.value: Matter intake service, contract review service with manual review queue, Okta SSO, shared audit event bus, and admin panel for user management
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates microservice architecture pattern and core attorney workflows before adding vendor and compliance services

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all service-to-service and client traffic; Okta SAML SSO with MFA; JWT 15-minute expiry; API Gateway rate limiting; WAF on ALB; per-service audit logging to centralized event store
security_baseline.rationale: High-risk confidential data requires defense-in-depth across all service boundaries

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript as micro-frontend shell using Module Federation 2.0; Tailwind CSS; React Query for server state
frontend_stack.rationale: Module Federation enables independent deployment of service-specific UIs without full shell rebuild

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Fastify per service; OpenAPI 3.1 contracts; shared npm library for JWT validation and audit event emission
backend_stack.rationale: Fastify provides low-overhead HTTP with typed schemas; shared library enforces security and audit consistency across services

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with per-service schema isolation; Redis 7 for session cache and inter-service pub/sub; no shared cross-service tables
data_platform.rationale: Schema-per-service enforces bounded context isolation; Redis pub/sub decouples cross-service event workflows

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate per service behind API Gateway and ALB; Nx-managed monorepo with affected-build CI for independent service deployments
hosting_target.rationale: ECS Fargate isolates service scaling; Nx affected builds prevent unnecessary redeployments across the monorepo

privacy_retention_policy.value: Matter and contract data retained 7 years per legal hold policy; vendor onboarding records 5 years; audit logs 3 years; GDPR deletion workflow for any EU counterparty personal data
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Legal hold obligations and regulatory retention requirements drive retention tiers

future_scope.value: AI-assisted contract clause extraction, litigation hold management service, outside counsel e-billing integration, and mobile app for field attorneys
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap validated with legal ops leadership

constraints.value: Each microservice must be independently deployable with zero downtime; monorepo with Nx affected builds; Okta is the mandatory identity provider; no shared cross-service database tables
constraints.rationale: Bounded context isolation and independent deployability are core architectural requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed tracing across service boundaries; structured JSON logs with correlation IDs; custom dashboards for matter intake SLA, contract review queue depth, and vendor onboarding completion rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Cross-service request tracing is critical for diagnosing failures in event-driven workflows

execution_preference.value: Parallel squad model with one squad per service; 2-week sprints; API contracts locked before UI development begins per service
execution_preference.rationale: Parallel development across services requires API-first contract locking to prevent integration bottlenecks

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU counterparty personal data; SOC 2 Type II for data handling controls; attorney-client privilege requires strict access logging and data residency in us-east-1
compliance_context.rationale: High risk and confidential data sensitivity trigger mandatory compliance context

project.goal: Independently deployable microservices platform for in-house legal department operations
project.target_users: Attorneys, paralegals, legal ops admins, compliance officers, vendor admins
project.timeline: MVP in 16 weeks; full platform in 36 weeks

frontend.module_federation: Module Federation 2.0 for service UI remotes
frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for shell-level UI state

backend.api: OpenAPI 3.1 contracts per service, versioned at /api/v1

security.auth: Okta SAML SSO with MFA, JWT 15-minute expiry
security.rate_limiting: API Gateway request throttling per client ID

data.cache: Redis 7 for sessions and pub/sub event coordination
data.isolation: No cross-service foreign keys or shared tables

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Nx affected builds
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate per service behind API Gateway
devops.deployment: Independent rolling updates with zero-downtime constraint

confirmed_decisions.service_mesh: No service mesh in MVP; direct HTTPS between services

reasoner_reviews.product: The four-service scope (matter intake, contract review, vendor onboarding, compliance reporting) maps cleanly onto distinct legal ops workflows. Starting with matter intake and contract review in MVP is the right call - these are the highest daily-use services. The admin panel should be a thin shell concern, not a fifth service, to avoid added deployment complexity in phase 1.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Module Federation 2.0 is the right choice for the micro-frontend shell - it allows each squad to deploy UI changes independently without requiring shell rebuilds. The no-shared-table constraint is critical to maintain; temptation to join across service databases will emerge during development and must be enforced at the architectural level. Redis pub/sub is acceptable for MVP event coordination but should be evaluated against EventBridge at scale given its fan-out limitations.
reasoner_reviews.security: Okta SAML with per-service JWT validation is sound. The shared npm library for JWT validation is important - any inconsistency in token parsing across services creates privilege escalation risk. Ensure the audit event bus is append-only and services cannot retroactively modify audit records. WAF rules must cover the API Gateway layer, not just the ALB.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The Nx affected-build constraint is achievable but requires strict dependency graph hygiene in the monorepo. Services must not import from each other source - only from the shared library. The 16-week MVP timeline is tight for two services plus SSO and audit infrastructure; descope the admin panel to post-MVP if needed.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The per-service PostgreSQL schema approach introduces operational overhead: separate migration pipelines per service, separate connection pool configurations, and separate backup schedules. Confirm the DevOps team has capacity for this before committing to schema-per-service over schema-per-database on a shared cluster.

backend.service_design: Four Fastify services: matter-intake-service (matter creation, triage routing, assignment), contract-review-service (review queue, clause flagging, approval workflow), vendor-onboarding-service (checklist management, document collection, risk scoring), compliance-reporting-service (report generation, scheduled aggregation jobs). Shared npm package @legalops/core exports JWT validator, audit emitter, and error handler middleware.
backend.api_patterns: Each service exposes OpenAPI 3.1 spec at /api/v1/openapi.json. Cross-service calls via internal HTTPS with service account JWT. Public-facing endpoints behind API Gateway with request validation. Event coordination via Redis pub/sub channels: contract.approved, vendor.onboarded, matter.assigned.
backend.business_modules: matter-intake: MatterController, TriageEngine (rule-based routing by practice area and risk flag), AssignmentQueue. contract-review: ReviewQueueManager, ClauseFlagService, ApprovalWorkflow (multi-step with escalation). vendor-onboarding: ChecklistEngine, DocumentCollector, RiskScorer. compliance-reporting: ReportScheduler, AggregationJob, ExportFormatter (PDF and XLSX).
backend.failure_handling: Each service implements circuit breaker for cross-service HTTP calls using opossum. Redis pub/sub failures fall back to polling the source service API. Database connection pool exhaustion triggers 503 with Retry-After header. Dead-letter pattern for failed audit event emissions stored in local audit_dlq table for reconciliation.
backend.scaling_notes: matter-intake and contract-review scale independently on ECS based on queue depth metrics. compliance-reporting runs as a lower-priority service with scheduled scaling during off-peak report generation windows. API Gateway provides per-service throttling to prevent one service traffic from saturating shared infrastructure.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 shell app hosts the module federation container. Service UIs are compiled as Module Federation remotes: @legalops/matter-intake-remote, @legalops/contract-review-remote, @legalops/vendor-onboarding-remote, @legalops/compliance-remote. Shell handles global navigation, Okta session management, and notification toasts.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Shell routes: /matters (loads matter-intake remote), /contracts (loads contract-review remote), /vendors (loads vendor-onboarding remote), /reports (loads compliance remote), /admin (shell-native user management). Each remote lazy-loads only when its route is activated.
frontend.state_management: React Query per remote for server state with 5-minute stale time. Zustand store in shell for cross-remote shared state: current user identity, notification queue, and active matter context for deep-linking between services.
frontend.frontend_risks: Module Federation version skew between shell and remotes if React versions diverge. Mitigation: pin shared React version as a singleton in webpack federation config and enforce via monorepo peer dependency checks.

security.auth_design: Okta SAML 2.0 IdP with Next.js shell as SP. On successful assertion, shell mints a short-lived JWT (15-minute expiry) signed with RS256 and distributes to remotes via postMessage. Each Fastify service validates JWT using shared @legalops/core validator with JWKS endpoint caching.
security.authorization_model: JWT claims carry roles array and department. Fastify preHandler guards check role against per-route policy definitions stored in config. Contract review service enforces matter-level ACL: only attorneys assigned to a matter can access its contracts.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every service emits structured audit events to a Redis stream consumed by a dedicated audit-aggregator service that writes to append-only audit_events table in a separate audit schema. Events include: user_id, service, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip, timestamp. No service has write access to the audit schema.
security.security_risks: Risk: Module Federation remote loading from separate origins requires CORS configuration that could be overly permissive. Mitigation: restrict Access-Control-Allow-Origin to the shell specific domain in each remote deployment config.

data.entities: matters (id, matter_number, title, practice_area, status ENUM intake/active/closed, assigned_attorney_id, created_at) in matter_intake schema. contracts (id, matter_id, title, counterparty_name, s3_document_key, status ENUM draft/in_review/approved/executed, review_due_at, executed_at) in contract_review schema. vendors (id, name, tax_id_encrypted, risk_tier ENUM low/medium/high, onboarding_status ENUM invited/in_progress/approved/rejected) in vendor_onboarding schema. compliance_reports (id, report_type, period_start, period_end, s3_export_key, status) in compliance schema. audit_events (id, service, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, payload_json, timestamp) in audit schema.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with four schemas on a shared multi-AZ RDS instance; separate IAM database users per service. Redis 7 Streams for audit event fan-out. S3 with SSE-S3 for contract document storage; presigned URLs with 15-minute TTL.
data.retention_and_deletion: Matter and contract records: 7-year legal hold. Vendor onboarding records: 5 years. Audit events: 3 years then archived to S3 Glacier. GDPR deletion: EU counterparty personal fields nullified with GDPR_DELETED token on verified request; deletion record retained in audit log.
data.migration_notes: Each service owns its own Flyway migration scripts executed during deployment. Migration failures trigger deployment rollback. Cross-schema migrations are prohibited - coordinate via new API version instead.

devops.deployment_topology: Nx monorepo with four service packages and four frontend remote packages. GitHub Actions runs Nx affected graph to determine which services and remotes need rebuild. ECS Fargate task definitions per service with separate ALB target groups. API Gateway routes by service path prefix: /api/matters, /api/contracts, /api/vendors, /api/reports.
devops.ci_cd_design: PR: Nx affected lint plus Jest unit tests plus Supertest integration tests. Merge to main: Nx affected build plus Docker push to ECR plus ECS rolling update for affected services only. Shell and remotes deployed independently via separate GitHub Actions jobs triggered by Nx affected output.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with Node.js tracer injected at ECS task startup. Trace context propagated via W3C traceparent header across service-to-service HTTPS calls. Custom Datadog dashboards: matter intake daily volume, contract review queue depth and aging, vendor onboarding funnel completion rates, compliance report generation duration p95.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS deployment circuit breaker enabled: if more than 50% of replacement tasks fail health checks, rollback to previous task definition automatically. Nx release tags enable git-based rollback to prior service image by rerunning deployment workflow with prior tag.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: Nx affected graph accuracy degrades if shared library changes are not correctly declared as dependencies. Mitigation: strict package.json dependency declarations and automated dependency graph validation in CI.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a high-scale microservices legal ops platform. Key decisions: Module Federation 2.0 for independent UI deployment per squad; schema-per-service PostgreSQL isolation with no cross-service joins; Okta SAML as the single identity plane with short-lived JWTs distributed to remotes; Redis pub/sub for cross-service event coordination with fallback polling. The append-only audit event store via Redis Streams and a dedicated aggregator service is critical given confidential data sensitivity and SOC 2 requirements.
target_output.title: Legal Operations Microservices Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale fullstack legal operations platform structured as four independently deployable Fastify microservices (matter intake, contract review, vendor onboarding, compliance reporting) composed in a Next.js 14 micro-frontend shell via Module Federation 2.0. Each service owns its PostgreSQL schema, scales on ECS Fargate independently via Nx-affected CI, and emits structured audit events to an append-only central audit store. Okta SAML SSO with per-service JWT validation and matter-level ACL enforcement protects confidential matter, contract, and vendor data under GDPR and SOC 2 controls.
target_output.architecture_overview: Micro-frontend shell (Next.js 14 deployed to S3 and CloudFront) dynamically loads four Module Federation remotes at runtime: matter-intake, contract-review, vendor-onboarding, and compliance-reporting. Each remote communicates with its dedicated Fastify service behind API Gateway path-based routing (/api/matters, /api/contracts, /api/vendors, /api/reports). Services run as isolated ECS Fargate task families with separate ALB target groups. Inter-service communication uses internal HTTPS with service-account JWTs; cross-service domain events flow through Redis Streams on pub/sub channels (contract.approved, vendor.onboarded). No service accesses another service PostgreSQL schema. A dedicated audit-aggregator sidecar service consumes the audit stream and writes to an append-only audit_events table in an isolated audit schema with no service DELETE or UPDATE grants.
target_output.technology_stack: Shell: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Module Federation 2.0, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand. Service runtime: Node.js 20, Fastify 4, OpenAPI 3.1, @legalops/core shared npm library. Databases: PostgreSQL 16 with four isolated schemas, Flyway migrations per service. Cache and events: Redis 7 Streams for audit fan-out and pub/sub coordination. Storage: S3 with SSE-S3 for contract documents, presigned URLs 15-min TTL. Auth: Okta SAML 2.0, RS256 JWTs, JWKS endpoint. Infrastructure: AWS ECS Fargate, API Gateway, ALB with WAF, ECR, Nx monorepo, GitHub Actions with Nx affected, Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Matter Intake Service: new matter submission with practice area classification, triage rule engine routing by risk flag and department, attorney assignment queue with SLA tracking. Contract Review Service: upload queue for contracts requiring review, clause flagging against configurable standard-terms library, multi-step approval workflow with escalation to senior counsel. Vendor Onboarding Service: vendor invitation portal, configurable onboarding checklist (insurance certificates, NDAs, SOC 2 reports), risk tier scoring, approval with automated status notifications. Compliance Reporting Service: scheduled aggregation jobs producing SOC 2 evidence packs, GDPR data mapping reports, and matter activity summaries as PDF or XLSX exports. Admin Panel (shell-native): user provisioning, role assignment, service health status, audit log viewer.
target_output.system_components: API Gateway (AWS): path-based routing to four service ALB target groups, request validation, per-client throttling. ALB with WAF: OWASP rule set, rate limiting, TLS termination. ECS Fargate: one cluster with separate task families - matter-intake (2-8 tasks), contract-review (2-6 tasks), vendor-onboarding (1-4 tasks), compliance-reporting (1-2 tasks with scheduled scale-out). PostgreSQL 16 RDS: four schemas on shared multi-AZ cluster with separate IAM users per service. Redis 7 (ElastiCache): Streams for audit events, pub/sub for domain event coordination. S3: contract document store with SSE-S3. Audit Aggregator: dedicated ECS task consuming Redis audit stream, writing to append-only audit_events table. Module Federation Shell: Next.js deployed to S3 and CloudFront, loading service remotes from their own CloudFront distributions.
target_output.workflows: Matter Intake Workflow: Attorney submits new matter via intake remote; matter-intake-service validates form, applies triage rules based on practice area code and risk flag, assigns to available attorney in rotation, emits matter.assigned event to Redis, records custom metric for daily intake volume in Datadog. Contract Review and Cross-Service Trigger: Paralegal uploads contract PDF via presigned S3 upload URL; contract-review-service extracts text, flags non-standard clauses against clauses library, places in review queue; assigned attorney reviews and approves; on final approval service emits contract.approved to Redis, which triggers vendor-onboarding-service if contract is a vendor agreement - vendor-onboarding-service creates onboarding record and invites vendor contact via email.
target_output.data_model: matters (id UUID PK, matter_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, title, practice_area ENUM litigation/corporate/ip/employment/regulatory, status ENUM intake/active/closed/archived, assigned_attorney_id, created_at, updated_at) in matter_intake schema. contracts (id UUID PK, matter_id, title, counterparty_name, s3_document_key, status ENUM draft/in_review/approved/executed, review_due_at, executed_at, approver_id) in contract_review schema. vendors (id UUID PK, name, tax_id_encrypted, risk_tier ENUM low/medium/high, onboarding_status ENUM invited/in_progress/approved/rejected, onboarding_completed_at) in vendor_onboarding schema. compliance_reports (id UUID PK, report_type ENUM soc2_evidence/gdpr_mapping/matter_activity, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, s3_export_key, generated_at, status ENUM queued/generating/complete) in compliance schema. audit_events (id UUID PK, service, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address INET, payload_json JSONB, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) in audit schema - append only.
target_output.api_design: matter-intake-service: POST /api/v1/matters (create new matter), GET /api/v1/matters (list with status and attorney filters), GET /api/v1/matters/:id (detail), PATCH /api/v1/matters/:id/assign (reassign attorney), GET /api/v1/matters/queue (unassigned triage queue). contract-review-service: POST /api/v1/contracts (create with S3 presigned upload), GET /api/v1/contracts?status=in_review (review queue), GET /api/v1/contracts/:id (detail with clause flags), POST /api/v1/contracts/:id/approve (submit approval decision), GET /api/v1/contracts/:id/presigned-download (15-min URL). vendor-onboarding-service: POST /api/v1/vendors, GET /api/v1/vendors/:id/checklist, PATCH /api/v1/vendors/:id/checklist/:item_id, POST /api/v1/vendors/:id/approve. compliance-reporting-service: POST /api/v1/reports (queue generation), GET /api/v1/reports/:id. All: Authorization: Bearer JWT, 401 on missing/expired, 403 on insufficient role.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta SAML 2.0 with MFA enforced for all users; shell mints RS256 JWT (15-minute expiry) on SAML assertion; JWKS endpoint cached by each service with 5-minute TTL. Matter-level ACL in contract-review-service: attorney must be assigned to the matter to access its contracts. Audit events are append-only: no service is granted DELETE or UPDATE on audit_events. WAF on ALB: OWASP Core Rule Set, IP reputation blocking, SQL injection and XSS rules. S3 contract documents: SSE-S3 encryption, no public bucket access, presigned URLs 15-minute TTL with requester IP binding. GDPR: EU counterparty personal fields nullified on verified deletion request with deletion audit record retained. SOC 2 Type II: evidence packs from compliance-reporting-service include access logs, change records, and availability metrics. Data residency: us-east-1 only, no cross-region replication for privileged matter data.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Nx monorepo: affected:build determines which service packages and frontend remotes changed per PR merge. GitHub Actions: PR triggers affected lint, Jest unit tests, and Supertest integration tests. Merge to main: affected build, Docker push to ECR, ECS UpdateService rolling deployment (minimum 50% healthy). Each service has its own ECS task definition, service, and target group. Flyway migrations run as ECS one-off tasks before service update. Module Federation remotes deployed to individual S3 and CloudFront distributions; shell references remote entry points via runtime config from Parameter Store enabling remote updates without shell redeployment. Environments: dev (single AZ, shared RDS), staging (multi-AZ, production-mirrored), production (multi-AZ us-east-1a/1c, ECS circuit breaker enabled). Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with IAM role-based access per service, 30-day rotation for database credentials.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with Node.js tracer auto-injected via ECS task environment. W3C traceparent headers propagated across service-to-service HTTPS calls and Redis pub/sub message envelopes enabling cross-service distributed traces. Custom dashboards: matter intake daily volume and triage SLA breach rate, contract review queue depth by aging bucket (0-3 days / 3-7 days / 7+ days), vendor onboarding funnel completion rates by checklist stage, compliance report generation duration p95. PagerDuty escalation: error rate above 2% on any service for 5 minutes, p99 latency above 3 seconds on matter-intake or contract-review, audit aggregator consumer lag above 1000 messages.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS auto-scaling per service: matter-intake and contract-review scale on ALB request count target (1000 req/task); vendor-onboarding and compliance-reporting scale on CPU 60% target. Compliance-reporting scales to zero tasks overnight and scales up on scheduled trigger for nightly report runs, saving approximately 65% on idle compute. PostgreSQL on shared multi-AZ RDS with Read Replica for compliance reporting queries to isolate heavy aggregation from transactional traffic. Estimated base cost at 500 daily active users: approximately $2800/month. Nx affected builds reduce CI compute costs by 60-70% versus full-monorepo rebuilds.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (weeks 1-16): matter-intake-service with triage engine and attorney assignment, contract-review-service with manual review queue and clause flagging, Okta SAML SSO, append-only audit aggregator, admin panel as shell-native pages, GitHub Actions CI with Nx affected builds, Datadog APM. Phase 2 - Vendor and Compliance (weeks 17-28): vendor-onboarding-service with configurable checklist and risk scoring, compliance-reporting-service with SOC 2 evidence pack and GDPR mapping report, cross-service event wiring so contract.approved triggers vendor onboarding. Phase 3 - Scale and Polish (weeks 29-36): ECS auto-scaling policies tuned on production data, Read Replica for compliance queries, GDPR deletion workflow, performance optimization on contract review clause flagging pipeline.
target_output.development_guidelines: Each service is a separate Nx library project with its own package.json, tsconfig, and Dockerfile. No cross-service imports in source - only from @legalops/core shared package. OpenAPI 3.1 spec must be committed alongside service code; spec changes require API review before merge. Jest unit test coverage gate: 80% for service modules, 70% for frontend remotes. Supertest integration tests for all POST and PATCH endpoints covering happy path and auth failure cases. Feature flags via AWS Parameter Store for gradual rollout of new service UI remotes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Module Federation remote loading adds 200-400ms latency on first navigation to a new service UI due to remote manifest fetch and bundle download. Mitigation: preload remote manifests in shell on initial page load using link prefetch for predictable navigation paths. Risk: Redis pub/sub is fire-and-forget - if vendor-onboarding-service is down when contract.approved fires, the event is lost. Mitigation: upgrade to Redis Streams with consumer groups and explicit acknowledgement for guaranteed delivery. Tradeoff: schema-per-service on a shared RDS cluster simplifies operations versus per-service RDS instances but creates noisy-neighbor risk during heavy compliance report aggregation runs; addressed by routing compliance-reporting to a Read Replica.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Module Federation 2.0 over iframes or server-side composition for micro-frontend approach due to shared React context support and independent deployment capability. Selected Redis Streams over AWS EventBridge for MVP event coordination to reduce vendor lock-in complexity. Confirmed schema-per-service over database-per-service to reduce RDS instance costs while maintaining bounded context isolation via IAM user restrictions.

metadata.notes: High-scale legal ops microservices platform with Module Federation micro-frontend, schema-per-service PostgreSQL isolation, and append-only audit event store.

profile.domain: Legal Conflict-of-Interest Management

project_goal.value: Full-stack application replacing manual conflict-of-interest check spreadsheets and email threads with a structured digital workflow featuring a searchable party database, automated conflict search, and multi-level reviewer approval routing
project_goal.rationale: Manual process creates malpractice exposure; digitised workflow enforces ABA Model Rule compliance

target_users.value: Law firm attorneys, paralegals, conflicts clerks, and managing partners reviewing conflict determinations
target_users.rationale: All firm roles involved in intake and conflict clearance workflows

project_class.rationale: Requires React frontend, FastAPI backend, and PostgreSQL persistence

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Full workflow application with Okta auth, admin config panel, and matter management system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-step approval routing and fuzzy party name search add complexity above basic CRUD

risk_level.rationale: Conflict-of-interest data is legally privileged; missed conflicts create malpractice liability

data_sensitivity.rationale: Party identities, matter descriptions, and conflict findings are attorney-client privileged

external_exposure.rationale: Internal firm tool; no external client or partner access

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: conflicts_requestor, conflicts_reviewer, ethics_counsel, admin; Okta group-mapped
access_model.rationale: Approval routing logic depends on role hierarchy; ethics counsel escalation requires distinct role

feature_scope.value: Conflict check request submission, automated party name search against active and former matter database, conflict findings report generation, multi-level reviewer approval routing, waiver record management, admin configuration of routing rules, Clio matter management webhook integration
feature_scope.rationale: Complete conflict clearance lifecycle from intake through waiver

mvp_scope.value: Request submission with party name fuzzy search, conflict findings display, two-level reviewer routing (reviewer then partner), immutable decision audit log
mvp_scope.rationale: Core clearance workflow sufficient to eliminate spreadsheet dependency

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, Okta MFA required for all roles, immutable audit log, no PII in application logs
security_baseline.rationale: Privileged legal data requires strong access controls and complete audit trail

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; TypeScript enforcement for complex approval state machine

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Fast iteration, async support for concurrent search queries, strong typing with Pydantic

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Full-text search with pg_trgm for fuzzy party name matching; ACID transactions for audit log immutability

hosting_target.value: AWS Elastic Beanstalk
hosting_target.rationale: Firm existing AWS footprint; EB simplifies deployment for small DevOps team

privacy_retention_policy.value: Conflict check records and audit logs retained for 7 years per state bar record-keeping requirements; waiver records retained for matter lifetime plus 7 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Bar ethics rules require retention of conflict determination records

future_scope.value: AI-assisted conflict prediction scoring, integration with second matter management system, client portal for conflict waiver acknowledgement
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP enhancements after workflow digitisation is proven

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing Clio matter management system via webhook; Elastic Beanstalk single-region us-east-1; budget under $800/month
constraints.rationale: Firm has existing Clio subscription and AWS account

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM, structured JSON logging with correlation IDs, no PII in log fields
observability_baseline.rationale: Firm existing New Relic subscription; PII exclusion required for privileged data

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with demo to managing partner at end of each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Firm stakeholders want visibility into progress

compliance_context.value: ABA Model Rules 1.7, 1.8, 1.9, 1.10 (conflicts of interest); state ethics rules for jurisdiction of firm operation; bar record-retention requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Application directly supports ethics rule compliance obligations

project.goal: Replace manual conflict-of-interest spreadsheet process with digital workflow
project.target_users: Attorneys, paralegals, conflicts clerks, managing partners
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks

frontend.state: React Query for server state; Zustand for approval workflow step state

security.auth: Okta OIDC with group-to-role mapping
security.mfa: Required for all roles via Okta policy
security.audit: Immutable conflict_decisions table with trigger-protected rows

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 with pg_trgm extension for fuzzy name search

devops.ci: GitHub Actions

constraints.integration: Clio webhook receiver for new matter events

reasoner_reviews.product: The transition from spreadsheets to a structured digital workflow is the right first step. The party database with fuzzy name search is the highest-value feature — catching near-miss conflicts such as maiden names and entity variants is exactly where manual processes fail. Waiver record management closes the compliance loop. Rounds 1 and 2 established the core workflow; round 3 targets approval routing correctness and audit log immutability.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async routes suit concurrent party similarity search queries that fan out across multiple PostgreSQL similarity scans. The approval routing state machine should be represented as a PostgreSQL state column with allowed transitions enforced at the application layer. Elastic Beanstalk multi-container Docker is adequate at this scale but requires connection draining config to avoid dropping in-flight approvals during rolling deploys.
reasoner_reviews.security: The immutable audit log design is critical for ABA compliance. PostgreSQL row-level security combined with a trigger that rejects UPDATE and DELETE on conflict_decisions is the correct implementation — application-layer-only immutability is insufficient. Okta MFA policy must be enforced at the identity provider level so application-level bypass is impossible. PII exclusion from logs must be tested explicitly in CI.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Budget of $800/month constrains the deployment to a single Elastic Beanstalk environment with t3.medium instances. New Relic APM Python agent adds minimal overhead. The Clio webhook receiver must be idempotent since Clio retries failed deliveries.

backend.service_design: FastAPI routers: /api/v1/conflict-checks, /api/v1/parties, /api/v1/approvals, /api/v1/waivers, /api/v1/admin, /api/v1/webhooks/clio. ApprovalService enforces state machine transition allowlist: draft→searching→pending_review→approved/declined/waived.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful JSON. POST /conflict-checks triggers async party search. GET /conflict-checks/{id}/findings returns results. POST /conflict-checks/{id}/decision requires justification field. PATCH /conflict-checks/{id}/escalate routes to ethics_counsel.
backend.failure_handling: Party search runs via FastAPI BackgroundTasks; results written to conflict_findings table; client polls with 202 Accepted until search_complete. Clio webhook receiver returns 200 immediately, processes asynchronously to tolerate Clio 10-second timeout.
backend.backend_risks: pg_trgm similarity scans over large party tables may be slow without GIN index; test with EXPLAIN ANALYZE. Approval state machine transitions must be idempotent to handle duplicate decision submissions.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting. Pages: /dashboard (pending checks queue), /checks/new (request submission), /checks/:id (findings review and decision), /parties (party search), /admin/routing (approval routing configuration).
frontend.pages_and_flows: New Conflict Check: attorney submits matter details with party names triggering search. Findings Review: clerk annotates matches as conflict/potential/no-conflict, forwards to assigned reviewer. Approval Decision: partner selects clear/waiver-required/decline with mandatory justification text.
frontend.state_management: React Query for check status polling and findings data. Zustand for multi-step approval form state across the decision wizard.

security.auth_design: Okta OIDC with PKCE flow. FastAPI validates JWT ID tokens against Okta JWKS endpoint. Role claims extracted from Okta groups claim.
security.authorization_model: RBAC enforced in FastAPI dependency injection: conflicts_requestor (submit), conflicts_reviewer (annotate findings), ethics_counsel (escalation decisions), admin (routing config). Reviewers cannot access checks outside their assignment queue.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: conflict_decisions table protected by PostgreSQL trigger rejecting UPDATE and DELETE. All decision events record reviewer_id, timestamp, role, justification, ip_address. New Relic log shipping excludes fields matching party_name and client_name patterns.

data.entities: conflict_check_requests (id, requesting_attorney_id, matter_name, check_status ENUM, submitted_at, completed_at). parties (id, display_name, name_variants JSONB, party_type ENUM, source_matter_id) with GIN index via pg_trgm. conflict_findings (id, check_id, matched_party_id, match_score, finding_status ENUM). conflict_decisions (id, check_id, reviewer_id, decision ENUM, justification NOT NULL, decided_at, ip_address). waiver_records (id, check_id, waiver_text, consented_parties JSONB, executed_at). received_webhooks (clio_delivery_id PK, received_at, payload_hash).
data.schema_notes: pg_trgm GIN index on parties.display_name. Partial index on conflict_check_requests.check_status for active queue queries. PostgreSQL trigger on conflict_decisions: BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE raises exception unconditionally.
data.retention_and_deletion: Conflict decisions and waiver records retained 7 years. Automatic archival to cold storage after 3 years via pg_partman range partitioning by submitted_at.

devops.deployment_topology: Elastic Beanstalk multi-container: nginx sidecar (TLS termination, static serving) and FastAPI container (Uvicorn). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ in same VPC. Secrets Manager for Okta client secret and DB password.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy, pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build and ECR push on merge to main. eb deploy via EB CLI in Actions workflow. Alembic migration applied as one-off docker exec before traffic switches.
devops.rollback_strategy: EB environment swap rollback via eb swap if health check fails post-deploy. Alembic downgrade script prepared for each migration.

ISS-001.title: Approval state machine allows invalid transitions
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan had no enforcement of allowed state transitions; any API call could set arbitrary check_status.

history.note: Architect reviewer flagged missing transition guard
Transition allowlist added to ApprovalService

ISS-002.title: Audit log immutability relies only on application layer
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan lacked database-level trigger protecting conflict_decisions from UPDATE and DELETE.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged application-only immutability as insufficient
PostgreSQL trigger added rejecting UPDATE and DELETE on conflict_decisions

ISS-003.title: Clio webhook receiver not idempotent
ISS-003.detail: Clio may retry webhook delivery on timeout; duplicate matter events could create duplicate conflict check requests.

history.note: Identified during integration review

focus_issues.title: Clio webhook receiver not idempotent
focus_issues.detail: Clio may retry webhook delivery on timeout; duplicate matter events could create duplicate conflict check requests.

latest_recommendations: Add idempotency key deduplication to Clio webhook receiver using Clio delivery ID stored in received_webhooks table
Add pytest test for duplicate Clio webhook delivery to verify single conflict_check_request is created
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Conflict-of-Interest Management System — Round 2 Plan

convergence_state.reason: Medium-severity idempotency gap in Clio webhook receiver unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Significant improvement from round 1 with state machine guard and database-level audit log protection added. Remaining gap is Clio webhook idempotency creating duplicate request risk.
strengths: PostgreSQL trigger on conflict_decisions is the correct implementation for immutable audit log
State machine transition allowlist prevents invalid status jumps
pg_trgm GIN index design is correct for fuzzy party name search at expected scale
concerns: Clio webhook receiver lacks idempotency; duplicate delivery creates duplicate conflict check requests
No explicit test for the PostgreSQL trigger behaviour in CI suite
recommendations: Implement received_webhooks deduplication table keyed on Clio delivery-id header
Add pytest test that sends duplicate Clio webhook events and verifies only one conflict_check_request is created

issue_updates.title: Clio webhook receiver not idempotent
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan did not add deduplication logic to the webhook receiver despite Clio retry risk being known

previous_plan.title: Conflict-of-Interest Management System — Round 2 Plan
previous_plan.executive_summary: React 18 and FastAPI fullstack application replacing conflict-of-interest spreadsheets with searchable party database, automated pg_trgm conflict search, multi-level approval routing enforced by state machine, and PostgreSQL-trigger-protected immutable decision audit log.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL trigger on conflict_decisions rejecting UPDATE/DELETE. Okta MFA enforced at IDP level. RBAC via FastAPI dependency injection checking Okta group role claims.
previous_plan.workflows: New check submission triggers async party search via BackgroundTasks. Findings forwarded through two-level approval routing with mandatory justification. Ethics counsel escalation available at any routing level.
previous_plan.data_model: conflict_check_requests, parties with pg_trgm GIN index, conflict_findings, conflict_decisions (trigger-protected), waiver_records.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 focuses on ISS-003: Clio webhook idempotency. The fix is a received_webhooks deduplication table keyed on the Clio X-Clio-Delivery-Id header value, checked atomically via INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING before creating any conflict_check_request. All prior security and state machine work from rounds 1 and 2 is preserved intact. The plan should also add an explicit pytest test for duplicate delivery behaviour to close the testing gap flagged by the auditor.

fix_report.action_taken: Added received_webhooks table (clio_delivery_id VARCHAR PK, received_at TIMESTAMP, payload_hash VARCHAR) to PostgreSQL schema via new Alembic migration. Clio webhook endpoint now performs INSERT INTO received_webhooks VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; if zero rows inserted (delivery already seen), returns HTTP 200 without creating a conflict_check_request. pytest fixture added that replays the same Clio webhook payload twice and asserts conflict_check_requests count remains 1.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Clio webhook retries produce no duplicate conflict check requests; idempotency verified by automated test in CI

target_output.title: Conflict-of-Interest Management System
target_output.executive_summary: React 18 and FastAPI fullstack application for law firm conflict-of-interest clearance workflows. Replaces spreadsheet-and-email processes with a searchable party database using pg_trgm fuzzy name matching, automated conflict findings generation, multi-level approval routing with state-machine-enforced transitions, PostgreSQL-trigger-protected immutable decision audit log, and idempotent Clio matter management webhook integration.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier fullstack on AWS Elastic Beanstalk: React 18 SPA served via nginx sidecar communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI backend running on Python 3.12 in a multi-container EB environment. PostgreSQL 16 on RDS with pg_trgm extension handles fuzzy party name similarity search via GIN index and enforces audit log immutability via a trigger-protected conflict_decisions table. Okta OIDC provides MFA-enforced authentication with RBAC group claims mapping to four roles. Clio webhook receiver is idempotent via a received_webhooks deduplication table keyed on the Clio delivery ID header. New Relic APM agent provides correlated distributed tracing across request and background search task lifecycles.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, Vite. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Pydantic v2, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, python-jose for JWT validation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with pg_trgm and uuid-ossp extensions; RDS Multi-AZ in production. Auth: Okta OIDC PKCE flow, FastAPI OAuth2 security dependency. Infrastructure: AWS Elastic Beanstalk multi-container Docker, ECR, RDS, Secrets Manager, ACM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Conflict Check Submission: attorney submits new matter details with party and adverse party names; system initiates async pg_trgm similarity search. Conflict Findings Review: conflicts clerk reviews match results above 0.4 threshold, annotates each as conflict/potential/no-conflict, forwards to reviewer queue. Approval Routing: two-level routing (conflicts_reviewer then partner); ethics counsel escalation available; all decisions require non-empty justification. Waiver Management: waiver_required decisions create waiver records with consented party list. Admin Configuration: admin role manages routing rules and role-to-user assignments. Clio Integration: new matter webhook events auto-populate draft conflict check requests with idempotency via received_webhooks table.
target_output.system_components: nginx sidecar container (TLS termination and React SPA static serving). FastAPI application container (Uvicorn workers, background party search tasks). PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (pg_trgm GIN index on parties, trigger-protected conflict_decisions, received_webhooks deduplication table). Okta OIDC provider (MFA policy enforcement, group-to-role mapping). AWS Secrets Manager (Okta client secret, RDS credentials with 30-day rotation). New Relic APM Python agent (PII field exclusion configured). ECR for Docker image registry. Elastic Beanstalk environment with enhanced health reporting.
target_output.workflows: Clio Matter Webhook: Clio POST to /api/v1/webhooks/clio; receiver extracts X-Clio-Delivery-Id header, inserts into received_webhooks ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; if new delivery, creates draft conflict_check_request pre-populated with matter name and parties; returns 200 immediately. On duplicate delivery, INSERT returns zero rows, endpoint returns 200 without creating any record. Manual Conflict Check Submission: attorney fills /checks/new with matter description and party names; submits POST /api/v1/conflict-checks; FastAPI triggers background party search using pg_trgm similarity against parties and adverse_parties tables; client polls GET /conflict-checks/{id}/findings until check_status becomes pending_review. Approval Routing: conflicts clerk annotates each finding, forwards via state transition searching→pending_review; ApprovalService validates transition against allowlist. Reviewer submits POST /api/v1/conflict-checks/{id}/decision; if waiver_required, waiver_record created; if declined, matter intake halted. All decisions inserted to trigger-protected conflict_decisions table.
target_output.data_model: conflict_check_requests (id UUID PK, requesting_attorney_id FK, matter_name TEXT, check_status ENUM draft/searching/pending_review/approved/declined/waived, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP). parties (id UUID PK, display_name VARCHAR, name_variants JSONB, party_type ENUM individual/entity, source_matter_id FK) with GIN index on display_name via pg_trgm. conflict_findings (id UUID PK, check_id FK, matched_party_id FK, match_score DECIMAL, match_basis TEXT, finding_status ENUM flagged/cleared/potential, annotated_by_id FK). adverse_parties (id UUID PK, check_id FK, party_name VARCHAR, relationship_to_matter TEXT). conflict_decisions (id UUID PK, check_id FK, reviewer_id FK, reviewer_role VARCHAR, decision ENUM clear/waiver_required/declined/escalated, justification TEXT NOT NULL, decided_at TIMESTAMP, ip_address INET) with trigger rejecting UPDATE and DELETE. waiver_records (id UUID PK, check_id FK, waiver_text TEXT, consented_parties JSONB, executed_at TIMESTAMP, approved_by_id FK). received_webhooks (clio_delivery_id VARCHAR PK, received_at TIMESTAMP, payload_hash VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/conflict-checks (create request, triggers async search, returns 202 with check ID and polling URL). GET /api/v1/conflict-checks?status=pending_review (reviewer queue filtered by role). GET /api/v1/conflict-checks/{id}/findings (similarity results with match_score and finding_status). POST /api/v1/conflict-checks/{id}/decision (body: decision enum, justification string). PATCH /api/v1/conflict-checks/{id}/escalate (routes to ethics_counsel queue). POST /api/v1/waivers (create waiver record for waiver_required decisions). GET /api/v1/parties/search?q=... (fuzzy name lookup for submission-time deduplication). POST /api/v1/webhooks/clio (idempotent matter event receiver, always returns 200). All endpoints require Okta JWT; role-gated via FastAPI dependency.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta OIDC PKCE with firm-wide MFA policy enforced at identity provider; FastAPI validates ID token against Okta JWKS. RBAC via FastAPI Depends checking Okta groups claim: conflicts_requestor, conflicts_reviewer, ethics_counsel, admin. PostgreSQL trigger on conflict_decisions: BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE EXECUTE FUNCTION reject_immutable_audit() raises 'P0001' exception unconditionally. TLS 1.3 via ACM certificate on EB load balancer. RDS encryption at rest with AWS KMS. Secrets Manager for all credentials with 30-day auto-rotation. New Relic log agent field exclusion list: party_name, client_name, display_name, matter_name. ABA Model Rules 1.7-1.10 compliance: every conflict determination captures reviewer identity, role, timestamp, ip_address, and mandatory justification in an immutable record.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Elastic Beanstalk multi-container environment defined in Dockerrun.aws.json: nginx sidecar on port 443 (ACM TLS), FastAPI on Uvicorn port 8000. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ db.t3.medium in same VPC private subnet. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build and ECR push on merge to main, eb deploy --staged on tag. Alembic migration applied via docker exec one-off against production RDS before traffic switches. Rolling deployment with 30-second health-check grace period on /api/v1/health endpoint returning DB connectivity and Okta reachability. EB environment swap rollback if health check fails post-deploy.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM Python agent with distributed trace correlation across HTTP routes and background party search tasks. Custom metrics: conflict_check.party_search_duration_ms (p50/p95), approval_queue.pending_review_depth, decision.time_to_resolve_hours. Structured JSON logs with correlation_id, user_id (opaque), check_id, action — party_name and client_name fields excluded from all log events. Alert policy: party search p95 exceeding 10 seconds, approval queue depth above 20 open checks, RDS connection pool above 80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $430/month for two t3.medium EB instances, RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ, ECR storage, and New Relic Pro agent. pg_trgm similarity search scales to 200k party records with GIN index before requiring query plan optimisation. FastAPI async workers handle concurrent search background tasks without blocking API routes. At firm growth beyond 500 attorneys consider an RDS read replica to isolate party search load from approval write transactions.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): party database with pg_trgm search, conflict check submission and async search, two-level approval routing with state machine, immutable conflict_decisions with PostgreSQL trigger, React SPA for clerk and partner workflows, Okta OIDC integration. Phase 2 (3 weeks): Clio webhook integration with received_webhooks idempotency, waiver record management, admin routing rule configuration panel. Phase 3 (2 weeks): New Relic APM instrumentation with custom metrics dashboards, SES email notifications to assigned reviewers on queue assignment.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. ruff for linting with custom rule banning f-string interpolation of party_name and client_name. pytest with 80% coverage gate; pytest-asyncio for async route tests. Required test cases: pg_trgm search returns correct similarity scores above threshold, state machine rejects invalid status transitions, PostgreSQL trigger rejects UPDATE on conflict_decisions, Clio webhook returns 200 on duplicate delivery without creating second request, RBAC rejects cross-role endpoint access. Alembic convention: trigger and index definitions as raw SQL in upgrade() functions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: pg_trgm similarity scans degrade as parties table exceeds 200k rows; mitigated by GIN index on display_name and name_variants with EXPLAIN ANALYZE benchmarking in CI test suite. Risk: EB rolling deploy drops in-flight approval decisions; mitigated by connection draining and 30-second health-check grace period. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL trigger for audit immutability over application-only enforcement adds operational complexity but eliminates bypass risk. Tradeoff: async party search with client polling over synchronous response is necessary for search latency of 2-5 seconds on large party databases.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: pg_trgm threshold 0.4 confirmed as default with admin-configurable override stored in routing_rules table. Clio webhook idempotency implemented via received_webhooks deduplication keyed on X-Clio-Delivery-Id header. Okta group names mapped to RBAC roles via static config with admin override capability.

metadata.notes: Law firm conflict-of-interest workflow digitisation; round 3 resolves Clio webhook idempotency via received_webhooks deduplication table.

profile.domain: Legal Operations Contract Data Consolidation

project_goal.value: Consolidate contract metadata from three siloed CLM systems acquired through M&A into a unified Snowflake data lake for legal ops reporting on obligations and renewals
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates manual cross-system queries reducing analyst time on fragmented data reconciliation

target_users.value: Legal ops analysts and in-house counsel reporting on contract obligations, renewal dates, and counterparty exposure
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of consolidated contract data outputs

project_class.rationale: No UI needed; pure batch ETL from three source CLM APIs into a data warehouse

capabilities.rationale: Pipeline-only capability set with no frontend or real-time components

complexity_level.rationale: Three source integrations with schema normalization but manageable contract volume

risk_level.rationale: Confidential contract data but internal-only access and no real-time exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Contract terms, counterparty names, financial obligations, and governing law are confidential

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs within corporate AWS environment; no external network access

access_model.value: IAM roles for pipeline execution; Snowflake RBAC with LEGAL_OPS_ANALYST role for analyst query access
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access using AWS-native identity management

feature_scope.value: Nightly ETL from three CLM APIs, canonical schema transformation via dbt, data quality validation with quarantine, Snowflake ANALYTICS mart, data lineage tracking
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline feature set required for consolidation objective

mvp_scope.value: Single CLM source (Ironclad) with dbt staging model and Snowflake load to validate canonical contract schema before integrating remaining two sources
mvp_scope.rationale: De-risks schema design before committing to multi-source integration

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all CLM API calls, Snowflake column-level encryption for contract financial obligation values, AWS Secrets Manager for CLM API credentials
security_baseline.rationale: Minimum controls for confidential contract data in transit and at rest

frontend_stack.value: None - pipeline only; analysts use BI tools directly against Snowflake ANALYTICS schema
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI component required for batch pipeline delivery

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with dbt Core 1.8 and Apache Airflow 2.9 on MWAA
backend_stack.rationale: Standard legal ops data stack; dbt for declarative transformation, Airflow for orchestration

data_platform.value: Snowflake enterprise tier as target warehouse; PostgreSQL 16 RDS for pipeline run state and quarantine metadata
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake chosen for analyst query performance and isolation from source CLM systems

hosting_target.value: MWAA on AWS us-east-1 for Airflow; ECS Fargate for dbt runner tasks launched by Airflow EcsOperator
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Airflow reduces ops burden; Fargate for ephemeral dbt execution

privacy_retention_policy.value: Contract metadata retained 7 years per records management policy; pipeline execution logs retained 90 days; GDPR erasure for EU counterparty canonical names on request
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Legal hold requirements for contract data and GDPR compliance

future_scope.value: Near-real-time CLM webhook streaming, NLP-assisted obligation extraction from contract text, self-service renewal alert subscriptions for legal ops
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enhancements after stable batch baseline established

constraints.value: Must complete nightly run by 06:00 UTC; API-only access to source CLM systems (no direct database access); CLM-C vendor enforces 100 req/min rate limit
constraints.rationale: CLM vendor SLA restrictions and maintenance window constraints

observability_baseline.value: CloudWatch for MWAA DAG metrics, dbt run summaries posted to Slack after each run, PagerDuty on DAG failure or SLA breach
observability_baseline.rationale: Minimal viable monitoring for nightly batch pipeline reliability

execution_preference.value: Phased delivery: one CLM source integration per sprint; schema-first design with dbt model review by legal ops analysts before each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Reduces integration risk across three distinct CLM API schemas and taxonomies

compliance_context.value: GDPR Article 28 processor obligations for EU counterparty personal data; SOX obligations tracking for public company legal team contract data lineage
compliance_context.rationale: EU contract counterparties and SOX financial obligation audit trail requirements

project.goal: Nightly consolidation of contract metadata from Ironclad, DocuSign CLM, and Salesforce CPQ into Snowflake ANALYTICS schema
project.target_users: Legal ops analysts querying unified renewal dates, obligation summaries, counterparty exposure
project.timeline: Three sprints, one CLM source per sprint

backend.api_clients: httpx async with exponential backoff retry for CLM API pagination

security.secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for CLM-A, CLM-B, CLM-C API credentials with 90-day rotation
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for all outbound CLM API calls
security.data_at_rest: Snowflake column encryption for total_value and governing_law fields

data.warehouse: Snowflake enterprise with RAW, STAGING, INTERMEDIATE, ANALYTICS schemas
data.metadata_store: PostgreSQL 16 RDS for pipeline_runs and quarantine_records
data.lineage: dbt lineage graph hosted via dbt Docs on S3+CloudFront

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: dbt compile + dbt test on PR; pytest for extractors on merge to main
devops.hosting: MWAA for Airflow; ECS Fargate for dbt runner
devops.environments: dev, staging, production Snowflake databases with environment-specific credentials

constraints.api_only: No direct database access to source CLM systems
constraints.window: Nightly run must complete by 06:00 UTC
constraints.rate_limits: CLM-C capped at 100 req/min requiring token-bucket throttling

open_questions.taxonomy_conflict: How to handle conflicting obligation type taxonomies across Ironclad, DocuSign CLM, and Salesforce CPQ

reasoner_reviews.product: The consolidation objective is clear and high-value for the legal ops team. Canonical schema design is the critical path risk given obligation type taxonomies vary significantly across Ironclad, DocuSign CLM, and Salesforce CPQ. Recommend involving legal ops analysts in taxonomy review before sprint 1 delivery to avoid downstream mart rework.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Python extractors with dbt transformation is the right architectural split. dbt handles declarative SQL transformations well but the extraction layer needs careful retry and idempotency logic given CLM API rate limits and the 4-hour nightly window constraint. The quarantine table approach is sound but needs an explicit re-processing workflow to avoid stale error records accumulating.
reasoner_reviews.security: AWS Secrets Manager rotation for CLM API keys should be automated quarterly. Snowflake column-level security policies for financial obligation fields need RBAC role definitions before analyst onboarding. Pipeline ECS task role should have write-only access to STAGING schema, not ANALYTICS, to enforce separation of transformation and consumption roles.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 4-hour nightly window is feasible at current contract volumes but the architecture must use cursor-based incremental extraction to avoid full re-pulls as the M&A contract corpus grows. The CLM-C rate limit of 100 req/min requires a token-bucket throttler in the extractor to prevent API ban.

backend.service_design: Three independent Airflow DAGs (clm_ironclad_extract, clm_docusign_extract, clm_sfcpq_extract) with shared Python extractor base class implementing cursor pagination, retry, and S3 landing; single dbt runner DAG consuming all three source schemas
backend.api_patterns: Cursor-based pagination for incremental extraction using updated_after parameter; exponential backoff with jitter for transient 429/503 responses; token-bucket throttler for CLM-C 100 req/min limit
