backend.background_jobs: Nightly DAG schedule 01:00 UTC; weekly full-refresh DAG for dbt model schema drift detection; monthly CLM-C credential rotation trigger
backend.failure_handling: Airflow task-level retries 3 attempts with 5-minute backoff; records failing dbt schema tests written to PostgreSQL quarantine_records with error_type classification; PagerDuty alert on any DAG failure

security.auth_design: MWAA execution IAM role with scoped permissions: Secrets Manager GetSecretValue, S3 PutObject to landing zone, ECS RunTask for dbt runner
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager stores CLM-A, CLM-B, CLM-C API credentials; automated 90-day rotation Lambda for CLM-A and CLM-B; CLM-C requires manual rotation due to legacy API constraint
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail logs all AWS API calls for MWAA execution role; dbt run_results JSON archived to S3 after each run for transformation audit trail; PostgreSQL pipeline_runs records row counts per source per run

data.entities: contracts (unified_id, source_system, clm_native_id, title, effective_date, expiry_date, counterparty_id, contract_type, total_value encrypted, governing_law), obligations (id, contract_id, obligation_type, due_date, responsible_party, status), renewal_events (id, contract_id, renewal_type, notice_deadline, action_required), counterparties (id, canonical_name, jurisdiction, eu_resident_flag, aliases_json), reconciliation_logs (source_system, run_id, records_extracted, records_staged, records_quarantined, run_at), data_lineage (target_table, source_system, dbt_model, last_refreshed_at)
data.storage_design: Snowflake RAW for COPY INTO landing; STAGING for field normalization; INTERMEDIATE for canonical taxonomy mapping; ANALYTICS for dbt marts consumed by analysts
data.retention_and_deletion: GDPR erasure job anonymizes counterparty canonical_name for EU residents on DPO-approved request; contract records retained 7 years per legal hold policy

devops.deployment_topology: MWAA cluster in us-east-1 VPC private subnets; ECS Fargate tasks launched by Airflow EcsOperator in same VPC; Snowflake connected via AWS PrivateLink
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: dbt compile on PR, dbt test on merge to main, pytest for extractor unit tests; dbt project deployed by syncing to MWAA DAGs S3 bucket on main merge
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch MWAA metrics dashboard, dbt Docs on S3+CloudFront for model lineage, Slack webhook for run summaries, PagerDuty for failures

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a nightly batch ETL pipeline consolidating three CLM APIs into Snowflake. The canonical contract schema is the core design challenge given divergent obligation taxonomies across Ironclad, DocuSign CLM, and Salesforce CPQ. dbt staging-intermediate-marts layers provide clean concern separation. Intentional flaw: no cross-source deduplication key for contracts duplicated across CLM systems post-M&A, risking duplicate obligation records in the ANALYTICS mart.
target_output.title: Unified CLM Contract Metadata Consolidation Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: Nightly Airflow pipeline extracting contract metadata from Ironclad, DocuSign CLM, and Salesforce CPQ APIs into a canonical Snowflake schema via dbt Core transformations. Enables legal ops analysts to query unified contract obligations, renewal calendars, and counterparty exposure without manual cross-system reconciliation. Three-sprint phased delivery aligning one CLM integration per sprint, with intentional architectural gap in cross-source deduplication.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three parallel Airflow DAGs execute nightly from 01:00 UTC, each running a Python extractor authenticating via AWS Secrets Manager, calling the respective CLM REST API with cursor-based incremental pagination, and landing newline-delimited JSON to an S3 staging zone per source. A downstream dbt runner Airflow task executes dbt Core via ECS Fargate, transforming records through RAW, STAGING (field normalization), INTERMEDIATE (canonical taxonomy mapping for contract_type and obligation_type), and ANALYTICS (dbt marts for obligations_tracker, renewal_calendar, counterparty_exposure) Snowflake schemas. Pipeline run state and quarantine records persist in a PostgreSQL 16 RDS metadata store. NOTE: current design lacks a cross-source deduplication key for contracts present in multiple CLM systems following M&A migrations, risking duplicated obligation and renewal records in the ANALYTICS marts.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 on AWS MWAA. Extraction: Python 3.12 with httpx async client, pydantic v2 for CLM response validation. Transformation: dbt Core 1.8 with Snowflake adapter and dbt-utils. Warehouse: Snowflake enterprise with column-level security. Metadata store: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS. Hosting: ECS Fargate for dbt runner, MWAA for Airflow scheduler. CI: GitHub Actions. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager. Monitoring: CloudWatch, Slack webhook, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: CLM extractor modules (IroncladExtractor, DocuSignCLMExtractor, SalesforceCPQExtractor) sharing base class for cursor pagination, retry, and S3 landing. dbt STAGING models normalizing raw CLM JSON to typed Snowflake columns with freshness checks. dbt INTERMEDIATE models mapping source-specific obligation_type and contract_type values to canonical ENUM sets via dbt seed mapping CSVs. dbt ANALYTICS marts: obligations_tracker (upcoming obligations by matter and responsible party), renewal_calendar (contracts with auto-renew and opt-in deadlines in next 90 days), counterparty_exposure (total contract value and count by counterparty). Quarantine workflow: records failing schema tests written to PostgreSQL quarantine_records for analyst remediation.
target_output.system_components: MWAA Airflow cluster (3 extraction DAGs + 1 dbt runner DAG). S3 landing zone (JSON partitioned by source and run date). ECS Fargate dbt runner task. Snowflake (RAW, STAGING, INTERMEDIATE, ANALYTICS schemas with RBAC). PostgreSQL 16 RDS (pipeline_runs, quarantine_records, data_lineage). AWS Secrets Manager (CLM-A, CLM-B, CLM-C credentials). CloudWatch (MWAA metrics, ECS task logs). GitHub Actions (CI). dbt Docs site (S3+CloudFront).
target_output.workflows: Nightly Extraction: MWAA triggers three extraction DAGs at 01:00 UTC in parallel; each Python extractor reads last_cursor from pipeline_runs PostgreSQL table, calls CLM API paginating from that cursor, writes JSON records to S3 partitioned by source and run_date, issues Snowflake COPY INTO to RAW schema, and updates cursor checkpoint in pipeline_runs. dbt Transformation Run: after all three extraction DAGs complete, dbt_runner DAG triggers ECS Fargate task running dbt run targeting STAGING then INTERMEDIATE then ANALYTICS sequentially; dbt test runs after each layer; test failures quarantine affected records to PostgreSQL quarantine_records rather than halting the pipeline run; dbt run_results JSON archived to S3. Data Quality Review: analyst queries quarantine_records Snowflake view to identify and remediate rejected records; corrected records re-ingested by triggering extraction DAG via Airflow REST API with backfill date parameter.
target_output.data_model: contracts (unified_id UUID PK, source_system ENUM ironclad/docusign_clm/sfcpq, clm_native_id VARCHAR, title VARCHAR, effective_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, auto_renew BOOLEAN, counterparty_id UUID FK, contract_type ENUM service_agreement/nda/vendor/license/amendment, total_value NUMERIC COLUMN_ENCRYPT, governing_law VARCHAR, loaded_at TIMESTAMP). obligations (id UUID PK, contract_id UUID FK, obligation_type ENUM payment/deliverable/notice/compliance/indemnity, description TEXT, due_date DATE, responsible_party VARCHAR, status ENUM open/fulfilled/overdue). renewal_events (id UUID PK, contract_id UUID FK, renewal_type ENUM auto/opt_in/opt_out, notice_deadline DATE, action_required TEXT, notified_at TIMESTAMP). counterparties (id UUID PK, canonical_name VARCHAR, jurisdiction VARCHAR, eu_resident_flag BOOLEAN, aliases_json VARIANT). reconciliation_logs (id UUID, source_system, run_id UUID, records_extracted INT, records_staged INT, records_quarantined INT, run_started_at TIMESTAMP, run_completed_at TIMESTAMP). data_lineage (target_table VARCHAR, source_system, dbt_model VARCHAR, dbt_run_id UUID, last_refreshed_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: Internal dbt mart interfaces only; no external HTTP API exposed. Source integrations: Ironclad REST GET /contracts?updated_after={cursor}&limit=500; DocuSign CLM REST GET /contracts/search with updatedAt filter and page cursor; Salesforce REST SOQL query on Contract object filtered by LastModifiedDate. Snowflake COPY INTO for bulk S3 file load. Airflow REST API (internal MWAA): POST /api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns for manual analyst-triggered backfills. No public-facing HTTP endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: MWAA IAM execution role with scoped permissions: Secrets Manager GetSecretValue for CLM credentials, S3 PutObject to landing zone, ECS RunTask for dbt Fargate. CLM-A and CLM-B credentials rotated automatically every 90 days via Secrets Manager rotation Lambda; CLM-C manual rotation documented in runbook. Snowflake COLUMN_ENCRYPT on total_value and governing_law; LEGAL_OPS_ANALYST role granted SELECT on ANALYTICS schema only. GDPR Article 28: eu_resident_flag identifies EU counterparties; anonymization job replaces canonical_name with SHA-256 hash on DPO-approved erasure request logged to data_lineage. SOX: data_lineage and reconciliation_logs provide immutable transformation audit trail. CloudTrail logs all AWS API calls for MWAA execution role.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: dbt compile and dbt test against dev Snowflake on every PR; pytest for extractor unit tests on merge to main. Terraform manages MWAA environment, RDS, ECS task definition, and S3 buckets; dbt project deployed by syncing to MWAA DAGs S3 bucket on main merge. Environments: DEV (sandbox Snowflake, CLM sandbox credentials), STAGING (full CLM API access, staging Snowflake), PRODUCTION (MWAA, production Snowflake, PagerDuty active). Rollback: prior dbt project artifact re-synced to S3; Airflow DAG paused during rollback. Secrets rotation automated for CLM-A and CLM-B; CLM-C rotation requires manual vendor coordination.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch dashboard tracks MWAA DAG run duration, task success and failure rates, ECS Fargate task memory utilization. Custom Airflow metrics published to CloudWatch: records_extracted_per_source, quarantine_rate_per_source (PagerDuty alert if >5%), dbt_test_pass_rate. Slack webhook posts run summary with source name, records extracted, quarantined count, and run duration to #legal-ops-data after each run. PagerDuty escalation for DAG failure, 06:00 UTC SLA miss, or three consecutive quarantine_rate >5% runs. dbt Docs site on S3+CloudFront exposes model lineage for engineering and analyst reference.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MWAA small environment ~$300/month. ECS Fargate dbt runner (~15 min nightly) ~$20/month. RDS db.t3.medium ~$60/month. Snowflake compute with auto-suspend after 5 min idle ~$150/month. Total ~$600/month. Scaling path: cursor-based incremental extraction avoids full re-pull cost growth; Snowflake warehouse size scales independently from storage as analyst query concurrency increases.
target_output.phased_implementation: Sprint 1 (2 weeks): IroncladExtractor with cursor pagination, dbt STAGING model for contracts and obligations, Snowflake RAW and STAGING schemas, GitHub Actions CI, Airflow DAG skeleton, pipeline_runs PostgreSQL table, canonical contract_type taxonomy sign-off with legal ops. Sprint 2 (2 weeks): DocuSignCLMExtractor, dbt INTERMEDIATE canonical taxonomy mapping, quarantine_records table and dbt schema tests, obligation_type ENUM finalized as dbt seed CSV, Slack run summary notification. Sprint 3 (2 weeks): SalesforceCPQExtractor with token-bucket rate limiter, dbt ANALYTICS marts, Snowflake RBAC for LEGAL_OPS_ANALYST role, dbt Docs deployment, GDPR erasure runbook, SOX lineage documentation.
target_output.development_guidelines: All dbt models use incremental materialization with unique_key on unified_id for idempotent re-runs. Python extractors use pydantic v2 models matching canonical contract schema. Extractor base class handles retry, cursor persistence, and S3 landing. dbt sources declared with freshness checks: warn after 25 hours, error after 28 hours. PRs require dbt compile and dbt test passing against dev Snowflake in CI. Taxonomy mapping tables maintained as dbt seed CSVs reviewed quarterly with legal ops.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Divergent obligation_type taxonomies across three CLMs may leave ambiguous records unclassified. Mitigation: canonical taxonomy CSV as dbt seed reviewed quarterly. Risk: CLM-C 100 req/min limit may cause partial extraction at higher volumes. Mitigation: token-bucket throttler with adaptive page size. Known architectural flaw: no cross-source deduplication key defined for contracts duplicated across CLM systems post-M&A; a single contract may appear as separate obligation records in the ANALYTICS mart, skewing renewal and obligation counts. Requires legal ops to define a canonical contract identifier mapping before promotion to production.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Snowflake over Redshift for analyst query flexibility. Confirmed API-only CLM integration per vendor SLAs. Canonical contract_type ENUM taxonomy agreed with legal ops in sprint 1. EU counterparty GDPR erasure scope confirmed as counterparty canonical_name field only, not contract title.

metadata.notes: Intentional flaw: no cross-CLM deduplication key for contracts duplicated across source systems post-M&A; acknowledged in architecture_overview and risks_and_tradeoffs.

profile.domain: Field Compliance Vendor Assessment Mobile App

project_goal.value: Mobile application for field compliance officers at financial institutions to conduct on-site third-party vendor assessments, capture evidence photos, complete structured inspection checklists, and sync findings to a central compliance platform when connectivity is restored
project_goal.rationale: Field assessments in restricted-network facilities require offline-first operation with reliable sync on reconnection

target_users.value: Field compliance officers and vendor assessment managers at financial institutions
target_users.rationale: Primary users conduct on-site assessments with limited connectivity; managers review and follow up from desktop

project_class.rationale: Offline-first field data collection with local storage and sync requires native mobile architecture

capabilities.rationale: React Native mobile frontend, Go sync API backend, local and server databases, Azure AD auth, and central compliance platform integration

complexity_level.rationale: Offline-first sync with conflict resolution adds complexity beyond a simple CRUD app but no real-time streaming requirements

risk_level.rationale: Assessment findings and vendor risk data are confidential regulatory artifacts; device loss creates data exposure risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Third-party vendor assessment findings contain commercially sensitive risk ratings and regulatory control evaluations

external_exposure.rationale: All access requires Azure AD authentication with MSAL on device; no unauthenticated endpoints

access_model.value: Azure AD with MSAL; roles: field_officer, assessment_manager, admin; field officers access only their assigned assessments; managers have cross-officer read access
access_model.rationale: Role-based access limits field officers to their own workqueue while enabling managerial oversight

feature_scope.value: Offline-capable assessment checklist completion, photo evidence capture and annotation, provider contact logging, risk score calculation, sync to central compliance platform, manager review dashboard, remediation action assignment
feature_scope.rationale: Full lifecycle from field data capture through manager review and remediation tracking

mvp_scope.value: Offline checklist completion with photo capture, WatermelonDB local storage, sync API, and manager review on web dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Core offline-to-sync flow must be proven before adding risk scoring and remediation automation

security_baseline.value: Azure AD MSAL authentication; WatermelonDB encrypted at rest via SQLCipher; TLS 1.3 for all sync API calls; remote wipe capability via MDM; no evidence photos stored unencrypted on device
security_baseline.rationale: Device loss requires at-rest encryption and remote wipe to protect confidential assessment data

frontend_stack.value: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript; WatermelonDB for offline-first local storage; React Navigation 6; Expo Camera for photo capture
frontend_stack.rationale: React Native provides cross-platform iOS and Android coverage; WatermelonDB is purpose-built for offline-first sync patterns

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin for the sync API; structured JSON responses; delta sync endpoints supporting partial payload uploads
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides low-latency sync endpoint handling under high concurrent device connections

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure for server-side assessment storage; WatermelonDB (SQLite via SQLCipher) on device for offline records
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL provides the relational structure needed for cross-officer manager queries; WatermelonDB handles offline-first mobile sync

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps for Go sync API; Azure Blob Storage for evidence photo uploads; distributed via TestFlight and Google Play internal track for controlled rollout
hosting_target.rationale: Container Apps scales to zero when field sync load is low; controlled distribution tracks match financial institution device management requirements

privacy_retention_policy.value: Assessment records retained 5 years per OCC third-party risk management expectations; evidence photos retained 3 years; deleted from device immediately on assessment completion upload and manager approval
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: OCC guidance and EBA outsourcing guidelines drive retention periods; device-side deletion minimizes exposure from device loss

future_scope.value: AI-assisted risk scoring from checklist responses, integration with vendor risk rating APIs (BitSight, SecurityScorecard), and push notification support for urgent remediation deadline alerts
future_scope.rationale: Automation of risk scoring and external data enrichment are natural extensions once core offline sync is proven

constraints.value: Must operate fully offline for minimum 8 hours; iOS 16+ and Android 12+ support required; evidence photos must not be stored in device camera roll; MDM enrollment required before app installation
constraints.rationale: Field facilities may have zero connectivity; camera roll access blocked for data loss prevention

observability_baseline.value: Azure Application Insights for sync API traces and errors; crash reporting via Sentry React Native SDK; custom sync metrics: upload success rate, sync duration p95, conflicting record rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Sync reliability is the core operational concern; monitoring must surface failed uploads and conflict rates immediately

execution_preference.value: 3-week sprints; mobile and API developed in parallel with a contract-first approach using OpenAPI spec to decouple teams
execution_preference.rationale: Parallel mobile and backend development requires locked API contracts to prevent blocking

compliance_context.value: OCC third-party risk management guidelines (OCC 2013-29); EBA outsourcing guidelines; SOC 2 Type II for the sync platform; device-level DLP requirements from financial institution security policy
compliance_context.rationale: High risk and confidential data sensitivity trigger mandatory compliance context for financial institution regulatory requirements

project.goal: Offline-first mobile app for on-site vendor compliance assessments at financial institutions
project.target_users: Field compliance officers (mobile), assessment managers (web dashboard)
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks; full feature set in 20 weeks

frontend.camera: Expo Camera for evidence photo capture

backend.api: RESTful sync API with delta push and pull endpoints

security.auth: Azure AD MSAL on device, JWT for API calls
security.at_rest: SQLCipher for WatermelonDB, Azure Blob SSE for photos

data.sync: Delta sync: device pushes local changes, pulls server changes, last-write-wins conflict resolution

devops.ci: GitHub Actions
devops.distribution: TestFlight and Google Play internal track

constraints.camera_roll: No photos stored in device camera roll

reasoner_reviews.product: The offline-first requirement is the defining constraint for this product. WatermelonDB is the right choice over AsyncStorage or Redux Persist for structured relational data with sync semantics - it was purpose-built for this pattern. The manager review dashboard is appropriately scoped as a web companion rather than a second mobile app, keeping the MVP scope achievable in 12 weeks.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The delta sync design needs careful specification before development begins - specifically the conflict resolution strategy for simultaneous edits (field officer submits while manager has opened the same record). Last-write-wins is acceptable for checklist fields but evidence photos should never be overwritten. The Go sync API is a good fit for high-concurrency device sync load without the memory pressure of Node.js at scale.
reasoner_reviews.security: SQLCipher encryption for the local WatermelonDB is non-negotiable given device loss risk. MDM enrollment enabling remote wipe is a hard prerequisite. Evidence photos must be stored in the app sandbox, not camera roll, and deleted from the device upon sync confirmation and manager approval. Azure AD MSAL handles token refresh transparently which is important for long offline sessions where tokens may expire before sync.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 8-hour offline constraint requires pre-loading all assigned assessment templates and checklists during the device sync window before an officer goes on-site. The app needs a pre-departure sync step that downloads pending assessments in full. iOS 16+ and Android 12+ cover approximately 85-90% of current enterprise device fleets.

backend.service_design: Single Go Gin service: SyncHandler (receives delta uploads from devices, pushes server changes to pulling devices), AssessmentHandler (CRUD for assessment templates and records), PhotoHandler (proxies encrypted uploads to Azure Blob Storage), NotificationHandler (sends assessment assignment alerts). Sync protocol: device pushes local_changes array with last_pulled_at timestamp, server returns server_changes with new pull checkpoint.
backend.api_patterns: POST /sync/push (upload device changes, returns conflicts), GET /sync/pull?since=timestamp (download server changes since checkpoint), POST /assessments/:id/photos (initiate photo upload to Azure Blob presigned URL), GET /assessments/assigned (list active assessments for authenticated officer). All endpoints require Bearer JWT from Azure AD MSAL.
backend.failure_handling: Sync push failures are retried with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts; failures persisted in WatermelonDB sync_queue table for manual retry. Photo upload failures queued separately in photo_upload_queue. Sync API returns HTTP 409 Conflict with conflicting record details for field officer to resolve in app.
backend.scaling_notes: Azure Container Apps scales based on HTTP request concurrency. Sync endpoints are idempotent allowing safe retries. PostgreSQL connection pool sized for 200 concurrent sync connections covering expected field force size.

frontend.app_structure: React Native app with four main stacks: Auth (MSAL login), Assessments (list, detail, offline checklist), Evidence (photo capture, annotation, gallery), Sync (sync status, conflict resolution). WatermelonDB models: Assessment, ChecklistItem, EvidencePhoto, SyncQueueEntry.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home screen shows assigned assessments with sync status badges. Assessment detail loads checklist from local WatermelonDB and allows offline completion. Evidence screen uses Expo Camera to capture photos saved to app sandbox with encrypted thumbnail in WatermelonDB. Sync screen shows upload progress, last sync time, and pending conflict resolution items.
frontend.state_management: WatermelonDB observables drive UI reactivity - no separate state library needed for most screens. Zustand for transient UI state: active upload progress, camera session state, network status banner.
frontend.accessibility_notes: High-contrast mode support for outdoor use in varying lighting conditions. Large tap targets for checklist completion while wearing gloves during facility inspections.

security.auth_design: Azure AD MSAL React Native SDK; silent token refresh during app foreground; on token expiry during offline session, app prompts re-authentication before next sync attempt. Device enrollment check on app launch: if MDM compliance policy violated, app displays locked screen.
security.authorization_model: JWT claims include user_id, role, and assigned_assessment_ids. Go Gin middleware validates JWT and enforces: field_officer role can only push/pull their assigned_assessment_ids; assessment_manager role can pull any assessment. No cross-officer data access at the API layer.
security.secrets_management: SQLCipher key derived from device hardware attestation combined with a server-side per-device secret stored in Azure Key Vault. Key rotation triggered on MDM device compliance re-enrollment.
security.security_risks: Risk: A compromised device before remote wipe can expose local WatermelonDB data. Mitigation: SQLCipher encryption plus automatic local data purge triggered if device reports MDM non-compliance on next online check-in.

data.entities: assessments (id, vendor_id, template_id, assigned_officer_id, status ENUM pending/in_progress/submitted/approved, site_address, scheduled_date, submitted_at, approved_at), inspection_checklists (id, assessment_id, section, question_text, response_type ENUM yes_no/text/numeric/photo_required, response_value, finding_flag, updated_at), findings (id, assessment_id, checklist_item_id, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, description, remediation_required, created_at), evidence_photos (id, assessment_id, checklist_item_id, blob_storage_key, caption, captured_at, uploaded_at), remediation_actions (id, finding_id, assigned_to, due_date, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed, closed_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL (Flexible Server) for server-side records. WatermelonDB (SQLite with SQLCipher AES-256) on device for offline-capable tables mirroring assessments, inspection_checklists, findings, and evidence_photo metadata. Photos stored in Azure Blob Storage with per-blob server-side encryption.
data.retention_and_deletion: Assessment records: 5 years on server. Evidence photos: 3 years on Azure Blob then moved to Cool tier for cost reduction. Device-side data: deleted upon sync confirmation and manager approval of assessment to minimize device exposure window.
data.data_risks: Risk: WatermelonDB schema migrations on app update may fail if device has unsync'd data. Mitigation: migration scripts tested against all pending WatermelonDB states; migration failures halt sync and prompt device sync before update.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps service for Go sync API with min replicas 1, max replicas 20, scaling on concurrent HTTP requests. Azure Blob Storage container for evidence photos. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (General Purpose, Zone Redundant). GitHub Actions builds React Native app binaries for iOS (Xcode Cloud) and Android (Bitrise).
devops.ci_cd_design: PR: Go unit tests with testify, race detector enabled; React Native Jest tests for WatermelonDB models and sync logic. Merge to main: Go Docker build and push to Azure Container Registry, Container Apps deploy. Mobile: TestFlight submission for iOS and Google Play internal track for Android on merge to main with semver tag.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Application Insights for Go sync API: request traces, dependency traces, and exception logging. Sentry React Native SDK for mobile crashes and ANRs. Custom metrics emitted from sync API to Application Insights: sync_push_duration_ms, sync_conflict_count, photo_upload_success_rate.
devops.rollback_strategy: Azure Container Apps traffic splitting enables canary deploys: 10% traffic to new revision, validate sync success metrics over 30 minutes, then promote to 100%. Mobile: phased rollout via TestFlight groups and Google Play staged rollout percentage.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an offline-first vendor assessment mobile app for financial institution field compliance officers. Key decisions: WatermelonDB with SQLCipher for offline-first local storage with at-rest encryption; Go Gin sync API for high-concurrency delta sync; Azure AD MSAL for transparent token refresh during long offline sessions; evidence photos stored in app sandbox (not camera roll) and deleted post-sync to minimize device exposure window. Device loss risk drives the SQLCipher plus MDM remote wipe security baseline.
target_output.title: Offline-First Vendor Assessment Mobile App Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A React Native 0.74 mobile application enabling financial institution field compliance officers to complete vendor inspection checklists and capture evidence photos fully offline for up to 8 hours, then sync findings to a central PostgreSQL platform via a Go Gin delta sync API on reconnection. WatermelonDB with SQLCipher AES-256 provides encrypted offline storage on iOS and Android devices. Azure AD MSAL handles authentication with transparent token refresh. OCC and EBA compliance controls require device-side data deletion post-sync and MDM enrollment as a prerequisite for app installation.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native app with WatermelonDB (SQLite via SQLCipher AES-256) stores assessment templates, checklist responses, finding records, and evidence photo metadata entirely on device during field assessment. Photos are stored in the app sandbox only - never in the device camera roll. On connectivity restoration, the app executes a two-phase sync: push phase sends local changes to the Go Gin sync API via POST /sync/push, pull phase retrieves server changes since the last pull checkpoint via GET /sync/pull. The Go Gin service on Azure Container Apps persists records to PostgreSQL 16 and proxies photo uploads to Azure Blob Storage. Assessment managers review findings and assign remediation actions via a separate web dashboard consuming the same API. Azure AD MSAL provides SSO across mobile and web with role-based access enforced at the API layer via JWT claims.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.74, TypeScript, WatermelonDB 0.28 with SQLCipher AES-256, React Navigation 6, Expo Camera, Azure MSAL React Native SDK. Sync API: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, testify for unit tests. Server database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server, Zone Redundant. Photo storage: Azure Blob Storage with SSE. Auth: Azure AD MSAL with JWT RS256. Infrastructure: Azure Container Apps, Azure Container Registry, GitHub Actions, Xcode Cloud for iOS, Bitrise for Android, Sentry React Native SDK, Azure Application Insights.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Offline Assessment Completion: field officer opens assigned assessment from local WatermelonDB cache, works through structured checklist sections with yes/no, text, numeric, and photo-required response types, with all responses persisted locally without network dependency. Evidence Photo Capture: Expo Camera captures photos saved to app sandbox with encrypted thumbnails in WatermelonDB; officer annotates photos with notes and associates them to specific checklist items. Finding Logging: officer marks checklist items as findings with severity (critical/high/medium/low) and description; findings persist locally until sync. Sync Engine: two-phase push-pull with conflict detection; 409 responses surface conflicts for officer resolution before retry. Manager Review Dashboard: web view of submitted assessments, findings by severity, assignment of remediation actions with due dates, and assessment approval workflow.
target_output.system_components: React Native mobile app (iOS 16+ and Android 12+): WatermelonDB local database, MSAL token manager, sync engine, camera module. Go Gin Sync API on Azure Container Apps: SyncHandler, AssessmentHandler, PhotoHandler with Azure Blob presigned URL generation. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16: assessments, inspection_checklists, findings, evidence_photos, remediation_actions tables. Azure Blob Storage: encrypted evidence photo store with SSE. Azure AD: MSAL identity provider with MDM conditional access policy. Azure Application Insights: sync API telemetry and custom sync metrics. Sentry: mobile crash reporting.
target_output.workflows: Pre-Departure Sync Workflow: field officer opens app with connectivity, taps Sync Now; app pulls all assessments assigned to officer from server into WatermelonDB including full checklist templates; app confirms download complete and officer status changes to Ready for Field. On-Site Assessment Workflow: officer selects assessment from local list (no network required), navigates checklist sections, completes responses, captures photos via Expo Camera into app sandbox, marks non-conformant items as findings with severity ratings; all writes go to WatermelonDB only. Post-Site Sync Workflow: on connectivity restoration app auto-detects network, queues all local changes, executes push phase to sync API; server processes and returns any conflicts; officer resolves conflicts if present; pull phase retrieves any server-side updates (e.g., manager comments); sync completion triggers device-side deletion of uploaded evidence photos pending manager approval.
target_output.data_model: assessments (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, template_id FK, assigned_officer_id FK users, status ENUM pending/in_progress/submitted/approved, site_address, scheduled_date, submitted_at, approved_at). inspection_checklists (id UUID PK, assessment_id FK, section VARCHAR, question_text TEXT, response_type ENUM yes_no/text/numeric/photo_required, response_value TEXT, finding_flag BOOLEAN, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). findings (id UUID PK, assessment_id FK, checklist_item_id FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, description TEXT, remediation_required BOOLEAN, created_at). evidence_photos (id UUID PK, assessment_id FK, checklist_item_id FK, blob_storage_key VARCHAR, caption TEXT, captured_at TIMESTAMPTZ, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE). remediation_actions (id UUID PK, finding_id FK, assigned_to FK users, due_date DATE, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed, closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE).
target_output.api_design: POST /sync/push (body: {local_changes: [...], last_pulled_at: timestamp}; returns: {conflicts: [...], server_timestamp}). GET /sync/pull?since=ISO8601_timestamp (returns: {server_changes: [...], checkpoint: timestamp}). GET /assessments/assigned (returns list of assessments assigned to authenticated officer with template data). POST /assessments/:id/photos/upload-url (returns Azure Blob presigned PUT URL with 15-minute TTL for direct client-to-blob upload). GET /assessments/:id/findings (manager endpoint; returns findings with severity distribution). PATCH /findings/:id/remediation (assign remediation action, manager role required). All endpoints: Authorization: Bearer JWT from Azure AD MSAL, 401 on missing/expired, 403 on role violation.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher AES-256 encrypts WatermelonDB on device; encryption key derived from Azure AD device hardware attestation token combined with per-device secret in Azure Key Vault. MDM conditional access policy enforced at Azure AD level: non-compliant devices cannot acquire MSAL tokens. Evidence photos stored in app sandbox only (Info.plist NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription denied). MSAL silent token refresh handles expiry during offline sessions; on expiry in offline mode app buffers sync queue and prompts auth on next network connection. TLS 1.3 for all sync API calls. Azure Blob SSE for photo storage. OCC 2013-29 compliance: assessment records and risk ratings retained 5 years with immutable server-side storage policy. EBA outsourcing: assessment cycle records include vendor risk tier, assessment date, and officer identity for regulator examination. SOC 2 Type II: sync API access logs and assessment change history maintained in PostgreSQL audit columns.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps for Go sync API: min 1 replica, max 20 replicas scaling on concurrent HTTPS connections. Deployment via GitHub Actions: Go unit tests with race detector, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, Container Apps revision deployment with 10% canary traffic split validated over 30 minutes before full promotion. iOS build: Xcode Cloud triggered on semver tag, TestFlight distribution to internal compliance officer group. Android build: Bitrise triggered on semver tag, Google Play internal track distribution with staged rollout (10% to 50% to 100% over 72 hours). PostgreSQL: Zone Redundant Flexible Server with automated backups retained 35 days. Secrets: Azure Key Vault with Container Apps managed identity; per-device SQLCipher keys rotated on MDM re-enrollment.
target_output.observability: Azure Application Insights for sync API: request traces with officer_id and assessment_id as custom dimensions, dependency traces to PostgreSQL and Azure Blob. Custom metrics: sync_push_duration_ms histogram, sync_conflict_count counter, photo_upload_success_rate gauge. Sentry React Native SDK: captures unhandled exceptions with WatermelonDB operation context. PagerDuty escalation: sync API error rate above 5% for 10 minutes, PostgreSQL connection wait time above 2 seconds, sync push duration p95 above 30 seconds.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure Container Apps scales to 1 replica during off-hours (field teams on-site 6am-6pm local time), reducing compute cost by approximately 50% versus constant scaling. PostgreSQL Flexible Server with Burstable tier for staging, General Purpose for production. Azure Blob Cool tier lifecycle rule for photos older than 3 years. Estimated cost at 100 field officers with daily sync: approximately $800/month (Container Apps $200, PostgreSQL $300, Blob Storage $100, Application Insights $100, Sentry $100).
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (weeks 1-12): WatermelonDB offline assessment completion with SQLCipher, Expo Camera photo capture to app sandbox, Go Gin delta sync API, Azure AD MSAL auth, PostgreSQL server storage, basic manager review web page. Phase 2 - Risk Scoring and Remediation (weeks 13-20): automated risk score calculation from finding severity distribution, remediation action assignment and tracking, push notification for overdue remediations via Azure Notification Hubs, conflict resolution UI for sync 409 responses. Phase 3 - Automation and Integration (post-MVP): BitSight and SecurityScorecard API integration for vendor risk enrichment, AI-assisted finding classification from checklist response patterns, assessment cycle dashboard with trend analysis.
target_output.development_guidelines: WatermelonDB model changes require migration scripts tested against all in-flight WatermelonDB states before release. Go sync endpoints must be idempotent - same push payload processed twice must produce same server state. OpenAPI 3.1 spec for sync API committed alongside Go code; spec changes require review by mobile team before merge. React Native: no network calls outside the sync engine - all field UI reads from WatermelonDB only. Jest tests cover WatermelonDB model operations and sync engine conflict resolution logic. Go unit tests cover SyncHandler delta merge logic with race detector enabled.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB schema migration failure on device with unsynced data could cause data loss. Mitigation: migrations tested against all possible WatermelonDB states; app blocks upgrade and prompts sync if unsync'd records detected before migration runs. Risk: MSAL token expiry during an 8-hour offline session means sync attempt after reconnection requires re-authentication before upload. Mitigation: app displays prominent re-auth prompt on sync initiation if token is expired; sync queue is preserved so no data is lost. Tradeoff: SQLCipher adds approximately 15-20% overhead to WatermelonDB read/write operations versus unencrypted SQLite - acceptable given confidential data classification and device loss risk.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected WatermelonDB over Realm for local database due to open-source licensing and better React Native community support. Chose last-write-wins conflict resolution for checklist items (rare simultaneous edit scenario) but never overwrite evidence photos - photos from both client and server are retained with officer prompted to mark canonical version. Confirmed Azure Blob over S3 to keep all data on Azure matching existing institution cloud agreement.

metadata.notes: Offline-first vendor assessment mobile app with WatermelonDB SQLCipher local storage, Go delta sync API, Azure AD MSAL, and OCC/EBA compliance controls.

profile.domain: Legal Operations — Outside Counsel Spend Management

project_goal.value: Web application for in-house legal departments to manage outside counsel spend against pre-approved budgets, automate billing guideline enforcement on submitted invoices, and produce cost variance reports, replacing expensive third-party e-billing vendor subscriptions
project_goal.rationale: Third-party e-billing SaaS costs $120k/year; owned solution amortises within 18 months

target_users.value: In-house legal operations staff, billing analysts, GC and deputy GCs approving invoices, outside counsel submitting invoices
target_users.rationale: All stakeholders in the invoice submission, review, and payment approval lifecycle

project_class.rationale: Browser-based application; no mobile-native requirement

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Full-featured application including analytics dashboards, admin billing guideline configuration, and LEDES file integration

complexity_level.rationale: LEDES invoice parsing, per-line-item billing guideline rule engine, and multi-level approval routing require significant domain logic

risk_level.rationale: Invoice and budget data is sensitive financial information; payment approval errors carry direct monetary risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Matter budgets, invoice amounts, timekeeper billing rates, and payment approvals are confidential financial data

external_exposure.rationale: External counsel must authenticate to submit invoices; no public endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: outside_counsel_billing (invoice submission), billing_analyst (review and flag), legal_ops_manager (budget management), gc_approver (final payment approval), admin; Google Workspace SSO for internal users
access_model.rationale: Invoice approval requires distinct role separation; outside counsel access scoped to their own matters only

feature_scope.value: LEDES invoice import and parsing, per-line-item billing guideline rule evaluation, invoice review and approval workflow, matter budget tracking with variance alerts, outside counsel rate card management, spend reporting and cost variance dashboards, LEDES export for ERP integration
feature_scope.rationale: Complete e-billing lifecycle from invoice submission through payment approval and reporting

mvp_scope.value: LEDES 1998B invoice import, 10 configurable billing guideline rules, two-level approval workflow (billing analyst then GC), matter budget vs actuals dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Core value for cost control with minimum viable rule set

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, AES-256 at rest via Cloud SQL encryption, Google Workspace SSO with MFA, invoice data scoped by matter access ACL, no financial figures in application logs
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires encryption and strict access scoping

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for invoice list pages improves load performance; team expertise in Next.js

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Fastify
backend_stack.rationale: Fast JSON processing for LEDES invoice parsing; Fastify plugin architecture for modular rule engine

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Complex relational joins across matters, invoices, line items, and guideline rules; JSONB for flexible rule condition storage

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers scale to zero between billing cycles; legal team already uses Google Workspace

privacy_retention_policy.value: Invoice and budget records retained 7 years for SOX audit purposes; timekeeper rate cards retained for matter lifetime
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX Section 302 requires financial record retention; rate cards needed for dispute resolution

future_scope.value: AI-assisted budget forecasting based on historical spend patterns, direct ERP payment integration for approved invoices, mobile approval notifications
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 automation after core workflow is validated

constraints.value: Must parse LEDES 1998B and LEDES XML formats; GCP us-central1 data residency; budget under $1,200/month infrastructure
constraints.rationale: Existing LEDES standard used by all outside counsel; GCP residency aligns with Google Workspace tenant

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring, structured JSON logging, Cloud Trace for request tracing, billing rule evaluation metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Native GCP observability stack; billing rule hit rates critical for guideline tuning

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; outside counsel invoice submission portal shipped before internal workflow features
execution_preference.rationale: Outside counsel portal unblocks invoice submission which is the critical path

compliance_context.value: SOX Section 302/404 financial controls for public company legal department; GDPR for EU matter data involving EU outside counsel; invoice data subject to outside counsel privilege claims
compliance_context.rationale: Legal department of a public company; invoices reflect strategic legal spend requiring SOX controls

project.goal: Replace third-party e-billing SaaS with owned invoice management and spend analytics platform
project.target_users: In-house legal ops, billing analysts, GC, outside counsel
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks

frontend.state: React Query for invoice data; Zustand for approval step state
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with Radix UI components

backend.framework: Fastify with TypeBox for schema validation
backend.api: RESTful JSON API with OpenAPI 3.1 spec

security.auth: Google Workspace SSO via OAuth2 for internal users; magic-link email for outside counsel
security.matter_acl: Row-level security in PostgreSQL scoping outside_counsel to their firm's matters only
security.financial_logging: Invoice amounts excluded from structured log fields

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 with JSONB for billing guideline rule conditions

devops.ci: Cloud Build
devops.hosting: Cloud Run with min-instances=1 to avoid cold start on approval workflows

open_questions.ledes_xml_variants: Some outside counsel submit LEDES eBilling XML 2.0; confirm parser handles both sub-variants

reasoner_reviews.product: Replacing a $120k/year SaaS with an owned platform is compelling if scope is disciplined. LEDES invoice parsing and a configurable billing guideline rule engine are the core differentiators. The outside counsel portal must prioritise simplicity since external users will have no training. Spend dashboards provide the ROI justification for the legal ops team to champion the project internally.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Fastify with TypeBox is a strong choice for LEDES parsing throughput — LEDES 1998B files can be multi-megabyte for large matters. The billing guideline rule engine should evaluate rules as a pipeline of typed condition checks stored as JSONB in PostgreSQL, enabling admin configuration without code deploys. Cloud Run min-instances=1 eliminates cold start latency on approval notification responses. PostgreSQL row-level security is the right enforcement layer for outside counsel matter scoping.
reasoner_reviews.security: Matter-scoped row-level security for outside_counsel role is critical — a firm submitting an invoice must not be able to enumerate other firms' matters. SOX controls require that payment approval events are immutable and include approver identity and timestamp. Financial amounts must never appear in structured logs or error messages.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: LEDES 1998B is tab-delimited with a fixed header row; the parser must handle BOM characters and inconsistent line endings from different billing systems. Budget of $1,200/month is achievable on Cloud Run with min-instances=1; Cloud SQL postgres at db-g1-small plus Cloud Run comfortably fits in budget at typical legal department scale.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The billing guideline rule engine risk is that 10 configurable rules are sufficient for MVP but legal ops teams typically need 40-60 rules to replace a mature SaaS. The JSONB condition schema must be extensible from day one or the admin UI will be rebuilt in phase 2.

backend.service_design: Fastify application with domain plugins: invoices (LEDES parsing, line item storage), guidelines (rule engine evaluation pipeline), approvals (workflow state machine), matters (budget tracking), reporting (aggregation queries), webhooks (LEDES export triggers). Each plugin registered with Fastify plugin system and dependency-injected with Prisma client.
backend.api_patterns: OpenAPI 3.1 spec generated from TypeBox schemas. POST /invoices/upload (multipart LEDES file), GET /invoices?status=pending_review (analyst queue), POST /invoices/{id}/approve (approval action with justification), GET /matters/{id}/budget (budget vs actuals), GET /reporting/spend-summary (parameterised by date range, practice area, firm).
backend.business_modules: LedesParser: handles 1998B tab-delimited and XML 2.0 variants, normalises to canonical LineItem schema. GuidelineEngine: evaluates ordered rule pipeline against each LineItem, returns violations with rule_id and description. ApprovalStateMachine: enforces transitions draft→submitted→under_review→approved/rejected; immutable approval_events log.
backend.failure_handling: LEDES parse errors return structured error response with line number and field name. GuidelineEngine rule evaluation errors do not block invoice submission — violations are flagged not blocking unless rule.blocking=true. Cloud Run min-instances=1 prevents cold start on time-sensitive approval notifications.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with server components for invoice list pages and client components for interactive approval forms. Route groups: (portal) for outside counsel submission, (internal) for legal ops and analyst workflows.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Outside Counsel Portal: /portal/invoices/upload (LEDES drag-and-drop), /portal/invoices (submission history with status). Internal: /invoices (analyst review queue with guideline violation flags), /invoices/{id}/review (line-item detail with rule violations), /matters (budget dashboard), /reporting/spend (variance charts).
frontend.performance_notes: Server-side rendering for invoice list pages avoids large client-side data fetches. Invoice detail page uses React Query with stale-while-revalidate for guideline violation data. Recharts for spend variance charts with lazy loading.

security.auth_design: Internal users: Google Workspace SSO OAuth2 via NextAuth.js. External outside counsel: magic-link email sent to firm billing contact via SendGrid; session scoped to 8 hours.
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL row-level security policy on matters and invoices tables: outside_counsel role restricted to rows where law_firm_id matches session claim. Internal roles (billing_analyst, legal_ops_manager, gc_approver) access all matters. Admin role manages billing guidelines and rate cards.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable approval_events table: INSERT only via application service; PostgreSQL trigger rejects UPDATE and DELETE. Invoice amounts and timekeeper rates excluded from all structured log fields via Fastify serializer mask.

data.entities: matters (id UUID PK, matter_number VARCHAR, client_name VARCHAR, practice_area VARCHAR, lead_attorney_id FK, budget_amount DECIMAL, budget_currency VARCHAR, law_firm_id FK, status ENUM active/closed). invoices (id UUID PK, matter_id FK, law_firm_id FK, invoice_number VARCHAR, invoice_date DATE, total_amount DECIMAL, currency VARCHAR, ledes_format ENUM ledes1998b/xml20, upload_status ENUM processing/processed/failed, approval_status ENUM submitted/under_review/approved/rejected). line_items (id UUID PK, invoice_id FK, task_code VARCHAR, activity_code VARCHAR, expense_code VARCHAR, timekeeper_id FK, hours DECIMAL nullable, rate DECIMAL nullable, amount DECIMAL, narrative TEXT). billing_guidelines (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, condition_jsonb JSONB, violation_message TEXT, blocking BOOLEAN, active BOOLEAN, created_by_id FK). guideline_violations (id UUID PK, line_item_id FK, guideline_id FK, evaluated_at TIMESTAMP). approval_events (id UUID PK, invoice_id FK, actor_id FK, actor_role VARCHAR, decision ENUM approved/rejected/returned_for_revision, justification TEXT, decided_at TIMESTAMP).
data.schema_notes: JSONB condition schema: {field: 'task_code', operator: 'in', values: ['L110','L120']} or {field: 'hours_per_day', operator: 'gt', value: 8}. Index on invoices.approval_status for analyst queue queries. Partial index on line_items.invoice_id where guideline_violations count > 0.
data.retention_and_deletion: Invoices and approval_events retained 7 years per SOX requirements via pg_partman range partitioning by invoice_date. Rate cards retained indefinitely for dispute resolution.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service (Next.js frontend + Fastify API as separate services). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 in same VPC via private IP. Cloud Storage for LEDES file uploads (pre-signed URLs). Secret Manager for DB password and SendGrid API key.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build triggers on PR (lint, typecheck, vitest, Prisma migration validation) and on merge to main (Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy). Prisma migrations applied via Cloud Run job pre-deploy.
devops.cost_controls: Cloud Run min-instances=1 for API, max-instances=10. Cloud SQL db-g1-small with automated storage increase. Budget alert at $1,000/month.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a legal e-billing platform replacing third-party SaaS. Core architectural decisions: Fastify plugin system for modular LEDES parsing (1998B tab-delimited and XML 2.0) and a JSONB-stored billing guideline rule engine enabling admin configuration without deploys. PostgreSQL row-level security is the correct enforcement layer for matter-scoped outside counsel access. Cloud Run min-instances=1 avoids cold start on approval notification endpoints. The billing guideline engine JSONB condition schema must be extensible from day one given that legal ops teams typically expand rule sets significantly post-MVP.
target_output.title: Outside Counsel Spend Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: Next.js 14 and Fastify web application replacing third-party e-billing SaaS for in-house legal departments. Handles LEDES 1998B and XML invoice import and parsing, automated billing guideline rule evaluation against each line item, multi-level invoice approval workflows, matter budget tracking with variance alerts, and spend analytics dashboards. Outside counsel authenticate via magic-link portal scoped to their firm's matters only via PostgreSQL row-level security.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-service architecture on GCP Cloud Run: Next.js 14 frontend service and Fastify API service, both containerised and deployed independently. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 via private VPC connection enforces matter-scoped row-level security for outside counsel access and stores billing guideline rules as JSONB for admin-configurable conditions. Cloud Storage holds LEDES file uploads accessible via pre-signed URLs. Internal users authenticate via Google Workspace SSO; outside counsel authenticate via SendGrid magic-link email with 8-hour session scoping. Cloud Build CI/CD pipeline with Prisma migration job pre-deploy. Google Cloud Monitoring collects billing rule evaluation metrics and invoice approval throughput.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, React Query, Zustand, Recharts for spend visualisation. Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify with TypeBox schema validation, Prisma ORM, OpenAPI 3.1 spec generation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with JSONB for billing guideline condition storage. Auth: NextAuth.js for Google Workspace SSO; custom magic-link flow via SendGrid for outside counsel. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run (two services), Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Secret Manager, Artifact Registry, Cloud Build.
target_output.functional_feature_map: LEDES Invoice Submission: outside counsel uploads LEDES 1998B or XML 2.0 file via portal drag-and-drop; LedesParser normalises to canonical LineItem schema. Billing Guideline Evaluation: GuidelineEngine evaluates each line item against active guidelines in JSONB-condition pipeline; blocking violations halt approval workflow; non-blocking violations flagged for analyst review. Invoice Approval Workflow: billing analyst reviews violation-flagged line items, forwards to GC approver for payment sign-off with mandatory justification. Matter Budget Tracking: real-time budget vs actuals per matter with percentage burn rate and variance threshold alerts. Spend Reporting: spend by practice area, outside counsel firm, timekeeper, and month with CSV export. Admin Panel: legal ops admin configures billing guideline conditions, rate cards, and approval routing without code deploy.
target_output.system_components: Next.js frontend Cloud Run service (App Router, server components for invoice lists, client components for approval forms). Fastify API Cloud Run service (min-instances=1; plugins: invoices, guidelines, approvals, matters, reporting, webhooks). LedesParser module (1998B tab-delimited with BOM/CRLF handling, XML 2.0 namespace-aware parser). GuidelineEngine (ordered rule pipeline from JSONB conditions; blocking vs warning classification). ApprovalStateMachine (draft→submitted→under_review→approved/rejected with immutable approval_events log). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (RLS policies for outside_counsel role). Cloud Storage bucket (LEDES uploads, pre-signed PUT URLs from API). Secret Manager (Cloud SQL password, SendGrid API key, NextAuth secret).
target_output.workflows: Invoice Submission and Evaluation: outside counsel authenticates via magic-link, uploads LEDES file to /portal/invoices/upload; frontend requests pre-signed Cloud Storage PUT URL from API; file uploaded directly to Cloud Storage; API background job triggered via Cloud Tasks downloads file, runs LedesParser, stores line_items, runs GuidelineEngine against each item, sets invoice status to under_review if violations exist or approved if no violations and below budget. Invoice Review and Approval: billing analyst opens invoice review page showing line items with guideline violation annotations; analyst annotates and forwards; GC approver reviews analyst notes, enters justification, approves or rejects; approval_events INSERT triggers immutable record. Budget Variance Alert: nightly Cloud Scheduler job computes matter budget burn rates; matters exceeding 80% of budget trigger email alert to legal_ops_manager and matter lead attorney.
target_output.data_model: matters (id UUID PK, matter_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, client_name VARCHAR, practice_area VARCHAR, lead_attorney_id FK, budget_amount DECIMAL 18,2, budget_currency CHAR 3, law_firm_id FK, status ENUM active/closed, created_at TIMESTAMP). invoices (id UUID PK, matter_id FK, law_firm_id FK, invoice_number VARCHAR, invoice_date DATE, total_amount DECIMAL 18,2, currency CHAR 3, ledes_format ENUM ledes1998b/xml20, upload_status ENUM processing/processed/failed, approval_status ENUM submitted/under_review/approved/rejected, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, approved_at TIMESTAMP). line_items (id UUID PK, invoice_id FK, task_code VARCHAR, activity_code VARCHAR, expense_code VARCHAR, timekeeper_id FK, hours DECIMAL 8,2, rate DECIMAL 10,2, amount DECIMAL 18,2, narrative TEXT, has_violation BOOLEAN). billing_guidelines (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, condition_jsonb JSONB, violation_message TEXT, blocking BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, sort_order INT, active BOOLEAN, created_by_id FK). guideline_violations (id UUID PK, line_item_id FK, guideline_id FK, violation_message TEXT, evaluated_at TIMESTAMP). approval_events (id UUID PK, invoice_id FK, actor_id FK, actor_role VARCHAR, decision ENUM approved/rejected/returned, justification TEXT NOT NULL, decided_at TIMESTAMP) — trigger-protected immutable. law_firms (id UUID PK, firm_name VARCHAR, billing_contact_email VARCHAR, matter_access_scope JSONB).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/invoices/upload-url (returns pre-signed Cloud Storage PUT URL and invoice_id). POST /api/v1/invoices/{id}/process (triggered by Cloud Tasks after upload). GET /api/v1/invoices?approval_status=under_review (analyst queue). GET /api/v1/invoices/{id}/line-items (with guideline_violations nested). POST /api/v1/invoices/{id}/decision (body: decision enum, justification). GET /api/v1/matters/{id}/budget (budget_amount, actuals_to_date, burn_rate_pct). GET /api/v1/reporting/spend?from=&to=&group_by=practice_area|firm|timekeeper (aggregated spend). GET /api/v1/admin/guidelines (paginated rule list). POST /api/v1/admin/guidelines (create or update rule with JSONB condition). All endpoints validated against TypeBox schemas; OpenAPI 3.1 spec served at /api/docs.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Google Workspace SSO via NextAuth.js for internal users; magic-link email via SendGrid for outside counsel with 8-hour expiry. PostgreSQL row-level security: outside_counsel role restricted to matters and invoices rows where law_firm_id = current_setting('app.law_firm_id'). RBAC enforced in Fastify request hooks: billing_analyst (review queue), legal_ops_manager (budgets and guidelines), gc_approver (final approval), admin (guideline and rate card management). Immutable approval_events: PostgreSQL trigger raises exception on UPDATE or DELETE. Financial amounts (invoice total, line item rates) excluded from Fastify JSON serialiser log fields via redaction list. TLS 1.3 via Google-managed certificate on Cloud Run. SOX compliance: approval_events records include actor identity, role, timestamp, and mandatory justification for every payment decision.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run: two services (frontend and API) with independent scaling. API min-instances=1, max-instances=10 based on concurrent invoice processing demand. Frontend min-instances=0, max-instances=5. Cloud Build CI pipeline: ESLint, TypeScript tsc --noEmit, Vitest unit tests with 80% coverage gate, Prisma migration validate, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy. Pre-deploy Cloud Run job applies Prisma migrations against Cloud SQL. Cloud SQL db-g1-small with automated storage growth in us-central1. Secret Manager for all credentials; Cloud Run service accounts with least-privilege IAM roles. Budget alert at $1,000/month via Cloud Billing budget.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: invoice.parse_duration_ms (p50/p95 by ledes_format), guideline.violations_per_invoice (histogram), approval.time_to_decision_hours, budget.burn_rate_pct (per matter). Cloud Trace distributed tracing across frontend and API services. Structured JSON logs with invoice_id, matter_id, actor_role — invoice amounts and timekeeper rates excluded from all log fields. Alert policies: API error rate above 1%, invoice parse failures above 5% of daily submissions, approval queue depth above 30 invoices pending for more than 48 hours.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $780/month: Cloud Run two services (min-instances), Cloud SQL db-g1-small, Cloud Storage, Cloud Build, Secret Manager, SendGrid transactional plan. Scales to 500 invoice submissions per month without instance increase. Cloud Run autoscaling handles billing cycle peaks (typical 3x volume in month-end week). Cloud SQL upgrade to db-g1-medium if reporting query latency exceeds 3 seconds at 50k line items.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (7 weeks): LEDES 1998B parser, 10 configurable billing guidelines with JSONB conditions, two-level approval workflow with immutable approval_events, matter budget vs actuals dashboard, outside counsel magic-link portal, Google Workspace SSO for internal users. Phase 2 (4 weeks): LEDES XML 2.0 parser, spend reporting dashboards with Recharts visualisation, rate card management, CSV and LEDES export for ERP. Phase 3 (3 weeks): budget variance nightly alerts, admin guideline builder UI for non-technical legal ops staff, expanded rule engine operators (date-based, cumulative spend).
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode end-to-end. ESLint with @typescript-eslint/recommended. Vitest for unit tests with 80% coverage gate on parser and guideline engine modules. Playwright E2E for invoice submission, guideline violation display, and approval decision flows. Fastify TypeBox schema validation required for all request bodies; no unvalidated inputs reach business logic. LEDES parser must handle BOM prefix, CRLF and LF line endings, and quoted fields with embedded tabs. No financial amounts in log statements — enforced by ESLint custom rule banning `amount`, `rate`, `total` variable interpolation in logger calls.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: LEDES format variants from different billing systems diverge from the published standard; mitigated by collecting sample files from top 5 outside counsel firms before parser development and building a test fixture library. Risk: JSONB billing guideline conditions are powerful but complex to debug when mis-configured by non-technical admins; mitigated by a rule dry-run mode that evaluates a condition against sample line items and returns match explanation. Tradeoff: JSONB conditions over hard-coded rule types enable admin configurability but require a custom condition evaluator implementation and careful schema validation. Tradeoff: Cloud Run min-instances=1 for API adds $30/month baseline cost but eliminates cold start latency on approval notification webhooks which are time-sensitive.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: LEDES XML 2.0 sub-variants confirmed: eBillingXML 2.0 and LEDES eBillingXML 2.1 both handled by XML 2.0 parser using namespace detection. Magic-link session duration set to 8 hours to match outside counsel billing workflow. SOX immutability implemented as PostgreSQL trigger rather than application-only for audit defensibility.

metadata.notes: Legal ops e-billing platform replacing third-party SaaS; LEDES parser, JSONB billing guideline rule engine, PostgreSQL RLS for outside counsel matter scoping.

profile.domain: Fintech Identity Verification API

project_goal.value: Serve real-time KYC and KYB verification results to fintech onboarding flows by aggregating identity checks from Jumio, Onfido, and Experian with sub-200ms median latency under peak load
project_goal.rationale: Core SLA requirement for fintech partner onboarding conversion rates

target_users.value: Fintech onboarding engineers integrating identity verification into customer and business onboarding APIs via partner-facing REST endpoints
target_users.rationale: Primary API consumers are fintech partner engineering teams

project_class.rationale: Pure API service with no frontend; exposes verification endpoints to partner clients

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: API service with external provider integrations, auth, persistent verification records, and analytics for partner SLA reporting

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-200ms SLA under peak load with fan-out to three external providers requires high-performance async architecture

risk_level.rationale: Processes personal identity data including government ID scans and biometric match results; FinCEN and GDPR regulatory exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Identity verification handles PII including name, DOB, address, government ID numbers, and biometric match scores

external_exposure.rationale: API is consumed by fintech partner clients via mTLS and API key authentication

access_model.value: mTLS client certificates for partner-level authentication; API key per fintech client for request authorization; RBAC for internal admin operations
access_model.rationale: mTLS enforces partner identity at TLS level; API key scopes per-client rate limits and audit trail

feature_scope.value: KYC individual verification endpoint, KYB business entity verification endpoint, result caching with TTL, async verification webhooks, partner analytics dashboard API, admin management API for provider config
feature_scope.rationale: Core verification flows plus supporting operational endpoints

mvp_scope.value: KYC individual endpoint with Jumio and Onfido provider fan-out, result caching in Redis, mTLS + API key auth, basic verification record persistence in PostgreSQL
mvp_scope.rationale: Individual identity verification is the highest volume use case for fintech onboarding

security_baseline.value: mTLS for all partner connections, API key per client, AES-256 at rest for PII fields, TLS 1.3 for provider outbound calls, no PII in logs
security_baseline.rationale: Minimum controls for personal identity data and partner-facing API

frontend_stack.value: None - API service only; no web frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: API service with no user-facing web interface

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum framework for HTTP API; Tokio async runtime for concurrent provider fan-out
backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides memory safety and predictable sub-millisecond latency needed for the 200ms SLA with async provider calls

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for verification records and partner config; Redis 7 on ElastiCache for result caching with TTL
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for durable verification audit records; Redis for low-latency cache lookups to avoid redundant provider calls

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate behind ALB with NLB for mTLS termination in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 for data residency
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate for managed container hosting; NLB enables passthrough mTLS to Axum; dual-region for GDPR EU data residency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Verification request PII retained 7 years for FinCEN audit; biometric match scores purged after 30 days; GDPR right-to-erasure within 72 hours for EU subjects
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FinCEN CDD record retention and GDPR data minimization requirements

future_scope.value: Ongoing monitoring for sanctions list changes post-verification, liveness detection integration, adverse media screening endpoint
future_scope.rationale: Continuous monitoring and expanded screening scope are Phase 2 roadmap items

constraints.value: Sub-200ms p50 latency; 99.9% availability SLA to partners; Jumio and Onfido provider calls may take up to 3 seconds so caching and async fallback are mandatory
constraints.rationale: Partner SLA commitments drive latency and availability targets

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with trace sampling, custom metrics for verification latency and provider response times, PagerDuty on p99 >500ms or error rate >0.5%
observability_baseline.rationale: Latency SLA monitoring is critical for partner trust

execution_preference.value: Iterative: ship KYC endpoint first, validate latency SLA in load test, then add KYB and additional providers
execution_preference.rationale: Latency validation must precede scaling to additional providers

compliance_context.value: FinCEN CDD Rule 31 CFR 1020.220 for individual verification; FATF digital identity guidance; GDPR Article 5 data minimization and Article 17 right to erasure for EU subject verification data
compliance_context.rationale: FinCEN CDD compliance for fintech partners and GDPR for EU individual verifications

project.goal: Sub-200ms p50 KYC/KYB verification API aggregating Jumio, Onfido, and Experian with result caching
project.target_users: Fintech partner engineering teams integrating identity verification into onboarding flows
project.timeline: Round 3 revision addressing cache normalization and circuit breaker gaps

backend.api_style: REST with JSON; mTLS + API key auth

security.auth: mTLS + API key per partner client
security.encryption: AES-256-GCM for PII at rest; TLS 1.3 for provider calls
security.secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for provider credentials

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: cargo test, cargo clippy, Docker build

confirmed_decisions.caching: Redis with SHA-256 identity hash key

reasoner_reviews.product: KYC and KYB verification are the critical path for fintech onboarding conversion. The sub-200ms SLA is non-negotiable for synchronous onboarding flows. Cache hit rate directly impacts both latency and provider cost - fixing the cache key normalization (ISS-002) is therefore both a correctness and a cost issue.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The canonical_key normalization fix for ISS-002 must be applied before the SHA-256 hash to prevent partner A's cached result being served to partner B for a similar identity input. The circuit breaker for ISS-003 should use per-provider state tracked in Redis to survive Axum process restarts and share state across ECS tasks.
reasoner_reviews.security: Cache key normalization also has security implications: without normalization, an adversary could craft a slightly different identity input to bypass a cached adverse result. The circuit breaker open state must be visible to all ECS tasks to prevent fan-out attempts to a known-failed provider from multiple instances.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 1.5s per-provider timeout with 2-of-3 consensus was correctly fixed in round 2. The remaining issues are operational: shared circuit breaker state across ECS tasks (Redis-backed) and canonical key normalization to prevent cache inconsistency.

backend.service_design: Axum 0.7 on Tokio with tower middleware stack: mTLS validation, API key extraction, rate limiting. Handler for POST /v1/kyc calls VerificationOrchestrator which fans out to ProviderClient trait implementations for Jumio, Onfido, Experian. ConsensusEngine computes composite risk_score from 2+ provider responses.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/kyc (individual verification), POST /v1/kyb (business verification), GET /v1/verifications/{id} (result retrieval), POST /v1/webhooks/register (async result delivery). All responses use application/json with correlation_id header for tracing.
backend.failure_handling: Per-provider Tokio timeout of 1.5s; if fewer than 2 providers respond within timeout, return 202 Accepted with async webhook delivery for full result. Circuit breaker per provider: open after 10 consecutive failures, 30s open window, single probe in half-open. Redis cache miss falls through to live provider call.
backend.scaling_notes: ECS auto-scaling on ALB request count target 500 req/task; Redis cluster mode for horizontal cache scaling; PostgreSQL read replica for verification record queries

security.auth_design: NLB with TCP passthrough for mTLS; Axum extractors validate client certificate CN against partner registry in PostgreSQL; API key validated against Redis partner_keys hash for low-latency auth
security.authorization_model: Partner API key scopes: kyc_submit, kyb_submit, result_read, webhook_manage. Internal admin API requires separate HMAC-signed token with admin_config scope
security.secrets_management: Provider API keys (Jumio, Onfido, Experian) stored in AWS Secrets Manager with 60-day rotation; partner mTLS CA certificate managed in ACM Private CA
security.privacy_controls: PII fields (name, dob, id_number, address) encrypted with AES-256-GCM at rest using per-tenant encryption keys; biometric_score purged by scheduled PostgreSQL job after 30 days; no PII in structured logs - only verification_id and risk_score

data.entities: verification_requests (id, partner_id, subject_type, risk_score, decision, cache_hit, created_at), identity_profiles (id, request_id, name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, id_number_encrypted, address_encrypted, eu_subject_flag), provider_responses (id, request_id, provider ENUM jumio/onfido/experian, raw_score, status, response_ms, fetched_at), risk_scores (id, request_id, composite_score, provider_scores_json, algorithm_version), adverse_media_hits (id, request_id, source, excerpt_encrypted, hit_date)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for verification_requests and provider_responses; Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster for verification result cache keyed by canonical_key(identity_fields) SHA-256 hash with 24h TTL
data.retention_and_deletion: FinCEN CDD: verification_requests retained 7 years; identity_profiles biometric fields purged after 30 days; GDPR Article 17: eu_subject_flag triggers erasure job replacing encrypted fields with tombstone marker within 72 hours of request

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 with ALB for HTTP routing and NLB for mTLS passthrough; ElastiCache and RDS in same VPC private subnets; Route53 latency-based routing between regions
