target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 CLI revision addressing two blocking issues. ISS-001: suppression validator now cross-references each annotation rule ID against the policy pack's rule index at scan initialization, with warning for unknown IDs and --strict-suppressions flag for CI enforcement. ISS-002: SARIF 2.1.0 output replaced with typed Go struct hierarchy enforcing all required fields at compile time; validated against GitHub SARIF schema fixtures in TestSARIFSchema. Both fixes are isolated to suppression and output modules without affecting parse or evaluation performance.

fix_report.action_taken: Added SuppressionValidator.ValidateAgainstPack(pack *PolicyPack) method that builds a map[string]bool of valid rule IDs from pack.Rules at scan init; emits stderr warning for each suppression with unknown rule_id; --strict-suppressions flag exits with code 3 on any unknown suppression; documented in suppression annotation syntax section
fix_report.expected_outcome: Suppressions referencing non-existent rule IDs surface as warnings (or failures in strict mode); future rule additions cannot be silently bypassed by pre-existing suppressions with matching IDs

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced hand-rolled SARIF map generation with typed Go structs (SARIFLog, SARIFRun, SARIFTool, SARIFDriver, SARIFResult, SARIFPhysicalLocation, SARIFRegion) covering all required SARIF 2.1.0 fields including runs[].tool.driver.informationUri and results[].locations[].physicalLocation.region.startLine; added TestSARIFSchema using GitHub sarif-schema-2.1.0.json to validate generated output
fix_report.expected_outcome: SARIF output passes GitHub Advanced Security ingestion; schema test catches any future regressions in required field population

target_output.title: IaC Compliance Scanner CLI - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: Round 2 revision of the Go 1.22 IaC compliance scanner CLI. Resolves suppression bypass risk (ISS-001) by validating annotation rule IDs against the loaded policy pack manifest with configurable strict mode. Fixes SARIF 2.1.0 schema compliance (ISS-002) by replacing hand-rolled output generation with typed Go structs and adding schema validation tests against GitHub Advanced Security ingestion requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Go 1.22 binary with three top-level subcommands: 'scan', 'policy', and 'config'. The scan pipeline runs in four stages: (1) FileDiscovery walks the --path directory collecting .tf and .yaml/.json CloudFormation files; (2) Parser dispatches to HCLParser (hashicorp/hcl v2) or CFNParser (encoding/json + cfn-go-template) producing a []Resource AST per file using a goroutine worker pool sized to runtime.NumCPU(); (3) RuleEngine iterates each Resource against loaded PolicyPack.Rules, invoking the rule's CheckFunc(resource Resource) -> *Finding; (4) OutputFormatter serializes findings to SARIF 2.1.0 typed structs or JSON based on --output flag. SuppressionValidator runs between stages 3 and 4, filtering findings whose resource address matches a suppression annotation after validating the annotation's rule_id against the policy pack manifest. Policy packs are embedded as YAML assets via go:embed compiled into the binary.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Go 1.22. HCL parsing: github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2 with gohcl for attribute decoding. CloudFormation parsing: encoding/json + custom CFN resource extractor. Policy definition: YAML via gopkg.in/yaml.v3, embedded with go:embed. CLI framework: github.com/spf13/cobra with github.com/spf13/viper for config file support. SARIF output: typed Go structs serialized via encoding/json. Distribution: goreleaser for multi-platform cross-compile; cosign for binary signing; syft for SBOM generation.
target_output.functional_feature_map: scan subcommand: --path (directory to scan), --policy-pack (CIS/NIST/PCI-DSS or path to custom YAML pack), --output (sarif|json|table), --output-file (optional file path), --fail-on (critical|high|medium|low), --strict-suppressions (exit non-zero on unknown suppression rule IDs), --verbose (per-rule timing), --exclude (glob patterns for file exclusion). policy subcommand: list (show embedded packs with version and rule count), validate (validate custom YAML rule file syntax). config subcommand: generate-suppression (output HCL comment for a given rule ID and resource address). Built-in policy packs: cis-aws-1.4 (88 rules), nist-800-53-rev5 (54 rules), pci-dss-4.0 (42 rules).
target_output.system_components: FileDiscovery (os.WalkDir with .tf and .yaml/.json CloudFormation file filter, glob-based exclusion, symlink guard). HCLParser (hashicorp/hcl v2 parsing terraform blocks and resource stanzas into Resource structs with file_path and start_line). CFNParser (JSON/YAML unmarshalling of CloudFormation template Resources section). RuleEngine (iterates []Resource against PolicyPack.Rules using CheckFunc interface; goroutine worker pool for parallel file parsing). SuppressionValidator (builds rule_id set from PolicyPack at init; filters findings by suppression annotations; warns on unknown rule_ids; --strict-suppressions exits code 3). SARIFFormatter (typed SARIFLog/Run/Tool/Driver/Result/PhysicalLocation/Region structs; all required SARIF 2.1.0 fields populated; encoding/json serialization). JSONFormatter (flat findings array with resource_address, rule_id, severity, message, file_path, start_line). go:embed policy packs (YAML assets compiled into binary for offline operation).
target_output.workflows: CI Pipeline Scan: CI agent runs iacscanner scan --path ./infrastructure/terraform --policy-pack cis-aws-1.4 --output sarif --output-file findings.sarif --fail-on high; scanner discovers .tf files, parses HCL via goroutine pool, evaluates each resource against 88 CIS rules, validates suppression annotations against policy pack manifest (warning on unknown rule IDs), serializes SARIF 2.1.0 output to findings.sarif, exits non-zero if any high/critical findings unsuppressed; GitHub Actions uploads findings.sarif to Advanced Security code scanning. Suppression Annotation Workflow: engineer runs iacscanner config generate-suppression --rule-id CIS-AWS-1.4-S3-1 --resource aws_s3_bucket.logs to get formatted HCL comment; adds comment above resource block with mandatory justification and ticket fields; subsequent scan validates rule ID against policy pack manifest before suppressing. Custom Policy Authoring: engineer writes custom-rules.yaml with rule_id, severity, resource_type, and check logic; runs iacscanner policy validate --file custom-rules.yaml to check syntax; merges custom pack with built-in via --policy-pack cis-aws-1.4,./custom-rules.yaml.
target_output.data_model: In-memory only. Resource struct: {Type string, Name string, FilePath string, StartLine int, Attributes map[string]interface{}}. Rule struct: {ID string, PolicyPack string, Severity string (critical/high/medium/low), Framework string (cis/nist/pci_dss), Description string, CheckFunc func(Resource) *Finding}. Finding struct: {RuleID string, ResourceType string, ResourceName string, FilePath string, StartLine int, EndLine int, Severity string, Message string, Suppressed bool, SuppressionNote string}. SuppressionAnnotation struct: {RuleID string, ResourceAddress string, Justification string, Ticket string}. SARIF output structs: SARIFLog{Version '2.1.0', Runs []SARIFRun}, SARIFRun{Tool SARIFTool, Results []SARIFResult}, SARIFTool{Driver SARIFDriver{Name 'iacscanner', InformationUri 'https://github.com/org/iacscanner', Rules []SARIFReportingDescriptor}}, SARIFResult{RuleId, Message, Locations []SARIFLocation{PhysicalLocation{ArtifactLocation, Region{StartLine, EndLine}}}}.
target_output.api_design: CLI command interface: iacscanner scan [flags], iacscanner policy list, iacscanner policy validate --file <path>, iacscanner config generate-suppression --rule-id <id> --resource <address>. Exit codes: 0 (clean scan or all findings suppressed), 1 (findings at or above --fail-on threshold), 2 (scan error: parse failure, policy pack not found), 3 (--strict-suppressions: unknown rule ID in annotation). Stdout: SARIF JSON or JSON findings array or ASCII table. Stderr: warnings (unknown suppression IDs, parse warnings) and verbose timing. No HTTP API; no persistent state; no network calls.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Offline operation: no network calls during scan; policy packs embedded in binary. Binary supply chain: cosign signing with Sigstore ephemeral OIDC key in GitHub Actions release workflow; SBOM generated via syft and attached to GitHub Release; checksum file published for all platform binaries. Secrets redaction: Terraform variables with sensitive=true annotation have their default values replaced with REDACTED in SARIF physicalLocation excerpts to prevent accidental secret exposure in findings output. Path traversal: --path validated against current working directory; symlink following disabled by default; scan aborts with error if path escapes the provided root. Suppression integrity (ISS-001 fixed): SuppressionValidator cross-references each annotation rule_id against PolicyPack.Rules index; unknown rule IDs produce stderr warnings; --strict-suppressions flag enforces strict policy by exiting code 3.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: CI on every PR: go test ./... with -race flag, go vet ./..., golangci-lint run --enable-all with agreed suppression list, benchmark tests for parser performance regression. Release workflow (tag push): goreleaser cross-compiles for 5 platforms; cosign signs each binary with ephemeral OIDC key; syft generates SBOM; Docker multi-arch image (linux/amd64 + linux/arm64) pushed to GHCR with version tag; GitHub Release created with binaries, checksums, and SBOM. Users verify binary: cosign verify-blob --certificate-identity-regexp github.com/org/iacscanner --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com iacscanner.linux.amd64.
target_output.observability: Scan summary written to stdout as JSON (scan_duration_ms, files_scanned, resources_evaluated, findings_count by severity, suppressed_count). --verbose flag adds per-rule timing (rule_id, avg_evaluation_us) to stderr. --debug flag logs AST node counts per file and rule match attempt counts to stderr. No external telemetry; no usage metrics transmitted. scan_duration_ms benchmark tracked in CI to catch performance regressions in parser or rule engine.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost - stateless binary. Development cost: goreleaser Pro for multi-platform release automation ($50/month). Scan performance scales linearly with file count; goroutine worker pool sizes to available CPU cores. 1,000-file scan benchmark maintained in CI; regression alert if >30 seconds on 4-core runner.
target_output.phased_implementation: Round 2 deliverables (current sprint): SuppressionValidator.ValidateAgainstPack() with unit tests covering known/unknown rule IDs; --strict-suppressions flag; typed SARIF 2.1.0 Go structs; TestSARIFSchema validation against GitHub SARIF schema fixture. Next phase: CloudFormation JSON/YAML parser with CFN resource type coverage for core services; nist-800-53-rev5 and pci-dss-4.0 policy packs. Future phase: custom YAML rule authoring, IDE plugin, Pulumi support.
target_output.development_guidelines: Every Rule struct must have a unit test with at least one compliant resource and one non-compliant resource as table-driven test cases. SuppressionValidator.ValidateAgainstPack() tests must cover: valid rule ID accepted, unknown rule ID produces warning, --strict-suppressions exits code 3. TestSARIFSchema must validate generated SARIF against sarif-schema-2.1.0.json using gojsonschema; test fixture includes GitHub Advanced Security required fields. Parser benchmarks in bench_test.go track HCLParser and CFNParser throughput in resources/second. Binary size checked in CI: go tool nm output piped to size check, fail if compressed binary exceeds 50MB.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk (ISS-001 resolved): SuppressionValidator now validates rule IDs against policy pack manifest; --strict-suppressions enables CI enforcement of suppression hygiene. Risk (ISS-002 resolved): SARIF typed structs enforce required fields at compile time; schema test catches regressions. Remaining risk: HCL parsing of complex Terraform module compositions with variable interpolation may produce incomplete attribute maps for rules relying on interpolated values; mitigated by documenting unsupported patterns and emitting parse warnings. Tradeoff: go:embed policy packs increase binary size but eliminate network dependency and versioning complexity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SARIF 2.1.0 as primary CI output format (GitHub Advanced Security, GitLab SAST, Azure DevOps). Confirmed suppression annotation via HCL inline comment (not separate .iacscanner-ignore file). Confirmed policy pack versioning as semantic version in YAML manifest. Confirmed --strict-suppressions as opt-in flag (not default) to avoid breaking existing pipelines.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision fixing suppression rule ID validation (ISS-001) and SARIF 2.1.0 schema compliance (ISS-002) for IaC compliance scanner CLI.

profile.domain: Small Law Firm Billing and Time Entry

project_goal.value: Desktop application for small law firm billing administrators to generate, review, and export client invoices from time entry records without requiring a full practice management subscription
project_goal.rationale: Solo and small-firm practitioners cannot justify $3,000/year SaaS billing subscriptions; offline-capable desktop tool reduces costs

target_users.value: Billing administrators, paralegals, and solo practitioners at solo and 2-5 attorney firms
target_users.rationale: Small firm staff handle billing alongside other duties; tool must be simple enough for non-technical users

project_class.rationale: Offline-capable Electron application; no server dependency required

capabilities.rationale: Local UI, embedded Node.js business logic, and SQLite persistence; no auth or network services

complexity_level.rationale: Core CRUD for time entries and invoice generation; no complex integrations or distributed components

risk_level.rationale: Financial billing data and client records stored locally; loss or corruption has direct business impact

data_sensitivity.rationale: Time entries with hourly rates, invoice amounts, and payment records are financial and client-confidential data

external_exposure.rationale: Application and database run entirely on the user's workstation; no network-accessible endpoints

access_model.value: Local PIN code protecting application launch; single-user per installation
access_model.rationale: Shared workstation protection without full user management overhead

feature_scope.value: Time entry recording with client and matter assignment, hourly rate application from fee schedules, invoice generation with customisable firm branding, PDF invoice export, payment recording against invoices, outstanding balance reports, CSV export for accounting software
feature_scope.rationale: Complete billing lifecycle from time capture through payment tracking

mvp_scope.value: Time entry CRUD, invoice generation from unbilled entries, PDF export with firm branding, basic client ledger view
mvp_scope.rationale: Core billing workflow sufficient to replace manual invoice creation

security_baseline.value: Local PIN protection for application access; SQLite database encrypted with SQLCipher; automatic screen lock after 10 minutes of inactivity
security_baseline.rationale: Protect billing data on shared or unattended workstations

frontend_stack.value: Electron 31 with Vue.js 3
frontend_stack.rationale: Electron for desktop packaging; Vue 3 with Composition API for reactive form-heavy billing UI

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 embedded in Electron
backend_stack.rationale: Electron main process handles SQLite via better-sqlite3 and PDF generation via pdfkit

data_platform.value: SQLite via better-sqlite3
data_platform.rationale: Embedded database with no external infrastructure; SQLCipher extension for encryption at rest

hosting_target.value: Electron desktop distribution via electron-builder
hosting_target.rationale: Cross-platform installer packages for Windows and macOS

privacy_retention_policy.value: All data stored locally on user workstation; user responsible for backup; no cloud retention; IOLTA trust account entries retained per state bar requirements (minimum 5 years)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Local-only application; bar trust account rules apply to the firm's records not the software

future_scope.value: QuickBooks Online export for direct accounting integration, optional cloud backup to user-provided S3 bucket, multi-user support for 2-attorney firms
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 integrations for firms that grow beyond solo practice

constraints.value: Must run offline; Windows 10+ and macOS 12+; installer size under 150MB; no mandatory registration or cloud account
constraints.rationale: Small firm IT constraints; practitioners in areas with unreliable internet need full offline operation

observability_baseline.value: Sentry local crash reporting via electron-sentry; application event log written to local app data directory
observability_baseline.rationale: Crash reports help support without requiring cloud telemetry

execution_preference.value: Single release cadence; ship stable builds over frequent updates given small firm IT environment
execution_preference.rationale: Small firm practitioners do not want frequent automatic updates disrupting billing workflows

compliance_context.value: State bar billing practice rules (fee reasonableness, itemised billing); IOLTA trust accounting requirements for trust deposits and disbursements; state-specific interest on unpaid invoices rules
compliance_context.rationale: Law firm billing directly governed by state bar rules; IOLTA trust ledger must be accurate

project.goal: Desktop billing tool replacing manual invoice creation for solo and small-firm practitioners
project.target_users: Billing admins and solo practitioners
project.timeline: MVP in 8 weeks

frontend.state: Pinia for client, matter, and time entry state

security.auth: Local PIN hash stored in SQLite (argon2 in main process)
security.encryption: SQLCipher for database encryption at rest

data.migrations: Manual SQL migration scripts run at app startup

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Vitest and electron-builder
devops.packaging: electron-builder for Windows NSIS and macOS DMG

open_questions.sqlcipher_windows: SQLCipher requires native compilation on Windows; confirm electron-rebuild pipeline handles this

reasoner_reviews.product: The tool correctly scopes to the small firm billing lifecycle without overbuilding. Time entry capture, invoice generation with firm branding, and payment tracking are the right core features. The PDF export quality is critical for client-facing invoices. Round 1 established basic structure; round 2 should address the missing fee schedule application logic and the IOLTA trust entry separation from regular billing entries.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: better-sqlite3 synchronous API is correct for Electron main process since it avoids async callback complexity in IPC handlers. SQLCipher native extension compilation must be verified on Windows through electron-rebuild in CI. pdfkit invoice generation should use a template module with firm logo, matter description, time entry line items, and running total — the layout logic is non-trivial enough to warrant its own InvoiceRenderer class.
reasoner_reviews.security: PIN hash should use argon2 in the Electron main process — never store plaintext or bcrypt which has known weaknesses at low cost factors. SQLCipher key must be derived from the PIN via PBKDF2 before being passed to the PRAGMA key command. Auto-lock must clear the Vue.js reactive state of loaded invoice data, not just hide the UI.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Installer size under 150MB is achievable: Electron 31 base is about 90MB, Vue 3 bundle under 500KB, pdfkit under 2MB. SQLCipher adds about 3MB. electron-builder NSIS installer with LZMA compression typically produces 100-110MB Windows installer.

backend.service_design: Electron main process IPC handlers grouped by domain: time-entries (CRUD), clients (CRUD), matters (CRUD), fee-schedules (rate card management), invoices (generate, mark-paid), payments (record, query), reports (outstanding balances, CSV export). Each handler calls a corresponding service module that executes better-sqlite3 prepared statements.
backend.api_patterns: Electron ipcMain.handle for all renderer-to-main communication. Channels: 'time-entries:create', 'time-entries:list', 'invoices:generate', 'invoices:export-pdf', 'payments:record', 'reports:outstanding-balances'. All handlers wrapped in try/catch returning {success, data, error} envelope.
backend.business_modules: FeeScheduleApplicator: resolves effective hourly rate for a timekeeper on a given matter by checking matter-specific rate override, then client-default rate, then firm-default rate. InvoiceGenerator: aggregates unbilled time entries for a client-date-range, applies FeeScheduleApplicator rates, calculates subtotal, tax if applicable, and total. InvoiceRenderer: pdfkit layout for firm letterhead, client address, matter description, itemised time entry table, payment terms, and total amount due.
backend.failure_handling: SQLite write failures caught and returned to renderer as error envelope. PDF generation errors return error envelope with pdfkit error message. Startup migration runner applies pending SQL scripts in order and rolls back on error with user-visible alert.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA in Electron renderer process. Pinia stores: clientStore, matterStore, timeEntryStore, invoiceStore, settingsStore (firm branding, fee schedules). Vue Router for main navigation: /time-entries, /clients, /matters, /invoices, /reports.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Time Entry Form: date picker, client select, matter select, duration input (hours:minutes), rate display from FeeScheduleApplicator, narrative field. Invoice Preview: generates preview before PDF export showing all unbilled entries with calculated totals; user confirms before generating. Payment Recording: invoice list with status badges; click to record payment amount and date.
frontend.performance_notes: better-sqlite3 synchronous queries in main process return results fast enough for the scale of small firm data (under 10k time entries). No virtualisation needed for entry lists at this scale.

security.auth_design: PIN entry on application launch; main process verifies PIN against argon2 hash stored in SQLite settings table. SQLCipher PRAGMA key derived from PIN + stored salt via PBKDF2-SHA256 100k iterations before database open.
security.secrets_management: No external credentials. PIN hash stored in encrypted SQLite database. Auto-lock: renderer emits lock event on inactivity timeout; main process clears in-memory database connection cache and forces PIN re-entry on next access.
security.privacy_controls: Client financial data stays on local filesystem in SQLite database. Sentry crash reports filtered to exclude time entry narratives and invoice amounts via beforeSend hook.

data.entities: clients (id INTEGER PK, display_name TEXT, address TEXT, email TEXT, phone TEXT, default_rate DECIMAL). matters (id INTEGER PK, client_id FK, matter_name TEXT, matter_number TEXT, status TEXT, matter_rate_override DECIMAL nullable). time_entries (id INTEGER PK, matter_id FK, timekeeper_name TEXT, entry_date DATE, hours DECIMAL, rate DECIMAL, narrative TEXT, billed_flag INTEGER). invoices (id INTEGER PK, client_id FK, invoice_number TEXT, invoice_date DATE, due_date DATE, subtotal DECIMAL, tax_amount DECIMAL, total_amount DECIMAL, status TEXT). invoice_line_items (id INTEGER PK, invoice_id FK, time_entry_id FK, description TEXT, hours DECIMAL, rate DECIMAL, amount DECIMAL). payment_records (id INTEGER PK, invoice_id FK, payment_date DATE, amount DECIMAL, payment_method TEXT). fee_schedules (id INTEGER PK, schedule_name TEXT, default_hourly_rate DECIMAL, created_at TEXT).
data.schema_notes: SQLite WAL mode enabled for safe concurrent reads during PDF generation. billed_flag on time_entries updated to 1 when included in an invoice; prevents double-billing.
data.retention_and_deletion: IOLTA trust entries (if implemented in Phase 2) must be retained minimum 5 years per state bar rules. Regular client and billing data retained per user preference with export-before-delete confirmation dialog.

devops.deployment_topology: electron-builder generates Windows NSIS installer and macOS DMG. electron-rebuild ensures SQLCipher native module compiles for the packaged Electron Node.js ABI version.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Vitest unit tests for FeeScheduleApplicator and InvoiceGenerator, electron-builder dry-run on PR. Tag-triggered release builds Windows and macOS artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases.
devops.cost_controls: No infrastructure cost. GitHub Actions free tier sufficient for build and test runs.

ISS-001.title: Fee schedule rate resolution logic not defined
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan had no FeeScheduleApplicator logic; invoices would apply a flat rate without considering matter-specific or client-default overrides, creating billing errors.

history.note: Billing logic gap flagged during product review
FeeScheduleApplicator module added with three-tier rate resolution

ISS-002.title: PIN verification does not derive SQLCipher key from PIN
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan hashed the PIN for authentication but opened the SQLite database with a hardcoded passphrase, defeating the purpose of PIN-based access control for data encryption.

history.note: Security reviewer identified decoupled PIN and encryption key

focus_issues.title: PIN verification does not derive SQLCipher key from PIN
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan hashed the PIN for authentication but opened the SQLite database with a hardcoded passphrase, defeating the purpose of PIN-based access control for data encryption.

latest_recommendations: Derive SQLCipher PRAGMA key from PIN via PBKDF2-SHA256 with stored salt so database cannot be opened without PIN
Store only the PBKDF2 salt in unencrypted settings; never store the derived key
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Small Law Firm Billing Desktop Application — Initial Architecture

convergence_state.reason: Medium-severity SQLCipher key derivation gap unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Reasonable structure for a simple desktop billing tool but missing the rate resolution logic that is the core business differentiator, and the PIN-to-encryption-key coupling is broken.
strengths: Electron IPC handler structure is appropriate for the use case
SQLCipher selection for local encryption is correct
pdfkit for invoice PDF generation is practical
concerns: No fee schedule rate resolution logic means invoices would use flat rates only
PIN authentication and SQLCipher key are not coupled; database could be opened without PIN
InvoiceRenderer layout logic not specified enough to implement
recommendations: Add FeeScheduleApplicator with three-tier resolution: matter rate override, then client default rate, then fee schedule default rate
Derive SQLCipher PRAGMA key from PIN via PBKDF2-SHA256 with stored per-installation salt

issue_updates.title: PIN does not derive SQLCipher key
issue_updates.detail: Round 1 plan opened database with hardcoded passphrase; PIN check was authentication-only

previous_plan.title: Small Law Firm Billing Desktop Application — Initial Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Electron 31 and Vue 3 desktop billing application with SQLite storage and pdfkit PDF generation for solo and small law firm practitioners.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Electron main process with better-sqlite3, Vue 3 renderer with Pinia state management. SQLCipher for local database encryption.
previous_plan.data_model: clients, matters, time_entries, invoices, invoice_line_items, payment_records.

target_output.thinking_summary: ISS-002 requires coupling PIN verification to SQLCipher key derivation via PBKDF2-SHA256 so the database key is derived from the user's PIN at runtime rather than a hardcoded passphrase. The implementation: first-launch generates a random 32-byte salt stored in a plain settings.json file (not in the encrypted database, since the key is needed to open it); on PIN entry, PBKDF2-SHA256(PIN, salt, 100000 iterations) produces the encryption key passed to PRAGMA key. This round also carries forward ISS-001 resolution (FeeScheduleApplicator three-tier rate logic). The plan quality is weak — some sections are thinner than ideal given the simple project scope.

fix_report.action_taken: Added PBKDF2 key derivation to PIN verification flow in Electron main process. First-launch flow: generate 32-byte random salt via crypto.randomBytes, store in plain JSON settings file in Electron userData directory. PIN entry flow: PBKDF2-SHA256(pin, salt, 100000) produces 32-byte key; key passed as hex string to better-sqlite3 PRAGMA key='...'; argon2 hash of PIN also checked against stored hash for authentication; only if both succeed does application proceed. PIN change flow: re-derives new key, re-encrypts database with PRAGMA rekey='...'.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SQLite database cannot be opened without the correct PIN; encryption key is derived from user-supplied PIN rather than hardcoded passphrase

target_output.title: Small Law Firm Billing Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: Electron 31 and Vue 3 desktop application for solo and small law firm billing administration. Handles time entry recording with three-tier fee schedule rate resolution, invoice generation with customisable firm branding, PDF export via pdfkit, payment recording against outstanding invoices, and outstanding balance reporting with CSV export. SQLite database encrypted with SQLCipher using a key derived from the user's PIN via PBKDF2.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Electron desktop application with renderer process (Vue 3 SPA) communicating with main process via typed IPC channels. Main process embeds Node.js 20 running better-sqlite3 for synchronous SQLite access with SQLCipher encryption. Database key derived at runtime from user PIN via PBKDF2-SHA256 with per-installation salt stored in plain settings.json. pdfkit generates invoice PDFs in the main process. electron-builder packages Windows NSIS and macOS DMG installers. No network connectivity required; fully offline operation.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 31 renderer, Vue 3 with Composition API, Pinia, Vue Router, Tailwind CSS. Backend: Node.js 20 in Electron main process, better-sqlite3 with SQLCipher native extension, pdfkit 0.15 for PDF generation, argon2 for PIN hash, Node.js crypto module for PBKDF2 key derivation. Build: electron-builder with electron-rebuild for native module compilation. Testing: Vitest for unit tests. Crash reporting: Sentry with electron-sentry.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Time Entry Recording: date, client, matter, duration (hours:minutes), narrative; rate auto-populated from FeeScheduleApplicator. Invoice Generation: select client and date range; aggregate unbilled time entries; apply FeeScheduleApplicator three-tier rate resolution; calculate totals; preview before PDF export. Fee Schedule Management: admin sets firm default rates, client default overrides, and matter-specific rate overrides. PDF Export: pdfkit invoice with firm logo, letterhead, client address block, itemised time entry table, tax line if applicable, total due, and payment terms. Payment Recording: record payment amount, date, and method against invoice; update invoice status. Outstanding Balance Report: client ledger showing invoiced total, payments received, and outstanding balance; CSV export for accounting software import.
target_output.system_components: Electron main process (IPC handler registry, SQLite connection manager with PBKDF2 key derivation, service modules). Vue 3 renderer process (Pinia stores, Vue Router, component library). better-sqlite3 (synchronous SQLite; SQLCipher via sqlcipher native extension compiled by electron-rebuild). FeeScheduleApplicator (three-tier rate resolution: matter override, client default, fee schedule default). InvoiceGenerator (unbilled entry aggregation, rate application, total calculation). InvoiceRenderer (pdfkit document builder with firm branding template). MigrationRunner (startup SQL migration script executor with version tracking in migrations table). Settings store (JSON settings file in Electron userData for PBKDF2 salt and non-sensitive preferences).
target_output.workflows: First Launch: application creates SQLite database, generates 32-byte PBKDF2 salt via crypto.randomBytes, writes salt to settings.json. User sets PIN; argon2 hash stored in settings.json; PBKDF2(PIN, salt) key passed to PRAGMA key; database initialised with schema migrations. Daily Use: user launches application, enters PIN; main process derives PBKDF2 key and opens encrypted database; argon2 PIN hash verified; on success application loads. Time Entry to Invoice: user opens time entry form, selects client and matter; FeeScheduleApplicator resolves rate (matter override → client default → fee schedule default) and displays rate; user enters duration and narrative; entry saved with billed_flag=0. Billing admin selects client and date range on Invoice screen; InvoiceGenerator queries unbilled entries, applies rates, computes totals; user reviews preview; on confirm, InvoiceRenderer generates PDF via pdfkit; time entries updated to billed_flag=1; invoice record created. Payment Recording: admin opens invoice, records payment; payment_records INSERT; invoice status updated to paid if total payments equal invoice total.
target_output.data_model: clients (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, display_name TEXT NOT NULL, address TEXT, email TEXT, phone TEXT, default_hourly_rate DECIMAL). matters (id INTEGER PK, client_id FK, matter_name TEXT, matter_number TEXT UNIQUE, status TEXT DEFAULT active, matter_rate_override DECIMAL). time_entries (id INTEGER PK, matter_id FK, timekeeper_name TEXT, entry_date TEXT, hours DECIMAL NOT NULL, rate DECIMAL NOT NULL, narrative TEXT, billed_flag INTEGER DEFAULT 0). invoices (id INTEGER PK, client_id FK, invoice_number TEXT UNIQUE, invoice_date TEXT, due_date TEXT, subtotal DECIMAL, tax_amount DECIMAL DEFAULT 0, total_amount DECIMAL, status TEXT DEFAULT unpaid). invoice_line_items (id INTEGER PK, invoice_id FK, time_entry_id FK, description TEXT, hours DECIMAL, rate DECIMAL, amount DECIMAL). payment_records (id INTEGER PK, invoice_id FK, payment_date TEXT, amount DECIMAL, payment_method TEXT). fee_schedules (id INTEGER PK, schedule_name TEXT, default_hourly_rate DECIMAL). migrations (version INTEGER PK, applied_at TEXT).
target_output.api_design: Electron IPC channels: ipcMain.handle('clients:list') returns all clients. ipcMain.handle('time-entries:create', entry) returns saved entry with resolved rate. ipcMain.handle('invoices:generate', {clientId, fromDate, toDate}) returns preview data. ipcMain.handle('invoices:export-pdf', invoiceId) returns PDF buffer. ipcMain.handle('payments:record', {invoiceId, amount, date, method}) returns updated invoice. ipcMain.handle('reports:outstanding-balances') returns per-client balance summary. ipcMain.handle('auth:verify-pin', pin) returns {success, error}. All handlers return {success: Boolean, data: any, error: String nullable}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PIN to SQLCipher key coupling: first launch generates 32-byte salt stored in settings.json; on PIN entry, Node.js crypto.pbkdf2Sync(pin, salt, 100000, 32, 'sha256') produces database key passed to PRAGMA key. argon2 hash of PIN also verified for authentication. Auto-lock: Vue.js inactivity timer emits lock event after 10 minutes; main process closes SQLite connection; PIN required to re-derive key and reopen. PIN change: PRAGMA rekey re-encrypts database with new derived key. Sentry beforeSend hook excludes event data properties matching time_entry narrative, invoice amount, client name patterns. State bar billing compliance: fee_schedule rate reasonableness is the firm's responsibility; application enforces billed_flag to prevent duplicate billing of same time entry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: electron-builder configuration: targets Windows NSIS installer and macOS DMG with code signing. electron-rebuild in CI ensures SQLCipher compiles for Electron 31 Node.js ABI on both platforms. GitHub Actions: Vitest unit tests for FeeScheduleApplicator, InvoiceGenerator, and MigrationRunner; electron-builder dry-run on PR. Tag-triggered release builds both platform artifacts and uploads to GitHub Releases. Auto-update via electron-updater pointing to GitHub Releases; update applied on next restart with user confirmation dialog.
target_output.observability: Sentry electron-sentry for crash reporting with financial field filtering. Local application event log at Electron userData/app.log: startup, migration applied, invoice generated (invoice number only), payment recorded (invoice number only), PIN verification failed (no PIN value). No time entry rates or amounts in logs. MigrationRunner logs each applied migration script name and version number.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No infrastructure cost. GitHub Actions free tier sufficient for CI builds. SQLite handles small firm data volumes (under 50k time entries) with no performance concerns. For firms with multiple timekeepers entering concurrent time entries, WAL journal mode ensures readers do not block writers.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (5 weeks): time entry CRUD with FeeScheduleApplicator three-tier rate resolution, invoice generation and PDF export via pdfkit, payment recording, basic client ledger view, PIN authentication with PBKDF2-derived SQLCipher key, Windows and macOS builds. Phase 2 (3 weeks): outstanding balance reports with CSV export, fee schedule management UI, firm branding settings (logo upload, firm name, address), auto-lock on inactivity.
target_output.development_guidelines: Vitest for all business logic unit tests: FeeScheduleApplicator rate resolution, InvoiceGenerator total calculation, MigrationRunner script ordering. No financial amounts or client names in log statements. better-sqlite3 prepared statements required for all parameterised queries; no string concatenation in SQL. PBKDF2 key derivation isolated in a single KeyDerivation module testable without Electron dependencies. electron-rebuild must run before electron-builder in packaging pipeline to ensure SQLCipher ABI compatibility.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SQLCipher native extension compilation fails on Windows CI due to MSVC toolchain requirements; mitigated by electron-rebuild with node-gyp and MSVC Build Tools in GitHub Actions Windows runner. Risk: user forgets PIN with no recovery mechanism; mitigated by prompting for PIN backup code during setup and storing a recovery hint (not the PIN) in settings.json. Tradeoff: synchronous better-sqlite3 over async node-sqlite3 — correct for Electron main process where async has no benefit and simplifies error handling in IPC handlers. Tradeoff: local-only storage eliminates server costs and network dependency but requires user-managed backup; CSV and PDF export are the only data recovery paths.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: SQLCipher Windows compilation confirmed via electron-rebuild with MSVC in CI GitHub Actions runner. Auto-update frequency set to weekly check via electron-updater with user-visible notification. IOLTA trust account entries deferred to Phase 3 as a separate trust ledger module with sequential balance validation to meet state bar requirements.

metadata.notes: Small law firm offline billing desktop app; round 2 resolves PIN-to-SQLCipher key derivation gap via PBKDF2; some sections at minimum depth matching weak quality target.

profile.domain: AI Contract Risk Analysis SaaS Landing Page

project_goal.value: Marketing landing page for an AI-powered contract risk analysis SaaS that uses machine learning to flag non-standard clauses, score contract risk, and suggest negotiation redlines; targets corporate legal teams and procurement departments evaluating AI-assisted contract review tools
project_goal.rationale: Landing page drives trial signups and enterprise demo requests for the contract AI product

target_users.value: Corporate legal teams, procurement departments, and legal operations managers evaluating AI contract review tools
target_users.rationale: ICP is mid-to-enterprise legal and procurement buyers who need social proof and clear ROI messaging

project_class.rationale: Static marketing page with minimal server-side logic; Vercel Edge serverless function for demo submission proxy only

capabilities.rationale: Static Astro frontend with Segment and GA4 analytics; no auth, no backend database

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with one serverless function for demo form proxy; no complex state or auth

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored; demo submissions proxied to product API; no PII retained on landing page infrastructure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Landing page collects only trial signup emails forwarded to Segment; no sensitive data stored

external_exposure.rationale: Public-facing marketing page; no authentication required

access_model.value: Public unauthenticated access for all visitors; no login required
access_model.rationale: Marketing landing page must be fully publicly accessible for SEO and paid traffic

feature_scope.value: Hero section with AI risk score demo CTA, product feature highlights (clause flagging, risk scoring, redline suggestions), interactive sample contract risk analysis demo, customer testimonials from legal and procurement teams, pricing tier cards, trial signup form, enterprise demo request form integrated with Segment
feature_scope.rationale: Full conversion funnel from awareness through demo request

mvp_scope.value: Hero, feature highlights, sample contract demo widget, trial signup form, Segment tracking
mvp_scope.rationale: Core conversion elements sufficient for initial launch; testimonials and pricing added in phase 2

security_baseline.value: GDPR cookie consent banner for EU visitors; Content Security Policy headers; no third-party scripts loaded without consent; HSTS on Vercel domain
security_baseline.rationale: GDPR requires consent before loading GA4 and Segment on EU traffic

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript; Tailwind CSS; React islands for interactive demo widget and forms; no client-side JavaScript on static sections
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro zero-JS-by-default maximizes Core Web Vitals scores; React islands only where interactivity is needed

backend_stack.value: Vercel Edge Function for demo submission proxy (forwards sanitized contract sample to product API, returns anonymized risk analysis result)
backend_stack.rationale: Edge function runs at Vercel CDN edge for minimal latency; product API key kept server-side only

data_platform.value: None - no database; demo submissions stateless proxy via Edge Function; Segment collects trial signup events forwarded to HubSpot and internal BI
data_platform.rationale: Landing page has no persistence layer; all conversion data flows through Segment to downstream tools

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge with automatic CDN distribution; custom domain with Vercel-managed TLS
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel Edge provides optimal Astro hosting with instant global CDN and serverless function co-location

privacy_retention_policy.value: No visitor data stored on landing page infrastructure; cookie consent preferences stored in localStorage only; Segment collects signup email for CRM forwarding only; no long-term landing page data retention
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR compliance for EU visitors requires explicit consent before Segment and GA4 load

future_scope.value: A/B test variants for hero messaging via Vercel Edge Config, localized versions for EU legal market, video demo integration, comparison page versus legacy contract review tools
future_scope.rationale: Conversion optimization and market expansion are next priorities post-launch

constraints.value: Lighthouse Performance score must be 95+ on mobile; LCP under 2.5 seconds globally; no runtime database or server-side auth; GDPR cookie consent required for EU traffic before any analytics scripts load
constraints.rationale: Core Web Vitals directly impact paid acquisition costs and SEO ranking

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals tracking; Vercel Edge Function logs for demo API proxy errors; Segment for conversion funnel events (page_view, demo_submitted, signup_completed)
observability_baseline.rationale: Vercel Analytics provides real-user CWV data; Segment events drive attribution reporting in HubSpot

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development; GitHub Actions for CI; Vercel preview deployments per PR for stakeholder review
execution_preference.rationale: Preview deployments allow marketing team to review copy and layout changes before merge

project.goal: High-converting marketing landing page for AI contract risk analysis SaaS
project.target_users: Corporate legal teams and procurement managers
project.timeline: MVP live in 3 weeks; full feature set in 6 weeks

frontend.interactivity: React islands for demo widget and signup forms

backend.purpose: Demo submission proxy only - forwards contract sample to product API

security.consent: GDPR cookie banner before GA4 and Segment load
security.headers: Content Security Policy, HSTS via Vercel

devops.ci: GitHub Actions

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 1 landing page had the right content structure but the interactive sample contract demo was implemented as a client-side React component that eagerly loaded a 180KB demo payload on page load, hurting LCP. The demo widget should be lazy-loaded behind a user interaction trigger (clicking 'Try the Demo') to avoid penalizing visitors who never interact with it. The trial signup form also lacked a clear value proposition statement immediately above the email input field.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The round 1 Astro configuration was missing view transitions for smooth section scrolling and did not use Astro image optimization for the testimonial headshots, which were 400KB unoptimized PNGs causing CLS. All images must go through Astro Image component with WebP conversion and explicit width and height attributes. The Edge Function for demo proxy had no rate limiting, exposing the product API to potential abuse via the landing page.
reasoner_reviews.security: The GDPR cookie consent implementation in round 1 loaded GA4 and Segment scripts in the page head unconditionally, violating GDPR by sending data before consent. Scripts must be deferred and loaded only after explicit cookie consent interaction via the consent component. The Content Security Policy header was missing from the Vercel config.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse 95+ mobile requires careful attention to cumulative layout shift from testimonial images and the interactive demo widget. Use Astro Image with explicit dimensions for all images. Defer all third-party scripts (Segment, GA4, HubSpot forms) behind the consent gate. Ensure the demo React island does not eagerly hydrate on page load.

backend.service_design: Single Vercel Edge Function at /api/demo-proxy: accepts POST with contract_sample_text (max 2000 chars), sanitizes input to remove any PII patterns, forwards to product API /v1/analyze with server-side API key, returns anonymized risk analysis JSON (overall_risk_score, flagged_clause_types array, sample_redline_suggestion). Rate limited to 5 requests per IP per minute via Vercel Edge rate limiting.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/demo-proxy (body: {contract_sample: string max 2000 chars}; returns: {risk_score: number, flagged_clauses: string[], sample_redline: string} or {error: string}). No authentication. Rate limit: 5/min/IP. Edge Function cold start below 50ms via Vercel Edge runtime.
backend.failure_handling: Product API timeout (5 second limit) returns 504 with user-friendly error rendered in demo widget: 'Demo temporarily unavailable - try again in a moment.' Rate limit exceeded returns 429 with Retry-After header. Input exceeding 2000 characters rejected with 400 and UI message.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 site with pages: index.astro (landing page). Component structure: Hero.astro (static), FeatureHighlights.astro (static), DemoWidget (React island, client:visible for lazy hydration), Testimonials.astro (static with Astro Image optimization), PricingTiers.astro (static), TrialSignupForm (React island, client:idle), CookieConsent (React island, client:load for immediate consent capture). All non-interactive components are pure Astro with zero JS output.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero section with AI risk score animated counter and primary CTA 'Analyze a Contract Free'. Feature highlights: three-column grid showing clause flagging example, risk score dial, redline suggestion snippet. Demo widget: text area pre-populated with sample NDA indemnification clause; click Analyze triggers Edge Function proxy; result displays risk score, flagged clause types, and one sample redline. Trial signup: email input above the fold on mobile; on submit calls Segment identify and track events then redirects to product onboarding.
frontend.performance_notes: DemoWidget uses client:visible directive - hydrates only when scrolled into viewport. All images via Astro Image component with WebP format, explicit width and height, and loading=lazy except hero image (loading=eager, fetchpriority=high). Tailwind CSS purged to under 8KB. No render-blocking scripts. Segment and GA4 loaded only after cookie consent via dynamic script injection.
frontend.frontend_risks: Risk: React island hydration for DemoWidget may cause layout shift if the widget container does not have explicit height reserved before hydration. Mitigation: apply min-height CSS matching the widget dimensions to the island container in Astro static output.

security.auth_design: No authentication. Cookie consent gate implemented via Consent React island that stores choice in localStorage key gdpr_consent_v1. On consent, dynamically injects Segment snippet and GA4 gtag script. On rejection, no analytics scripts loaded for the session.
security.privacy_controls: Content Security Policy header configured in vercel.json: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' cdn.segment.com www.googletagmanager.com; img-src 'self' data: https:. HSTS max-age 31536000 with includeSubDomains. No third-party fonts - system font stack. EU traffic detection via Vercel Edge middleware to show consent banner before any page interaction on first visit.
security.security_risks: Risk: Edge Function demo proxy could be abused to enumerate product API responses at scale by bypassing the rate limit via rotating IPs. Mitigation: product API also enforces its own rate limiting by API key; landing page proxy adds an additional IP-level layer but is not the sole protection.

data.analytics_events: page_view (page, referrer, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign). demo_submitted (contract_sample_length, risk_score_returned, flagged_clause_count). demo_error (error_type). signup_started (source_section: hero/pricing/footer). signup_completed (email_domain_type: enterprise/smb, plan_selected). All events fired via Segment track/identify; forwarded to GA4 and HubSpot via Segment destinations.
data.entities: No database entities. Cookie consent state: localStorage only. Segment anonymous_id: Segment-managed cookie. Trial signup email forwarded to HubSpot Contact via Segment HubSpot destination - no landing page storage.
data.retention_and_deletion: No data retained on landing page infrastructure. Segment event data retained per Segment workspace settings. HubSpot contact records governed by product CRM data retention policy.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project connected to GitHub repo. Astro static build output deployed to Vercel Edge CDN globally. Edge Function bundled from /api/demo-proxy.ts. Custom domain configured with Vercel DNS; TLS managed by Vercel.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: PR triggers astro check (TypeScript type checking), Lighthouse CI against Vercel preview URL (mobile performance score gate 95+, LCP gate 2.5s). Vercel auto-deploys preview on PR open, production on merge to main. No manual approval needed for landing page deploys.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics (Real Experience Score, CWV per page and device type). Vercel Edge Function logs for demo proxy errors and rate limit hits. Segment for all conversion funnel events. Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions as PR gate prevents CWV regressions.
devops.rollback_strategy: Vercel instant rollback to previous deployment via Vercel dashboard or CLI in under 30 seconds. All deployments retained for 30 days.

previous_plan.title: AI Contract Risk Analysis Landing Page - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static landing page with React islands for demo widget and signup forms deployed to Vercel Edge, with Segment and GA4 for analytics.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site with Hero, FeatureHighlights, DemoWidget (React island client:load), Testimonials, PricingTiers, and TrialSignupForm sections. Vercel Edge Function proxies contract samples to product API.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: GDPR cookie consent banner present but GA4 and Segment script tags loaded unconditionally in BaseLayout head. CSP header not configured in vercel.json.
