target_output.thinking_summary: Revision round for a simple Astro 4 landing page addressing two high-severity issues: LCP regression from eager DemoWidget hydration and GDPR violation from unconditional analytics loading. Fixes are straightforward: change client:load to client:visible on DemoWidget and implement CookieConsent-gated dynamic script injection for GA4 and Segment. Also adding CSP headers, Edge Function rate limiting, and Lighthouse CI gate to prevent future regressions. Landing page architecture is fundamentally sound; these are targeted configuration fixes.

fix_report.action_taken: Changed DemoWidget Astro directive from client:load to client:visible; widget hydrates only when scrolled into the viewport eliminating the 180KB payload fetch on initial page load; hero image given fetchpriority=high to ensure it dominates LCP measurement
fix_report.expected_outcome: Mobile LCP returns to 1.8s baseline; Lighthouse mobile performance score recovers to 97+

fix_report.action_taken: Removed GA4 and Segment script tags from BaseLayout head; CookieConsent React island (client:load) stores consent in localStorage and on accept dynamically injects Segment snippet and GA4 gtag.js; EU traffic detection via Vercel Edge middleware shows consent banner on first visit before any page interaction
fix_report.expected_outcome: No analytics data transmitted to EU visitors before consent; GDPR Article 7 compliance achieved; consent stored in localStorage for session persistence

target_output.title: AI Contract Risk Analysis SaaS Landing Page Architecture - Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A high-performance Astro 4 marketing landing page for an AI-powered contract risk analysis SaaS, deployed to Vercel Edge with React islands for selective interactivity. Revision 2 fixes mobile LCP to 1.8 seconds by switching the interactive contract demo widget to client:visible deferred hydration and achieves GDPR compliance by gating Segment and GA4 analytics behind a consent component with dynamic script injection. An Edge Function proxies sample contract submissions to the product API keeping the API key server-side with IP-based rate limiting.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site with zero JavaScript on static sections and selective React islands for interactive elements. CookieConsent island (client:load) renders immediately to capture consent before any analytics fire. DemoWidget island (client:visible) hydrates only when scrolled into the viewport, eliminating payload fetch impact on LCP. TrialSignupForm island (client:idle) hydrates during browser idle time. All static sections (Hero, FeatureHighlights, Testimonials, PricingTiers) emit zero client-side JavaScript. Vercel Edge Function at /api/demo-proxy accepts contract text samples, sanitizes input, and proxies to the product API with server-side API key management and 5-requests-per-IP-per-minute rate limiting. Astro Image component handles all image assets with automatic WebP conversion, responsive srcset generation, and explicit dimensions preventing CLS.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4.x, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS (purged to under 8KB), React 18 islands for DemoWidget, CookieConsent, and TrialSignupForm. Hosting: Vercel Edge CDN with Edge Functions (demo proxy). Analytics: Segment (consent-gated), Google Analytics 4 (consent-gated via gtag.js), Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals. CRM: HubSpot via Segment destination. CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI. Images: Astro Image with Sharp for WebP conversion at build time.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero: headline with AI contract risk score animated counter, primary CTA button 'Analyze a Contract Free' scrolls to demo widget, static Astro component with zero JS. Feature Highlights: three-column grid showing clause flagging screenshot, risk score visualization, and redline suggestion example - all static images via Astro Image. Interactive Demo: DemoWidget React island with sample NDA indemnification clause pre-populated; visitor clicks Analyze; Edge Function proxy returns risk score and flagged clause types; results displayed in animated reveal. Testimonials: four testimonials from legal ops managers and procurement directors with headshots via Astro Image WebP at 64x64px. Pricing Tiers: three-tier pricing cards (Starter, Team, Enterprise) with feature lists; Enterprise tier links to demo request form. Trial Signup: email input with value proposition statement above fold on mobile; Segment identify and track on submit. Cookie Consent: GDPR consent banner on first visit for EU traffic; accept or reject; consent stored in localStorage.
target_output.system_components: Astro build: static HTML, CSS, and minimal JS output to Vercel Edge CDN. CookieConsent React island (client:load): renders consent banner immediately; dynamically injects analytics scripts on accept. DemoWidget React island (client:visible): lazy hydration on viewport entry; calls /api/demo-proxy via fetch; renders risk score and flagged clause result. TrialSignupForm React island (client:idle): calls Segment identify and track on submission; redirects to product onboarding URL. Vercel Edge Function (/api/demo-proxy): input sanitization, rate limiting, product API proxy, response sanitization. Astro Image: build-time WebP conversion and responsive srcset for all images. Vercel Edge Middleware: detects EU traffic via CF-IPCountry header, sets cookie consent required flag for CookieConsent island.
target_output.workflows: New Visitor Conversion Workflow: visitor lands on page from paid search; Vercel Edge Middleware checks CF-IPCountry; if EU, sets x-consent-required header consumed by CookieConsent island which renders consent banner before any analytics fire; visitor accepts; Segment and GA4 injected dynamically; page_view event fires with utm parameters. Demo Interaction Workflow: visitor scrolls to demo section; DemoWidget hydrates via client:visible trigger; visitor optionally edits pre-populated NDA indemnification clause text; clicks Analyze Contract; POST to /api/demo-proxy with contract_sample; Edge Function validates input length, checks rate limit, sanitizes text, forwards to product API with server API key; returns risk_score, flagged_clause_types, sample_redline; DemoWidget renders animated results; demo_submitted Segment event fires. Trial Signup Workflow: visitor clicks CTA or scrolls to signup form; TrialSignupForm hydrates; visitor enters email; on submit Segment identify fires with email and plan_selected; signup_completed event fired; visitor redirected to product onboarding.
target_output.data_model: No server-side data model. Client-side state: CookieConsent stores gdpr_consent_v1 key in localStorage with value accept or reject. DemoWidget holds transient UI state: input_text, loading boolean, risk_score, flagged_clauses, error_message - React useState only, not persisted. TrialSignupForm holds email input - React useState, submitted to Segment and cleared on success. Segment anonymous_id managed by Segment SDK in first-party cookie. No landing page database or server-side storage.
target_output.api_design: Single Edge Function endpoint: POST /api/demo-proxy. Request body: {contract_sample: string} (max 2000 chars enforced). Response success: {risk_score: number 0-100, flagged_clauses: string[] e.g. ['unlimited_liability', 'unilateral_termination'], sample_redline: string with suggested revision}. Response error: {error: 'rate_limited' | 'product_api_unavailable' | 'invalid_input'}. Rate limit: 5 requests per IP per minute via Vercel Edge rate limiting config in vercel.json. No API key or auth required from client. Product API key stored as Vercel environment variable PRODUCT_API_KEY, never exposed to client.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GDPR: CookieConsent island renders before any third-party scripts; GA4 and Segment injected dynamically only on explicit accept; EU traffic detected via CF-IPCountry in Vercel Edge Middleware. Content Security Policy in vercel.json headers: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' cdn.segment.com www.googletagmanager.com; img-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src 'self' api.segment.io www.google-analytics.com. HSTS max-age 31536000 includeSubDomains via Vercel. No PII stored on landing page infrastructure - signup email forwarded to Segment and immediately discarded from Edge Function memory. Edge Function demo proxy sanitizes input to remove email patterns and phone numbers before forwarding to product API.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project auto-deploys preview on PR open and production on merge to main. GitHub Actions PR workflow: astro check for TypeScript errors, then Lighthouse CI against Vercel preview URL with gates: mobile performance score 95+ and LCP under 2.5 seconds. Failed Lighthouse gates block merge. Production deploy instant via Vercel CDN propagation (under 30 seconds globally). Vercel Edge Function deployed alongside static site with same build. Environment variables: PRODUCT_API_KEY in Vercel encrypted environment variables, not in git.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics: Real Experience Score tracking LCP, FID, CLS per device type and geography. Segment events for full conversion funnel: page_view, demo_submitted, demo_error, signup_started, signup_completed - forwarded to GA4 and HubSpot via Segment destinations. Vercel Edge Function logs: demo proxy request count, rate limit hits, product API error rate. Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions PR workflow as regression prevention gate. Alert: if Segment demo_submitted events drop to zero for 2 hours during business hours, potential Edge Function failure to investigate.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan: $20/month covering unlimited static deploys, 100 GB bandwidth, and 100,000 Edge Function invocations/month. Segment free tier covers up to 1000 monthly tracked users sufficient for early traffic. GA4 free. Vercel Analytics included in Pro plan. Total infrastructure cost: approximately $20-50/month. Auto-scales globally via Vercel CDN with no capacity planning needed for static assets; Edge Function scales automatically to demand.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Launch (weeks 1-3): Hero, FeatureHighlights, DemoWidget (client:visible) with Edge Function proxy, TrialSignupForm, CookieConsent with GDPR-gated analytics, Lighthouse CI gate, CSP headers, Vercel Analytics. Phase 2 - Conversion Optimization (weeks 4-6): Testimonials with Astro Image WebP, PricingTiers, enterprise demo request form integrated with HubSpot directly, A/B hero messaging via Vercel Edge Config flag. Phase 3 - Expansion (post-launch): localized EU legal market variant, video demo integration, competitor comparison page.
target_output.development_guidelines: All new components default to pure Astro with zero client JS. Only add React island when interactivity is required; use the most deferred directive possible (client:visible or client:idle over client:load). Never add script tags in BaseLayout head directly - all third-party scripts go through CookieConsent dynamic injection. Astro Image required for all img tags; no raw img elements. Lighthouse CI gate enforced in PR workflow - performance regressions block merge. PRODUCT_API_KEY must never appear in client-side code or git history.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: client:visible hydration requires IntersectionObserver, which is not supported on very old browsers (IE11). Mitigation: IE11 is below 0.5% of target enterprise buyer traffic; acceptable tradeoff for LCP improvement. Risk: Vercel Edge Middleware IP geolocation via CF-IPCountry may misclassify some EU visitors as non-EU via VPN usage, loading analytics before consent for those users. Mitigation: opted for permissive consent (show banner to all visitors) via a feature flag to ensure GDPR compliance over precise geolocation. Tradeoff: Segment over direct GA4 adds a forwarding hop but provides unified event routing to HubSpot and BI without duplicating tracking code per destination.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose client:visible over client:interaction for DemoWidget because the demo is visible on most monitor sizes without scrolling and client:interaction would require an extra click before hydration begins, adding friction. Selected Segment as the event hub over direct GA4 to enable future BI destination additions without landing page code changes. Confirmed Vercel over Netlify for Edge Function and Analytics co-location with the Astro build.

metadata.notes: Revision 2 for AI contract risk analysis landing page. Fixes LCP regression via deferred demo widget hydration and achieves GDPR compliance via consent-gated analytics injection.

profile.domain: Legal Research - Court Opinion and Statute Corpus API

project_goal.value: REST API service exposing structured access to a 15M+ digitised court opinion, statute, and regulation corpus enriched with citation graphs, headnotes, and procedural history metadata, used by legal research SaaS products as a bulk data and search backend
project_goal.rationale: Partner legal research SaaS platforms need a reliable high-throughput corpus API for full-text search and citation analytics

target_users.value: Partner legal research SaaS engineering teams integrating the API into search and citation analysis product features
target_users.rationale: B2B partner API consumers; not direct end-user facing

project_class.rationale: Backend API with no frontend; partner-facing data access layer over legal corpus

capabilities.rationale: Search API, bulk export, partner authentication, usage analytics, and quota management

complexity_level.rationale: 15M+ documents, full-text search with facets, citation graph traversal, and nightly delta indexing at scale

risk_level.rationale: Public domain legal documents; no PII or financial data; partner API key authentication

data_sensitivity.rationale: Corpus enrichment (citation graph, headnotes) is internal proprietary asset on public domain documents

external_exposure.rationale: API accessible to approved partner platforms via API key; not open to general public

access_model.value: API key authentication with OAuth 2.0 client credentials for partners; per-partner rate limits and corpus subset access scopes enforced at API layer; quota tracking via BigQuery
access_model.rationale: Partners have different contract tiers with different corpus access rights and monthly document quotas

feature_scope.value: Full-text search with jurisdiction, date-range, court-level, and citation-count facets; document fetch by opinion/statute/regulation ID; citation graph traversal (citing, cited-by, citing-statutes up to 3 hops); bulk delta export as nightly Parquet snapshots by corpus scope; headnote and procedural history metadata; partner usage analytics; quota management
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for legal research SaaS partner integrations

mvp_scope.value: Full-text search with jurisdiction and date facets, document fetch by ID, citation graph one-hop traversal, nightly delta Parquet export, API key auth with per-second rate limiting
mvp_scope.rationale: Core search and export capabilities sufficient for initial partner integrations

security_baseline.value: API keys stored as SHA-256 hashes; HTTPS TLS 1.3 enforced at load balancer; API gateway rate limiting; Cloud Armor WAF blocking deep-pagination scraping patterns
security_baseline.rationale: Standard partner API security posture appropriate for low-risk public domain corpus

frontend_stack.value: None; API service only; partner usage analytics visible via Looker Studio dashboard linked from partner portal
frontend_stack.rationale: No frontend component in this service

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin framework; Elasticsearch 8 for full-text search and citation graph indexing; Redis 7 for query result caching and per-second rate limit counters
backend_stack.rationale: Go for high-throughput low-latency API; Elasticsearch for full-text search and aggregation at 15M+ document scale

data_platform.value: Elasticsearch 8 10-node cluster on GCP n2-standard-8 as primary search store; BigQuery for partner usage analytics; GCS for nightly Parquet delta exports
data_platform.rationale: Elasticsearch for sub-200ms document fetch and sub-500ms search; BigQuery for analytics without impacting search cluster

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in us-central1 with GCP HTTPS load balancer and Cloud Armor WAF
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot simplifies node management; Cloud Armor protects against corpus scraping abuse

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user PII stored; partner API key SHA-256 hashes retained 3 years for audit; request logs retained 1 year; corpus documents retained indefinitely
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No personal data in corpus; API logs retained for security audit

future_scope.value: Semantic search via dense vector embeddings (kNN in Elasticsearch); headnote classification endpoint; webhook notifications for new opinion publications by jurisdiction subscription; EMEA replica for partner latency
future_scope.rationale: Advanced legal research features and geographic expansion roadmap

constraints.value: Search API p99 latency under 500ms; document fetch p99 under 200ms; nightly delta export available by 06:00 UTC; support 500 concurrent partner connections
constraints.rationale: Partner SLA commitments in API contract

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring custom metrics for search latency and indexing lag; OpenTelemetry traces to Cloud Trace; partner-level request rate and error rate in Looker Studio
observability_baseline.rationale: Visibility into partner usage patterns and search performance critical for SLA management

execution_preference.value: API-first with OpenAPI 3.0 spec as contract; partner SDKs auto-generated from spec; versioned API (v1, v2) with 12-month deprecation policy
execution_preference.rationale: Partner integrations require stable API contracts

project.goal: High-throughput legal corpus search and bulk export API for partner legal research platforms
project.target_users: Partner SaaS engineering teams
project.timeline: v1.0 MVP in 10 weeks

backend.framework: Gin with middleware chain for auth, rate limiting, OTel tracing

security.auth: API key SHA-256 hash lookup in Redis partner registry
security.transport: TLS 1.3 via GCP HTTPS load balancer
security.abuse: Cloud Armor rules for deep pagination and scraping; Redis rate limit counters per partner

data.cache: Redis 7 for result cache and rate limit counters

devops.ci: Cloud Build: go vet, golangci-lint, go test ./... with Elasticsearch integration tests

reasoner_reviews.product: The round 1 plan correctly centres on search latency and bulk delta export as the primary partner value drivers. Citation graph traversal is a differentiating feature over commodity legal data APIs. The round 1 plan left partner corpus access scope enforcement underspecified — partners on different contract tiers (federal-only vs full corpus) must have their access enforced at the Elasticsearch query level, not just at the rate limit layer.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Elasticsearch index aliases per corpus scope tier is the correct mechanism for access control — middleware injects the alias into the Elasticsearch request context, preventing cross-scope access at the database routing layer rather than relying on application-layer filtering that could be bypassed. The round 1 plan described Redis caching but omitted the cache key strategy; cache keys must include corpus_scope to prevent one partner's cached results from being served to another partner with a different access tier.
reasoner_reviews.security: Partner API keys must be stored as SHA-256 hashes in the partner registry, never in plaintext — the round 1 plan did not specify this. Cloud Armor must include rules blocking deep pagination attacks (from offset > 1000 queries) since Elasticsearch performance degrades sharply beyond 1000 results per query window.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 500ms p99 search SLA requires warm Elasticsearch shard routing and Redis result caching. The nightly delta export must complete and be available at GCS by 06:00 UTC, requiring the export job to start no later than 03:00 UTC to allow buffer for 40M+ opinion corpus export time.

backend.service_design: Go Gin API with middleware chain: APIKeyAuthMiddleware (SHA-256 hash lookup in Redis partner registry), CorpusScopeMiddleware (injects Elasticsearch index alias for partner tier), RateLimitMiddleware (Redis INCR per-second counter per partner), OTelMiddleware (span creation with partner_id and corpus_scope attributes). Route groups: /v1/search, /v1/documents, /v1/citations, /v1/exports
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0 spec; paginated search with search_after cursor (avoids deep pagination memory pressure); citation graph endpoints return adjacency lists with configurable depth 1-3; bulk export returns GCS presigned URL to Parquet delta file
backend.failure_handling: Elasticsearch circuit breaker on 503 responses with 2-attempt retry and 100ms base backoff; Redis unavailability degrades gracefully — rate limiting disabled with alert fired, caching bypassed; all errors return structured JSON with request_id for partner debugging; application-level from_max=1000 guard returns 422 with search_after cursor guidance
backend.backend_risks: Elasticsearch deep-pagination attacks with large from offsets cause out-of-memory on data nodes; Cloud Armor and application-level from_max guard required before partner onboarding

security.auth_design: Partner registry in Redis: hash partner:{api_key_sha256} storing {partner_id, corpus_scope ENUM federal_only/full_corpus/statutes_only, rate_limit_rps INT, monthly_quota_docs INT}; X-API-Key header SHA-256 hashed and looked up per request
security.authorization_model: Elasticsearch index aliases per corpus scope: idx_federal_opinions (federal jurisdiction filter on opinions index), idx_full_corpus (all opinions, statutes, regulations), idx_statutes_only (statutes index); CorpusScopeMiddleware injects correct alias into request context; handlers never construct index names directly
security.abuse_prevention: Cloud Armor custom rule denying requests with from query param > 1000; Cloud Armor rate limit by X-Forwarded-For as secondary control; Redis per-second INCR counter per partner as primary rate control; API key revocation within 60s via Redis key deletion
security.security_risks: API key compromise allows corpus access up to the partner contracted scope; 90-day rotation recommended in partner agreement; revocation propagates within 60s Redis TTL

data.entities: opinions (opinion_id UUID, citation_string TEXT, jurisdiction_code KEYWORD, court_level KEYWORD, full_text TEXT with legal_text analyser, headnotes TEXT[], citation_refs KEYWORD[], citation_count INT, publication_date DATE, corpus_scope KEYWORD). statutes (statute_id, title, jurisdiction_code, section_number, full_text, effective_date). partner_usage_events (partner_id, endpoint, request_timestamp, documents_returned, response_ms, quota_consumed) in BigQuery.
data.storage_design: Elasticsearch 10-node opinions index with 2 primary shards x 1 replica per node; custom legal_text analyser with citation tokeniser (handles 'Brown v. Board' format), legal abbreviation normaliser, and Porter stemmer. Elasticsearch index aliases enforce corpus scope at routing layer. Redis partner registry (persistent RDB snapshot), result cache (5-min TTL, {corpus_scope}:{query_sha256} key), per-second rate counters (1s EX). GCS Parquet delta files partitioned by corpus_scope/YYYYMMDD.
data.data_risks: Elasticsearch index corruption on node failure; daily GCS snapshot with 30-day retention and quarterly restoration drill in staging

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot in us-central1; API Deployment with HPA min 3 / max 10 pods scaling on CPU 60% and custom search_latency_p99 metric; Elasticsearch on dedicated n2-standard-8 GCE nodes outside GKE; Redis Memorystore for GKE
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build on PR and main merge: go vet, golangci-lint, go test ./... with docker-compose Elasticsearch integration tests; on semver tag: docker build, push to Artifact Registry, helm upgrade to staging; smoke tests on staging; manual production promotion
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring: search_latency_p99 per endpoint, elasticsearch_indexing_lag_seconds, partner_quota_utilisation_pct. OTel traces to Cloud Trace. Looker Studio partner usage from BigQuery: daily requests by partner/endpoint, p99 latency trend, quota burn rate.
devops.devops_risks: GKE Autopilot node provisioning adds 60-90s on scale-out; pre-warm minimum 3 pods always running to handle baseline partner traffic; Elasticsearch rolling restarts coordinated with API deployment

ISS-001.title: Partner corpus access scope not enforced at Elasticsearch query level
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described per-partner rate limiting but did not specify Elasticsearch index aliases for corpus scope enforcement; a federal-only partner could query full corpus via undocumented endpoint parameters

history.note: Identified in architect review; index alias enforcement required before partner onboarding

ISS-002.title: Redis cache key does not include corpus_scope, risking cross-partner data leakage
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 caching design used query hash as cache key without corpus_scope prefix; a federal-only partner could receive cached results from a full-corpus partner's identical prior query containing restricted documents

history.note: Identified in security review; cache key must be scoped to corpus_scope tier

focus_issues.title: Partner corpus access scope not enforced at Elasticsearch query level
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan described rate limiting but not Elasticsearch index alias scope enforcement

focus_issues.title: Redis cache key does not include corpus_scope, risking cross-partner data leakage
focus_issues.detail: Cache key scoped only to query hash; cross-tier corpus scope leak possible

latest_recommendations: Implement Elasticsearch index aliases per corpus_scope tier and inject alias in CorpusScopeMiddleware
Cache key format must be {corpus_scope}:{query_sha256}:{search_after_cursor} to prevent cross-tier leakage while sharing hits within same tier
Specify API key SHA-256 hashing explicitly in security section
Add Cloud Armor rule blocking from query param > 1000 for deep pagination scraping defence
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Legal Corpus Search API Architecture

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity access control gaps unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Strong Elasticsearch architecture and Go API design overall, but two high-severity access control gaps must be resolved before partner onboarding: corpus scope not enforced at Elasticsearch query level, and Redis cache key scoping allows potential cross-partner document leakage.
strengths: Go Gin middleware chain design is clean and composable for adding reliability concerns
Elasticsearch custom legal_text analyser with citation tokeniser is domain-appropriate for opinion corpus
BigQuery for partner usage analytics decouples analytics workload from search cluster load
concerns: Corpus access scope enforcement described as application-layer filtering which could be bypassed
Cache key design does not account for different corpus access scope tiers per partner
API key storage mechanism not specified in security section
blocking_issues: Elasticsearch index alias per corpus_scope tier must be implemented before any restricted-scope partner is onboarded
Redis cache key must include corpus_scope to prevent a federal-only partner receiving full-corpus cached documents
recommendations: Define index aliases: idx_federal_opinions (federal jurisdiction filter), idx_full_corpus (all indices), idx_statutes_only; inject via CorpusScopeMiddleware
Cache key format: {corpus_scope}:{query_sha256}:{search_after_cursor} — enables same-tier cache sharing without cross-tier leakage
Store API keys as SHA-256 hashes in partner registry; log first 8 chars of key hash for debugging not plaintext
Block from > 1000 at application layer (422 with search_after guidance) and Cloud Armor layer

issue_updates.title: Partner corpus access scope not enforced at Elasticsearch query level
issue_updates.detail: architecture_overview and security_and_compliance did not specify Elasticsearch index alias enforcement mechanism

issue_updates.title: Redis cache key does not include corpus_scope
issue_updates.detail: system_components and observability sections described caching without specifying corpus_scope key prefix

previous_plan.title: Legal Corpus Search API Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22 Gin REST API on GKE Autopilot providing full-text search over 15M court opinions, statutes, and regulations via Elasticsearch 8; citation graph traversal; nightly Parquet delta exports to GCS; API key auth with per-partner rate limiting in Redis.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Go Gin API behind GCP HTTPS load balancer and Cloud Armor; middleware chain for auth, rate limiting, and OTel tracing; Elasticsearch 8 10-node cluster for search; Redis for result caching and rate limits; BigQuery for partner analytics. Corpus access scope described as application-layer filtering.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: API key authentication; TLS 1.3; Cloud Armor WAF; Redis rate limiting per partner. API key storage mechanism not specified. Cache key format not specified. Corpus scope enforcement at application layer only.
previous_plan.data_model: opinions, statutes, regulations in Elasticsearch. No index alias definitions for corpus scope tiers. Redis cache keys based on query hash only without corpus_scope prefix.

target_output.thinking_summary: This revision must fix two high-severity access control issues from round 1: corpus scope enforcement must move from application-layer filtering to Elasticsearch index alias routing (ISS-001), and the Redis cache key must be prefixed with corpus_scope to prevent cross-tier document leakage while preserving within-tier cache sharing (ISS-002). Both are structural changes to the middleware chain and data layer that affect architecture_overview, security_and_compliance, system_components, data_model, and workflows sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Elasticsearch index alias definitions (idx_federal_opinions, idx_full_corpus, idx_statutes_only) to data_model and system_components; added CorpusScopeMiddleware injecting alias into request context to architecture_overview, security_and_compliance, and workflows; removed application-layer filtering language
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partners with federal_only corpus scope cannot access state court opinions or statutes via any endpoint; enforced at Elasticsearch index routing layer before query execution

fix_report.action_taken: Updated Redis cache key format to {corpus_scope}:{query_sha256}:{search_after_cursor} in data_model, system_components, and api_design; added corpus_scope-scoped namespace explanation; added API key SHA-256 storage specification to security_and_compliance
fix_report.expected_outcome: Federal-only partner cannot receive cached search results containing full-corpus or statutes-only documents; partners on the same tier share cache hits improving Redis hit rate

target_output.title: Legal Corpus Search API Architecture — Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: Go 1.22 Gin REST API on GKE Autopilot providing full-text search, citation graph traversal, and bulk Parquet delta exports over a 15M+ court opinion, statute, and regulation corpus for partner legal research SaaS platforms. Elasticsearch index aliases enforce corpus access scope (federal-only, full corpus, statutes-only) at the database routing layer via CorpusScopeMiddleware. Redis result cache keys are prefixed with corpus_scope to prevent cross-tier document leakage while enabling within-tier cache sharing. API keys stored as SHA-256 hashes with per-second rate limits and monthly document quota tracking in BigQuery.
target_output.architecture_overview: Go Gin API on GKE Autopilot behind GCP HTTPS Load Balancer with Cloud Armor WAF. Request middleware chain: TLS termination at LB, X-API-Key SHA-256 hash lookup in Redis partner registry, corpus_scope resolved from partner record (federal_only/full_corpus/statutes_only), Elasticsearch index alias injected into request context by CorpusScopeMiddleware, per-second rate limit counter INCRed in Redis, OTel span created with partner_id and corpus_scope span attributes. Search and citation graph requests target corpus-scope-appropriate Elasticsearch aliases — idx_federal_opinions routes to federal circuit and district opinion documents only, idx_full_corpus routes to all 15M+ documents — enforcing licensing boundaries at the database routing layer. Redis result cache uses {corpus_scope}:{query_sha256}:{search_after_cursor} key format, enabling same-tier cache sharing while ensuring federal-only partners never receive full-corpus cached results. BigQuery receives streaming partner_usage_events for quota tracking and Looker Studio analytics dashboards.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22 with Gin framework, olivere/elastic v8 Go client for Elasticsearch, go-redis v9 for Redis, opentelemetry-go with W3C traceparent propagation. Search engine: Elasticsearch 8 with custom legal_text analyser (citation tokeniser for 'Plaintiff v. Defendant, Vol. Rep. (Year)' format, legal abbreviation normaliser, Porter stemmer). Cache and rate limiting: Redis Memorystore 7. Partner usage analytics: BigQuery streaming inserts. Nightly delta export: GCS Parquet files via go-parquet. CI: Cloud Build with docker-compose Elasticsearch integration tests. Hosting: GKE Autopilot, GCP HTTPS Load Balancer, Cloud Armor.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Full-Text Opinion Search: BM25 ranking with citation_count boost; jurisdiction (ENUM federal_circuit/federal_district/state_supreme/state_appellate), date_range, court_level, and citation_count_min facets; search_after cursor pagination; result highlight snippets. Citation Graph: citing opinions, cited-by opinions, and citing statutes traversal to configurable depth 1-3 returning adjacency list with metadata. Document Fetch: full opinion or statute by ID with headnotes, procedural_history, and citation_refs. Delta Export: GCS presigned URL to nightly Parquet snapshot partitioned by corpus_scope. Quota Management: per-second rate limiting via Redis and monthly document-fetch quota enforcement with 429 on breach and X-Quota-Remaining response header. Partner Usage Analytics: Looker Studio dashboard from BigQuery showing request volume, p99 latency, and monthly quota burn by partner.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot Deployment (3-10 pods HPA on CPU 60% and search_latency_p99 custom metric). GCP HTTPS Load Balancer with Cloud Armor (rules: DENY from > 1000, DENY scraping User-Agent patterns). Go Gin API with middleware chain: APIKeyAuthMiddleware, CorpusScopeMiddleware (index alias injection), RateLimitMiddleware, OTelMiddleware. Elasticsearch 8 cluster: 10 data nodes n2-standard-8, 3 dedicated master nodes, index aliases idx_federal_opinions/idx_full_corpus/idx_statutes_only/idx_regulations_only. Redis Memorystore 7: partner registry (persistent RDB), result cache ({corpus_scope}:{query_sha256} 5-min TTL), per-second rate counters (1s EX TTL). BigQuery dataset legal_corpus_analytics with partner_usage_events streaming table. GCS bucket legal-corpus-exports for Parquet delta files partitioned by corpus_scope/YYYYMMDD.
target_output.workflows: Partner Search Request: Partner sends GET /v1/search?q=disparate+impact&jurisdiction=9th_circuit&date_from=2018-01-01 with X-API-Key. APIKeyAuthMiddleware SHA-256 hashes the key, looks up partner:{key_sha256} in Redis to retrieve partner_id and corpus_scope=federal_only. CorpusScopeMiddleware injects index alias idx_federal_opinions into Gin context. RateLimitMiddleware INCRs partner:{partner_id}:rps with EX 1; returns 429 with Retry-After if over RPS limit. OpinionSearchHandler builds Elasticsearch bool query with jurisdiction:9th_circuit filter and date_range. Redis cache checked with key federal_only:{query_sha256}:{cursor}; hit returns in <5ms. Miss executes Elasticsearch query against idx_federal_opinions alias; result cached with 5-min TTL; response returned with search_after_cursor and X-Quota-Remaining. BigQuery streaming INSERT: partner_id, endpoint, response_ms, documents_returned, corpus_scope. Delta Export Generation: Cloud Scheduler triggers export Cloud Function at 03:00 UTC. Function queries Elasticsearch for opinions with publication_date >= yesterday partitioned by corpus_scope. Writes Parquet files to GCS legal-corpus-exports/{corpus_scope}/YYYYMMDD.parquet. On completion updates partner_usage_events with export_bytes; partners receive presigned GCS URL via POST to partner webhook endpoint.
target_output.data_model: Elasticsearch opinions index (15M docs): opinion_id KEYWORD, citation_string TEXT (e.g., 'Griggs v. Duke Power Co., 401 U.S. 424 (1971)'), jurisdiction_code KEYWORD, court_level KEYWORD, full_text TEXT (legal_text analyser), headnotes TEXT[], procedural_history TEXT, citation_refs KEYWORD[], citation_count INT, publication_date DATE, corpus_scope KEYWORD. Elasticsearch statutes index (500K docs): statute_id, title, jurisdiction_code, section_number, full_text (legal_text analyser), effective_date DATE. Elasticsearch index alias definitions: idx_federal_opinions (filter query: jurisdiction_code in [9th_circuit, 5th_circuit, ...all_federal_circuits, federal_district]), idx_full_corpus (all indices, no filter), idx_statutes_only (statutes index only), idx_regulations_only (regulations index only). Redis partner registry hash partner:{api_key_sha256}: {partner_id STRING, corpus_scope ENUM, rate_limit_rps INT, monthly_quota_docs INT}. Redis result cache key: {corpus_scope}:{query_sha256}:{search_after_cursor} with 5-min TTL — enables same-tier sharing, prevents cross-tier leakage. BigQuery partner_usage_events: partner_id STRING, endpoint STRING, request_timestamp TIMESTAMP, documents_returned INT64, response_ms INT64, quota_consumed INT64, corpus_scope STRING.
target_output.api_design: GET /v1/search — headers: X-API-Key; params: q (required), jurisdiction (multi), date_from, date_to, court_level, citation_count_min, size (max 50), search_after cursor; returns {results: [{opinion_id, citation_string, headline_snippet, publication_date, citation_count}], total_hits, search_after_cursor}; 422 if from param > 1000 with message 'use search_after cursor for pagination beyond 1000 results'. GET /v1/documents/{id} — returns full document with full_text, headnotes[], and procedural_history. GET /v1/citations/{opinion_id} — params: direction (citing/cited_by/citing_statutes), depth (1-3); returns adjacency list. GET /v1/exports/delta — returns {export_url STRING (GCS presigned, 1h TTL), corpus_scope, record_count, generated_at}. Response headers on all endpoints: X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-Quota-Remaining, X-Request-Id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API keys stored as SHA-256 hashes in Redis partner registry; plaintext key returned only at issuance, never stored; log entries record first 8 characters of key SHA hash for debugging without plaintext exposure. TLS 1.3 enforced at GCP HTTPS LB; Go API pods not directly internet-accessible. Corpus access scope enforced at Elasticsearch alias routing layer via CorpusScopeMiddleware — handlers receive pre-injected index alias and must never construct Elasticsearch index names directly; this prevents application-layer bypass. Redis cache namespace isolation: federal_only cache entries keyed under 'federal_only:' prefix; full_corpus under 'full_corpus:' — no cross-namespace reads are possible since namespaces are injected from partner registry, not from request parameters. Cloud Armor: DENY rule for requests with 'from=' query param value > 1000; DENY rule for common scraping User-Agent signatures (HTTrack, Scrapy, SiteSucker). Per-IP secondary rate limit at Cloud Armor as fallback if Redis unavailable. API key revocation: delete Redis hash key; takes effect within 60s across all API pods.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build CI on every PR: go vet, golangci-lint (errcheck, gosec, staticcheck), go test ./... with Elasticsearch integration tests against docker-compose 3-node cluster; on semver tag: docker build, push to Artifact Registry, helm upgrade --install to GKE staging; smoke test suite (search, fetch, citation, export, quota enforcement) against staging cluster; manual production promotion gate. GKE HPA: min 3 / max 10 pods; scale triggers on CPU > 60% or custom metric legal_corpus_api/search_latency_p99 > 400ms (pre-SLA-breach early warning). Elasticsearch rolling restarts coordinated with API deployment: Elasticsearch shard relocation must complete before API pods updated to avoid routing queries to upgrading nodes. Daily Elasticsearch GCS snapshot with 30-day retention; restoration drill in staging quarterly. Redis Memorystore maintenance window 03:00-04:00 UTC Sunday; API degrades gracefully without caching during window.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: legal_corpus_api/search_latency_p99 (target <500ms, alert at 400ms), legal_corpus_api/document_fetch_p99 (target <200ms), legal_corpus_api/elasticsearch_indexing_lag_seconds, legal_corpus_api/redis_cache_hit_rate (by corpus_scope namespace), legal_corpus_api/partner_quota_utilisation_pct. OpenTelemetry traces to Cloud Trace with span attributes: partner_id, corpus_scope, elasticsearch_index_alias, elasticsearch_shards_queried, cache_hit BOOL, retry_attempt INT. Looker Studio partner usage dashboard sourced from BigQuery: daily request volume by partner and endpoint, p99 latency trend, quota burn rate vs monthly limit, error rate by HTTP status. PagerDuty P1 on search_latency_p99 > 450ms sustained 5 minutes or 5xx error rate > 1%; P2 on any partner quota_utilisation_pct > 90%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Elasticsearch 10-node n2-standard-8 cluster: ~$2800/month GCP Compute Engine. GKE Autopilot API pods: ~$150/month at 3-pod baseline. Redis Memorystore m1-standard-4: ~$200/month. BigQuery streaming inserts: ~$40/month at 10M events/day. GCS Parquet exports: ~$20/month. Total ~$3210/month. Scaling: additional Elasticsearch data nodes handle corpus growth to 50M+ documents; GKE HPA auto-scales API pods for traffic bursts; Redis corpus_scope cache namespacing limits memory growth by partitioning cache entries by tier.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Elasticsearch cluster setup with opinions index and legal_text custom analyser, index alias definitions for all four corpus scope tiers, Go Gin API with full middleware chain (auth/scope/rate-limit/OTel), search endpoint with jurisdiction and date facets, document fetch by ID, citation graph 1-hop, API key management in Redis partner registry with SHA-256 hashing, nightly delta Parquet export to GCS, Cloud Build CI with Elasticsearch integration tests, GKE Autopilot deployment, Cloud Armor rules for deep pagination defence, Looker Studio basic usage dashboard. Phase 2 (6 weeks after MVP): Statutes and regulations indices, citation graph 2-3 hop traversal, BigQuery monthly quota tracking with enforcement, partner SDK generation from OpenAPI spec, headnote and procedural history enrichment in search response, SFTP batch indexing pipeline for new opinion ingestion. Phase 3 (future): Elasticsearch kNN dense vector semantic search; webhook notification subscriptions for new opinion publications; EMEA replica deployment for low-latency European partner access.
target_output.development_guidelines: OpenAPI 3.0 spec is the authoritative API contract — oapi-codegen validates spec conformance in Cloud Build CI; spec regressions block merge. CorpusScopeMiddleware is the exclusive source of Elasticsearch index alias — handlers must not construct index names; integration tests include assertions that federal_only partner cannot receive full-corpus documents. golangci-lint with errcheck enforced — no unhandled errors in request handlers. All test fixtures include partner_federal_only and partner_full_corpus personas to validate alias scoping in every search integration test. Cache key format documented in ARCHITECTURE.md; deviations require security review comment on PR.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Elasticsearch node failure reduces search capacity and may breach p99 SLA during node replacement. Mitigation: replica shards maintain query availability; GKE HPA reduces API pod count to match reduced ES capacity; Cloud Monitoring alert on red cluster health. Risk: Partner API key compromise allows corpus access up to contracted tier. Mitigation: 90-day rotation policy; revocation within 60s; Cloud Armor IP allowlisting available for partners requiring it. Tradeoff: Elasticsearch index alias per corpus scope vs Elasticsearch field-level security — alias approach avoids X-Pack Security license cost and is simpler operationally; field-level security offers more granular control if document-level access policies are needed in the future. Tradeoff: Redis result cache vs no cache — adds operational complexity but necessary to meet 500ms p99 under partner burst load; corpus_scope key namespacing adds minimal overhead.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Elasticsearch index aliases over field-level security for corpus scope enforcement — simpler, no additional license, enforced at routing layer. Chose corpus_scope as Redis cache key prefix over partner_id — enables cache sharing between partners on the same tier improving hit rate; partner_id prefix would fragment cache unnecessarily. Chose search_after cursor pagination over from/size to prevent deep pagination memory pressure on Elasticsearch data nodes per Elasticsearch best practices for large corpora. Chose BigQuery streaming inserts for partner_usage_events over Postgres to avoid adding load to search infrastructure.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision for 15M-document legal corpus search API; fixed Elasticsearch index alias scope enforcement (ISS-001) and Redis cache key corpus_scope prefixing (ISS-002).

profile.domain: Insurance Defense Legal Medical Record Management

project_goal.value: Desktop application for insurance defense paralegals to manage medical record requests, track provider response timelines, organize received records by treatment episode, and generate attorney summary chronologies
project_goal.rationale: Replaces costly third-party medical record retrieval services with an in-house managed workflow reducing per-case retrieval costs

target_users.value: Insurance defense paralegals and attorneys at law firms handling personal injury and workers compensation litigation
target_users.rationale: Primary workflow users managing medical record collection for litigation cases

project_class.rationale: Local Electron application with embedded Python sidecar; no cloud hosting required; operates on workstations within law firm network

capabilities.rationale: Electron UI, local Python processing sidecar, SQLite persistence; no auth service, AI, or external integrations beyond local file system

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-entity data model with deadline tracking logic but bounded scope for a local desktop application

risk_level.rationale: Handles protected health information (PHI) under HIPAA; attorney-client privilege applies to case strategy notes

data_sensitivity.rationale: Medical records contain PHI including diagnoses, treatment histories, medication records, and provider notes

external_exposure.rationale: Application runs entirely on workstation; no external network services; data stays on local disk

access_model.value: Local user accounts with bcrypt password hashing; session-based auth with auto-lock after 15 minutes inactivity
access_model.rationale: HIPAA minimum necessary access control; session lock prevents unauthorized access on unattended workstations

feature_scope.value: Medical record request creation with provider and date range specification, authorization form generation, provider response deadline tracking with follow-up reminders, received record ingestion and treatment episode organization, chronology summary generation for attorney review, case export to PDF
feature_scope.rationale: Full workflow from request creation through attorney-ready chronology delivery

mvp_scope.value: Record request creation with deadline tracking, received PDF ingestion and case association, basic chronology template populated with provider and date range data
mvp_scope.rationale: Core paralegal workflow for minimum viable practice use

security_baseline.value: SQLite database encrypted with SQLCipher AES-256; local user accounts with bcrypt passwords; auto-lock after 15 minutes inactivity; Sentry local crash reporting with no PHI in crash payloads
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA workstation security controls for PHI stored on local disk

frontend_stack.value: Electron 31 with React 18 and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Electron for cross-platform desktop distribution; React for component-based UI with TypeScript type safety

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 embedded sidecar process launched by Electron main process via IPC; FastAPI for local IPC HTTP server on localhost port
backend_stack.rationale: Python for PDF processing and chronology generation; FastAPI for clean IPC contract between Electron renderer and Python sidecar

data_platform.value: SQLite with SQLCipher AES-256 encryption for all case data; PDFs stored on local filesystem in case-specific directories
data_platform.rationale: SQLite suitable for single-workstation data volumes; SQLCipher provides HIPAA-grade encryption at rest without infrastructure overhead

hosting_target.value: Electron desktop application distributed via signed installer (NSIS on Windows, DMG on macOS); auto-update via electron-updater from private update server
hosting_target.rationale: Law firm workstations require signed installers for IT approval; auto-update for security patch delivery

privacy_retention_policy.value: Medical records retained per case retention schedule (minimum 7 years post-case closure); local database backup encrypted; no cloud sync; HIPAA minimum necessary access per user account
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA record retention and minimum necessary PHI access requirements

future_scope.value: Integration with practice management system for case sync, batch authorization form mailing via certified mail integration, AI-assisted treatment episode detection from PDF text
future_scope.rationale: Future integrations after stable standalone tool deployment

constraints.value: Must run on Windows 10/11 workstations with no cloud connectivity requirement; installer must pass Windows Defender SmartScreen via code signing; PDF processing must handle multi-hundred-page medical record packages
constraints.rationale: Law firm IT environment constraints and typical medical record document sizes

observability_baseline.value: Sentry local crash reporter capturing error type, stack trace, and app version only - no PHI in crash payloads; in-app activity log for audit trail of record access events
observability_baseline.rationale: HIPAA workstation access audit log requirement and crash diagnostics without PHI exposure

execution_preference.value: Desktop-first iterative delivery: core CRUD and deadline tracking first, then PDF ingestion, then chronology generation
execution_preference.rationale: Paralegal workflow validation before investing in PDF processing complexity

compliance_context.value: HIPAA minimum necessary standard for PHI access; HIPAA Security Rule workstation security controls; state-specific medical authorization form requirements for California, New York, Florida, Texas
compliance_context.rationale: PHI in medical records requires HIPAA compliance; state authorization form formats vary by jurisdiction where claimants are treated

project.goal: HIPAA-compliant desktop app for insurance defense paralegal medical record request workflow
project.target_users: Insurance defense paralegals and attorneys on Windows workstations
project.timeline: Round 3 addressing SQLCipher salt and PDF episode bundling gaps

security.encryption: SQLCipher AES-256 with per-database random salt
security.auth: Local bcrypt accounts + auto-lock
security.phi_logging: In-app audit log; no PHI in Sentry

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: pytest, Jest, Playwright E2E

reasoner_reviews.product: The ISS-003 static salt is a HIPAA Security Rule violation risk for PHI at-rest protection. The ISS-002 bundled record issue means paralegals cannot efficiently organize records from retrieval services that bundle multiple providers into a single PDF - which is common in high-volume litigation. Both are workflow-blocking for the target users.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ISS-003 fix is clear: generate 32 random bytes at database creation using os.urandom(32), write to case_db.salt alongside the encrypted database, and use PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with 600,000 iterations. ISS-002 fallback: use pdfplumber to extract dates from the PDF text layer, cluster pages by 90-day date windows using a simple sliding window, and assign each cluster as a separate episode with split_method='date_window'.
reasoner_reviews.security: The per-database random salt must be stored separately from the encrypted database to prevent an attacker with physical access from computing a rainbow table against the static application salt. The salt file should be stored at case_db.salt in the same directory as case_db.db; both files must be backed up together.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: pdfplumber date extraction from 500-page PDFs may take 15-30 seconds; this should run in the Python sidecar background task with a React Query loading state in the UI. 600,000 PBKDF2 iterations will add ~500ms to database open time on standard workstation hardware; acceptable given one-time cost per session.

backend.service_design: Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar on localhost:8765; endpoints for PDF ingestion, episode splitting, form generation, chronology assembly; launched by Electron main process via child_process.spawn
backend.business_modules: MedicalRecordProcessor (pdfplumber text extraction, provider detection, treatment date extraction), EpisodeSplitter (metadata-based primary + date-window fallback), AuthFormGenerator (Jinja2 state-specific templates), ChronologyBuilder (episode timeline assembly, provider grouping, attorney summary formatting)
backend.failure_handling: Python sidecar crash restarts automatically via Electron child_process 'close' event listener; React renderer shows error toast and retries IPC call; PDF parse errors log to in-app audit log without crashing

frontend.app_structure: Electron renderer React SPA with react-router; main sections: Cases, Requests, Received Records, Chronologies; Electron main process exposes contextBridge API for sidecar IPC and SQLite access
frontend.pages_and_flows: Case Dashboard (open cases with overdue request indicators), Request Creation (provider form + state selector + date ranges + auth form preview), Provider Tracker (pending requests by deadline with follow-up action buttons), Record Inbox (PDF upload + episode assignment review), Chronology Editor (episode timeline table + narrative generation + PDF export)
frontend.state_management: React Query for sidecar API calls with cache invalidation on record mutations; Zustand for session state (current user, case filter, drawer open state)

security.auth_design: Local user accounts stored in SQLite users table with bcrypt-hashed passwords; Electron session cookie for auth state; auto-lock timer resets on IPC activity; lock screen renders on timer expiry
security.privacy_controls: PHI access logged to audit_events SQLite table with user_id, action, case_id, record_id, timestamp; Sentry beforeSend hook strips any field containing patient_name, dob, or mrn patterns; no PHI transmitted externally
security.audit_and_logging_controls: In-app audit log viewer for paralegals and supervising attorneys; audit_events table append-only (no DELETE or UPDATE statements on audit rows); exported as CSV for HIPAA compliance documentation

data.entities: medical_record_requests (id, case_id, provider_id, claimant_id, date_range_start, date_range_end, auth_form_state, status, due_date, sent_at, received_at), providers (id, name, address, fax, contact_name, record_request_preference), claimants (id, name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, ssn_last4_encrypted, case_id), treatment_episodes (id, request_id, provider_id, episode_start_date, episode_end_date, page_range, split_method ENUM metadata/date_window/manual), received_records (id, request_id, file_path, received_date, page_count, ingested_at), chronology_summaries (id, case_id, generated_at, episode_ids_json, summary_text_encrypted, pdf_export_path), authorization_forms (id, request_id, state_jurisdiction, template_version, generated_at, form_file_path)
data.storage_design: SQLite 3.45 with SQLCipher 4.5 AES-256 CBC; per-database random salt stored in case_db.salt file; PDF files stored at AppData/MedRecords/{case_id}/{request_id}/ on Windows
data.retention_and_deletion: Cases marked closed prevent accidental deletion; manual archive export creates encrypted ZIP for long-term storage; in-app backup creates SQLCipher snapshot to user-selected location

devops.deployment_topology: Electron desktop bundle with embedded Python 3.12 interpreter (PyInstaller --onedir mode); NSIS installer for Windows signed with EV code signing certificate; DMG for macOS signed and notarized
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest for Python sidecar, Jest for React components, Playwright for E2E workflows on headed Electron; electron-builder for installer artifacts on tag push
devops.rollback_strategy: electron-updater allows rollback to previous version via admin override; SQLite schema migrations versioned with up/down scripts; migration run on app start with version check

ISS-001.title: Authorization form generation does not support state-specific form variants
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 and Round 2 plans used a single generic authorization form template; California CMIA, New York HIPAA form, and Florida-specific authorization forms have different required fields and attorney certification language that are not compatible with a single template

history.note: Single generic auth form template described
State-specific templates still not addressed despite audit feedback

ISS-002.title: PDF treatment episode splitting uses only provider metadata without date-based segmentation fallback
ISS-002.detail: The PDF ingestion pipeline splits received records into treatment episodes by matching provider name from PDF metadata, but multi-provider records bundled by the same retrieval service lack per-provider metadata, causing the entire bundle to be assigned to a single episode regardless of treatment dates

history.note: Episode splitting relies solely on PDF metadata provider field; bundled records missing metadata are not split

ISS-003.title: SQLCipher key derivation uses static salt rather than per-database random salt
ISS-003.detail: The database encryption implementation described in round 2 derives the SQLCipher key from PBKDF2(user_password, static_salt) where static_salt is a hardcoded constant in the application binary, reducing the brute-force resistance of the encryption key

history.note: Static salt identified in security review; per-database random salt not described

focus_issues.title: PDF treatment episode splitting fails for bundled records without per-provider metadata
focus_issues.detail: Episode splitting relies solely on PDF metadata provider field; bundled multi-provider records are assigned to a single episode

focus_issues.title: SQLCipher key derivation uses static salt reducing encryption strength
focus_issues.detail: PBKDF2 derivation uses hardcoded static_salt constant; per-database random salt required for HIPAA-grade at-rest encryption

latest_recommendations: Add date-range-based treatment episode fallback: when PDF metadata lacks provider field, split episodes by 90-day treatment date windows extracted from the PDF text layer via pdfplumber

Generate a random 32-byte salt at database creation time, store it in a dedicated salt file alongside the encrypted SQLite file, and use PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with 600,000 iterations using the per-database salt
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Medical Record Request Manager - Round 2

Describe date-based episode fallback for PDFs without provider metadata

Treatment episode date range tracking improved
concerns: SQLCipher key derivation static salt is a HIPAA security control weakness (ISS-003)
PDF episode splitting for bundled records without metadata still relies on provider field only (ISS-002)

Add pdfplumber date extraction fallback for episode splitting: when PDF metadata provider field absent, extract treatment dates from text layer and group into 90-day episode windows
