target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for insurance defense paralegal medical record desktop app. ISS-003 fix: per-database random 32-byte salt generated at database creation via os.urandom(32), stored in case_db.salt, used with PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 at 600,000 iterations for SQLCipher key derivation. ISS-002 fix: date-window episode splitting fallback using pdfplumber text extraction clustering pages into 90-day treatment date windows when PDF metadata lacks provider field. Both fixes localized to Python sidecar modules with no Electron frontend changes needed.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced static PBKDF2 salt with per-database random salt: DatabaseManager.create() calls os.urandom(32) and writes salt to case_db.salt; DatabaseManager.open() reads salt from case_db.salt before PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 key derivation with 600,000 iterations; salt file is excluded from version control and backed up alongside database in encrypted ZIP export
fix_report.expected_outcome: SQLCipher key derivation is database-specific; attacker with binary access cannot precompute keys against application-embedded static salt; meets HIPAA Security Rule workstation PHI encryption standard

fix_report.action_taken: Added EpisodeSplitter.date_window_split() fallback method in Python sidecar: uses pdfplumber to extract dates matching medical record date patterns (MM/DD/YYYY, Month DD YYYY) from each page; clusters consecutive pages with dates within a 90-day window using a sliding window algorithm; assigns each cluster as a treatment_episode with split_method='date_window'; paralegal can review and adjust episode boundaries in Record Inbox UI before saving
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bundled multi-provider record packages without per-provider PDF metadata are split into plausible treatment episodes based on date clustering; paralegal reviews and confirms boundaries before committing

target_output.title: Medical Record Request Manager - Round 3
target_output.executive_summary: Round 3 revision of the Electron 31 + React 18 desktop application for insurance defense paralegal medical record workflows. Resolves HIPAA PHI encryption gap (ISS-003) with per-database random salt for SQLCipher key derivation. Adds date-window episode splitting fallback (ISS-002) for bundled multi-provider PDFs lacking per-provider metadata. Application manages the full workflow from record request creation through state-specific authorization form generation, provider deadline tracking, episode-organized record ingestion, and attorney chronology generation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron 31 main process launches a Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar on localhost:8765 via child_process.spawn; the sidecar is a PyInstaller-bundled executable embedded in the Electron application package. React 18 renderer process communicates with the sidecar via Electron's contextBridge-exposed fetch API for PDF processing, form generation, and chronology operations. SQLite with SQLCipher 4.5 AES-256 stores all case data; key derived via PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256(password, per_db_random_salt, 600000) where the 32-byte random salt is generated at database creation and stored in a companion case_db.salt file. PDFs stored in case-specific subdirectories under AppData/MedRecords/. Jinja2 state-specific authorization form templates (CA, NY, FL, TX) in Python sidecar. Episode splitting uses provider metadata as primary method and pdfplumber date-window clustering as fallback for bundled records.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 31, React 18, TypeScript, react-router v6, React Query, Zustand, Tailwind CSS. Backend sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, pdfplumber for PDF text and metadata extraction, PyInstaller for sidecar bundling, Jinja2 for authorization form templates, SQLCipher via sqlcipher3 Python binding. Database: SQLite 3.45 with SQLCipher 4.5 AES-256 CBC. Encryption: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 (600,000 iterations) with per-database random salt. Distribution: electron-builder (NSIS Windows, DMG macOS), EV code signing certificate, electron-updater for auto-update. Testing: pytest (sidecar), Jest + React Testing Library (renderer), Playwright (Electron E2E). Crash reporting: Sentry with PHI-scrubbing beforeSend hook.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Case Dashboard: open cases with status summary, overdue request count badge, quick-add request button. Record Request Creation: provider selector, claimant data, treatment date ranges, state jurisdiction selector (CA/NY/FL/TX/Other), authorization form preview with state-specific template, due date calculator based on jurisdiction response requirements. Provider Tracker: pending requests sorted by due date with urgency color coding; one-click resend follow-up letter generation; received date recording. Record Inbox: PDF upload and association to request; EpisodeSplitter runs provider metadata detection first, falls back to date-window clustering for bundled records; paralegal reviews proposed episode boundaries in interactive PDF viewer before confirming. Chronology Generator: selects case episodes, calls ChronologyBuilder sidecar endpoint, presents editable treatment timeline table with provider, date range, and summary columns; PDF export via ReportLab.
target_output.system_components: Electron main process (app lifecycle, IPC bridge, Python sidecar spawn/restart, SQLite contextBridge exposure). React renderer (SPA with react-router, React Query, Zustand, Tailwind). Python FastAPI sidecar (localhost:8765): DatabaseManager (SQLCipher open/create with per-db salt), MedicalRecordProcessor (pdfplumber text extraction, provider name detection), EpisodeSplitter (metadata-primary + date-window fallback split logic), AuthFormGenerator (Jinja2 CA/NY/FL/TX templates), ChronologyBuilder (episode timeline assembly, ReportLab PDF export). SQLite+SQLCipher database (case data + audit log). Local filesystem (PDFs at AppData/MedRecords/{case_id}/{request_id}/). case_db.salt companion file (per-database random 32-byte salt). Sentry local crash reporter (PHI-scrubbed payloads only).
target_output.workflows: Medical Record Request Creation: paralegal opens Case, creates new Record Request, selects treating providers from provider directory, enters claimant date ranges, selects state jurisdiction; AuthFormGenerator renders state-specific Jinja2 template (CA CMIA form, NY HIPAA form, FL authorization, TX medical release) populated with claimant and attorney data; paralegal previews and prints form; request saved with due_date calculated from jurisdiction response deadline rules. Received Record Ingestion (bundled PDF): paralegal drops received PDF in Record Inbox; sidecar calls MedicalRecordProcessor.extract_metadata(); if PDF metadata contains provider field, EpisodeSplitter.metadata_split() creates episodes per provider; if metadata lacks provider field, EpisodeSplitter.date_window_split() runs pdfplumber over all pages extracting dates, clusters consecutive pages with dates within 90-day window, proposes episode clusters to paralegal in boundary review UI; paralegal accepts, adjusts, or manually overrides episode boundaries; confirmed episodes saved with split_method recorded. Chronology Generation: paralegal selects case and episode date range; ChronologyBuilder sidecar endpoint assembles episode timeline sorted by date, generates provider-grouped narrative with treatment date summaries; paralegal edits narrative in editor pane; PDF export via ReportLab with firm letterhead template.
target_output.data_model: medical_record_requests (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK, provider_id UUID FK, claimant_id UUID FK, date_range_start DATE, date_range_end DATE, auth_form_state ENUM ca/ny/fl/tx/other, auth_form_path TEXT, status ENUM draft/sent/follow_up/received/closed, due_date DATE, sent_at TIMESTAMP, received_at TIMESTAMP). providers (id UUID PK, name TEXT, address_encrypted TEXT, fax TEXT, contact_name TEXT). claimants (id UUID PK, name_encrypted TEXT, dob_encrypted TEXT, ssn_last4_encrypted TEXT, case_id UUID FK). treatment_episodes (id UUID PK, request_id UUID FK, provider_id UUID FK, episode_start_date DATE, episode_end_date DATE, page_range_start INT, page_range_end INT, split_method ENUM metadata/date_window/manual, confirmed_by_user BOOLEAN). received_records (id UUID PK, request_id UUID FK, file_path TEXT, received_date DATE, page_count INT, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). chronology_summaries (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK, generated_at TIMESTAMP, episode_ids_json TEXT, summary_text_encrypted TEXT, pdf_export_path TEXT). authorization_forms (id UUID PK, request_id UUID FK, state_jurisdiction TEXT, template_version TEXT, generated_at TIMESTAMP, form_file_path TEXT). audit_events (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, action TEXT, case_id UUID, record_id UUID, timestamp TIMESTAMP) - append-only. salt stored at case_db.salt: 32-byte random; key = PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256(password, salt, 600000).
target_output.api_design: Python sidecar REST API (localhost:8765, accessible only from Electron renderer via contextBridge fetch): POST /records/ingest {file_path, request_id} -> {episode_candidates[], split_method_used}; POST /records/confirm-episodes {request_id, episodes[]} -> {saved_episode_ids[]}; POST /forms/generate {request_id, state_jurisdiction, claimant_data} -> {form_file_path}; POST /chronology/build {case_id, episode_ids[]} -> {summary_text, pdf_path}; GET /health -> {status, sidecar_version}. Electron contextBridge exposes: db.query(sql, params) for SQLite reads; db.execute(sql, params) for writes; sidecar.call(endpoint, body) for Python sidecar IPC.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher AES-256 CBC encryption for all PHI at rest; key derivation: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256(user_password, per_db_random_salt, iterations=600000) where per_db_random_salt = os.urandom(32) written to case_db.salt at database creation; salt file backed up alongside database. Local user accounts: bcrypt-hashed passwords in SQLite users table; session token in Electron memory only; auto-lock after 15 minutes inactivity resets renderer state. Sentry beforeSend hook scrubs fields matching patient_name, dob, mrn, ssn patterns before transmitting crash payload. HIPAA audit log: audit_events table in SQLite; append-only enforcement via SQLite trigger preventing UPDATE/DELETE on audit_events; export to CSV for compliance documentation. State authorization forms: CA uses CMIA-compliant template, NY uses HIPAA authorization with NY-specific fields, FL uses Florida Chapter 456 authorization, TX uses Texas Health & Safety Code 181 compliant release.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest for Python sidecar with coverage >85%, Jest for React renderer components, Playwright E2E for core workflows (request creation, PDF ingestion, chronology export) on headed Electron in Windows Server runner. Release build: electron-builder packages PyInstaller sidecar bundle into Electron asar; NSIS installer for Windows signed with EV certificate (bypasses SmartScreen); DMG for macOS signed and notarized via Apple Developer ID. Auto-update: electron-updater checks private update server on app launch; update downloaded in background, paralegal prompted to restart. SQLite schema migrations via versioned up/down scripts run on app start; migration history stored in db_migrations table.
target_output.observability: In-app audit log table (audit_events) visible to supervising attorneys in Admin panel; records all PHI access events (record viewed, chronology generated, form printed) with user, case, and timestamp. Sentry local crash reporter: beforeSend strips PHI fields; captures Electron main process uncaught exceptions and Python sidecar stderr; events uploaded to Sentry on next network-available session. In-app activity feed shows recent case actions for paralegal session context. No server-side metrics; no telemetry beyond crash reports.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: One-time development cost; no recurring infrastructure cost beyond auto-update server hosting (~$10/month on S3 + CloudFront for installer distribution). SQLite scales to tens of thousands of cases on a single workstation without performance issues. PDF processing for 500-page records takes 15-30 seconds in Python sidecar; React Query loading state with progress indicator prevents UI blocking.
target_output.phased_implementation: Round 3 deliverables: per-database random salt for SQLCipher (ISS-003), pdfplumber date-window episode splitting fallback with paralegal review UI (ISS-002), unit tests for salt generation/rotation and episode splitting algorithm. Phase 1 complete (prior rounds): core CRUD, deadline tracking, state-specific authorization forms. Phase 2 (next): practice management system sync, batch mailing integration. Phase 3 (future): AI-assisted treatment episode detection from PDF text using local embedding model.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python sidecar: DatabaseManager must have pytest tests for per-database salt generation (each call to create() produces unique salt), database open with correct salt succeeds, open with wrong salt raises SQLCipher decryption error. EpisodeSplitter must have tests for metadata-primary path (provider field present), date-window fallback path (no metadata), manual override path. All PHI fields in SQLite must use AES-256 column-level encryption via cryptography.fernet before storage. Electron renderer: React Testing Library tests for Request Creation form with jurisdiction selector; Playwright E2E for full Request -> Ingest -> Chronology workflow on Windows runner. No hardcoded salt values in tests; test databases use ephemeral os.urandom(32) salts.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk (ISS-003 resolved): per-database random salt eliminates static salt rainbow table vulnerability; PBKDF2 at 600,000 iterations adds ~500ms database open time, acceptable for single-session cost. Risk (ISS-002 resolved): date-window fallback provides plausible episode splits for bundled records; paralegal confirmation step before committing prevents incorrect auto-splits from silently entering case records. Remaining risk: pdfplumber date extraction accuracy varies with scan quality of older medical records; low-confidence extractions should surface page-level date counts to help paralegal make informed boundary decisions. Tradeoff: PyInstaller sidecar bundling increases installer size (~120MB) but eliminates Python environment dependency on paralegal workstations.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed per-database salt stored at case_db.salt alongside case_db.db (not in database itself). Confirmed 600,000 PBKDF2 iterations per OWASP 2024 guidance. Confirmed date-window cluster size as 90 days based on typical treatment episode patterns in personal injury litigation. Confirmed paralegal review step before auto-split commit to prevent incorrect episode assignments.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision for HIPAA-compliant desktop medical record manager; fixes SQLCipher static salt (ISS-003) and PDF bundled record episode splitting (ISS-002).

profile.domain: Legal Practice Management Migration

project_goal.value: Java SDK for migrating structured matter and client data from PCLaw and Tabs3 practice management systems to modern cloud-based legal management platforms, providing mapping definitions, validation helpers, and rollback-safe import utilities
project_goal.rationale: Law firms need a reliable, rollback-safe programmatic migration path from legacy on-premise billing systems

target_users.value: IT consultants and system integrators serving law firms undergoing platform transitions from PCLaw or Tabs3 to cloud CLM
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are technical operators running migrations on behalf of law firm clients

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a Maven artifact consumed by integrator tooling; no hosted service

capabilities.rationale: Pure data transformation library with no frontend or hosted backend components

complexity_level.rationale: Complex field mapping and rollback logic but bounded scope as a library artifact

risk_level.rationale: Handles confidential client and financial data; errors during migration could corrupt IOLTA trust ledger balances

data_sensitivity.rationale: Matter records, client PII, and IOLTA trust account balances are legally privileged and bar-regulated

external_exposure.rationale: Library runs in the integrator environment with no public API surface

access_model.value: API key authentication to target platform APIs; library runs under invoking process credentials
access_model.rationale: No multi-user access model needed; library operates under the integrator process identity

feature_scope.value: PCLaw and Tabs3 export parsing, configurable field-mapping YAML definitions, target platform import utilities, multi-stage validation pipeline, rollback-safe transaction commits, migration state persistence via checkpoint store
feature_scope.rationale: Full migration lifecycle from extraction through validated import

mvp_scope.value: PCLaw fixed-width text parsing, matter and client record mapping, target API import with rollback on validation failure, JSON checkpoint idempotency
mvp_scope.rationale: Core migration path for the most common source system first

security_baseline.value: No credentials stored in library code; all API keys passed via environment or MigrationConfig; sensitive fields zeroed from memory after use; no contract field values in log output
security_baseline.rationale: Library must not become a credential storage vector; trust account data must not leak to logs

frontend_stack.value: none
frontend_stack.rationale: Library SDK with no UI component

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Maven
backend_stack.rationale: Target platform SDKs and integrator tooling predominantly Java; Maven for artifact distribution to Maven Central

data_platform.value: No managed database; migration state tracked via embedded H2 or flat JSON checkpoint files
data_platform.rationale: Library must run in environments without external database infrastructure

hosting_target.value: Maven Central (library artifact)
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Java artifact distribution for integrator consumption via Maven or Gradle

privacy_retention_policy.value: Library does not persist data beyond the migration run; checkpoint files deleted after successful completion per integrator policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimisation and bar rules require no unnecessary data retention beyond migration

future_scope.value: Additional source systems including Time Matters and Clio Migrate, async streaming API for large migrations exceeding 500k records
future_scope.rationale: Expand coverage to other legacy billing platforms post-MVP

constraints.value: Must run on JDK 11+; zero external runtime dependencies other than target platform client libraries; Maven artifact size under 5MB
constraints.rationale: Integrator environments may be restricted; small footprint required for enterprise firewalled environments

observability_baseline.value: SLF4J structured logging at DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR; progress callbacks via MigrationListener interface; validation error reports written to JSON
observability_baseline.rationale: Integrators need detailed logs for post-migration audit and consultant diagnosis

execution_preference.value: Single-threaded default with optional parallel record processing via configurable thread pool; SEQUENTIAL_ONLY enforcement for trust ledger entity type
execution_preference.rationale: Safe default for trust-account data; parallelism opt-in after integrator validation

compliance_context.value: State bar IOLTA trust account regulations requiring sequential balance integrity; GDPR Article 5 data minimisation for EU client records; attorney-client privilege preservation during data transfer
compliance_context.rationale: Trust account data migration is subject to bar oversight; EU client data requires GDPR-compliant handling

project.goal: Provide IT consultants with a validated, rollback-safe Java library for PCLaw and Tabs3 to cloud CLM migrations
project.target_users: IT consultants and legal technology integrators
project.timeline: GA within 3 months of MVP acceptance

backend.api: No hosted API; programmatic Java API surface only

security.auth: API key injection via MigrationConfig as char[]; no credential storage in library
security.data_handling: Sensitive field zeroing post-use; no plaintext key or financial value logging

data.source_formats: PCLaw fixed-width .txt and .mdb exports; Tabs3 .txt and .xml exports
data.state_tracking: Embedded H2 or JSON checkpoint files
data.target_platform: REST API of destination CLM platform

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Maven verify gate
devops.environments: local workstation only; no server environments

open_questions.tabs3_mdb_access: Tabs3 .mdb files may require JDBC-ODBC bridge on non-Windows; confirm supported extraction path

confirmed_decisions.logging: SLF4J with no concrete binding bundled

reasoner_reviews.product: The SDK correctly scopes to the migration lifecycle without over-engineering a hosted service. The rollback-safe import design is critical given that IOLTA trust account corruption carries bar discipline risk. PCLaw and Tabs3 are the two dominant legacy platforms so prioritising them first maximises immediate value for consulting engagements.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Java 21 virtual threads are available if parallelism is added later without rewriting the threading model. The H2 checkpoint approach is pragmatic for stateless execution environments but must be idempotent to survive interrupted runs via content-hash keying per record. RecordTypeLock enforcement for TrustLedgerEntry is the critical safety design that this round must cement.
reasoner_reviews.security: Credentials must never appear in checkpoint files or SLF4J output at any log level. The library should enforce a ZeroingConfig wrapper for API keys and trust account values to prevent heap dump exposure. GDPR data minimisation requires that intermediate deserialized objects containing EU client PII do not outlive the record processing scope.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: JDK 11 compatibility rules out Records and sealed classes in the public API surface; builder pattern required instead. Maven artifact size of 5MB is achievable if Jackson is declared provided scope in pom.xml.

backend.service_design: Public API surface: MigrationRunner (entry point), MigrationConfig (builder: source path, target credentials as char[], mapping file, thread count, checkpoint store path), MigrationResult (immutable summary). Internal modules: parsers/PCLawParser, parsers/Tabs3Parser, mapping/MappingEngine, validators/ValidationPipeline, importers/TargetPlatformImporter, state/CheckpointStore, dispatch/RecordTypeLock.
backend.api_patterns: Fluent builder for MigrationConfig. MigrationRunner.run() returns MigrationResult with per-record outcomes. Progress via MigrationListener interface. Checked exceptions wrapped in MigrationException hierarchy: ParseException, ValidationException, ImportException, RollbackException.
backend.failure_handling: ValidationPipeline collects all field-level errors per record before aborting batch. ImportException triggers TargetPlatformImporter.rollback() calling target DELETE endpoints for current batch. CheckpointStore marks failed records FAILED with error detail; retry excludes COMPLETED records by content hash.
backend.backend_risks: PCLaw MDB format requires JDBC-ODBC bridge on non-Windows; provide fallback CSV export path. Target platform API rate limits may throttle large migrations; exponential backoff with jitter required in TargetPlatformImporter.

security.auth_design: MigrationConfig accepts targetApiKey as char[] not String for explicit zeroing. ZeroingConfig.close() overwrites char[] with zero bytes after MigrationRunner completes. Custom SLF4J TurboFilter redacts strings matching API key pattern before any appender writes.
security.secrets_management: Library stores no secrets. Checkpoint files contain only record hashes, source IDs, target IDs, and status enums — no PII or credentials.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR-sensitive fields flagged in mapping definitions; library emits warning if processed without gdprAcknowledged=true in MigrationConfig. EU client record intermediate objects released for GC before next record begins.

data.entities: MatterRecord (source_id, matter_number, client_id, description, open_date, close_date, practice_area, billing_attorney_id). ClientRecord (source_id, client_number, display_name, address_json, email, phone, jurisdiction, gdpr_flagged). TimekeeperRecord (source_id, initials, display_name, rate_schedule_id). BillingRecord (source_id, matter_id, timekeeper_id, date, hours, rate, narrative, billed_flag). TrustLedgerEntry (source_id, matter_id, amount, transaction_type ENUM deposit/disbursement/fee, transaction_date, running_balance).
data.schema_notes: PCLaw fixed-width layout varies by version; parser uses version-discriminator from file header. Tabs3 XML validated against bundled XSD. Trust ledger entries require sequential processing to maintain running-balance integrity.
data.migration_notes: Matter-to-client foreign key integrity validated before any import begins. TrustLedgerEntry batches routed to single-threaded ordered executor by RecordTypeLock.SEQUENTIAL_ONLY regardless of configured thread pool size.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: mvn verify on PR (unit and integration tests with embedded H2), mvn deploy on tag to OSSRH staging, manual promote to Maven Central. Integration tests use WireMock stubs for target platform API including trust ledger POST ordering verification.
devops.backup_and_recovery: CheckpointStore persists progress to JSON file after each successful batch; integrators instructed to back up checkpoint before and after run. Rollback path documented in SDK README for partial failures.

ISS-001.title: Trust ledger parallel processing guard not enforced
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described opt-in parallelism for all record types including TrustLedgerEntry, risking non-sequential balance computation and IOLTA compliance violations.

history.note: Identified during security and compliance review of trust account processing

ISS-002.title: API key logging not explicitly prevented
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 had no SLF4J filter to redact API key values from DEBUG output; resolved by adding ZeroingConfig and custom TurboFilter redaction.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged credential leak risk in debug logging
ZeroingConfig wrapper and SLF4J TurboFilter redaction added

focus_issues.title: Trust ledger parallel processing guard not enforced
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan described opt-in parallelism for all record types including TrustLedgerEntry, risking non-sequential balance computation and IOLTA compliance violations.

latest_recommendations: Explicitly gate parallel processing so TrustLedgerEntry batches always run single-threaded via RecordTypeLock.SEQUENTIAL_ONLY regardless of thread pool config
Add WireMock integration test verifying that trust ledger POST requests arrive in chronological transaction_date order

convergence_state.reason: High-severity IOLTA compliance gap in trust ledger processing unresolved

strengths: Fluent builder API design is idiomatic Java and straightforward for integrators
Checkpoint-based idempotency handles interrupted runs correctly
ZeroingConfig approach for API key handling is security-forward
concerns: Trust ledger parallel processing not explicitly restricted despite balance-integrity requirement
Rollback logic for partial import batches not covered by integration test suite

recommendations: Add RecordTypeLock mechanism that forces single-threaded execution for trust_ledger entity type regardless of configured thread pool size
Document sequential processing requirement in Javadoc on TrustLedgerEntry importer class

issue_updates.title: Trust ledger parallel processing guard not enforced
issue_updates.detail: Round 1 plan described opt-in parallelism but provided no guard preventing its application to TrustLedgerEntry batches

previous_plan.title: PCLaw and Tabs3 Migration SDK — Initial Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Java 21 Maven library providing extraction, mapping, and validated import of PCLaw and Tabs3 practice management data to cloud CLM platforms with checkpoint-based idempotency and rollback-safe batch commits.
previous_plan.api_patterns: Fluent MigrationConfig builder; MigrationRunner.run() returning MigrationResult; MigrationListener progress callbacks.
previous_plan.data_model: MatterRecord, ClientRecord, TimekeeperRecord, BillingRecord, TrustLedgerEntry — parsed from source exports and transformed through MappingEngine before TargetPlatformImporter commits.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: ZeroingConfig for API key handling; no credential logging; GDPR field flagging in mapping definitions.

target_output.thinking_summary: ISS-001 requires adding RecordTypeLock enum to MigrationRunner dispatch logic registering TrustLedgerEntry as SEQUENTIAL_ONLY. This routes trust ledger batches to a single-threaded ordered executor bypassing the configurable thread pool. The running-balance validation must halt the migration before any import is committed if a balance inconsistency is detected. WireMock integration test should verify POST call ordering for trust entries. ISS-002 (SLF4J redaction) was resolved in round 1 and is preserved.

fix_report.action_taken: Added RecordTypeLock enum with SEQUENTIAL_ONLY and PARALLEL_ALLOWED values. MigrationRunner dispatch logic checks RecordTypeLock for entity type before routing to thread pool: TrustLedgerEntry registered as SEQUENTIAL_ONLY routes to single-threaded ordered queue; MatterRecord and ClientRecord remain PARALLEL_ALLOWED. Running-balance validation added: each TrustLedgerEntry checked against prior_balance + transaction_amount before import; mismatch halts run with IOLTA_BALANCE_MISMATCH error before any entries are committed. WireMock integration test added verifying trust ledger POST requests arrive in chronological transaction_date order. Javadoc updated on TrustLedgerEntry importer documenting SEQUENTIAL_ONLY enforcement and IOLTA compliance rationale.
fix_report.expected_outcome: TrustLedgerEntry migration always executes sequentially preserving running-balance integrity; IOLTA compliance verified by ordering test in CI

target_output.title: PCLaw and Tabs3 Migration SDK
target_output.executive_summary: Java 21 Maven library enabling IT consultants to migrate matter, client, billing, and trust ledger data from PCLaw fixed-width exports and Tabs3 XML files to cloud CLM platforms. Features configurable YAML field-mapping definitions, multi-stage ValidationPipeline with per-record error collection, rollback-safe batch imports with H2-backed CheckpointStore, and RecordTypeLock enforcement that routes TrustLedgerEntry batches to a single-threaded ordered executor for IOLTA running-balance integrity.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure library architecture distributed via Maven Central with no hosted runtime. Three-layer design: extraction layer (PCLawParser reading fixed-width source files with version-discriminating field position tables; Tabs3Parser reading XML against bundled XSD), transformation layer (MappingEngine applying YAML field definitions with optional transformer functions; ValidationPipeline collecting per-record violations before committing any batch), and import layer (TargetPlatformImporter posting to destination CLM REST API with exponential backoff and rollback DELETE on ImportException). MigrationRunner orchestrates all layers and enforces RecordTypeLock rules: TrustLedgerEntry batches routed to single-threaded ordered executor regardless of configured thread pool size; MatterRecord and ClientRecord batches eligible for parallel processing.
target_output.technology_stack: Java 21 (virtual-thread ready; JDK 11 public API surface). Maven 3.9 for build and Maven Central publishing via OSSRH nexus-staging-maven-plugin. Jackson 2.17 for JSON checkpoint serialization declared provided scope. SLF4J 2.x API with no concrete binding; integrators choose Logback or Log4j2. H2 2.x embedded database for CheckpointStore in large-volume mode. WireMock 3.x in test scope for CLM API simulation. JUnit 5 with AssertJ. SnakeYAML for mapping definition parsing.
target_output.functional_feature_map: PCLaw Extraction: fixed-width .txt parser with version-discriminating field-position tables; JDBC-ODBC bridge to CSV fallback path for MDB files on non-Windows environments. Tabs3 Extraction: XML parser validated against bundled XSD with schema-version detection from root element attribute. MappingEngine: loads YAML mapping definitions mapping source field paths to target CLM schema paths with optional transformer functions for date format conversion and status code translation. ValidationPipeline: collects all field-level violations per record before aborting batch; outputs JSON validation error report. TargetPlatformImporter: batched POST to CLM REST API; on ImportException triggers DELETE rollback for current batch. CheckpointStore: H2 or JSON file keyed by content hash of Salesforce record; records PENDING/COMPLETED/FAILED status. RecordTypeLock: SEQUENTIAL_ONLY for TrustLedgerEntry; PARALLEL_ALLOWED for all other entity types.
target_output.system_components: MigrationConfig (builder: sourceType, sourcePath, targetApiKey as char[], mappingDefinitionPath, threadPoolSize, checkpointStorePath, gdprAcknowledged). MigrationRunner (orchestrator; RecordTypeLock dispatch: SEQUENTIAL_ONLY routes to single-threaded ordered queue, PARALLEL_ALLOWED routes to ForkJoinPool). PCLawParser and Tabs3Parser (format-specific extraction producing domain record streams). MappingEngine (YAML-driven transformation with transformer function registry). ValidationPipeline (constraint validators with per-record error accumulation). TargetPlatformImporter (REST client with exponential backoff and batch rollback). CheckpointStore (H2 or JSON file; content-hash keyed; PENDING/COMPLETED/FAILED/SKIPPED states). ZeroingConfig (char[] API key lifecycle wrapper zeroed in MigrationRunner.close()). SLF4J TurboFilter (redacts strings matching API key regex from all appender output).
target_output.workflows: PCLaw Migration Run: integrator instantiates MigrationConfig with source path, YAML mapping, API key as char[], and checkpoint path. MigrationRunner calls PCLawParser to stream MatterRecord and ClientRecord objects. MappingEngine transforms each record. ValidationPipeline accumulates errors; if any record fails, batch aborted and JSON error report written. TargetPlatformImporter posts validated records in batches of 100. CheckpointStore marks each record COMPLETED after successful import. Trust Ledger Migration: MigrationRunner detects TrustLedgerEntry entity type and routes to SEQUENTIAL_ONLY single-threaded ordered executor. Records processed in chronological transaction_date order. Running-balance validation checks each entry: if prior_balance + transaction_amount does not equal recorded running_balance within tolerance, MigrationRunner halts with IOLTA_BALANCE_MISMATCH error before any entries are imported. All imports confirmed before CheckpointStore marks batch COMPLETED.
target_output.data_model: MatterRecord (source_id, matter_number, client_id FK, description, open_date, close_date nullable, practice_area, billing_attorney_id). ClientRecord (source_id, client_number, display_name, address_json, email, phone, jurisdiction, gdpr_flagged BOOLEAN). TimekeeperRecord (source_id, initials, display_name, rate_schedule_id FK). BillingRecord (source_id, matter_id FK, timekeeper_id FK, entry_date DATE, hours DECIMAL, rate DECIMAL, narrative TEXT, billed_flag BOOLEAN). TrustLedgerEntry (source_id, matter_id FK, amount DECIMAL, transaction_type ENUM deposit/disbursement/fee, transaction_date DATE, running_balance DECIMAL). MigrationCheckpoint (record_hash VARCHAR PK, entity_type VARCHAR, source_id VARCHAR, target_id VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/completed/failed/skipped, error_detail TEXT, processed_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: Public Java API: MigrationConfig.builder().sourceType(SourceType.PCLAWV14).sourcePath(Path).targetApiKey(char[]).mappingDefinition(Path).checkpointStore(Path).threadPoolSize(4).gdprAcknowledged(true).build(). MigrationRunner.run(config) returns MigrationResult(totalRecords, completedRecords, failedRecords, skippedRecords, validationReportPath, durationMs). MigrationListener interface: onStageStart(stage), onRecordProcessed(entityType, sourceId, status), onValidationError(entityType, sourceId, violations), onBalanceMismatch(entrySourceId, expected, actual), onComplete(result). Exception hierarchy: ParseException, ValidationException(entityType, recordId, violations), ImportException(entityType, batchIds, httpStatus), RollbackException, IoltaBalanceMismatchException(entrySourceId, expected, actual).
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key handling: targetApiKey accepted as char[] in MigrationConfig; ZeroingConfig.close() overwrites array with zero bytes after run completes. SLF4J TurboFilter redacts strings matching API key regex before any appender writes. IOLTA compliance: RecordTypeLock.SEQUENTIAL_ONLY enforced for TrustLedgerEntry; running-balance validation halts migration before import if mismatch detected; IoltaBalanceMismatchException raised with source entry ID and balance discrepancy for consultant diagnosis. GDPR: ClientRecord fields with gdpr_flagged emits warning without gdprAcknowledged=true; intermediate EU client objects released for GC before next record. CheckpointStore contains only content hashes, source IDs, and status enums — no PII or credentials.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Maven Central distribution via OSSRH staging. GitHub Actions CI: mvn verify on PR (unit tests, WireMock integration tests, Checkstyle, SpotBugs). Tag-triggered release: mvn deploy to OSSRH staging, automated Nexus close and promote. Integration test suite covers: happy-path PCLaw migration, validation failure with JSON error report, batch rollback on ImportException, resume after interrupted run, trust ledger sequential ordering (WireMock POST order assertion), IOLTA balance mismatch halt, GDPR flagging warning, ZeroingConfig char[] zeroing after close.
target_output.observability: SLF4J structured log events: source file open, parsing complete with record count, mapping complete, validation summary, import batch start/complete, trust ledger balance check per entry at DEBUG level (no amounts), checkpoint flush, run complete. MigrationListener callbacks surface stage events to integrator CLI or monitoring. Validation error JSON report written to configurable output path with per-record violation details. MigrationResult includes durationMs and per-entity-type throughput metrics.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No runtime infrastructure cost. For large migrations of 100k+ matters, CheckpointStore H2 mode recommended over JSON file. Thread pool size of 4 provides 3-4x throughput for MatterRecord and ClientRecord. TrustLedgerEntry throughput is single-threaded by design and bounded by target API rate limits; 500k trust entries estimated at 12-24 hours with backoff headroom.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): PCLaw fixed-width parser for MatterRecord, ClientRecord, BillingRecord; MappingEngine with YAML; ValidationPipeline; TargetPlatformImporter with WireMock coverage; CheckpointStore JSON mode; RecordTypeLock with TrustLedgerEntry SEQUENTIAL_ONLY; IOLTA running-balance validation; Maven Central pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): TrustLedgerEntry PCLaw parser; Tabs3 XML parser; H2 checkpoint mode; GDPR field flagging. Phase 3 (3 weeks): PCLaw MDB JDBC-ODBC extraction path; async streaming for 500k+ record migrations; Time Matters parser skeleton.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with JDK 11 public API surface; builder pattern not Records in public classes. Checkstyle with Google Java Style. SpotBugs with findSecBugs; any HIGH security finding blocks merge. No String fields for API keys in any class. All public API classes have Javadoc including thread-safety contract. Required integration tests: happy-path migration, validation failure, batch rollback, resume after interruption, trust ledger POST ordering via WireMock, IOLTA balance mismatch halt before any import, GDPR flagging warning.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PCLaw MDB format requires JDBC-ODBC bridge unavailable in Linux CI; mitigated by CSV export fallback path documented in SDK guide. Risk: target platform API rate limits throttle large migrations; mitigated by exponential backoff with jitter and CheckpointStore resume. Tradeoff: H2 over JSON checkpoint for large migrations adds an optional dependency but integrators can stay with JSON mode for small firms. Tradeoff: single-threaded TrustLedgerEntry processing sacrifices throughput for IOLTA compliance — non-negotiable given bar regulation stakes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: H2 confirmed as optional dependency for large-volume checkpoint; JSON file is default for small firm migrations. Tabs3 XML version detection reads version attribute from root element. GDPR acknowledgement flag chosen over silent field blocking to give integrators explicit control over EU data processing decisions.

metadata.notes: Java SDK for PCLaw/Tabs3 to cloud CLM migration; round 2 resolves IOLTA trust ledger sequential processing via RecordTypeLock enforcement.

profile.domain: AI Legal Research Assistant

project_goal.value: AI-powered legal research assistant allowing attorneys to ask natural-language questions about case law, statutes, and regulations, receiving synthesized answers with cited sources from a curated corpus of primary legal materials, as a greenfield internal tool for a mid-size law firm seeking to reduce manual research time
project_goal.rationale: Law firm leadership approved greenfield AI research tool after evaluating commercial alternatives as too expensive and inflexible for firm-specific corpus needs

target_users.value: Associates, partners, and paralegals at the firm; admin users for corpus management and usage monitoring
target_users.rationale: Associates are the primary daily users; partners need high-reliability citations; admins manage corpus and LLM usage budgets

project_class.rationale: Core value is LLM-driven semantic search and synthesis over a curated legal corpus; AI capabilities are the primary feature, not a supplementary addition

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack AI system with RAG pipeline, Okta auth, pgvector semantic search, LLM observability, and corpus management admin panel

complexity_level.rationale: RAG pipeline with hybrid search, citation provenance tracking, LLM hallucination guardrails, and attorney feedback loop constitute advanced AI system design

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect citations or hallucinated case holdings submitted in court filings create malpractice liability; LLM accuracy must be traceable and auditable

data_sensitivity.rationale: Attorney research queries and matter context contain attorney-client privileged information and work product

external_exposure.rationale: Internal firm tool; all access requires Okta SSO authentication; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Okta SSO with RBAC; roles: associate, partner, paralegal, corpus_admin, system_admin; associates and paralegals query only; partners can view all firm usage; admins manage corpus ingestion and monitor LLM usage costs
access_model.rationale: Role differentiation controls query history visibility and corpus management access

feature_scope.value: Natural-language legal research query interface, RAG-synthesized answers with cited source chunks, citation click-through to full source document, attorney feedback ratings on answer quality, research session history, corpus admin for document ingestion and re-embedding, LLM usage cost dashboard, query analytics for practice group reporting
feature_scope.rationale: Full workflow from attorney query through answer delivery, citation verification, and quality feedback

mvp_scope.value: Query interface with RAG pipeline (pgvector semantic search plus BM25 hybrid), cited answer display, citation click-through to source, Okta SSO, session history, and basic admin for corpus upload
mvp_scope.rationale: Core RAG query-and-cite flow must be proven before layering feedback loop and analytics dashboard

security_baseline.value: Okta SAML SSO with MFA; query logs classified as privileged work product and stored in us-east-1 only; LLM API calls use firm-specific API key with no query data retained by LLM provider; TLS 1.3 in transit; RLS on PostgreSQL query_sessions table restricting attorneys to their own sessions
security_baseline.rationale: Attorney research queries are privileged; data residency and provider data retention policies are critical for privilege preservation

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, App Router; Tailwind CSS; streaming response rendering via React Suspense for LLM token streaming; TipTap for citation annotation display
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js 14 App Router enables server-side rendering for session history and streaming UI updates for LLM token output

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; LangChain for RAG pipeline orchestration; sentence-transformers for embedding generation; Anthropic Claude 3 Sonnet as LLM via AWS Bedrock; BM25 full-text search via PostgreSQL tsvector
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI enables async streaming LLM responses; LangChain provides retrievalQA chain abstraction; AWS Bedrock keeps LLM calls within AWS trust boundary

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension for 1536-dim embeddings; AWS Bedrock Titan Embeddings for document chunk embedding; Redis 7 for session cache and rate limiting; S3 for raw source document storage
data_platform.rationale: pgvector provides hybrid vector and BM25 search within a single PostgreSQL instance, simplifying the retrieval layer

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for FastAPI backend; Next.js on Vercel or AWS Amplify; RDS PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector in us-east-1; Redis on ElastiCache; S3 for document corpus
hosting_target.rationale: AWS us-east-1 data residency for privileged query data; Bedrock keeps LLM calls within the same AWS region

privacy_retention_policy.value: Query sessions and answer traces retained 3 years per matter retention policy; attorney feedback ratings retained indefinitely for model quality tracking; raw query text treated as privileged work product with attorney-only access via RLS; LLM provider (Bedrock) configured with no-logging option
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Query content is privileged; provider-side logging would extend privilege waiver risk to the cloud provider

future_scope.value: Fine-tuning on firm's internal memos and past research memoranda, matter-scoped research sessions visible to entire matter team, real-time corpus updates from Westlaw API feeds, citation validity checking via Shepards or KeyCite API integration
future_scope.rationale: Firm-specific corpus tuning and citation validation are high-value year 2 investments

constraints.value: All query and answer data must remain in AWS us-east-1; Bedrock configured with no-logging; no query data sent to external LLM providers outside AWS; PostgreSQL RLS on query_sessions; corpus limited to publicly available primary legal materials in MVP (no client documents)
constraints.rationale: Attorney-client privilege and data residency requirements forbid query transmission to non-AWS providers or non-us-east-1 regions

observability_baseline.value: Datadog LLM Observability for token usage, latency, and hallucination detection metrics; Datadog APM for RAG pipeline trace visualization; custom metrics: retrieval_hit_rate, answer_feedback_positive_rate, avg_cited_chunks_per_answer, LLM cost per query
observability_baseline.rationale: LLM observability must surface retrieval quality degradation and cost spikes before they impact attorney productivity

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with weekly demo to firm's legal ops committee; AI pipeline components built and evaluated independently before integration; RAG quality benchmarked against a 50-question legal research gold standard before launch
execution_preference.rationale: AI system quality gates must be validated against domain-specific benchmarks, not just functional tests

compliance_context.value: ABA Formal Opinion 512 on generative AI (competence obligation to understand AI tool limitations, duty to supervise AI-generated work); attorney-client privilege for all query logs; SOC 2 Type II for data handling controls; state bar rules on AI disclosure vary by jurisdiction and must be tracked
compliance_context.rationale: High risk with confidential AI data requires ABA compliance context; LLM hallucination risk creates professional responsibility obligations for supervising attorneys

llm_integration.value: Anthropic Claude 3 Sonnet via AWS Bedrock; 1536-dim embeddings via Bedrock Titan Embeddings; LangChain RetrievalQA chain; max_tokens 2048 per answer; temperature 0 for deterministic citation-grounded synthesis; system prompt includes explicit instruction to only cite retrieved chunks and flag when question cannot be answered from corpus
llm_integration.rationale: Temperature 0 and corpus-only citation instruction minimize hallucination risk for attorney reliance on answers

project.goal: RAG-based AI legal research assistant for internal firm use with cited, auditable answers
project.target_users: Associates, partners, paralegals, corpus admins
project.timeline: MVP in 14 weeks; full feature set in 24 weeks

frontend.streaming: React Suspense with server-sent events for LLM token streaming

backend.rag: LangChain RetrievalQA with hybrid pgvector plus BM25 retrieval

security.auth: Okta SAML SSO with MFA
security.rls: PostgreSQL RLS on query_sessions by attorney

data.cache: Redis 7 for session cache and rate limiting

devops.ci: GitHub Actions
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate for API, AWS Amplify for Next.js

constraints.corpus_scope: Public primary legal materials in MVP

reasoner_reviews.product: By round 4 the core RAG pipeline quality has been validated against the 50-question benchmark (recall@5 of 0.87). The outstanding issue from round 3 is the citation provenance display in the UI - attorneys cannot currently see which specific sentence in a retrieved chunk supports a claim in the synthesized answer, only the document-level citation. Sentence-level citation highlighting requires chunk-level span tracking in the answer trace, which the current answer_traces table design does not capture.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The hybrid search (pgvector cosine similarity plus BM25) has been shown to outperform pure vector search for precise statutory citation queries. The remaining architectural gap is the embedding pipeline for corpus updates - currently corpus admins must re-embed all chunks when adding documents because there is no incremental ingestion path. The ingestion pipeline needs an add-document endpoint that embeds and upserts new chunks without triggering a full re-index.
reasoner_reviews.security: RLS on query_sessions is confirmed working from round 2. The outstanding concern from round 3 is the admin panel: corpus admins can currently view the list of query sessions (not content) for debugging. This should be restricted further - admins should see only aggregate usage metrics, not individual session metadata, to preserve query privilege.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The AWS Bedrock no-logging constraint has been verified as configurable per API call. The us-east-1 residency constraint is fully met. Round 3 introduced Redis caching of embedding vectors for recently queried terms which reduced Bedrock Titan API calls by 32% in staging load tests.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with four routers: ResearchRouter (POST /research/query for streaming RAG responses), SessionRouter (GET/DELETE /sessions for query history), CorpusRouter (admin-only POST /corpus/documents, GET /corpus/stats), AnalyticsRouter (admin-only GET /analytics/usage). RAG pipeline: HybridRetriever (pgvector ANN search plus BM25 tsvector, RRF fusion), LLMSynthesizer (LangChain RetrievalQA with Bedrock Claude 3 Sonnet, temperature 0), CitationBuilder (maps answer sentences to source chunk IDs with span positions), AnswerTraceWriter (persists full answer trace to PostgreSQL).
backend.llm_integration_design: AWS Bedrock Claude 3 Sonnet invoked via LangChain BedrockChat with streaming enabled. System prompt: 'You are a legal research assistant. Answer using ONLY the provided context chunks. For each factual claim, cite the chunk ID in brackets. If the context does not contain sufficient information to answer, respond: INSUFFICIENT_CORPUS_COVERAGE. Do not speculate or extrapolate beyond the cited material.' Embedding: Bedrock Titan Embeddings V2 (1536-dim) called via boto3 with no-logging option. Redis cache for recently computed embeddings (TTL 24h) to reduce Bedrock Titan API calls.
backend.api_patterns: POST /research/query (body: {question: string, session_id?: UUID}; streams: server-sent events with token chunks and citation events). GET /sessions (returns paginated session list for authenticated attorney, RLS enforced at DB). GET /sessions/:id/trace (returns full answer trace with chunk citations). POST /corpus/documents (admin: multipart upload of PDF or text, triggers ingestion pipeline). GET /analytics/usage (admin: token counts, cost, query volume by practice group).
backend.failure_handling: Bedrock API timeout (30s) streams a partial answer with CORPUS_TIMEOUT flag prompting attorney to retry. INSUFFICIENT_CORPUS_COVERAGE response renders as a styled notice in UI directing attorney to expand search terms or consult Westlaw. Redis cache miss falls through to Bedrock Titan with no error. PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion returns 503 with Retry-After header.
backend.background_jobs: Corpus ingestion job: on document upload, splits PDF into 512-token chunks with 50-token overlap, generates embeddings via Bedrock Titan, upserts document_chunks table with vector and tsvector columns. Runs as Celery task on Redis broker to avoid blocking the HTTP request. Nightly LLM usage aggregation job: summarizes Datadog LLM Observability metrics into usage_summaries table for admin cost dashboard.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router. Pages: /research (main query interface with streaming response), /sessions (query history list), /sessions/[id] (session detail with full trace), /admin/corpus (corpus management), /admin/analytics (usage dashboard). Server Components for session list and corpus stats; Client Components for query input, streaming answer display, and citation click-through.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Research page: textarea for legal question, submit triggers POST to streaming endpoint, React Suspense boundary shows skeleton while first tokens arrive, answer streams token-by-token with inline citation markers [1], [2] that are interactive; clicking a citation marker opens a side panel with the full retrieved chunk and a link to the source document in S3. Session history: list of past queries with date, question excerpt, and answer quality indicator from attorney feedback. Admin corpus page: document upload dropzone, ingestion status table showing chunk count and embedding status per document.
frontend.state_management: Server-side session data fetched via Next.js server actions. Client-side: Zustand for query interface state (question draft, streaming answer buffer, active citation panel). Streaming: EventSource consuming server-sent events from FastAPI streaming endpoint; tokens appended to answer buffer; citation events trigger side panel.
frontend.performance_notes: First token latency target: under 2 seconds from query submission. Retrieval phase benchmarked at 120ms p95 in staging (pgvector ANN with HNSW index ef_search=100). LLM TTFT (time to first token) via Bedrock Claude 3 Sonnet approximately 800ms. Total TTFT budget: 1.5 seconds including network.

security.auth_design: Okta SAML 2.0 with MFA enforced for all users. Next.js middleware validates Okta JWT on every request. FastAPI dependency checks JWT claim 'roles' on every endpoint. Admin routes require corpus_admin or system_admin claim.
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL RLS policy on query_sessions: attorney can SELECT their own sessions only (WHERE user_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id')). Admin corpus endpoints require corpus_admin role claim in JWT. Analytics endpoints aggregate-only: no individual session metadata visible to admins. Answer trace stored with user_id but RLS ensures only the originating attorney can retrieve it.
security.privacy_controls: All query text and answer traces stored only in us-east-1 RDS. Bedrock API calls use RequestMetadata with no-logging flag to prevent Anthropic from training on firm queries. Redis session cache stores only session_id to answer mapping (not raw query text). S3 source documents in private bucket with no public access; presigned URLs for attorney click-through with 15-minute TTL.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every RAG query logged to query_audit_log table: user_id, query_hash (SHA-256 of query text, not plaintext), session_id, retrieved_chunk_ids, answer_token_count, latency_ms, timestamp. Raw query text not logged to audit table to preserve privilege. Datadog LLM Observability captures token counts and latency without query content.

data.entities: legal_documents (id UUID PK, title, citation_reference, document_type ENUM case_opinion/statute/regulation/secondary, jurisdiction, publication_date, s3_key, ingested_at, chunk_count). document_chunks (id UUID PK, document_id FK, chunk_index INT, chunk_text TEXT, embedding vector(1536), tsvector_content tsvector, token_count INT, created_at). query_sessions (id UUID PK, user_id, question_text TEXT, answer_text TEXT, retrieved_chunk_ids UUID[], answer_trace_json JSONB, feedback_rating INT NULLABLE, feedback_note TEXT NULLABLE, created_at, latency_ms). usage_summaries (id UUID PK, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, practice_group VARCHAR, total_queries INT, total_tokens BIGINT, estimated_cost_usd DECIMAL).
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension. HNSW index on document_chunks.embedding (ef_construction=200, m=16) for sub-100ms ANN search. GIN index on document_chunks.tsvector_content for BM25 full-text search. RLS enabled on query_sessions. S3 bucket private with SSE-S3 for raw document storage. Redis 7 ElastiCache: embedding cache with 24h TTL, session rate limiting (10 queries/minute/user).
data.consistency_notes: Ingestion pipeline uses upsert on document_chunks to support incremental document additions without re-indexing existing chunks. Chunk embeddings are idempotent - re-ingesting the same document produces identical embedding vectors (same model, same text). HNSW index rebuilt nightly if chunk count grows more than 10% since last build.
data.analytics_events: query_submitted (session_id, user_role, question_length_tokens, retrieved_chunk_count). answer_completed (session_id, total_tokens, latency_ms, ttft_ms, corpus_coverage_flag). feedback_submitted (session_id, rating 1-5, has_note). citation_clicked (session_id, chunk_id, document_id).

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: FastAPI service (2-8 tasks, auto-scale on CPU 70%), Celery worker service (1-4 tasks, auto-scale on Redis queue depth), corpus ingestion task (on-demand ECS task triggered by S3 upload event). Next.js on AWS Amplify connected to GitHub. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ us-east-1. ElastiCache Redis cluster-mode disabled (single primary with replica). S3 corpus bucket us-east-1 with versioning.
devops.ci_cd_design: PR: pytest unit tests for HybridRetriever, CitationBuilder, and LLMSynthesizer mock tests; Next.js type check and ESLint. Merge to main: Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling update, Amplify Next.js build. Pre-production gate: RAG quality benchmark (50-question legal research set) with recall@5 minimum 0.85 - failing benchmark blocks deploy.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog LLM Observability: token counts per query, TTFT histogram, hallucination detection via citation verification (checks if cited chunk_id exists in retrieved set). Datadog APM: FastAPI request traces with RAG pipeline span breakdown (retrieval_ms, embedding_ms, llm_ms, citation_build_ms). Custom metrics: retrieval_hit_rate, feedback_positive_rate (ratings 4-5 / total), monthly_llm_cost_usd.
devops.cost_controls: Redis embedding cache reduces Bedrock Titan calls by approximately 30% on repeated legal term queries. ECS scheduled scaling: scale down Celery workers overnight (11pm-6am ET). Reserved Instances for RDS and ElastiCache baselines. LLM cost alert: Datadog monitor triggers PagerDuty if daily Bedrock spend exceeds $150 (approximately 3x normal daily cost).

ISS-001.title: No sentence-level citation span tracking in answer traces
ISS-001.detail: answer_traces table stores retrieved_chunk_ids but not the specific sentence spans within chunks that support each claim in the synthesized answer. Attorneys cannot identify which sentence in a 512-token chunk justifies a cited claim.

history.note: Partner feedback: citation click-through shows full 512-token chunk but not the specific supporting sentence
Round 3 plan acknowledged but deferred span tracking to post-MVP

ISS-002.title: Corpus admin panel exposes individual session metadata
ISS-002.detail: Round 3 updated admin analytics to aggregate-only views; corpus admins now see only token counts and query volumes per practice group, not individual session metadata or query hashes.

history.note: Security review: admins could see session list including timestamps and user IDs
Admin analytics restricted to aggregate usage_summaries table; RLS prevents admin access to query_sessions content

ISS-003.title: No incremental corpus ingestion - full re-index required on document addition
ISS-003.detail: Adding a new document to the corpus currently triggers a full HNSW index rebuild on the document_chunks table, causing 15-20 minute disruption to query performance on the production database.

history.note: Operations observation: corpus admin uploaded 50 new circuit court opinions and query latency spiked to 8 seconds for 20 minutes during HNSW rebuild

focus_issues.title: No sentence-level citation span tracking in answer traces
focus_issues.detail: Attorneys cannot identify which sentence in a retrieved chunk supports a specific claim; only document-level citations visible.

focus_issues.title: No incremental corpus ingestion - full re-index required on document addition
focus_issues.detail: New document uploads trigger full HNSW index rebuild causing 15-20 minute query latency spikes.

latest_recommendations: Add span_start_char and span_end_char fields to answer_traces JSONB structure for each citation, populated by CitationBuilder using sentence boundary detection on retrieved chunk text

Implement incremental HNSW index update: new document chunks inserted into document_chunks table and HNSW index updated via pgvector CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on a new partition, swapped in without blocking queries
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: AI Legal Research Assistant Architecture - Round 3

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues: sentence-level citation spans and incremental corpus ingestion blocking production operations

previous_audits.summary: Round 2 established a working RAG pipeline with hybrid retrieval scoring recall@5 of 0.84. Admin privilege visibility issue resolved partially. Core outstanding issues: no sentence-level citation tracking and incremental ingestion gap not yet addressed.
strengths: Hybrid pgvector plus BM25 retrieval with RRF fusion is well-specified
RLS on query_sessions correctly enforced
LangChain RetrievalQA with temperature 0 and corpus-only citation instruction sound
concerns: answer_traces JSONB lacks span position fields for sentence-level citations
HNSW index rebuild on document addition disrupts query performance
blocking_issues: Citation granularity insufficient for attorney reliance: chunk-level only, not sentence-level
recommendations: Add citation span tracking to CitationBuilder and answer_traces schema
Implement incremental HNSW index update via pgvector partitioned index

issue_updates.title: No sentence-level citation span tracking
issue_updates.detail: answer_trace_json stores chunk_ids only

issue_updates.title: Corpus admin panel exposes individual session metadata
issue_updates.detail: Partial fix: admin can still see session list without content

previous_audits.summary: Round 3 resolved admin visibility issue and improved retrieval quality. ISS-001 sentence-level citation and ISS-003 incremental ingestion remain as blocking issues requiring architectural changes in round 4.
strengths: Admin analytics restricted to aggregate usage_summaries; ISS-002 fully resolved
Redis embedding cache reduces Bedrock costs 32% in staging
RAG benchmark recall@5 improved to 0.87
concerns: Sentence-level citation span tracking still missing from CitationBuilder and answer_traces schema
HNSW full rebuild on corpus addition creates production latency spikes

Implement pgvector partitioned index strategy for incremental corpus updates without blocking query path

issue_updates.title: No incremental corpus ingestion
