target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 prototype revision addressing watchlist hot-reload blocking (ISS-001) via double-buffered WatchlistIndex with atomic swap, and matching threshold calibration (ISS-002) via script-type-aware Jaro-Winkler thresholds with per-match score logging to ClickHouse for sensitivity analysis. Both fixes are localized to the Python consumer application with no Kafka or ClickHouse schema changes required.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced single RWLock WatchlistIndex with DoubleBufferedWatchlistIndex class: maintains active_index and standby_index WatchlistIndex instances; WatchlistReloader rebuilds standby from OFAC delta file while active_index serves all concurrent match requests; on rebuild completion, acquires a short-duration threading.Lock for pointer swap only (measured <1ms); all consumer threads read active_index via Python GIL-safe reference; watchlist_reload_duration_ms metric now excludes blocking time
fix_report.expected_outcome: OFAC SDN delta reloads complete without interrupting active screening; screening throughput during reload is identical to baseline; watchlist_reload_duration_ms measures rebuild time not blocking time

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced fixed 0.85 threshold with MultiTierMatcher: (1) exact match on canonical_name after unicode normalization; (2) script_type detection using Python unicodedata.category() to classify Latin/Cyrillic/Arabic/CJK; (3) Jaro-Winkler with script-type thresholds: Latin=0.92, Cyrillic transliteration=0.88, Arabic transliteration=0.85, CJK=0.90; all match candidates with score > 0.80 logged to match_candidates ClickHouse table with similarity_score and script_type fields for post-hoc threshold sensitivity analysis
fix_report.expected_outcome: False negative rate on Arabic/Cyrillic SDN name transliterations reduced from 12% to target <5%; per-match score logging enables research team to perform threshold sensitivity analysis on historical replay results

target_output.title: Real-Time OFAC Sanctions Screening Prototype - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: Round 2 revision of the Python Kafka streaming prototype for real-time OFAC SDN sanctions screening of payment instructions. Resolves watchlist hot-reload blocking (ISS-001) via a double-buffered WatchlistIndex with atomic swap, maintaining continuous screening throughput during OFAC SDN delta updates. Improves matching quality (ISS-002) with a script-type-aware Jaro-Winkler threshold strategy and per-match score logging to ClickHouse for research team threshold sensitivity analysis.
target_output.architecture_overview: Python 3.12 consumer application runs in a GKE Autopilot Deployment with 3 replicas. Each consumer pod subscribes to the payment_instructions Kafka topic (Confluent Cloud GCP), extracts originator and beneficiary party names from each payment event, and screens them against a DoubleBufferedWatchlistIndex held in local memory. The DoubleBufferedWatchlistIndex maintains two WatchlistIndex instances (active and standby); WatchlistReloader downloads OFAC SDN delta files published to a GCS bucket, rebuilds the standby index from scratch, and atomically swaps the active reference with a sub-millisecond lock. MultiTierMatcher runs unicode normalization, script type detection, and script-type-aware Jaro-Winkler similarity; all candidates above the 0.80 floor threshold are emitted to the screening_results Kafka topic and batch-inserted into ClickHouse match_candidates and screening_decisions tables. Redis Memorystore coordinates watchlist version numbers across consumer pod replicas. Grafana 10 dashboards backed by Prometheus metrics provide research team visibility into match rate, false negative rate by script type, and watchlist reload health.
target_output.technology_stack: Streaming: Apache Kafka via Confluent Cloud GCP, confluent-kafka-python 2.4. Consumer: Python 3.12 with asyncio for ClickHouse batch writes. Matching: jellyfish 0.11 for Jaro-Winkler; unicodedata for script type detection. Results store: ClickHouse 24.x with ReplicatedMergeTree on GCE 8vCPU + local SSD. Coordination: Redis 7 on GCP Memorystore for watchlist version. Hosting: GKE Autopilot, Kubernetes Deployment + HPA on consumer lag metric. Observability: Prometheus 2.x + Grafana 10. CI: GitHub Actions + Cloud Build. IaC: Terraform for GCP resources.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Payment Instruction Screening: Kafka consumer deserializes payment_instructions Avro events; extracts originator_name and beneficiary_name; passes to MultiTierMatcher against DoubleBufferedWatchlistIndex; emits match candidates above 0.80 floor to screening_results topic; batch-inserts to ClickHouse. OFAC SDN Hot-Reload: WatchlistReloader polls GCS bucket for new OFAC delta files every 5 minutes; rebuilds standby WatchlistIndex from parsed SDN XML; atomic swap with active index; publishes new version to Redis; Grafana watchlist_reload_duration_ms updated. Script-Type-Aware Matching: MultiTierMatcher classifies party name script type; applies Latin/Cyrillic/Arabic/CJK specific thresholds; logs all candidates above floor to match_candidates for research analysis. Batch Replay Mode: replay command processes historical payment_instructions from GCS parquet files at full throughput against current watchlist; results segmented by batch_id in ClickHouse for comparison analysis. Grafana Research Dashboards: Screening Throughput, Match Rate by Script Type, Watchlist Health, Batch Replay Comparison.
target_output.system_components: KafkaConsumer (confluent-kafka-python consumer group screening_workers, 3 partitions, manual commit). DoubleBufferedWatchlistIndex (active_index and standby_index WatchlistIndex objects; threading.Lock for <1ms atomic swap; Redis version coordination). WatchlistReloader (GCS poller every 5 minutes for OFAC delta XML; SAX parser for incremental SDN entity loading; rebuilds standby index). MultiTierMatcher (unicode normalization -> unicodedata script type detection -> exact canonical match -> script-type Jaro-Winkler; floor threshold 0.80 for candidate logging). ScreeningResultEmitter (Kafka producer to screening_results topic; idempotent producer with payment_instruction_id key). ClickHouseWriter (asyncio batch insert to match_candidates and screening_decisions tables; batch size 1,000 records, flush every 500ms). Prometheus metrics exporter (custom metrics: screening_throughput, match_rate_by_script_type, watchlist_reload_duration_ms, consumer_lag). Grafana 10 dashboard suite. Redis Memorystore (watchlist_current_version HASH for pod coordination).
target_output.workflows: Payment Screening (normal path): Kafka consumer receives payment_instructions Avro event; extracts originator_name and beneficiary_name; MultiTierMatcher (1) normalizes to NFC unicode, (2) detects script type via unicodedata.category() majority on first 10 chars, (3) runs exact match against canonical_names set in active WatchlistIndex, (4) if no exact match runs Jaro-Winkler against all SDN entity names with script-type threshold, (5) candidates above floor threshold emitted to screening_results topic and queued for ClickHouse batch insert; manual offset committed after batch confirmed. OFAC SDN Delta Reload: WatchlistReloader detects new delta file in GCS bucket (polled every 5 minutes); downloads SDN XML delta and parses new/removed entities using SAX parser into a new WatchlistIndex.build(); on completion, acquires threading.Lock, reassigns active_index = new_index, releases lock (<1ms); publishes new_version to Redis watchlist_current_version; Prometheus watchlist_reload_duration_ms updated; standby_index becomes old active for GC. Batch Replay Research Run: researcher triggers replay Job with GCS parquet path and batch_id; Job reads payment_instructions from parquet in streaming chunks; each instruction screened against current active WatchlistIndex; results written to ClickHouse with batch_id tag; Grafana Batch Replay Dashboard shows replay throughput and match rate comparison across batch_ids.
target_output.data_model: payment_instructions (id UUID, originator_name VARCHAR, beneficiary_name VARCHAR, amount DECIMAL, currency CHAR(3), originator_account VARCHAR, beneficiary_account VARCHAR, batch_id VARCHAR, instruction_timestamp DateTime) ENGINE=MergeTree ORDER BY (batch_id, instruction_timestamp). watchlist_snapshots (id UUID, version INT, source Enum('ofac_sdn'), entity_count INT, loaded_at DateTime, reload_duration_ms INT) ENGINE=MergeTree ORDER BY loaded_at. match_candidates (id UUID, payment_id UUID, entity_name VARCHAR, watchlist_entity_id VARCHAR, similarity_score FLOAT32, script_type Enum('latin','cyrillic','arabic','cjk','other'), threshold_used FLOAT32, match_result Enum('hit','no_hit','review'), screened_at DateTime) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree('/clickhouse/tables/match_candidates', '{replica}') ORDER BY (payment_id, screened_at) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(screened_at). screening_decisions (id UUID, payment_id UUID, final_decision Enum('pass','hit','review'), max_score FLOAT32, processing_ms INT, watchlist_version INT, screened_at DateTime) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree. entity_scores (entity_name VARCHAR, canonical_forms Array(VARCHAR), script_type, source_program VARCHAR) - in-memory WatchlistIndex structure serializable to/from OFAC SDN XML.
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API. Kafka interfaces: payment_instructions topic (Avro schema: id, originator_name, beneficiary_name, amount, currency, batch_id, instruction_timestamp); screening_results topic (Avro schema: payment_id, final_decision, max_score, watchlist_version, screened_at). Internal admin: Python management CLI commands: reload-watchlist (force immediate reload), replay-batch --gcs-path --batch-id (trigger historical replay), get-watchlist-stats (current entity count and version). Grafana data source: ClickHouse HTTP endpoint (internal only, GKE cluster network). Redis keys: watchlist_current_version (HASH: version, entity_count, loaded_at).
target_output.security_and_compliance: GKE Workload Identity binds consumer pod service account to GCP IAM role with Kafka API access, GCS bucket read, Memorystore access, and ClickHouse secrets. Kafka mTLS with Confluent Cloud-managed certificates. ClickHouse: originator_name_encrypted and beneficiary_name_encrypted columns use AES-256-GCM; Grafana service account has SELECT on match_candidates and screening_decisions views only (no raw payment_instructions access). OFAC compliance: all match_candidates above 0.80 threshold logged with similarity_score for regulatory audit; false_negative_rate_by_script_type metric tracked in Grafana for research documentation. FinCEN FIN-2020-A002: prototype documents screening coverage rate and latency for regulatory evaluation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform manages GCP resources: GKE Autopilot cluster, Memorystore Redis, GCE ClickHouse instance, GCS buckets for OFAC deltas and replay parquets. GitHub Actions: pytest (consumer, matcher, double-buffer unit tests), Cloud Build Docker image on merge to main, kubectl rolling update to GKE. HorizontalPodAutoscaler scales consumer pods 2-6 based on Kafka consumer lag metric from Strimzi Kafka lag exporter. GKE Autopilot handles node provisioning; no node management required. OFAC SDN update delivery: OFAC publishes delta files to GCS bucket via scheduled Cloud Function that fetches from sdnlist.treasury.gov.
target_output.observability: Prometheus custom metrics from consumer pods: screening_throughput_per_second (counter), match_rate_by_script_type (gauge per script_type label), watchlist_reload_duration_ms (histogram), consumer_lag_ms (gauge, from Kafka consumer group lag via JMX exporter). Grafana dashboards: Screening Performance (throughput, p99 latency, match rate), Watchlist Health (reload timeline, entity count delta, reload duration), Script Type Analysis (match rate and false negative rate by script type for research team), Batch Replay Comparison (match rate comparison across historical batch_ids). PagerDuty: consumer_lag_ms >5000ms (pipeline falling behind), watchlist_reload_duration_ms >60s (abnormal reload), match_rate drop >50% from 1h baseline (potential watchlist corruption).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot consumer pods (3x e2-standard-4) ~$250/month. Confluent Cloud Kafka (standard cluster) ~$200/month. ClickHouse on GCE n2-standard-8 + 500GB local SSD ~$350/month. Memorystore Redis ~$50/month. Total ~$900/month for research environment. Batch replay at 1,400 instructions/second across 3 pods processes 10M instructions in ~2 hours, meeting constraint. HPA scales to 6 pods for sustained high-volume replay without manual intervention.
target_output.phased_implementation: Round 2 deliverables (current sprint): DoubleBufferedWatchlistIndex implementation with unit tests for concurrent read during swap; MultiTierMatcher with script-type detection and per-script thresholds; match_candidates ClickHouse table with similarity_score and script_type columns; Grafana Script Type Analysis dashboard panel. Next phase: calibrate thresholds against expanded OFAC name variant test set; batch replay job implementation for 10M instruction research validation. Final phase: research report documenting false negative rate, threshold sensitivity curves, and architecture recommendation for production implementation.
target_output.development_guidelines: DoubleBufferedWatchlistIndex must have concurrent read tests: spawn 100 threads reading active_index while reload swap executes; assert zero blocking time >1ms during swap. MultiTierMatcher unit tests must cover: Latin name exact match, Latin Jaro-Winkler at 0.92 threshold, Cyrillic transliteration at 0.88, Arabic at 0.85; use OFAC publicly available name variant examples. All match_candidates above 0.80 floor must be logged to ClickHouse regardless of final threshold decision to preserve research data for sensitivity analysis. pytest parametrize for threshold sensitivity tests: run MultiTierMatcher with threshold range 0.80-0.95 in 0.01 steps against OFAC test set and assert false_negative_rate < 0.05 at chosen thresholds.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk (ISS-001 resolved): DoubleBufferedWatchlistIndex atomic swap eliminates screening blackout during OFAC SDN reloads; swap lock duration measured <1ms in benchmark tests; reload_duration_ms metric now accurately reflects rebuild time. Risk (ISS-002 resolved): script-type-aware thresholds reduce Arabic/Cyrillic false negative rate from 12% to target <5%; per-match score logging enables ongoing calibration. Remaining risk: Jaro-Winkler struggles with structured entity names (company names with legal suffixes like LLC, Ltd, GmbH) that are common in KYB-style screening; future work should explore token-based similarity for business entity names. Tradeoff: each consumer pod holds a full in-memory WatchlistIndex (~200MB for full SDN list); at 6 pods this is 1.2GB total watchlist memory, acceptable for GKE Autopilot e2-standard-4 pods with 16GB RAM.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed double-buffered index over distributed Redis-cached index for watchlist (simpler, lower latency). Confirmed GCS bucket as OFAC delta delivery mechanism via Cloud Function polling sdnlist.treasury.gov. Confirmed floor threshold of 0.80 for candidate logging (all candidates above floor recorded for research; only above script-type threshold counted as decision hits). Confirmed per-replica independent WatchlistIndex rebuild (no Redis-based watchlist sharing) with Redis version number for coordination only.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision for OFAC sanctions screening prototype; fixes watchlist hot-reload blocking (ISS-001) via double-buffered index and matching threshold calibration (ISS-002) via script-type-aware Jaro-Winkler.

profile.domain: E-Discovery Database Migration

project_goal.value: Desktop application for litigation support professionals to migrate Concordance MDB and Summation DII databases to EDRM XML or Relativity DAT/OPT load file formats, preserving document family relationships, bates numbering, and coding field mappings
project_goal.rationale: Core business requirement for e-discovery platform transition workflows

target_users.value: Litigation support specialists and e-discovery vendors processing client platform migrations
target_users.rationale: Primary end users identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Self-contained Windows installer for offline operation on attorney workstations

capabilities.rationale: WPF UI, C# migration engine, and SQLite state tracking cover required capabilities

complexity_level.rationale: Complex parsing logic but bounded scope and no network dependencies

risk_level.rationale: Handles privileged attorney-client litigation materials subject to FRCP ESI production obligations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Source databases contain privileged work product and court-ordered production sets

external_exposure.rationale: No network connectivity; operates entirely on the local Windows machine

access_model.value: Local license key enforced at startup; no user accounts or network authentication required
access_model.rationale: Offline desktop deployment precludes central auth; license protects IP

feature_scope.value: Concordance MDB and Summation DII import, field mapping wizard, bates renumbering, EDRM XML export, Relativity DAT/OPT export, document family re-encoding, error and exception report
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for e-discovery migration use case

mvp_scope.value: Concordance MDB import, single field mapping profile, EDRM XML export, error report
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for first client pilot migration

security_baseline.value: License key tamper detection, no telemetry or outbound network calls, temporary work files written to encrypted scratch directory using DPAPI, audit log of migration operations stored locally
security_baseline.rationale: Confidentiality of litigation materials requires zero data exfiltration

frontend_stack.value: WPF with C# .NET 8 and MVVM pattern
frontend_stack.rationale: Team Windows desktop expertise; WPF sufficient for wizard-driven workflow UI

backend_stack.value: C# .NET 8 class libraries for parsing, mapping, and export engine
backend_stack.rationale: Single-language stack reduces context switching; .NET 8 performance for large MDB files

data_platform.value: SQLite 3.45 for migration state tracking and audit log; no external database
data_platform.rationale: Embedded database suitable for offline desktop; no server dependencies

hosting_target.value: Windows desktop MSI installer distributed via vendor portal download
hosting_target.rationale: Self-hosted distribution model; no cloud infrastructure required

privacy_retention_policy.value: No litigation data retained by the tool; source files remain on client machine; audit log retained locally until user clears it
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Attorney-client privilege mandates zero data retention outside client environment

future_scope.value: Summation DII import, cloud-hosted batch migration service, Nuix Workstation export format
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after MVP validation

constraints.value: Must run offline on Windows 10/11; no .NET runtime installation required (self-contained publish); MSI under 150 MB
constraints.rationale: Client security policies prohibit internet-connected migration tools on attorney workstations

observability_baseline.value: Local structured log file (NLog) per migration session; Windows Event Log integration for critical errors; no remote telemetry
observability_baseline.rationale: Offline operation precludes remote observability; local logs sufficient for support tickets

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with milestone delivery to beta testers after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Tight feedback loop with litigation support specialist testers

compliance_context.value: FRCP Rule 34 ESI production format obligations; court-mandated Relativity or EDRM XML specification compliance; attorney-client privilege safeguards for work product in transit
compliance_context.rationale: High risk rating and confidential data sensitivity require explicit compliance framing

project.goal: Migrate Concordance and Summation litigation databases to EDRM XML and Relativity load file formats
project.target_users: Litigation support specialists at e-discovery vendor firms
project.timeline: MVP in 8 weeks, full feature set in 16 weeks

backend.framework: Class libraries with no web framework
backend.parsing: Custom MDB reader via System.Data.OleDb, DII text parser

security.auth: Local license key validation
security.data_handling: DPAPI-encrypted scratch directory, no outbound network

data.db: SQLite 3.45 for migration state and error ledger
data.state: Migration job table tracks document-level status per run

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with .NET 8 SDK
devops.packaging: dotnet publish self-contained, WiX MSI toolchain

reasoner_reviews.product: The wizard-driven workflow covering source selection, field mapping, bates renumbering, and export format choice maps directly to how litigation support specialists operate during platform migrations. The error report is essential for court-production accountability.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The previous plan had a critical gap: it routed temp work files through the Windows %TEMP% directory without DPAPI encryption, leaving privileged litigation materials exposed to other local processes. This round must address that. The SQLite state model is sound but needs a per-job transaction boundary so partial migrations can be resumed without reprocessing.
reasoner_reviews.security: FRCP ESI obligations require that output files are byte-for-byte reproducible given the same input and mapping profile. The plan must document that the export engine is deterministic and that no transformation silently drops metadata fields. DPAPI scratch encryption is non-negotiable.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Self-contained .NET 8 publish keeps the MSI under 150 MB and eliminates runtime prerequisites on attorney workstations. The WiX installer must handle upgrade scenarios without leaving orphaned SQLite state files from prior versions.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The round-1 plan listed Summation DII parsing in MVP scope, contradicting the locked contract. The MVP is Concordance MDB only. The plan also described a 'telemetry heartbeat' which violates the zero-outbound-network constraint. Both issues remain unresolved.

backend.service_design: MigrationEngine class library with three pipeline stages: ConcordanceReader (OleDb MDB traversal), FieldMapper (configurable profile application), and ExportWriter (EDRM XML or Relativity DAT/OPT serializer). Each stage exposes a cancellable async interface so the WPF UI can report progress.
backend.business_modules: DocumentFamilyResolver rebuilds parent-child relationships from Concordance BEGATTACH/ENDATTACH fields. BatesRenumberer applies client-supplied prefix and padding rules. CodingFieldValidator checks for unmappable legacy fields and writes exceptions to the error ledger.
backend.failure_handling: Per-document exception capture writes to error_reports table in SQLite with source path, error code, and unmapped field list. Migration can be configured to halt-on-first-error or continue-and-log mode. Partially completed jobs store last-processed document ID so resume is possible.
backend.backend_risks: OleDb dependency on 32-bit Access ODBC driver may require 32-bit process on modern 64-bit Windows. Mitigate by shipping the Access Database Engine 2016 redistributable in the MSI. Large MDB files (>10 GB) may exhaust address space in 32-bit mode.

frontend.app_structure: WPF MVVM with five wizard ViewModels: SourceSelectionViewModel, FieldMappingViewModel, BatesConfigViewModel, ExportFormatViewModel, and MigrationProgressViewModel. CommunityToolkit.Mvvm for command and property boilerplate.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Source Selection page lets user browse for MDB or DII file and validates it opens successfully. Field Mapping page shows source-field-to-target-field grid with auto-mapped suggestions. Bates Config page captures prefix, start number, and padding length. Export Format page selects EDRM XML or Relativity DAT/OPT. Progress page streams document-count updates and shows live error count with drill-down.
frontend.frontend_risks: WPF DataGrid binding to large document counts (>500K rows) requires virtualisation; use VirtualizingPanel.IsVirtualizing=True and ObservableCollection page-loading pattern to avoid UI thread stalls.

security.auth_design: License key validated against a locally stored HMAC-SHA256 signature at startup. No online activation server. Key encodes expiry date and licensed feature flags.
security.secrets_management: DPAPI ProtectedData.Protect() encrypts the scratch work directory path and any intermediate export buffers. Encryption scope is CurrentUser so files are unreadable if moved to another machine.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: NLog structured log captures migration_job_id, document_id, stage, outcome, and timestamp for every processed document. Log file is written to the user's AppData folder and rotated per migration session.
security.security_risks: License key reverse-engineering risk. Mitigate with .NET obfuscation (Obfuscar) on the engine assembly and short key expiry windows (90 days) requiring renewal.

data.entities: source_databases (id, file_path, format ENUM concordance/summation, doc_count, opened_at), document_families (id, source_db_id, parent_bates, child_bates_list, relationship_type ENUM attachment/embedded), coding_fields (id, source_db_id, legacy_field_name, mapped_target_field, data_type, unmappable_flag), bates_ranges (id, migration_map_id, prefix, start_number, end_number, padding), migration_maps (id, created_at, source_db_id, export_format ENUM edrm_xml/relativity_dat, field_profile_json), export_packages (id, migration_map_id, output_directory, doc_count, error_count, completed_at), error_reports (id, migration_map_id, document_id, stage, error_code, detail, created_at)
data.schema_notes: All tables in a per-job SQLite file stored in AppData. Indexes on document_families.parent_bates and coding_fields.legacy_field_name for fast lookup during family resolution pass.
data.migration_notes: Upgrade installer must migrate SQLite schema using embedded EF Core migrations, and must not delete state files from prior versions so users can reference historical migration logs.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions builds .NET 8 self-contained win-x64 publish, then WiX Toolset v4 packages into a signed MSI. Signing uses a code-signing certificate stored in GitHub Actions secret. Artefact uploaded to vendor portal S3 bucket as versioned release.
devops.ci_cd_design: Pipeline stages: restore, build, unit-test (xUnit), integration-test (MDB fixture files), WiX MSI build, code-sign, upload. PR gate requires all tests green. Release branch triggers full pipeline plus draft GitHub release.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous MSI version retained on vendor portal for 90 days. Users can uninstall current version and reinstall prior MSI; SQLite state files are preserved in AppData across version changes.

ISS-001.title: Temp files written without encryption exposing privileged litigation materials
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan routed intermediate export buffers through %TEMP% without DPAPI protection, leaving privileged documents readable by other local processes on the attorney workstation.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer during round 1 audit

ISS-002.title: MVP scope includes Summation DII import contradicting locked contract
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan listed Summation DII parsing in the MVP delivery phase. The locked contract specifies Concordance MDB only for MVP; Summation is future scope.

ISS-003.title: Telemetry heartbeat violates zero-outbound-network constraint

previous_plan.title: Litigation Database Migration Desktop Tool - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Windows desktop application built with WPF C# .NET 8 to migrate Concordance and Summation litigation databases to EDRM XML and Relativity load file formats for e-discovery platform transitions.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Single-process WPF desktop application with MVVM presentation layer and a C# class library migration engine. SQLite tracks migration state. Optional telemetry ping on startup reports version usage.
previous_plan.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP: Concordance MDB and Summation DII import, EDRM XML export, basic error report. Phase 2: Relativity DAT/OPT export, bates renumbering, cloud batch service.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: License key validation at startup. Temp files written to %TEMP% directory during export. No DPAPI encryption applied to intermediate buffers.
previous_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: OleDb 32-bit driver dependency. Large MDB files may be slow. Telemetry data helps prioritise feature development.

target_output.thinking_summary: This revision plan must resolve three blocking issues from round 1: apply DPAPI encryption to all scratch and temp files (ISS-001), remove Summation DII from MVP scope to align with the locked contract (ISS-002), and eliminate all telemetry/outbound network references (ISS-003). The architecture is a self-contained WPF .NET 8 desktop tool with no server components, so security controls are local only. The plan quality is intentionally flawed: despite fixing ISS-001 and ISS-003, it will retain a subtle contract mismatch by placing Relativity DAT export in MVP scope when the contract specifies EDRM XML only for MVP.

fix_report.action_taken: All intermediate export buffers and scratch work files now use System.Security.Cryptography.ProtectedData.Protect() with DataProtectionScope.CurrentUser before writing to disk. Scratch directory is under AppData/Local/LitMigrate/scratch rather than %TEMP%. Files are decrypted on read and wiped with SecureDelete after migration completes.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Privileged litigation materials are unreadable by other local processes; satisfies FRCP ESI confidentiality obligations on the attorney workstation

fix_report.action_taken: Summation DII import moved from Phase 1 MVP to Phase 2 in phased_implementation. MVP now covers Concordance MDB import only, matching the locked contract mvp_scope field.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan is internally consistent with the frozen contract; no contract violations remain for MVP scope

fix_report.action_taken: All references to telemetry, heartbeat pings, and outbound network calls removed from architecture_overview, deployment_and_operations, and risks_and_tradeoffs. The plan now explicitly states zero outbound network traffic.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan complies with the hard zero-outbound-network constraint; suitable for deployment on air-gapped attorney workstations

target_output.title: Litigation Database Migration Desktop Tool - Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: A FRCP-compliant Windows desktop application built with WPF C# .NET 8 that migrates Concordance MDB litigation databases to EDRM XML format, preserving document family relationships, bates numbering, and coding field mappings. All intermediate processing uses DPAPI-encrypted scratch files, zero outbound network traffic, and produces a per-migration audit log for court-production accountability. MVP covers Concordance MDB import and EDRM XML export; Summation DII and Relativity DAT are Phase 2.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process WPF desktop application following MVVM pattern with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm. The presentation layer hosts a five-step wizard. A C# .NET 8 class library migration engine handles parsing, field mapping, bates renumbering, and EDRM XML serialisation. SQLite 3.45 embedded in AppData stores per-job migration state and error ledger. All temp files written to an AppData scratch directory encrypted via DPAPI CurrentUser scope. Zero outbound network calls; license key validated locally against HMAC-SHA256 signature. No telemetry or remote connectivity of any kind.
target_output.technology_stack: Presentation: WPF C# .NET 8 with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm and MaterialDesignThemes. Engine: C# .NET 8 class libraries, System.Data.OleDb for MDB access (32-bit process via ClickOnce sub-process), XmlWriter for EDRM XML serialisation. State: SQLite 3.45 via Microsoft.Data.Sqlite. Security: System.Security.Cryptography.ProtectedData DPAPI, Obfuscar obfuscation. Build: WiX Toolset v4 MSI, self-contained win-x64 publish. Logging: NLog 5 structured file sink.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Source Selection: browse for Concordance MDB, validate file opens via OleDb, display doc count and field list. Field Mapping Wizard: auto-suggest mappings from legacy field names to EDRM standard fields, allow manual overrides, save named profiles. Bates Configuration: capture prefix string, start number, zero-padding length, apply renumbering to family sets. EDRM XML Export: serialise document metadata, family links, and bates assignments to EDRM 2.0 XML schema. Error and Exception Report: per-document error ledger with unmapped field list, exportable to CSV. Migration State: SQLite job table with resume-capable cursor on last-processed document ID. Note: Relativity DAT/OPT export is Phase 2 and MVP scope.
target_output.system_components: WPF Wizard Shell (five-step MVVM navigator), MigrationEngine library (ConcordanceReader, FieldMapper, BatesRenumberer, EdrmXmlWriter, ErrorLedger), SQLite state database in AppData, DPAPI-encrypted scratch directory under AppData/Local/LitMigrate/scratch, NLog session log file per migration run, WiX MSI installer with Access Database Engine 2016 redistributable bundled, local license key validator (HMAC-SHA256 offline check).
target_output.workflows: Concordance MDB Import and EDRM XML Export: User browses for a .mdb file on the Source Selection page; ConcordanceReader opens it via OleDb, enumerates the document table, and populates the field list. On the Field Mapping page, FieldMapper auto-suggests mappings by comparing legacy field names against EDRM standard field vocabulary; the specialist reviews and adjusts. BatesRenumberer applies the configured prefix and padding to each document in family-sorted order. EdrmXmlWriter streams the EDRM 2.0 XML document, writing a Document element per record with Metadata, Tags, and Files child elements. Any document with an unmappable field is written to the error_reports table and counted in the live progress display. Family Relationship Resolution: DocumentFamilyResolver reads BEGATTACH and ENDATTACH column values from the Concordance MDB, groups records into parent-child sets, and assigns family_id to each group. The EDRM XML output encodes these relationships using the Document element's ParentDocumentID attribute so the receiving Relativity workspace correctly reconstructs attachment families.
target_output.data_model: source_databases (id INTEGER PK, file_path TEXT, format TEXT CHECK format IN ('concordance','summation'), doc_count INTEGER, opened_at DATETIME). document_families (id INTEGER PK, source_db_id FK, parent_bates TEXT, child_bates_list TEXT JSON array, relationship_type TEXT CHECK IN ('attachment','embedded')). coding_fields (id INTEGER PK, source_db_id FK, legacy_field_name TEXT, mapped_target_field TEXT, data_type TEXT, unmappable_flag INTEGER DEFAULT 0). bates_ranges (id INTEGER PK, migration_map_id FK, prefix TEXT, start_number INTEGER, end_number INTEGER, padding INTEGER). migration_maps (id INTEGER PK, created_at DATETIME, source_db_id FK, export_format TEXT CHECK IN ('edrm_xml','relativity_dat'), field_profile_json TEXT). export_packages (id INTEGER PK, migration_map_id FK, output_directory TEXT, doc_count INTEGER, error_count INTEGER, completed_at DATETIME). error_reports (id INTEGER PK, migration_map_id FK, document_id TEXT, stage TEXT, error_code TEXT, detail TEXT, created_at DATETIME). All tables in a per-job SQLite file in AppData/Local/LitMigrate/jobs/.
target_output.api_design: No HTTP API; local in-process interface. IMigrationEngine.StartAsync(MigrationConfig config, IProgress<MigrationProgress> progress, CancellationToken ct): Task<MigrationResult>. MigrationConfig carries source path, FieldMappingProfile, BatesConfig, ExportFormat. MigrationProgress reports docs_processed, docs_errored, current_stage. IMigrationEngine.ResumeAsync(Guid jobId, ...): resumes from last SQLite cursor. ILicenseValidator.Validate(string key): bool. IErrorLedger.GetReport(Guid jobId): IEnumerable<ErrorRecord>. IEdrmXmlWriter.WriteDocument(DocumentRecord doc): void - streaming XmlWriter, no full DOM in memory.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FRCP ESI compliance: output EDRM XML validated against EDRM 2.0 XSD schema before finalisation; export is deterministic given the same input and field mapping profile so reproduction requests can be satisfied. DPAPI: all intermediate buffers and the scratch directory under AppData/Local/LitMigrate/scratch are encrypted with ProtectedData.Protect(DataProtectionScope.CurrentUser); files unreadable if moved to another account or machine. Scratch cleanup: SecureDelete overwrites scratch files with three-pass zero-random-zero wipe after successful migration. License: HMAC-SHA256 offline validation; 90-day expiry encoded in key; Obfuscar obfuscation on engine assembly to deter reverse engineering. Zero network: no outbound calls of any kind; Windows firewall rule in MSI blocks the process from initiating network connections. Audit log: NLog structured session log records migration_job_id, document_id, stage, outcome per document for post-production review by litigation support specialists.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: restore, build (dotnet build -c Release), unit tests (xUnit with MDB fixture files checked into test assets), integration tests against a 10K-document synthetic Concordance MDB, Obfuscar obfuscation pass, dotnet publish --self-contained --runtime win-x64 -p:PublishSingleFile=true, WiX Toolset v4 MSI build bundling Access Database Engine 2016 redistributable, code-sign with EV certificate via Azure Key Vault signing service, artefact upload to vendor portal S3 bucket as versioned release. Installer handles upgrade scenarios: detects prior version, migrates AppData SQLite schema via EF Core embedded migrations, retains historical job files. No server infrastructure; no cloud compute costs beyond CI minutes and S3 storage.
target_output.observability: NLog 5 structured file sink writes one log file per migration session to AppData/Local/LitMigrate/logs/ with fields: timestamp, migration_job_id, document_id, stage, outcome, duration_ms. Windows Event Log integration for Critical-level errors (failed license check, DPAPI failure, OleDb driver missing) so IT administrators can diagnose issues without accessing application logs. Crash dumps written to AppData/Local/LitMigrate/crashes/ using Windows Error Reporting API. No remote monitoring or telemetry; support workflow relies on user-submitted log files attached to support tickets.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud infrastructure cost at runtime; CI pipeline uses GitHub Actions free tier for open-source or ~$0.008/minute for private repo. S3 storage for MSI artefacts under $1/month. Scaling is handled by selling additional seat licenses; the desktop process scales vertically with available RAM and CPU on the attorney workstation. Large MDB files (>5 GB) benefit from 16 GB RAM for in-memory family resolution; recommend minimum spec in documentation.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Concordance MDB import via OleDb, field mapping wizard with auto-suggest, bates renumbering engine, EDRM XML 2.0 export, error and exception report to CSV, DPAPI scratch encryption, local license key validation, NLog session logging, WiX MSI installer with Access Database Engine redistributable, GitHub Actions CI pipeline. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Summation DII text file import parser, Relativity DAT/OPT export writer, saved field mapping profiles, migration resume from last cursor, upgrade installer with schema migration. Phase 3 (future): Cloud-hosted batch migration service for high-volume vendor workflows, Nuix Workstation export format, web-based migration job dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: C# nullable reference types enabled project-wide. xUnit for unit tests; Moq for interface mocking; minimum 75% line coverage on engine library. Integration tests use synthetic MDB and DII fixture files committed to test-assets. No external HTTP calls anywhere in the codebase; CI enforces this via a custom Roslyn analyser. All SQLite schema changes via EF Core migrations; never raw ALTER TABLE in application code. WPF bindings use compiled x:Bind where possible for performance on large document lists. PR requires two reviewers; one must be the senior engineer with Concordance domain knowledge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OleDb 32-bit driver dependency requires launching a 32-bit subprocess on 64-bit Windows; mitigate by shipping Access Database Engine 2016 redistributable in MSI and testing on Windows 10 22H2 and Windows 11 23H2. Risk: Very large MDB files (>10 GB) may exhaust 32-bit address space; mitigate by streaming documents in 1000-record pages rather than loading the full table. Risk: EDRM XML output divergence from court-mandated production spec; mitigate by validating against EDRM 2.0 XSD and providing a spec version selector for different jurisdiction requirements. Tradeoff: DPAPI CurrentUser scope means migration jobs are not portable between machines; document this limitation clearly so specialists do not attempt to share job files across workstations. Known flaw (intentional): Relativity DAT/OPT export is included in the Phase 1 MVP deliverables list in the phased_implementation section despite the locked contract specifying EDRM XML only for MVP — this contract inconsistency is a training signal for the auditor.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected EDRM XML 2.0 over legacy EDRM 1.2 for broader Relativity and Nuix compatibility. Confirmed SQLite per-job file over a shared database to allow parallel migration jobs without locking contention. Chose DPAPI CurrentUser over full-disk encryption for lighter deployment footprint. License key expiry set to 90 days to limit exposure from key leakage.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of e-discovery database migration desktop tool. Fixes ISS-001 (DPAPI encryption), ISS-002 (Summation DII removed from MVP), ISS-003 (telemetry removed), but intentionally retains a subtle contract violation by including Relativity DAT/OPT in Phase 1 MVP scope despite the locked contract specifying EDRM XML only.
